26. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "head -300 /workspace/build.gradle | tail -200",
  "description": "Show middle section of build.gradle"
}
Output
"  userEnableTestCoverage = project.hasProperty(\"enableTestCoverage\") ? enableTestCoverage : false\n\n  userKeepAliveModeString = project.hasProperty(\"keepAliveMode\") ? keepAliveMode : \"daemon\"\n  userKeepAliveMode = KeepAliveMode.values().find(m -> m.name().toLowerCase().equals(userKeepAliveModeString))\n  if (userKeepAliveMode == null) {\n    def keepAliveValues = KeepAliveMode.values().collect(m -> m.name.toLowerCase())\n    throw new GradleException(\"Unexpected value for keepAliveMode property. Expected one of $keepAliveValues, but received: $userKeepAliveModeString\")\n  }\n\n  // See README.md for details on this option and the reasoning for the default\n  userScalaOptimizerMode = project.hasProperty(\"scalaOptimizerMode\") ? scalaOptimizerMode : \"inline-kafka\"\n  def scalaOptimizerValues = [\"none\", \"method\", \"inline-kafka\", \"inline-scala\"]\n  if (!scalaOptimizerValues.contains(userScalaOptimizerMode))\n    throw new GradleException(\"Unexpected value for scalaOptimizerMode property. Expected one of $scalaOptimizerValues, but received: $userScalaOptimizerMode\")\n\n  generatedDocsDir = new File(\"${project.rootDir}/docs/generated\")\n  repo = file(\"$rootDir/.git\").isDirectory() ? Grgit.open(currentDir: project.getRootDir()) : null\n\n  commitId = determineCommitId()\n\n  addParametersForTests = { name, options ->\n    // -parameters generates arguments with parameter names in TestInfo#getDisplayName.\n    // ref: https://github.com/junit-team/junit5/blob/4c0dddad1b96d4a20e92a2cd583954643ac56ac0/junit-jupiter-params/src/main/java/org/junit/jupiter/params/ParameterizedTest.java#L161-L164\n    if (name == \"compileTestJava\" || name == \"compileTestScala\")\n      options.compilerArgs << \"-parameters\"\n  }\n}\n\nallprojects {\n\n  repositories {\n    mavenCentral()\n  }\n\n  dependencyUpdates {\n    revision=\"release\"\n    resolutionStrategy {\n      componentSelection { rules ->\n        rules.all { ComponentSelection selection ->\n          boolean rejected = ['snap', 'alpha', 'beta', 'rc', 'cr', 'm'].any { qualifier ->\n            selection.candidate.version ==~ /(?i).*[.-]${qualifier}[.\\d-]*/\n          }\n          if (rejected) {\n            selection.reject('Release candidate')\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  configurations.all {\n    // zinc is the Scala incremental compiler, it has a configuration for its own dependencies\n    // that are unrelated to the project dependencies, we should not change them\n    if (name != \"zinc\") {\n      resolutionStrategy {\n        force(\n          // be explicit about the javassist dependency version instead of relying on the transitive version\n          libs.javassist,\n          // ensure we have a single version in the classpath despite transitive dependencies\n          libs.scalaLibrary,\n          libs.scalaReflect,\n          libs.jacksonAnnotations,\n          // be explicit about the Netty dependency version instead of relying on the version set by\n          // ZooKeeper (potentially older and containing CVEs)\n          libs.nettyHandler,\n          libs.nettyTransportNativeEpoll,\n          // be explicit about the reload4j version instead of relying on the transitive versions\n          libs.reload4j\n        )\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  task printAllDependencies(type: DependencyReportTask) {}\n\n  tasks.withType(Javadoc) {\n    options.charSet = 'UTF-8'\n    options.docEncoding = 'UTF-8'\n    options.encoding = 'UTF-8'\n    options.memberLevel = JavadocMemberLevel.PUBLIC  // Document only public members/API\n    // Turn off doclint for now, see https://blog.joda.org/2014/02/turning-off-doclint-in-jdk-8-javadoc.html for rationale\n    options.addStringOption('Xdoclint:none', '-quiet')\n    // Javadoc warnings should fail the build in JDK 15+ https://bugs.openjdk.org/browse/JDK-8200363\n    options.addBooleanOption('Werror', JavaVersion.current().isCompatibleWith(JavaVersion.VERSION_15))\n\n    // The URL structure was changed to include the locale after Java 8\n    if (JavaVersion.current().isJava11Compatible())\n      options.links \"https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/${JavaVersion.current().majorVersion}/docs/api/\"\n    else\n      options.links \"https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/\"\n  }\n}\n\ndef determineCommitId() {\n  def takeFromHash = 16\n  if (project.hasProperty('commitId')) {\n    commitId.take(takeFromHash)\n  } else if (repo != null) {\n    repo.head().id.take(takeFromHash)\n  } else {\n    \"unknown\"\n  }\n}\n\n\n\n\napply from: file('wrapper.gradle')\n\nif (repo != null) {\n  rat {\n    dependsOn subprojects.collect {\n      it.tasks.matching {\n        it.name == \"processMessages\" || it.name == \"processTestMessages\"\n      }\n    }\n\n    verbose.set(true)\n    reportDir.set(project.file('build/rat'))\n    stylesheet.set(file('gradle/resources/rat-output-to-html.xsl'))\n\n    // Exclude everything under the directory that git should be ignoring via .gitignore or that isn't checked in. These\n    // restrict us only to files that are checked in or are staged.\n    excludes = new ArrayList<String>(repo.clean(ignore: false, directories: true, dryRun: true))\n    // And some of the files that we have checked in should also be excluded from this check\n    excludes.addAll([\n        '**/.git/**',\n        '**/build/**',\n        'CONTRIBUTING.md',\n        'PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md',\n        'gradlew',\n        'gradlew.bat',\n        'gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.properties',\n        'trogdor/README.md',\n        '**/README.md',\n        '**/id_rsa',\n        '**/id_rsa.pub',\n        'checkstyle/suppressions.xml',\n        'streams/quickstart/java/src/test/resources/projects/basic/goal.txt',\n        'streams/streams-scala/logs/*',\n        'licenses/*',\n        '**/generated/**',\n        'clients/src/test/resources/serializedData/*',\n        'docker/test/fixtures/secrets/*',\n        'docker/examples/fixtures/secrets/*',\n        'docker/docker_official_images/.gitkeep'\n    ])\n  }\n} else {\n  rat.enabled = false\n}\nprintln(\"Starting build with version $version (commit id ${commitId == null ? \"null\" : commitId.take(8)}) using Gradle $gradleVersion, Java ${JavaVersion.current()} and Scala ${versions.scala}\")\nprintln(\"Build properties: maxParallelForks=$maxTestForks, maxScalacThreads=$maxScalacThreads, maxTestRetries=$userMaxTestRetries\")\n\nsubprojects {\n\n  // enable running :dependencies task recursively on all subprojects\n  // eg: ./gradlew allDeps\n  task allDeps(type: DependencyReportTask) {}\n  // enable running :dependencyInsight task recursively on all subprojects\n  // eg: ./gradlew allDepInsight --configuration runtime --dependency com.fasterxml.jackson.core:jackson-databind\n  task allDepInsight(type: DependencyInsightReportTask) {showingAllVariants = false} doLast {}\n\n  apply plugin: 'java-library'\n  apply plugin: 'checkstyle'\n  apply plugin: \"com.github.spotbugs\"\n\n  // We use the shadow plugin for the jmh-benchmarks module and the `-all` jar can get pretty large, so\n  // don't publish it\n  def shouldPublish = !project.name.equals('jmh-benchmarks')\n  def shouldPublishWithShadow = (['clients'].contains(project.name))\n\n  if (shouldPublish) {\n    apply plugin: 'maven-publish'\n    apply plugin: 'signing'\n\n    // Add aliases for the task names used by the maven plugin for backwards compatibility\n    // The maven plugin was replaced by the maven-publish plugin in Gradle 7.0\n    tasks.register('install').configure { dependsOn(publishToMavenLocal) }\n    tasks.register('uploadArchives').configure { dependsOn(publish) }\n  }\n\n  // apply the eclipse plugin only to subprojects that hold code. 'connect' is just a folder.\n  if (!project.name.equals('connect')) {\n    apply plugin: 'eclipse'\n    fineTuneEclipseClasspathFile(eclipse, project)\n  }\n\n  java {\n    consistentResolution {\n      // resolve the compileClasspath and then \"inject\" the result of resolution as strict constraints into the runtimeClasspath\n      useCompileClasspathVersions()\n    }\n  }\n\n  tasks.withType(JavaCompile) {\n    options.encoding = 'UTF-8'\n    options.compilerArgs << \"-Xlint:all\"\n    // temporary exclusions until all the warnings are fixed\n    if (!project.path.startsWith(\":connect\") && !project.path.startsWith(\":storage\"))\n      options.compilerArgs << \"-Xlint:-rawtypes\""

Output
"     1\u2192# Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n     2\u2192# contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with\n     3\u2192# this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n     4\u2192# The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n     5\u2192# (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n     6\u2192# the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192#\n     8\u2192#    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192#\n    10\u2192# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192# distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192# limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192#\n    17\u2192# This configuration file is intended for use in ZK-based mode, where Apache ZooKeeper is required.\n    18\u2192# See kafka.server.KafkaConfig for additional details and defaults\n    19\u2192#\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192############################# Server Basics #############################\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192# The id of the broker. This must be set to a unique integer for each broker.\n    24\u2192broker.id=0\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192############################# Socket Server Settings #############################\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192# The address the socket server listens on. If not configured, the host name will be equal to the value of\n    29\u2192# java.net.InetAddress.getCanonicalHostName(), with PLAINTEXT listener name, and port 9092.\n    30\u2192#   FORMAT:\n    31\u2192#     listeners = listener_name://host_name:port\n    32\u2192#   EXAMPLE:\n    33\u2192#     listeners = PLAINTEXT://your.host.name:9092\n    34\u2192#listeners=PLAINTEXT://:9092\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192# Listener name, hostname and port the broker will advertise to clients.\n    37\u2192# If not set, it uses the value for \"listeners\".\n    38\u2192#advertised.listeners=PLAINTEXT://your.host.name:9092\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192# Maps listener names to security protocols, the default is for them to be the same. See the config documentation for more details\n    41\u2192#listener.security.protocol.map=PLAINTEXT:PLAINTEXT,SSL:SSL,SASL_PLAINTEXT:SASL_PLAINTEXT,SASL_SSL:SASL_SSL\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192# The number of threads that the server uses for receiving requests from the network and sending responses to the network\n    44\u2192num.network.threads=3\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192# The number of threads that the server uses for processing requests, which may include disk I/O\n    47\u2192num.io.threads=8\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192# The send buffer (SO_SNDBUF) used by the socket server\n    50\u2192socket.send.buffer.bytes=102400\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192# The receive buffer (SO_RCVBUF) used by the socket server\n    53\u2192socket.receive.buffer.bytes=102400\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192# The maximum size of a request that the socket server will accept (protection against OOM)\n    56\u2192socket.request.max.bytes=104857600\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192############################# Log Basics #############################\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192# A comma separated list of directories under which to store log files\n    62\u2192log.dirs=/tmp/kafka-logs\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192# The default number of log partitions per topic. More partitions allow greater\n    65\u2192# parallelism for consumption, but this will also result in more files across\n    66\u2192# the brokers.\n    67\u2192num.partitions=1\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192# The number of threads per data directory to be used for log recovery at startup and flushing at shutdown.\n    70\u2192# This value is recommended to be increased for installations with data dirs located in RAID array.\n    71\u2192num.recovery.threads.per.data.dir=1\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192############################# Internal Topic Settings  #############################\n    74\u2192# The replication factor for the group metadata internal topics \"__consumer_offsets\" and \"__transaction_state\"\n    75\u2192# For anything other than development testing, a value greater than 1 is recommended to ensure availability such as 3.\n    76\u2192offsets.topic.replication.factor=1\n    77\u2192transaction.state.log.replication.factor=1\n    78\u2192transaction.state.log.min.isr=1\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192############################# Log Flush Policy #############################\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192# Messages are immediately written to the filesystem but by default we only fsync() to sync\n    83\u2192# the OS cache lazily. The following configurations control the flush of data to disk.\n    84\u2192# There are a few important trade-offs here:\n    85\u2192#    1. Durability: Unflushed data may be lost if you are not using replication.\n    86\u2192#    2. Latency: Very large flush intervals may lead to latency spikes when the flush does occur as there will be a lot of data to flush.\n    87\u2192#    3. Throughput: The flush is generally the most expensive operation, and a small flush interval may lead to excessive seeks.\n    88\u2192# The settings below allow one to configure the flush policy to flush data after a period of time or\n    89\u2192# every N messages (or both). This can be done globally and overridden on a per-topic basis.\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192# The number of messages to accept before forcing a flush of data to disk\n    92\u2192#log.flush.interval.messages=10000\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192# The maximum amount of time a message can sit in a log before we force a flush\n    95\u2192#log.flush.interval.ms=1000\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192############################# Log Retention Policy #############################\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192# The following configurations control the disposal of log segments. The policy can\n   100\u2192# be set to delete segments after a period of time, or after a given size has accumulated.\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
39. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/README.md",
  "limit": 150
}
Output
"     1\u2192Apache Kafka\n     2\u2192=================\n     3\u2192See our [web site](https://kafka.apache.org) for details on the project.\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192You need to have [Java](http://www.oracle.com/technetwork/java/javase/downloads/index.html) installed.\n     6\u2192\n     7\u2192We build and test Apache Kafka with Java 8, 11, 17 and 21. We set the `release` parameter in javac and scalac\n     8\u2192to `8` to ensure the generated binaries are compatible with Java 8 or higher (independently of the Java version\n     9\u2192used for compilation). Java 8 support project-wide has been deprecated since Apache Kafka 3.0, Java 11 support for\n    10\u2192the broker and tools has been deprecated since Apache Kafka 3.7 and removal of both is planned for Apache Kafka 4.0 (\n    11\u2192see [KIP-750](https://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=181308223) and\n    12\u2192[KIP-1013](https://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=284789510) for more details).\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192Scala 2.12 and 2.13 are supported and 2.13 is used by default. Scala 2.12 support has been deprecated since\n    15\u2192Apache Kafka 3.0 and will be removed in Apache Kafka 4.0 (see [KIP-751](https://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=181308218)\n    16\u2192for more details). See below for how to use a specific Scala version or all of the supported Scala versions.\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192### Build a jar and run it ###\n    19\u2192    ./gradlew jar\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192Follow instructions in https://kafka.apache.org/quickstart\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192### Build source jar ###\n    24\u2192    ./gradlew srcJar\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192### Build aggregated javadoc ###\n    27\u2192    ./gradlew aggregatedJavadoc\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192### Build javadoc and scaladoc ###\n    30\u2192    ./gradlew javadoc\n    31\u2192    ./gradlew javadocJar # builds a javadoc jar for each module\n    32\u2192    ./gradlew scaladoc\n    33\u2192    ./gradlew scaladocJar # builds a scaladoc jar for each module\n    34\u2192    ./gradlew docsJar # builds both (if applicable) javadoc and scaladoc jars for each module\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192### Run unit/integration tests ###\n    37\u2192    ./gradlew test # runs both unit and integration tests\n    38\u2192    ./gradlew unitTest\n    39\u2192    ./gradlew integrationTest\n    40\u2192    \n    41\u2192### Force re-running tests without code change ###\n    42\u2192    ./gradlew test --rerun\n    43\u2192    ./gradlew unitTest --rerun\n    44\u2192    ./gradlew integrationTest --rerun\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192### Running a particular unit/integration test ###\n    47\u2192    ./gradlew clients:test --tests RequestResponseTest\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192### Repeatedly running a particular unit/integration test ###\n    50\u2192    I=0; while ./gradlew clients:test --tests RequestResponseTest --rerun --fail-fast; do (( I=$I+1 )); echo \"Completed run: $I\"; sleep 1; done\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192### Running a particular test method within a unit/integration test ###\n    53\u2192    ./gradlew core:test --tests kafka.api.ProducerFailureHandlingTest.testCannotSendToInternalTopic\n    54\u2192    ./gradlew clients:test --tests org.apache.kafka.clients.MetadataTest.testTimeToNextUpdate\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192### Running a particular unit/integration test with log4j output ###\n    57\u2192By default, there will be only small number of logs output while testing. You can adjust it by changing the `log4j.properties` file in the module's `src/test/resources` directory.\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192For example, if you want to see more logs for clients project tests, you can modify [the line](https://github.com/apache/kafka/blob/trunk/clients/src/test/resources/log4j.properties#L21) in `clients/src/test/resources/log4j.properties` \n    60\u2192to `log4j.logger.org.apache.kafka=INFO` and then run:\n    61\u2192    \n    62\u2192    ./gradlew cleanTest clients:test --tests NetworkClientTest   \n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192And you should see `INFO` level logs in the file under the `clients/build/test-results/test` directory.\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192### Specifying test retries ###\n    67\u2192By default, each failed test is retried once up to a maximum of five retries per test run. Tests are retried at the end of the test task. Adjust these parameters in the following way:\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192    ./gradlew test -PmaxTestRetries=1 -PmaxTestRetryFailures=5\n    70\u2192    \n    71\u2192See [Test Retry Gradle Plugin](https://github.com/gradle/test-retry-gradle-plugin) for more details.\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192### Generating test coverage reports ###\n    74\u2192Generate coverage reports for the whole project:\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192    ./gradlew reportCoverage -PenableTestCoverage=true -Dorg.gradle.parallel=false\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192Generate coverage for a single module, i.e.: \n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192    ./gradlew clients:reportCoverage -PenableTestCoverage=true -Dorg.gradle.parallel=false\n    81\u2192    \n    82\u2192### Building a binary release gzipped tar ball ###\n    83\u2192    ./gradlew clean releaseTarGz\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192The release file can be found inside `./core/build/distributions/`.\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192### Building auto generated messages ###\n    88\u2192Sometimes it is only necessary to rebuild the RPC auto-generated message data when switching between branches, as they could\n    89\u2192fail due to code changes. You can just run:\n    90\u2192 \n    91\u2192    ./gradlew processMessages processTestMessages\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192### Running a Kafka broker in KRaft mode\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192Using compiled files:\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192    KAFKA_CLUSTER_ID=\"$(./bin/kafka-storage.sh random-uuid)\"\n    98\u2192    ./bin/kafka-storage.sh format -t $KAFKA_CLUSTER_ID -c config/kraft/server.properties\n    99\u2192    ./bin/kafka-server-start.sh config/kraft/server.properties\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192Using docker image:\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192    docker run -p 9092:9092 apache/kafka:3.7.0\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192### Running a Kafka broker in ZooKeeper mode\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192Using compiled files:\n   108\u2192\n   109\u2192    ./bin/zookeeper-server-start.sh config/zookeeper.properties\n   110\u2192    ./bin/kafka-server-start.sh config/server.properties\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192>Since ZooKeeper mode is already deprecated and planned to be removed in Apache Kafka 4.0, the docker image only supports running in KRaft mode\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192### Cleaning the build ###\n   115\u2192    ./gradlew clean\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192### Running a task with one of the Scala versions available (2.12.x or 2.13.x) ###\n   118\u2192*Note that if building the jars with a version other than 2.13.x, you need to set the `SCALA_VERSION` variable or change it in `bin/kafka-run-class.sh` to run the quick start.*\n   119\u2192\n   120\u2192You can pass either the major version (eg 2.12) or the full version (eg 2.12.7):\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192    ./gradlew -PscalaVersion=2.12 jar\n   123\u2192    ./gradlew -PscalaVersion=2.12 test\n   124\u2192    ./gradlew -PscalaVersion=2.12 releaseTarGz\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192### Running a task with all the scala versions enabled by default ###\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192Invoke the `gradlewAll` script followed by the task(s):\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192    ./gradlewAll test\n   131\u2192    ./gradlewAll jar\n   132\u2192    ./gradlewAll releaseTarGz\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192### Running a task for a specific project ###\n   135\u2192This is for `core`, `examples` and `clients`\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192    ./gradlew core:jar\n   138\u2192    ./gradlew core:test\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192Streams has multiple sub-projects, but you can run all the tests:\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192    ./gradlew :streams:testAll\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192### Listing all gradle tasks ###\n   145\u2192    ./gradlew tasks\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192### Building IDE project ####\n   148\u2192*Note that this is not strictly necessary (IntelliJ IDEA has good built-in support for Gradle projects, for example).*\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192    ./gradlew eclipse\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

1 claude claude  25874 Feb 24 00:31 BrokerLifecycleManager.scala\n-rw-r--r-- 1 claude claude  31320 Feb 24 00:31 BrokerServer.scala\n-rw-r--r-- 1 claude claude  28895 Feb 24 00:31 51. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/config/kraft/server.properties",
  "limit": 100
}
Output
"     1\u2192# Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n     2\u2192# contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with\n     3\u2192# this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n     4\u2192# The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n     5\u2192# (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n     6\u2192# the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192#\n     8\u2192#    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192#\n    10\u2192# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192# distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192# limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192#\n    17\u2192# This configuration file is intended for use in KRaft mode, where\n    18\u2192# Apache ZooKeeper is not present.\n    19\u2192#\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192############################# Server Basics #############################\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192# The role of this server. Setting this puts us in KRaft mode\n    24\u2192process.roles=broker,controller\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192# The node id associated with this instance's roles\n    27\u2192node.id=1\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192# The connect string for the controller quorum\n    30\u2192controller.quorum.voters=1@localhost:9093\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192############################# Socket Server Settings #############################\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192# The address the socket server listens on.\n    35\u2192# Combined nodes (i.e. those with `process.roles=broker,controller`) must list the controller listener here at a minimum.\n    36\u2192# If the broker listener is not defined, the default listener will use a host name that is equal to the value of java.net.InetAddress.getCanonicalHostName(),\n    37\u2192# with PLAINTEXT listener name, and port 9092.\n    38\u2192#   FORMAT:\n    39\u2192#     listeners = listener_name://host_name:port\n    40\u2192#   EXAMPLE:\n    41\u2192#     listeners = PLAINTEXT://your.host.name:9092\n    42\u2192listeners=PLAINTEXT://:9092,CONTROLLER://:9093\n    43\u2192\n    44\u2192# Name of listener used for communication between brokers.\n    45\u2192inter.broker.listener.name=PLAINTEXT\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192# Listener name, hostname and port the broker or the controller will advertise to clients.\n    48\u2192# If not set, it uses the value for \"listeners\".\n    49\u2192advertised.listeners=PLAINTEXT://localhost:9092,CONTROLLER://localhost:9093\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192# A comma-separated list of the names of the listeners used by the controller.\n    52\u2192# If no explicit mapping set in `listener.security.protocol.map`, default will be using PLAINTEXT protocol\n    53\u2192# This is required if running in KRaft mode.\n    54\u2192controller.listener.names=CONTROLLER\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192# Maps listener names to security protocols, the default is for them to be the same. See the config documentation for more details\n    57\u2192listener.security.protocol.map=CONTROLLER:PLAINTEXT,PLAINTEXT:PLAINTEXT,SSL:SSL,SASL_PLAINTEXT:SASL_PLAINTEXT,SASL_SSL:SASL_SSL\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192# The number of threads that the server uses for receiving requests from the network and sending responses to the network\n    60\u2192num.network.threads=3\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192# The number of threads that the server uses for processing requests, which may include disk I/O\n    63\u2192num.io.threads=8\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192# The send buffer (SO_SNDBUF) used by the socket server\n    66\u2192socket.send.buffer.bytes=102400\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192# The receive buffer (SO_RCVBUF) used by the socket server\n    69\u2192socket.receive.buffer.bytes=102400\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192# The maximum size of a request that the socket server will accept (protection against OOM)\n    72\u2192socket.request.max.bytes=104857600\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192############################# Log Basics #############################\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192# A comma separated list of directories under which to store log files\n    78\u2192log.dirs=/tmp/kraft-combined-logs\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192# The default number of log partitions per topic. More partitions allow greater\n    81\u2192# parallelism for consumption, but this will also result in more files across\n    82\u2192# the brokers.\n    83\u2192num.partitions=1\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192# The number of threads per data directory to be used for log recovery at startup and flushing at shutdown.\n    86\u2192# This value is recommended to be increased for installations with data dirs located in RAID array.\n    87\u2192num.recovery.threads.per.data.dir=1\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192############################# Internal Topic Settings  #############################\n    90\u2192# The replication factor for the group metadata internal topics \"__consumer_offsets\" and \"__transaction_state\"\n    91\u2192# For anything other than development testing, a value greater than 1 is recommended to ensure availability such as 3.\n    92\u2192offsets.topic.replication.factor=1\n    93\u2192transaction.state.log.replication.factor=1\n    94\u2192transaction.state.log.min.isr=1\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192############################# Log Flush Policy #############################\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192# Messages are immediately written to the filesystem but by default we only fsync() to sync\n    99\u2192# the OS cache lazily. The following configurations control the flush of data to disk.\n   100\u2192# There are a few important trade-offs here:\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

65. Task @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Trace topic creation flow end-to-end",
  "prompt": "Explore the Apache Kafka codebase to understand how topic creation works. Please trace the complete flow from client to broker:\n\n1. **Client-Side Topic Creation:**\n   - Look for TopicCommand class in /workspace/tools/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/tools/\n   - Find the CreateTopic implementation\n   - Identify what request it sends (likely CreateTopicsRequest)\n\n2. **Request Handling:**\n   - Find CreateTopicsRequest class (likely in clients/src/main/java)\n   - Locate the server-side handler (likely in core/src/main/scala/kafka/server)\n   - Look for KafkaApis.scala or similar request handler\n\n3. **Broker-Side Topic Creation:**\n   - Find ReplicaManager or TopicHandler classes\n   - Look for LogManager.createLog() or similar methods\n   - Trace the actual partition and log creation\n\n4. **Metadata Updates:**\n   - Find how broker updates cluster metadata (ZooKeeper or KRaft)\n   - Look for controller communication\n   - Find topic state management\n\nKey search patterns:\n- \"CreateTopic\" to find topic creation logic\n- \"CreateTopicsRequest\" to find request definitions\n- \"createTopic\" or \"createLog\" methods\n- Files with names like *TopicCommand*, *Topics*, *CreateTopics*\n- ReplicaManager, LogManager, AdminManager classes",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "a1bb062bb058901cf",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive analysis. Let me create the final report:\n\n## Apache Kafka Topic Creation Flow - Complete Analysis\n\nI've traced the complete flow of topic creation in Apache Kafka 3.9.0. Here's the comprehensive breakdown from client to broker to storage:\n\n### 1. CLIENT-SIDE TOPIC CREATION\n\n**File:** `/workspace/tools/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/tools/TopicCommand.java`\n\nThe client-side entry point uses the `TopicService` class which uses the Kafka Admin API:\n\n```java\npublic void createTopic(TopicCommandOptions opts) throws Exception {\n    CommandTopicPartition topic = new CommandTopicPartition(opts);\n    createTopic(topic);\n}\n\npublic void createTopic(CommandTopicPartition topic) throws Exception {\n    NewTopic newTopic;\n    if (topic.hasReplicaAssignment()) {\n        newTopic = new NewTopic(topic.name, topic.replicaAssignment);\n    } else {\n        newTopic = new NewTopic(topic.name, topic.partitions, \n            topic.replicationFactor.map(Integer::shortValue));\n    }\n    \n    Map<String, String> configsMap = topic.configsToAdd.stringPropertyNames()\n        .stream()\n        .collect(Collectors.toMap(name -> name, \n            name -> topic.configsToAdd.getProperty(name)));\n    \n    newTopic.configs(configsMap);\n    CreateTopicsResult createResult = adminClient.createTopics(\n        Collections.singleton(newTopic),\n        new CreateTopicsOptions().retryOnQuotaViolation(false));\n    createResult.all().get();\n    System.out.println(\"Created topic \" + topic.name + \".\");\n}\n```\n\nKey classes:\n- `NewTopic` - encapsulates topic configuration\n- `CreateTopicsOptions` - options for creation (quota violation handling)\n- `Admin` client - handles communication with broker\n\n---\n\n### 2. REQUEST/RESPONSE PROTOCOL\n\n**Request:** `/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/requests/CreateTopicsRequest.java`\n\n```java\npublic class CreateTopicsRequest extends AbstractRequest {\n    private final CreateTopicsRequestData data;\n    \n    public CreateTopicsRequest(CreateTopicsRequestData data, short version) {\n        super(ApiKeys.CREATE_TOPICS, version);\n        this.data = data;\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Response:** `/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/requests/CreateTopicsResponse.java`\n\nPossible error codes:\n- `REQUEST_TIMED_OUT(7)`\n- `INVALID_TOPIC_EXCEPTION(17)`\n- `TOPIC_AUTHORIZATION_FAILED(29)`\n- `TOPIC_ALREADY_EXISTS(36)`\n- `INVALID_PARTITIONS(37)`\n- `INVALID_REPLICATION_FACTOR(38)`\n- `INVALID_REPLICA_ASSIGNMENT(39)`\n- `INVALID_CONFIG(40)`\n- `NOT_CONTROLLER(41)`\n\n---\n\n### 3. BROKER-SIDE REQUEST HANDLING\n\n**File:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaApis.scala`\n\nThe broker routes CREATE_TOPICS requests with special handling:\n\n```scala\ncase ApiKeys.CREATE_TOPICS => \n    maybeForwardToController(request, handleCreateTopicsRequest)\n```\n\n**Handler:** `handleCreateTopicsRequest` (line 2002-2098)\n\nThe flow:\n1. Checks if controller is active\n2. Validates authorization (CLUSTER or TOPIC CREATE permission)\n3. Filters out internal topics (e.g., `__cluster_metadata`)\n4. Calls `adminManager.createTopics()` with metadata callback\n\n```scala\ndef handleCreateTopicsRequest(request: RequestChannel.Request): Unit = {\n    val zkSupport = metadataSupport.requireZkOrThrow(...)\n    val controllerMutationQuota = quotas.controllerMutation.newQuotaFor(...)\n    \n    // ... validation checks ...\n    \n    zkSupport.adminManager.createTopics(\n        createTopicsRequest.data.timeoutMs,\n        createTopicsRequest.data.validateOnly,\n        toCreate,\n        authorizedForDescribeConfigs,\n        controllerMutationQuota,\n        handleCreateTopicsResults)\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 4. ADMIN MANAGER - TOPIC CREATION ORCHESTRATION\n\n**File:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/ZkAdminManager.scala`\n\nThe `createTopics` method (lines 159-258) performs the core topic creation logic:\n\n```scala\ndef createTopics(timeout: Int,\n                 validateOnly: Boolean,\n                 toCreate: Map[String, CreatableTopic],\n                 includeConfigsAndMetadata: Map[String, CreatableTopicResult],\n                 controllerMutationQuota: ControllerMutationQuota,\n                 responseCallback: Map[String, ApiError] => Unit): Unit = {\n\n    // 1. Validate and prepare metadata for each topic\n    val brokers = metadataCache.getAliveBrokers()\n    val metadata = toCreate.values.map(topic => {\n        // Check topic doesn't already exist\n        if (metadataCache.contains(topic.name))\n            throw new TopicExistsException(...)\n        \n        // Resolve replica assignments (auto or manual)\n        val assignments = if (topic.assignments.isEmpty) {\n            // Auto-assign replicas to brokers\n            AdminUtils.assignReplicasToBrokers(\n                brokers, resolvedNumPartitions, resolvedReplicationFactor)\n        } else {\n            // Use provided manual assignments\n            topic.assignments.forEach { assignment =>\n                assignments(assignment.partitionIndex) = \n                    assignment.brokerIds.asScala.map(a => a: Int)\n            }\n        }\n        \n        // Validate assignment and configuration\n        adminZkClient.validateTopicCreate(topic.name, assignments, configs)\n        validateTopicCreatePolicy(...)\n        \n        // Populate metadata and configs for response\n        maybePopulateMetadataAndConfigs(..., topic.name, configs, assignments)\n        \n        if (validateOnly) {\n            CreatePartitionsMetadata(topic.name, assignments.keySet)\n        } else {\n            // Write to ZooKeeper\n            controllerMutationQuota.record(assignments.size)\n            adminZkClient.createTopicWithAssignment(\n                topic.name, configs, assignments, validate = false)\n            populateIds(...)\n            CreatePartitionsMetadata(topic.name, assignments.keySet)\n        }\n    }).toBuffer\n    \n    // 2. Handle immediate responses or delayed operations\n    if (timeout <= 0 || validateOnly || !metadata.exists(...)) {\n        // Immediate response\n        responseCallback(results)\n    } else {\n        // 3. Create delayed operation and watch for partition leaders\n        val delayedCreate = new DelayedCreatePartitions(\n            timeout, metadata, this, responseCallback)\n        val delayedCreateKeys = toCreate.values.map(topic => TopicKey(topic.name))\n        topicPurgatory.tryCompleteElseWatch(delayedCreate, delayedCreateKeys)\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Key responsibilities:**\n- Validate topic doesn't exist\n- Calculate replica assignments (or use provided ones)\n- Write topic configs to ZooKeeper\n- Create partition assignments in ZooKeeper\n- Queue delayed operation to wait for leader election\n\n---\n\n### 5. ZOOKEEPER WRITES\n\n**File:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/zk/AdminZkClient.scala`\n\n```scala\ndef createTopicWithAssignment(topic: String,\n                              config: Properties,\n                              partitionReplicaAssignment: Map[Int, Seq[Int]],\n                              validate: Boolean = true,\n                              usesTopicId: Boolean = false): Unit = {\n    \n    if (validate)\n        validateTopicCreate(topic, partitionReplicaAssignment, config)\n    \n    // Write topic configuration\n    zkClient.setOrCreateEntityConfigs(ConfigType.TOPIC, topic, config)\n    \n    // Create partition assignment in ZooKeeper\n    writeTopicPartitionAssignment(topic, \n        partitionReplicaAssignment.map { case (k, v) => \n            k -> ReplicaAssignment(v) \n        },\n        isUpdate = false, \n        usesTopicId)\n}\n\nprivate def writeTopicPartitionAssignment(topic: String, \n                                          replicaAssignment: Map[Int, ReplicaAssignment],\n                                          isUpdate: Boolean, \n                                          usesTopicId: Boolean = false): Unit = {\n    val assignment = replicaAssignment.map { \n        case (partitionId, replicas) => \n            (new TopicPartition(topic, partitionId), replicas) \n    }.toMap\n    \n    if (!isUpdate) {\n        val topicIdOpt = if (usesTopicId) Some(Uuid.randomUuid()) else None\n        zkClient.createTopicAssignment(topic, topicIdOpt, \n            assignment.map { case (k, v) => k -> v.replicas })\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**ZooKeeper paths created:**\n- `/config/topics/{topic-name}` - topic configuration\n- `/brokers/topics/{topic-name}` - partition assignment\n- Topic ID is stored as part of the assignment\n\n---\n\n### 6. DELAYED OPERATION - WAITING FOR LEADERS\n\n**File:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/DelayedCreatePartitions.scala`\n\nOnce the metadata is written to ZooKeeper, a `DelayedCreatePartitions` operation waits for all partition leaders to be elected:\n\n```scala\nclass DelayedCreatePartitions(delayMs: Long,\n                              createMetadata: Seq[CreatePartitionsMetadata],\n                              adminManager: ZkAdminManager,\n                              responseCallback: Map[String, ApiError] => Unit)\n  extends DelayedOperation(delayMs) {\n  \n  override def tryComplete(): Boolean = {\n    val leaderlessPartitionCount = createMetadata\n        .filter(_.error.isSuccess)\n        .foldLeft(0) { case (topicCounter, metadata) =>\n            topicCounter + missingLeaderCount(metadata.topic, metadata.partitions)\n        }\n    \n    if (leaderlessPartitionCount == 0) {\n        trace(\"All partitions have a leader, completing the delayed operation\")\n        forceComplete()\n    } else {\n        false\n    }\n  }\n  \n  override def onComplete(): Unit = {\n    val results = createMetadata.map { metadata =>\n        if (metadata.error.isSuccess && \n            missingLeaderCount(metadata.topic, metadata.partitions) > 0)\n            (metadata.topic, new ApiError(Errors.REQUEST_TIMED_OUT, null))\n        else\n            (metadata.topic, metadata.error)\n    }.toMap\n    responseCallback(results)\n  }\n  \n  private def isMissingLeader(topic: String, partition: Int): Boolean = {\n    val partitionInfo = adminManager.metadataCache.getPartitionInfo(topic, partition)\n    partitionInfo.forall(_.leader == LeaderAndIsr.NoLeader)\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Completion criteria:**\n- All partitions have elected leaders\n- OR timeout expires\n- OR error occurs\n\n---\n\n### 7. CONTROLLER - PARTITION LEADER ELECTION\n\nWhen ZooKeeper watches trigger (via `/brokers/topics/{topic}` change):\n\n1. **KafkaController** detects new topic partitions\n2. Assigns partition leaders from replica list (typically first replica)\n3. Sends **LeaderAndIsrRequest** to brokers with new partition state\n4. Brokers acknowledge with partition logs\n\n---\n\n### 8. PARTITION AND LOG CREATION ON BROKERS\n\n**File:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/log/LogManager.scala`\n\nWhen `LeaderAndIsrRequest` arrives, brokers create the actual log storage via `getOrCreateLog`:\n\n```scala\ndef getOrCreateLog(topicPartition: TopicPartition, \n                   isNew: Boolean = false, \n                   isFuture: Boolean = false,\n                   topicId: Option[Uuid], \n                   targetLogDirectoryId: Option[Uuid] = Option.empty): UnifiedLog = {\n    \n    logCreationOrDeletionLock synchronized {\n        val log = getLog(topicPartition, isFuture).getOrElse {\n            if (!isNew && offlineLogDirs.nonEmpty)\n                throw new KafkaStorageException(...)\n            \n            // Determine log directory\n            val logDirs: List[File] = {\n                // Prefer specified directory or use next available\n                if (preferredLogDir != null)\n                    List(new File(preferredLogDir))\n                else\n                    nextLogDirs()  // Round-robin across data directories\n            }\n            \n            val logDirName = if (isFuture)\n                UnifiedLog.logFutureDirName(topicPartition)\n            else\n                UnifiedLog.logDirName(topicPartition)\n            \n            // Create: /log.dir/{topic}-{partition}/\n            val logDir = logDirs.map(createLogDirectory(_, logDirName))\n                .find(_.isSuccess).get\n            \n            // Create UnifiedLog instance\n            val config = fetchLogConfig(topicPartition.topic)\n            val log = UnifiedLog(\n                dir = logDir,\n                config = config,\n                logStartOffset = 0L,\n                recoveryPoint = 0L,\n                maxTransactionTimeoutMs = maxTransactionTimeoutMs,\n                producerStateManagerConfig = producerStateManagerConfig,\n                scheduler = scheduler,\n                time = time,\n                brokerTopicStats = brokerTopicStats,\n                logDirFailureChannel = logDirFailureChannel,\n                topicId = topicId,\n                keepPartitionMetadataFile = keepPartitionMetadataFile,\n                remoteStorageSystemEnable = remoteStorageSystemEnable)\n            \n            currentLogs.put(topicPartition, log)\n            info(s\"Created log for partition $topicPartition in $logDir\")\n            log\n        }\n        \n        // Assign topic ID if needed\n        if (log.topicId.isEmpty) {\n            topicId.foreach(log.assignTopicId)\n        }\n        \n        log\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Files created on disk:**\n- `/log.dir/{topic}-{partition}/` directory\n- Index files (`.index`, `.timeindex`)\n- Log segment files (`.log`)\n- Leader epoch cache (`.leader-epoch-checkpoint`)\n\n---\n\n### 9. REPLICA MANAGER - LEADERSHIP ESTABLISHMENT\n\n**File:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/ReplicaManager.scala`\n\n```scala\ndef becomeLeaderOrFollower(correlationId: Int,\n                           leaderAndIsrRequest: LeaderAndIsrRequest,\n                           onLeadershipChange: (...) => Unit): LeaderAndIsrResponse = {\n    // Process partition state changes\n    val partitionStates = request.partitionStates() // Map of TopicPartition -> LeaderAndIsrPartitionState\n    \n    val (leaderPartitions, followerPartitions) = \n        partitionStates.partition { case (_, state) =>\n            state.leader == brokerId\n        }\n    \n    // Make this broker leader for assigned partitions\n    val makeLeadersResult = makeLeaders(controllerId, controllerEpoch, \n        leaderPartitions, correlationId)\n    \n    // Make this broker follower for other partitions  \n    val makeFollowersResult = makeFollowers(controllerId, controllerEpoch,\n        followerPartitions, leaderPartitions, correlationId)\n    \n    // Returns response with partition errors\n}\n```\n\nFor each new partition, the ReplicaManager calls LogManager.getOrCreateLog() to create the storage.\n\n---\n\n### 10. METADATA UPDATES - ZOOKEEPER TO BROKERS\n\n**Metadata cache update flow:**\n\n1. **ZooKeeper watcher** detects `/brokers/topics/{topic}` node creation\n2. **KafkaController** reads assignment from ZK\n3. **Controller sends LeaderAndIsrRequest** to all replicas with leader assignment\n4. **Brokers update MetadataCache** with new partition metadata\n5. **Brokers create partition logs** via LogManager\n6. **Brokers reply to LeaderAndIsrRequest**\n7. **Controller updates ISR** (in-sync replicas) in ZK after replicas ACK\n\n---\n\n### 11. COMPLETE TOPIC CREATION SEQUENCE DIAGRAM\n\n```\nClient (TopicCommand)\n  |\n  +---> Admin API\n         |\n         +---> CreateTopicsRequest\n                |\n                v\n         Broker (KafkaApis)\n          |\n          +---> Check if controller active\n          |\n          +---> Authorize request\n          |\n          +---> ZkAdminManager.createTopics()\n                |\n                +---> Validate topic doesn't exist\n                |\n                +---> Calculate replica assignments\n                |\n                +---> Write to ZooKeeper:\n                |     - /config/topics/{topic}\n                |     - /brokers/topics/{topic}\n                |\n                +---> Return results with timeout\n                |\n                +---> DelayedCreatePartitions waits for leaders\n                      |\n                      v\n                KafkaController (watches ZK)\n                |\n                +---> Detects new topic in ZK\n                |\n                +---> Selects leader (first replica)\n                |\n                +---> Sends LeaderAndIsrRequest to brokers\n                      |\n                      v\n                All Brokers (replicas)\n                |\n                +---> Process LeaderAndIsrRequest\n                |\n                +---> Call LogManager.getOrCreateLog()\n                |\n                +---> Create log directories and files:\n                |     - {log.dir}/{topic}-{partition}/\n                |     - Index and segment files\n                |\n                +---> Broker becomes leader/follower\n                |\n                +---> Reply to LeaderAndIsrRequest\n                |\n                v\n                Controller updates ISR in ZK\n                |\n                v\n                MetadataCache updated on all brokers\n                |\n                v\n         DelayedCreatePartitions completes\n         |\n         +---> Sends CreateTopicsResponse to client\n                |\n                v\n            Client receives response\n```\n\n---\n\n### KEY FILES INVOLVED\n\n1. **Client:**\n   - `/workspace/tools/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/tools/TopicCommand.java` - CLI tool\n   - `/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/requests/CreateTopicsRequest.java` - Request protocol\n\n2. **Broker Request Handling:**\n   - `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaApis.scala` - Request dispatcher (line 208, 2002)\n\n3. **Topic Creation Logic:**\n   - `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/ZkAdminManager.scala` - Core creation orchestration (line 159)\n   - `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/zk/AdminZkClient.scala` - ZK operations (line 102)\n\n4. **Delayed Operations:**\n   - `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/DelayedCreatePartitions.scala` - Leader election waiter\n\n5. **Log Storage:**\n   - `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/log/LogManager.scala` - Log creation (line 1033)\n   - `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/cluster/Partition.scala` - Partition state management\n\n6. **Replica Management:**\n   - `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/ReplicaManager.scala` - Leadership election\n\n---\n\nThis flow ensures atomic, durable topic creation with proper leader election and metadata consistency across the cluster.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Explore the Apache Kafka codebase to understand how topic creation works. Please trace the complete flow from client to broker:\n\n1. **Client-Side Topic Creation:**\n   - Look for TopicCommand class in /workspace/tools/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/tools/\n   - Find the CreateTopic implementation\n   - Identify what request it sends (likely CreateTopicsRequest)\n\n2. **Request Handling:**\n   - Find CreateTopicsRequest class (likely in clients/src/main/java)\n   - Locate the server-side handler (likely in core/src/main/scala/kafka/server)\n   - Look for KafkaApis.scala or similar request handler\n\n3. **Broker-Side Topic Creation:**\n   - Find ReplicaManager or TopicHandler classes\n   - Look for LogManager.createLog() or similar methods\n   - Trace the actual partition and log creation\n\n4. **Metadata Updates:**\n   - Find how broker updates cluster metadata (ZooKeeper or KRaft)\n   - Look for controller communication\n   - Find topic state management\n\nKey search patterns:\n- \"CreateTopic\" to find topic creation logic\n- \"CreateTopicsRequest\" to find request definitions\n- \"createTopic\" or \"createLog\" methods\n- Files with names like *TopicCommand*, *Topics*, *CreateTopics*\n- ReplicaManager, LogManager, AdminManager classes",

java.util.Arrays;\n    63\u2192import java.util.Collection;\n    64\u2192import java.util.Collections;\n    65\u2192import java.util.Comparator;\n    66\u2192import java.util.HashSet;\n
