org.apache.kafka.server.common.AdminOperationException\n    50\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.config.{ConfigType, QuotaConfigs, ZooKeeperInternals}\n    51\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.config.ServerLogConfigs.CREATE_TOPIC_POLICY_CLASS_NAME_CONFIG\n    52\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.config.ServerLogConfigs.ALTER_CONFIG_POLICY_CLASS_NAME_CONFIG\n    53\u2192import org.apache.kafka.storage.internals.log.LogConfig\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192import scala.collection.{Map, mutable, _}\n    56\u2192import scala.jdk.CollectionConverters._\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192object ZkAdminManager {\n    59\u2192  def clientQuotaPropsToDoubleMap(props: Map[String, String]): Map[String, Double] = {\n    60\u2192    props.map { case (key, value) =>\n    61\u2192      val doubleValue = try value.toDouble catch {\n    62\u2192        case _: NumberFormatException =>\n    63\u2192          throw new IllegalStateException(s\"Unexpected client quota configuration value: $key -> $value\")\n    64\u2192      }\n    65\u2192      key -> doubleValue\n    66\u2192    }\n    67\u2192  }\n    68\u2192}\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192class ZkAdminManager(val config: KafkaConfig,\n    72\u2192                     val metrics: Metrics,\n    73\u2192                     val metadataCache: MetadataCache,\n    74\u2192                     val zkClient: KafkaZkClient) extends Logging {\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192  this.logIdent = \"[Admin Manager on Broker \" + config.brokerId + \"]: \"\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192  private val topicPurgatory = DelayedOperationPurgatory[DelayedOperation](\"topic\", config.brokerId)\n    79\u2192  private val adminZkClient = new AdminZkClient(zkClient, Some(config))\n    80\u2192  private val configHelper = new ConfigHelper(metadataCache, config, new ZkConfigRepository(adminZkClient))\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192  private val createTopicPolicy =\n    83\u2192    Option(config.getConfiguredInstance(CREATE_TOPIC_POLICY_CLASS_NAME_CONFIG, classOf[CreateTopicPolicy]))\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192  private val alterConfigPolicy =\n    86\u2192    Option(config.getConfiguredInstance(ALTER_CONFIG_POLICY_CLASS_NAME_CONFIG, classOf[AlterConfigPolicy]))\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192  def hasDelayedTopicOperations: Boolean = topicPurgatory.numDelayed != 0\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192  private val defaultNumPartitions = config.numPartitions.intValue()\n    91\u2192  private val defaultReplicationFactor = config.defaultReplicationFactor.shortValue()\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192  /**\n    94\u2192    * Try to complete delayed topic operations with the request key\n    95\u2192    */\n    96\u2192  def tryCompleteDelayedTopicOperations(topic: String): Unit = {\n    97\u2192    val key = TopicKey(topic)\n    98\u2192    val completed = topicPurgatory.checkAndComplete(key)\n    99\u2192    debug(s\"Request key ${key.keyLabel} unblocked $completed topic requests.\")\n   100\u2192  }\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192  private def validateTopicCreatePolicy(topic: CreatableTopic,\n   103\u2192                                        resolvedNumPartitions: Int,\n   104\u2192                                        resolvedReplicationFactor: Short,\n   105\u2192                                        assignments: Map[Int, Seq[Int]]): Unit = {\n   106\u2192    createTopicPolicy.foreach { policy =>\n   107\u2192      // Use `null` for unset fields in the public API\n   108\u2192      val numPartitions: java.lang.Integer =\n   109\u2192        if (topic.assignments().isEmpty) resolvedNumPartitions else null\n   110\u2192      val replicationFactor: java.lang.Short =\n   111\u2192        if (topic.assignments().isEmpty) resolvedReplicationFactor else null\n   112\u2192      val javaAssignments = if (topic.assignments().isEmpty) {\n   113\u2192        null\n   114\u2192      } else {\n   115\u2192        assignments.map { case (k, v) =>\n   116\u2192          (k: java.lang.Integer) -> v.map(i => i: java.lang.Integer).asJava\n   117\u2192        }.asJava\n   118\u2192      }\n   119\u2192      val javaConfigs = new java.util.HashMap[String, String]\n   120\u2192      topic.configs.forEach(config => javaConfigs.put(config.name, config.value))\n   121\u2192      policy.validate(new RequestMetadata(topic.name, numPartitions, replicationFactor,\n   122\u2192        javaAssignments, javaConfigs))\n   123\u2192    }\n   124\u2192  }\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192  private def maybePopulateMetadataAndConfigs(metadataAndConfigs: Map[String, CreatableTopicResult],\n   127\u2192                                              topicName: String,\n   128\u2192                                              configs: Properties,\n   129\u2192                                              assignments: Map[Int, Seq[Int]]): Unit = {\n   130\u2192    metadataAndConfigs.get(topicName).foreach { result =>\n   131\u2192      val logConfig = LogConfig.fromProps(config.extractLogConfigMap, configs)\n   132\u2192      val createEntry = configHelper.createTopicConfigEntry(logConfig, configs, includeSynonyms = false, includeDocumentation = false)(_, _)\n   133\u2192      val topicConfigs = configHelper.allConfigs(logConfig).map { case (k, v) =>\n   134\u2192        val entry = createEntry(k, v)\n   135\u2192        new CreatableTopicConfigs()\n   136\u2192          .setName(k)\n   137\u2192          .setValue(entry.value)\n   138\u2192          .setIsSensitive(entry.isSensitive)\n   139\u2192          .setReadOnly(entry.readOnly)\n   140\u2192          .setConfigSource(entry.configSource)\n   141\u2192      }.toList.asJava\n   142\u2192      result.setConfigs(topicConfigs)\n   143\u2192      result.setNumPartitions(assignments.size)\n   144\u2192      result.setReplicationFactor(assignments(0).size.toShort)\n   145\u2192    }\n   146\u2192  }\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192  private def populateIds(metadataAndConfigs: Map[String, CreatableTopicResult],\n   149\u2192                                              topicName: String) : Unit = {\n   150\u2192    metadataAndConfigs.get(topicName).foreach { result =>\n   151\u2192        result.setTopicId(zkClient.getTopicIdsForTopics(Predef.Set(result.name())).getOrElse(result.name(), Uuid.ZERO_UUID))\n   152\u2192    }\n   153\u2192  }\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192  /**\n   156\u2192    * Create topics and wait until the topics have been completely created.\n   157\u2192    * The callback function will be triggered either when timeout, error or the topics are created.\n   158\u2192    */\n   159\u2192  def createTopics(timeout: Int,\n   160\u2192                   validateOnly: Boolean,\n   161\u2192                   toCreate: Map[String, CreatableTopic],\n   162\u2192                   includeConfigsAndMetadata: Map[String, CreatableTopicResult],\n   163\u2192                   controllerMutationQuota: ControllerMutationQuota,\n   164\u2192                   responseCallback: Map[String, ApiError] => Unit): Unit = {\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192    // 1. map over topics creating assignment and calling zookeeper\n   167\u2192    val brokers = metadataCache.getAliveBrokers()\n   168\u2192    val metadata = toCreate.values.map(topic =>\n   169\u2192      try {\n   170\u2192        if (metadataCache.contains(topic.name))\n   171\u2192          throw new TopicExistsException(s\"Topic '${topic.name}' already exists.\")\n   172\u2192\n   173\u2192        val nullConfigs = topic.configs.asScala.filter(_.value == null).map(_.name)\n   174\u2192        if (nullConfigs.nonEmpty)\n   175\u2192          throw new InvalidConfigurationException(s\"Null value not supported for topic configs: ${nullConfigs.mkString(\",\")}\")\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192        if ((topic.numPartitions != NO_NUM_PARTITIONS || topic.replicationFactor != NO_REPLICATION_FACTOR)\n   178\u2192            && !topic.assignments().isEmpty) {\n   179\u2192          throw new InvalidRequestException(\"Both numPartitions or replicationFactor and replicasAssignments were set. \" +\n   180\u2192            \"Both cannot be used at the same time.\")\n   181\u2192        }\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192        val resolvedNumPartitions = if (topic.numPartitions == NO_NUM_PARTITIONS)\n   184\u2192          defaultNumPartitions else topic.numPartitions\n   185\u2192        val resolvedReplicationFactor = if (topic.replicationFactor == NO_REPLICATION_FACTOR)\n   186\u2192          defaultReplicationFactor else topic.replicationFactor\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192        val assignments = if (topic.assignments.isEmpty) {\n   189\u2192          CoreUtils.replicaToBrokerAssignmentAsScala(AdminUtils.assignReplicasToBrokers(\n   190\u2192            brokers.asJavaCollection, resolvedNumPartitions, resolvedReplicationFactor))\n   191\u2192        } else {\n   192\u2192          val assignments = new mutable.HashMap[Int, Seq[Int]]\n   193\u2192          // Note: we don't check that replicaAssignment contains unknown brokers - unlike in add-partitions case,\n   194\u2192          // this follows the existing logic in TopicCommand\n   195\u2192          topic.assignments.forEach { assignment =>\n   196\u2192            assignments(assignment.partitionIndex) = assignment.brokerIds.asScala.map(a => a: Int)\n   197\u2192          }\n   198\u2192          assignments\n   199\u2192        }\n   200\u2192        trace(s\"Assignments for topic $topic are $assignments \")\n   201\u2192\n   202\u2192        val configs = new Properties()\n   203\u2192        topic.configs.forEach(entry => configs.setProperty(entry.name, entry.value))\n   204\u2192        adminZkClient.validateTopicCreate(topic.name, assignments, configs)\n   205\u2192        validateTopicCreatePolicy(topic, resolvedNumPartitions, resolvedReplicationFactor, assignments)\n   206\u2192\n   207\u2192        // For responses with DescribeConfigs permission, populate metadata and configs. It is\n   208\u2192        // safe to populate it before creating the topic because the values are unset if the\n   209\u2192        // creation fails.\n   210\u2192        maybePopulateMetadataAndConfigs(includeConfigsAndMetadata, topic.name, configs, assignments)\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192        if (validateOnly) {\n   213\u2192          CreatePartitionsMetadata(topic.name, assignments.keySet)\n   214\u2192        } else {\n   215\u2192          controllerMutationQuota.record(assignments.size)\n   216\u2192          adminZkClient.createTopicWithAssignment(topic.name, configs, assignments, validate = false, config.usesTopicId)\n   217\u2192          populateIds(includeConfigsAndMetadata, topic.name)\n   218\u2192          CreatePartitionsMetadata(topic.name, assignments.keySet)\n   219\u2192        }\n   220\u2192      } catch {\n   221\u2192        // Log client errors at a lower level than unexpected exceptions\n   222\u2192        case e: TopicExistsException =>\n   223\u2192          debug(s\"Topic creation failed since topic '${topic.name}' already exists.\", e)\n   224\u2192          CreatePartitionsMetadata(topic.name, e)\n   225\u2192        case e: ThrottlingQuotaExceededException =>\n   226\u2192          debug(s\"Topic creation not allowed because quota is violated. Delay time: ${e.throttleTimeMs}\")\n   227\u2192          CreatePartitionsMetadata(topic.name, e)\n   228\u2192        case e: ApiException =>\n   229\u2192          info(s\"Error processing create topic request $topic\", e)\n   230\u2192          CreatePartitionsMetadata(topic.name, e)\n   231\u2192        case e: ConfigException =>\n   232\u2192          info(s\"Error processing create topic request $topic\", e)\n   233\u2192          CreatePartitionsMetadata(topic.name, new InvalidConfigurationException(e.getMessage, e.getCause))\n   234\u2192        case e: Throwable =>\n   235\u2192          error(s\"Error processing create topic request $topic\", e)\n   236\u2192          CreatePartitionsMetadata(topic.name, e)\n   237\u2192      }).toBuffer\n   238\u2192\n   239\u2192    // 2. if timeout <= 0, validateOnly or no topics can proceed return immediately\n   240\u2192    if (timeout <= 0 || validateOnly || !metadata.exists(_.error.is(Errors.NONE))) {\n   241\u2192      val results = metadata.map { createTopicMetadata =>\n   242\u2192        // ignore topics that already have errors\n   243\u2192        if (createTopicMetadata.error.isSuccess && !validateOnly) {\n   244\u2192          (createTopicMetadata.topic, new ApiError(Errors.REQUEST_TIMED_OUT, null))\n   245\u2192        } else {\n   246\u2192          (createTopicMetadata.topic, createTopicMetadata.error)\n   247\u2192        }\n   248\u2192      }.toMap\n   249\u2192      responseCallback(results)\n   250\u2192    } else {\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"   250\u2192    } else {\n   251\u2192      // 3. else pass the assignments and errors to the delayed operation and set the keys\n   252\u2192      val delayedCreate = new DelayedCreatePartitions(timeout, metadata, this,\n   253\u2192        responseCallback)\n   254\u2192      val delayedCreateKeys = toCreate.values.map(topic => TopicKey(topic.name)).toBuffer\n   255\u2192      // try to complete the request immediately, otherwise put it into the purgatory\n   256\u2192      topicPurgatory.tryCompleteElseWatch(delayedCreate, delayedCreateKeys)\n   257\u2192    }\n   258\u2192  }\n   259\u2192\n   260\u2192  /**\n   261\u2192    * Delete topics and wait until the topics have been completely deleted.\n   262\u2192    * The callback function will be triggered either when timeout, error or the topics are deleted.\n   263\u2192    */\n   264\u2192  def deleteTopics(timeout: Int,\n   265\u2192                   topics: Set[String],\n   266\u2192                   controllerMutationQuota: ControllerMutationQuota,\n   267\u2192                   responseCallback: Map[String, Errors] => Unit): Unit = {\n   268\u2192    // 1. map over topics calling the asynchronous delete\n   269\u2192    val metadata = topics.map { topic =>\n   270\u2192        try {\n   271\u2192          controllerMutationQuota.record(metadataCache.numPartitions(topic).getOrElse(0).toDouble)\n   272\u2192          adminZkClient.deleteTopic(topic)\n   273\u2192          DeleteTopicMetadata(topic, Errors.NONE)\n   274\u2192        } catch {\n   275\u2192          case _: TopicAlreadyMarkedForDeletionException =>\n   276\u2192            // swallow the exception, and still track deletion allowing multiple calls to wait for deletion\n   277\u2192            DeleteTopicMetadata(topic, Errors.NONE)\n   278\u2192          case e: ThrottlingQuotaExceededException =>\n   279\u2192            debug(s\"Topic deletion not allowed because quota is violated. Delay time: ${e.throttleTimeMs}\")\n   280\u2192            DeleteTopicMetadata(topic, e)\n   281\u2192          case e: Throwable =>\n   282\u2192            error(s\"Error processing delete topic request for topic $topic\", e)\n   283\u2192            DeleteTopicMetadata(topic, e)\n   284\u2192        }\n   285\u2192    }\n   286\u2192\n   287\u2192    // 2. if timeout <= 0 or no topics can proceed return immediately\n   288\u2192    if (timeout <= 0 || !metadata.exists(_.error == Errors.NONE)) {\n   289\u2192      val results = metadata.map { deleteTopicMetadata =>\n   290\u2192        // ignore topics that already have errors\n   291\u2192        if (deleteTopicMetadata.error == Errors.NONE) {\n   292\u2192          (deleteTopicMetadata.topic, Errors.REQUEST_TIMED_OUT)\n   293\u2192        } else {\n   294\u2192          (deleteTopicMetadata.topic, deleteTopicMetadata.error)\n   295\u2192        }\n   296\u2192      }.toMap\n   297\u2192      responseCallback(results)\n   298\u2192    } else {\n   299\u2192      // 3. else pass the topics and errors to the delayed operation and set the keys\n   300\u2192      val delayedDelete = new DelayedDeleteTopics(timeout, metadata.toSeq, this, responseCallback)\n   301\u2192      val delayedDeleteKeys = topics.map(TopicKey).toSeq\n   302\u2192      // try to complete the request immediately, otherwise put it into the purgatory\n   303\u2192      topicPurgatory.tryCompleteElseWatch(delayedDelete, delayedDeleteKeys)\n   304\u2192    }\n   305\u2192  }\n   306\u2192\n   307\u2192  def createPartitions(timeoutMs: Int,\n   308\u2192                       newPartitions: Seq[CreatePartitionsTopic],\n   309\u2192                       validateOnly: Boolean,\n   310\u2192                       controllerMutationQuota: ControllerMutationQuota,\n   311\u2192                       callback: Map[String, ApiError] => Unit): Unit = {\n   312\u2192    val allBrokers = adminZkClient.getBrokerMetadatas()\n   313\u2192    val allBrokerIds = allBrokers.map(_.id)\n   314\u2192\n   315\u2192    // 1. map over topics creating assignment and calling AdminUtils\n   316\u2192    val metadata = newPartitions.map { newPartition =>\n   317\u2192      val topic = newPartition.name\n   318\u2192\n   319\u2192      try {\n   320\u2192        val existingAssignment = zkClient.getFullReplicaAssignmentForTopics(immutable.Set(topic)).map {\n   321\u2192          case (topicPartition, assignment) =>\n   322\u2192            if (assignment.isBeingReassigned) {\n   323\u2192              // We prevent adding partitions while topic reassignment is in progress, to protect from a race condition\n   324\u2192              // between the controller thread processing reassignment update and createPartitions(this) request.\n   325\u2192              throw new ReassignmentInProgressException(s\"A partition reassignment is in progress for the topic '$topic'.\")\n   326\u2192            }\n   327\u2192            topicPartition.partition -> assignment\n   328\u2192        }\n   329\u2192        if (existingAssignment.isEmpty)\n   330\u2192          throw new UnknownTopicOrPartitionException(s\"The topic '$topic' does not exist.\")\n   331\u2192\n   332\u2192        val oldNumPartitions = existingAssignment.size\n   333\u2192        val newNumPartitions = newPartition.count\n   334\u2192        val numPartitionsIncrement = newNumPartitions - oldNumPartitions\n   335\u2192        if (numPartitionsIncrement < 0) {\n   336\u2192          throw new InvalidPartitionsException(\n   337\u2192            s\"Topic currently has $oldNumPartitions partitions, which is higher than the requested $newNumPartitions.\")\n   338\u2192        } else if (numPartitionsIncrement == 0) {\n   339\u2192          throw new InvalidPartitionsException(s\"Topic already has $oldNumPartitions partitions.\")\n   340\u2192        }\n   341\u2192\n   342\u2192        val newPartitionsAssignment = Option(newPartition.assignments).map { assignmentMap =>\n   343\u2192          val assignments = assignmentMap.asScala.map {\n   344\u2192            createPartitionAssignment => createPartitionAssignment.brokerIds.asScala.map(_.toInt)\n   345\u2192          }\n   346\u2192          val unknownBrokers = assignments.flatten.toSet -- allBrokerIds\n   347\u2192          if (unknownBrokers.nonEmpty)\n   348\u2192            throw new InvalidReplicaAssignmentException(\n   349\u2192              s\"Unknown broker(s) in replica assignment: ${unknownBrokers.mkString(\", \")}.\")\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. "   100\u2192   * @param usesTopicId Boolean indicating whether the topic ID will be created\n   101\u2192   */\n   102\u2192  def createTopicWithAssignment(topic: String,\n   103\u2192                                config: Properties,\n   104\u2192                                partitionReplicaAssignment: Map[Int, Seq[Int]],\n   105\u2192                                validate: Boolean = true,\n   106\u2192                                usesTopicId: Boolean = false): Unit = {\n   107\u2192    if (validate)\n   108\u2192      validateTopicCreate(topic, partitionReplicaAssignment, config)\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192    info(s\"Creating topic $topic with configuration $config and initial partition \" +\n   111\u2192      s\"assignment $partitionReplicaAssignment\")\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192    // write out the config if there is any, this isn't transactional with the partition assignments\n   114\u2192    zkClient.setOrCreateEntityConfigs(ConfigType.TOPIC, topic, config)\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192    // create the partition assignment\n   117\u2192    writeTopicPartitionAssignment(topic, partitionReplicaAssignment.map { case (k, v) => k -> ReplicaAssignment(v) },\n   118\u2192      isUpdate = false, usesTopicId)\n   119\u2192  }\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192  /**\n   122\u2192   * Validate topic creation parameters. Note that this method is indirectly used by the\n   123\u2192   * TopicCommand via the `createTopicWithAssignment` method.\n   124\u2192   *\n   125\u2192   * @param topic The name of the topic\n   126\u2192   * @param partitionReplicaAssignment The assignments of the topic\n   127\u2192   * @param config The config of the topic\n   128\u2192   */\n   129\u2192  def validateTopicCreate(topic: String,\n   130\u2192                          partitionReplicaAssignment: Map[Int, Seq[Int]],\n   131\u2192                          config: Properties): Unit = {\n   132\u2192    Topic.validate(topic)\n   133\u2192    if (zkClient.isTopicMarkedForDeletion(topic)) {\n   134\u2192      throw new TopicExistsException(s\"Topic '$topic' is marked for deletion.\")\n   135\u2192    }\n   136\u2192    if (zkClient.topicExists(topic))\n   137\u2192      throw new TopicExistsException(s\"Topic '$topic' already exists.\")\n   138\u2192    else if (Topic.hasCollisionChars(topic)) {\n   139\u2192      val allTopics = zkClient.getAllTopicsInCluster()\n   140\u2192      // check again in case the topic was created in the meantime, otherwise the\n   141\u2192      // topic could potentially collide with itself\n   142\u2192      if (allTopics.contains(topic))\n   143\u2192        throw new TopicExistsException(s\"Topic '$topic' already exists.\")\n   144\u2192      val collidingTopics = allTopics.filter(Topic.hasCollision(topic, _))\n   145\u2192      if (collidingTopics.nonEmpty) {\n   146\u2192        throw new InvalidTopicException(s\"Topic '$topic' collides with existing topics: ${collidingTopics.mkString(\", \")}\")\n   147\u2192      }\n   148\u2192    }\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192    if (partitionReplicaAssignment.values.map(_.size).toSet.size != 1)\n   151\u2192      throw new InvalidReplicaAssignmentException(\"All partitions should have the same number of replicas\")\n   152\u2192\n   153\u2192    partitionReplicaAssignment.values.foreach(reps =>\n   154\u2192      if (reps.size != reps.toSet.size)\n   155\u2192        throw new InvalidReplicaAssignmentException(\"Duplicate replica assignment found: \" + partitionReplicaAssignment)\n   156\u2192    )\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192    val partitionSize = partitionReplicaAssignment.size\n   159\u2192    val sequenceSum = partitionSize * (partitionSize - 1) / 2\n   160\u2192    if (partitionReplicaAssignment.size != partitionReplicaAssignment.toSet.size ||\n   161\u2192        partitionReplicaAssignment.keys.filter(_ >= 0).sum != sequenceSum)\n   162\u2192        throw new InvalidReplicaAssignmentException(\"partitions should be a consecutive 0-based integer sequence\")\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192    LogConfig.validate(Collections.emptyMap(), config,\n   165\u2192      kafkaConfig.map(_.extractLogConfigMap).getOrElse(Collections.emptyMap()),\n   166\u2192      kafkaConfig.exists(_.remoteLogManagerConfig.isRemoteStorageSystemEnabled()),\n   167\u2192      true)\n   168\u2192  }\n   169\u2192\n   170\u2192  private def writeTopicPartitionAssignment(topic: String, replicaAssignment: Map[Int, ReplicaAssignment],\n   171\u2192                                            isUpdate: Boolean, usesTopicId: Boolean = false): Unit = {\n   172\u2192    try {\n   173\u2192      val assignment = replicaAssignment.map { case (partitionId, replicas) => (new TopicPartition(topic,partitionId), replicas) }.toMap\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192      if (!isUpdate) {\n   176\u2192        val topicIdOpt = if (usesTopicId) Some(Uuid.randomUuid()) else None\n   177\u2192        zkClient.createTopicAssignment(topic, topicIdOpt, assignment.map { case (k, v) => k -> v.replicas })\n   178\u2192      } else {\n   179\u2192        val topicIds = zkClient.getTopicIdsForTopics(Set(topic))\n   180\u2192        zkClient.setTopicAssignment(topic, topicIds.get(topic), assignment)\n   181\u2192      }\n   182\u2192      debug(\"Updated path %s with %s for replica assignment\".format(TopicZNode.path(topic), assignment))\n   183\u2192    } catch {\n   184\u2192      case _: NodeExistsException => throw new TopicExistsException(s\"Topic '$topic' already exists.\")\n   185\u2192      case e2: Throwable => throw new AdminOperationException(e2.toString)\n   186\u2192    }\n   187\u2192  }\n   188\u2192\n   189\u2192  /**\n   190\u2192   * Creates a delete path for a given topic\n   191\u2192   * @param topic Topic name to delete\n   192\u2192   */\n   193\u2192  def deleteTopic(topic: String): Unit = {\n   194\u2192    if (zkClient.topicExists(topic)) {\n   195\u2192      try {\n   196\u2192        zkClient.createDeleteTopicPath(topic)\n   197\u2192      } catch {\n   198\u2192        case _: NodeExistsException => throw new TopicAlreadyMarkedForDeletionException(\n   199\u2192          \"topic %s is already marked for deletion\".format(topic))\n   200\u2192        case e: Throwable => throw new AdminOperationException(e.getMessage)\n   201\u2192       }\n   202\u2192    } else {\n   203\u2192      throw new UnknownTopicOrPartitionException(s\"Topic `$topic` to delete does not exist\")\n   204\u2192    }\n   205\u2192  }\n   206\u2192\n   207\u2192  /**\n   208\u2192   * Add partitions to existing topic with optional replica assignment. Note that this\n   209\u2192   * method is used by the TopicCommand.\n   210\u2192   *\n   211\u2192   * @param topic Topic for adding partitions to\n   212\u2192   * @param existingAssignment A map from partition id to its assignment\n   213\u2192   * @param allBrokers All brokers in the cluster\n   214\u2192   * @param numPartitions Number of partitions to be set\n   215\u2192   * @param replicaAssignment Manual replica assignment, or none\n   216\u2192   * @param validateOnly If true, validate the parameters without actually adding the partitions\n   217\u2192   * @return the updated replica assignment\n   218\u2192   */\n   219\u2192  def addPartitions(topic: String,\n   220\u2192                    existingAssignment: Map[Int, ReplicaAssignment],\n   221\u2192                    allBrokers: Seq[BrokerMetadata],\n   222\u2192                    numPartitions: Int = 1,\n   223\u2192                    replicaAssignment: Option[Map[Int, Seq[Int]]] = None,\n   224\u2192                    validateOnly: Boolean = false): Map[Int, Seq[Int]] = {\n   225\u2192\n   226\u2192    val proposedAssignmentForNewPartitions = createNewPartitionsAssignment(\n   227\u2192      topic,\n   228\u2192      existingAssignment,\n   229\u2192      allBrokers,\n   230\u2192      numPartitions,\n   231\u2192      replicaAssignment\n   232\u2192    )\n   233\u2192\n   234\u2192    if (validateOnly) {\n   235\u2192      (existingAssignment ++ proposedAssignmentForNewPartitions)\n   236\u2192        .map { case (k, v) => k -> v.replicas }\n   237\u2192    } else {\n   238\u2192      createPartitionsWithAssignment(topic, existingAssignment, proposedAssignmentForNewPartitions)\n   239\u2192        .map { case (k, v) => k -> v.replicas }\n   240\u2192    }\n   241\u2192  }\n   242\u2192\n   243\u2192  /**\n   244\u2192   * Create assignment to add the given number of partitions while validating the\n   245\u2192   * provided arguments.\n   246\u2192   *\n   247\u2192   * @param topic Topic for adding partitions to\n   248\u2192   * @param existingAssignment A map from partition id to its assignment\n   249\u2192   * @param allBrokers All brokers in the cluster\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

org.apache.kafka.metadata.properties.{MetaProperties, MetaPropertiesEnsemble, PropertiesUtils}\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192import java.util.{OptionalLong, Properties}\n    44\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.common.MetadataVersion\n    45\u2192import org.apache.kafka.storage.internals.log.LogConfig.MessageFormatVersion\n    46\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.metrics.KafkaMetricsGroup\n    47\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.util.{FileLock, Scheduler}\n    48\u2192import org.apache.kafka.storage.internals.log.{CleanerConfig, LogConfig, LogDirFailureChannel, ProducerStateManagerConfig, RemoteIndexCache}\n    49\u2192import org.apache.kafka.storage.internals.checkpoint.CleanShutdownFileHandler\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192import java.util\n    52\u2192import scala.annotation.nowarn\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192/**\n    55\u2192 * The entry point to the kafka log management subsystem. The log manager is responsible for log creation, retrieval, and cleaning.\n    56\u2192 * All read and write operations are delegated to the individual log instances.\n    57\u2192 *\n    58\u2192 * The log manager maintains logs in one or more directories. New logs are created in the data directory\n    59\u2192 * with the fewest logs. No attempt is made to move partitions after the fact or balance based on\n    60\u2192 * size or I/O rate.\n    61\u2192 *\n    62\u2192 * A background thread handles log retention by periodically truncating excess log segments.\n    63\u2192 */\n    64\u2192@threadsafe\n    65\u2192class LogManager(logDirs: Seq[File],\n    66\u2192                 initialOfflineDirs: Seq[File],\n    67\u2192                 configRepository: ConfigRepository,\n    68\u2192                 val initialDefaultConfig: LogConfig,\n    69\u2192                 val cleanerConfig: CleanerConfig,\n    70\u2192 val flushStartOffsetCheckpointMs: Long,\n    74\u2192                 val retentionCheckMs: Long,\n    75\u2192                 val maxTransactionTimeoutMs: Int,\n    76\u2192                 val producerStateManagerConfig: ProducerStateManagerConfig,\n    77\u2192                 val producerIdExpirationCheckIntervalMs: Int,\n    78\u2192                 interBrokerProtocolVersion: MetadataVersion,\n    79\u2192                 scheduler: Scheduler,\n    80\u2192                 brokerTopicStats: BrokerTopicStats,\n    81\u2192                 logDirFailureChannel: LogDirFailureChannel,\n    82\u2192                 time: Time,\n    83\u2192                 val keepPartitionMetadataFile: Boolean,\n    84\u2192                 remoteStorageSystemEnable: Boolean,\n    85\u2192                 val initialTaskDelayMs: Long) extends Logging {\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192  import LogManager._\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192  private val metricsGroup = new KafkaMetricsGroup(this.getClass)\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192  private val logCreationOrDeletionLock = new Object\n    92\u2192  private val currentLogs = new Pool[TopicPartition, UnifiedLog]()\n    93\u2192  // Future logs are put in the directory with \"-future\" suffix. Future log is created when user wants to move replica\n    94\u2192  // from one log directory to another log directory on the same broker. The directory of the future log will be renamed\n    95\u2192  // to replace the current log of the partition after the future log catches up with the current log\n    96\u2192  private val futureLogs = new Pool[TopicPartition, UnifiedLog]()\n    97\u2192  // Each element in the queue contains the log object to be deleted and the time it is scheduled for deletion.\n    98\u2192  private val logsToBeDeleted = new LinkedBlockingQueue[(UnifiedLog, Long)]()\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192  // Map of stray partition to stray log. This holds all stray logs detected on the broker.\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

19\u2192\n    20\u2192import kafka.api.LeaderAndIsr\n    21\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.protocol.Errors\n    22\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.ApiError\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192import scala.collection._\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192/**\n    27\u2192  * The create metadata maintained by the delayed create topic or create partitions operations.\n    28\u2192  */\n    29\u2192case class CreatePartitionsMetadata(topic: String, partitions: Set[Int], error: ApiError)\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192object CreatePartitionsMetadata {\n    32\u2192  def apply(topic: String, partitions: Set[Int]): CreatePartitionsMetadata = {\n    33\u2192    CreatePartitionsMetadata(topic, partitions, ApiError.NONE)\n    34\u2192  }\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192  def apply(topic: String, error: Errors): CreatePartitionsMetadata = {\n    37\u2192    CreatePartitionsMetadata(topic, Set.empty, new ApiError(error, null))\n    38\u2192  }\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192  def apply(topic: String, throwable: Throwable): CreatePartitionsMetadata = {\n    41\u2192    CreatePartitionsMetadata(topic, Set.empty, ApiError.fromThrowable(throwable))\n    42\u2192  }\n    43\u2192}\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192/**\n    46\u2192  * A delayed create topic or create partitions operation that is stored in the topic purgatory.\n    47\u2192  */\n    48\u2192class DelayedCreatePartitions(delayMs: Long,\n    49\u2192                              createMetadata: Seq[CreatePartitionsMetadata],\n    50\u2192                              adminManager: ZkAdminManager,\n    51\u2192                              responseCallback: Map[String, ApiError] => Unit)\n    52\u2192  extends DelayedOperation(delayMs) {\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192  /**\n    55\u2192    * The operation can be completed if all of the topics that do not have an error exist and every partition has a\n    56\u2192    * leader in the controller.\n    57\u2192    * See KafkaController.onNewTopicCreation\n    58\u2192    */\n    59\u2192  override def tryComplete() : Boolean = {\n    60\u2192    trace(s\"Trying to complete operation for $createMetadata\")\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192    val leaderlessPartitionCount = createMetadata.filter(_.error.isSuccess).foldLeft(0) { case (topicCounter, metadata) =>\n    63\u2192      topicCounter + missingLeaderCount(metadata.topic, metadata.partitions)\n    64\u2192    }\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192    if (leaderlessPartitionCount == 0) {\n    67\u2192      trace(\"All partitions have a leader, completing the delayed operation\")\n    68\u2192      forceComplete()\n    69\u2192    } else {\n    70\u2192      trace(s\"$leaderlessPartitionCount partitions do not have a leader, not completing the delayed operation\")\n    71\u2192      false\n    72\u2192    }\n    73\u2192  }\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192  /**\n    76\u2192    * Check for partitions that are still missing a leader, update their error code and call the responseCallback\n    77\u2192    */\n    78\u2192  override def onComplete(): Unit = {\n    79\u2192    trace(s\"Completing operation for $createMetadata\")\n    80\u2192    val results = createMetadata.map { metadata =>\n    81\u2192      // ignore topics that already have errors\n    82\u2192      if (metadata.error.isSuccess && missingLeaderCount(metadata.topic, metadata.partitions) > 0)\n    83\u2192        (metadata.topic, new ApiError(Errors.REQUEST_TIMED_OUT, null))\n    84\u2192      else\n    85\u2192        (metadata.topic, metadata.error)\n    86\u2192    }.toMap\n    87\u2192    responseCallback(results)\n    88\u2192  }\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192  override def onExpiration(): Unit = {}\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192  private def missingLeaderCount(topic: String, partitions: Set[Int]): Int = {\n    93\u2192    partitions.foldLeft(0) { case (counter, partition) =>\n    94\u2192      if (isMissingLeader(topic, partition)) counter + 1 else counter\n    95\u2192    }\n    96\u2192  }\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192  private def isMissingLeader(topic: String, partition: Int): Boolean = {\n    99\u2192    val partitionInfo = adminManager.metadataCache.getPartitionInfo(topic, partition)\n   100\u2192    partitionInfo.forall(_.leader == LeaderAndIsr.NoLeader)\n   101\u2192  }\n   102\u2192}\n   103\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

org.apache.kafka.common.record.FileRecords.TimestampAndOffset\n    41\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.record.{MemoryRecords, RecordBatch}\n    42\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.requests._\n org.apache.kafka.metadata.LeaderRecoveryState\n    46\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.common.MetadataVersion\n    47\u2192import org.apache.kafka.storage.internals.log.{AppendOrigin, FetchDataInfo, FetchIsolation, FetchParams, LeaderHwChange, LogAppendInfo, LogOffsetMetadata, LogOffsetSnapshot, LogOffsetsListener, LogReadInfo, LogStartOffsetIncrementReason, VerificationGuard}\n    48\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.metrics.KafkaMetricsGroup\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192import scala.collection.{Map, Seq}\n    51\u2192import scala.jdk.CollectionConverters._\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192/**\n    54\u2192 * Listener receives notification from an Online Partition.\n    55\u2192 *\n    56\u2192 * A listener can be (re-)registered to an Online partition only. The listener\n    57\u2192 * is notified as long as the partition remains Online. When the partition fails\n    58\u2192 * or is deleted, respectively `onFailed` or `onDeleted` are called once. No further\n    59\u2192 * notifications are sent after this point on.\n    60\u2192 *\n    61\u2192 * Note that the callbacks are executed in the thread that triggers the change\n    62\u2192 * AND that locks may be held during their execution. They are meant to be used\n    63\u2192 * as notification mechanism only.\n    64\u2192 */\n    65\u2192trait PartitionListener {\n    66\u2192  /**\n    67\u2192   * Called when the Log increments its high watermark.\n    68\u2192   */\n    69\u2192  def onHighWatermarkUpdated(partition: TopicPartition, offset: Long): Unit = {}\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192  /**\n    72\u2192   * Called when the Partition (or replica) on this broker has a failure (e.g. goes offline).\n    73\u2192   */\n    74\u2192  def onFailed(partition: TopicPartition): Unit = {}\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192  /**\n    77\u2192   * Called when the Partition (or replica) on this broker is deleted. Note that it does not mean\n    78\u2192   * that the partition was deleted but only that this broker does not host a replica of it any more.\n    79\u2192   */\n    80\u2192  def onDeleted(partition: TopicPartition): Unit = {}\n    81\u2192}\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192trait AlterPartitionListener {\n    84\u2192  def markIsrExpand(): Unit\n    85\u2192  def markIsrShrink(): Unit\n    86\u2192  def markFailed(): Unit\n    87\u2192}\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192class DelayedOperations(topicPartition: TopicPartition,\n    90\u2192                        produce: DelayedOperationPurgatory[DelayedProduce],\n    91\u2192                        fetch: DelayedOperationPurgatory[DelayedFetch],\n    92\u2192                        deleteRecords: DelayedOperationPurgatory[DelayedDeleteRecords]) {\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192  def checkAndCompleteAll(): Unit = {\n    95\u2192    val requestKey = TopicPartitionOperationKey(topicPartition)\n    96\u2192    fetch.checkAndComplete(requestKey)\n    97\u2192    produce.checkAndComplete(requestKey)\n    98\u2192    deleteRecords.checkAndComplete(requestKey)\n    99\u2192  }\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192  def numDelayedDelete: Int = deleteRecords.numDelayed\n   102\u2192}\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192object Partition {\n   105\u2192  private val metricsGroup = new KafkaMetricsGroup(classOf[Partition])\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192  def apply(topicIdPartition: TopicIdPartition,\n   108\u2192            time: Time,\n   109\u2192            replicaManager: ReplicaManager): Partition = {\n   110\u2192    Partition(\n   111\u2192      topicPartition = topicIdPartition.topicPartition(),\n   112\u2192      topicId = Option(topicIdPartition.topicId()),\n   113\u2192      time = time,\n   114\u2192      replicaManager = replicaManager)\n   115\u2192  }\n   116\u2192  def apply(topicPartition: TopicPartition,\n   117\u2192            time: Time,\n   118\u2192            replicaManager: ReplicaManager,\n   119\u2192            topicId: Option[Uuid] = None): Partition = {\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192    val isrChangeListener = new AlterPartitionListener {\n   122\u2192      override def markIsrExpand(): Unit = {\n   123\u2192        replicaManager.isrExpandRate.mark()\n   124\u2192      }\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192      override def markIsrShrink(): Unit = {\n   127\u2192        replicaManager.isrShrinkRate.mark()\n   128\u2192      }\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192      override def markFailed(): Unit = replicaManager.failedIsrUpdatesRate.mark()\n   131\u2192    }\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192    val delayedOperations = new DelayedOperations(\n   134\u2192      topicPartition,\n   135\u2192      replicaManager.delayedProducePurgatory,\n   136\u2192      replicaManager.delayedFetchPurgatory,\n   137\u2192      replicaManager.delayedDeleteRecordsPurgatory)\n   138\u2192\n   139\u2192    new Partition(topicPartition,\n   140\u2192      _topicId = topicId,\n   141\u2192      replicaLagTimeMaxMs = replicaManager.config.replicaLagTimeMaxMs,\n   142\u2192      interBrokerProtocolVersion = replicaManager.metadataCache.metadataVersion(),\n   143\u2192      localBrokerId = replicaManager.config.brokerId,\n   144\u2192      localBrokerEpochSupplier = replicaManager.brokerEpochSupplier,\n   145\u2192      time = time,\n   146\u2192      alterPartitionListener = isrChangeListener,\n   147\u2192      delayedOperations = delayedOperations,\n   148\u2192      metadataCache = replicaManager.metadataCache,\n   149\u2192      logManager = replicaManager.logManager,\n   150\u2192      alterIsrManager = replicaManager.alterPartitionManager)\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. "   385\u2192      override def accept(permits: lang.Integer): Unit = controllerMutationQuota.record(permits.doubleValue())\n   386\u2192    }\n   387\u2192  }\n   388\u2192\n   389\u2192  def createTopics(\n   390\u2192    context: ControllerRequestContext,\n   391\u2192    request: CreateTopicsRequestData,\n   392\u2192    hasClusterAuth: Boolean,\n   393\u2192    getCreatableTopics: Iterable[String] => Set[String],\n   394\u2192    getDescribableTopics: Iterable[String] => Set[String]\n   395\u2192  ): CompletableFuture[CreateTopicsResponseData] = {\n   396\u2192    val topicNames = new util.HashSet[String]()\n   397\u2192    val duplicateTopicNames = new util.HashSet[String]()\n   398\u2192    request.topics().forEach { topicData =>\n   399\u2192      if (!duplicateTopicNames.contains(topicData.name())) {\n   400\u2192        if (!topicNames.add(topicData.name())) {\n   401\u2192          topicNames.remove(topicData.name())\n   402\u2192          duplicateTopicNames.add(topicData.name())\n   403\u2192        }\n   404\u2192      }\n   405\u2192    }\n   406\u2192\n   407\u2192    val allowedTopicNames = topicNames.asScala.diff(Set(Topic.CLUSTER_METADATA_TOPIC_NAME))\n   408\u2192\n   409\u2192    val authorizedTopicNames = if (hasClusterAuth) {\n   410\u2192      allowedTopicNames\n   411\u2192    } else {\n   412\u2192      getCreatableTopics.apply(allowedTopicNames)\n   413\u2192    }\n   414\u2192    val describableTopicNames = getDescribableTopics.apply(allowedTopicNames).asJava\n   415\u2192    val effectiveRequest = request.duplicate()\n   416\u2192    val iterator = effectiveRequest.topics().iterator()\n   417\u2192    while (iterator.hasNext) {\n   418\u2192      val creatableTopic = iterator.next()\n   419\u2192      if (duplicateTopicNames.contains(creatableTopic.name()) ||\n   420\u2192          !authorizedTopicNames.contains(creatableTopic.name())) {\n   421\u2192        iterator.remove()\n   422\u2192      }\n   423\u2192    }\n   424\u2192    controller.createTopics(context, effectiveRequest, describableTopicNames).thenApply { response =>\n   425\u2192      duplicateTopicNames.forEach { name =>\n   426\u2192        response.topics().add(new CreatableTopicResult().\n   427\u2192          setName(name).\n   428\u2192          setErrorCode(INVALID_REQUEST.code).\n   429\u2192          setErrorMessage(\"Duplicate topic name.\"))\n   430\u2192      }\n   431\u2192      topicNames.forEach { name =>\n   432\u2192        if (name == Topic.CLUSTER_METADATA_TOPIC_NAME) {\n   433\u2192          response.topics().add(new CreatableTopicResult().\n   434\u2192            setName(name).\n   435\u2192            setErrorCode(INVALID_REQUEST.code).\n   436\u2192            setErrorMessage(s\"Creation of internal topic ${Topic.CLUSTER_METADATA_TOPIC_NAME} is prohibited.\"))\n   437\u2192        } else if (!authorizedTopicNames.contains(name)) {\n   438\u2192          response.topics().add(new CreatableTopicResult().\n   439\u2192            setName(name).\n   440\u2192            setErrorCode(TOPIC_AUTHORIZATION_FAILED.code).\n   441\u2192            setErrorMessage(\"Authorization failed.\"))\n   442\u2192        }\n   443\u2192      }\n   444\u2192      response\n   445\u2192    }\n   446\u2192  }\n   447\u2192\n   448\u2192  def handleApiVersionsRequest(request: RequestChannel.Request): CompletableFuture[Unit] = {\n   449\u2192    // Note that controller returns its full list of supported ApiKeys and versions regardless of current\n   450\u2192    // authentication state (e.g., before SASL authentication on an SASL listener, do note that no\n   451\u2192    // Kafka protocol requests may take place on an SSL listener before the SSL handshake is finished).\n   452\u2192    // If this is considered to leak information about the controller version a workaround is to use SSL\n   453\u2192    // with client authentication which is performed at an earlier stage of the connection where the\n   454\u2192    // ApiVersionRequest is not available.\n   455\u2192    val apiVersionRequest = request.body[ApiVersionsRequest]\n   456\u2192    if (apiVersionRequest.hasUnsupportedRequestVersion) {\n   457\u2192      requestHelper.sendResponseMaybeThrottle(request,\n   458\u2192        requestThrottleMs => apiVersionRequest.getErrorResponse(requestThrottleMs, UNSUPPORTED_VERSION.exception))\n   459\u2192    } else if (!apiVersionRequest.isValid) {\n   460\u2192      requestHelper.sendResponseMaybeThrottle(request,\n   461\u2192        requestThrottleMs => apiVersionRequest.getErrorResponse(requestThrottleMs, INVALID_REQUEST.exception))\n   462\u2192    } else {\n   463\u2192      requestHelper.sendResponseMaybeThrottle(request,\n   464\u2192        requestThrottleMs => apiVersionManager.apiVersionResponse(requestThrottleMs, request.header.apiVersion() < 4))\n   465\u2192    }\n   466\u2192    CompletableFuture.completedFuture[Unit](())\n   467\u2192  }\n   468\u2192\n   469\u2192  private def authorizeAlterResource(requestContext: RequestContext,\n   470\u2192                                     resource: ConfigResource): ApiError = {\n   471\u2192    resource.`type` match {\n   472\u2192      case ConfigResource.Type.BROKER | ConfigResource.Type.CLIENT_METRICS =>\n   473\u2192        if (authHelper.authorize(requestContext, ALTER_CONFIGS, CLUSTER, CLUSTER_NAME)) {\n   474\u2192          new ApiError(NONE)\n   475\u2192        } else {\n   476\u2192          new ApiError(CLUSTER_AUTHORIZATION_FAILED)\n   477\u2192        }\n   478\u2192      case ConfigResource.Type.TOPIC =>\n   479\u2192        if (authHelper.authorize(requestContext, ALTER_CONFIGS, TOPIC, resource.name)) {\n   480\u2192          new ApiError(NONE)\n   481\u2192        } else {\n   482\u2192          new ApiError(TOPIC_AUTHORIZATION_FAILED)\n   483\u2192        }\n   484\u2192      case rt => new ApiError(INVALID_REQUEST, s\"Unexpected resource type $rt.\")\n   485\u2192    }\n   486\u2192  }\n   487\u2192\n   488\u2192  def handleLegacyAlterConfigs(request: RequestChannel.Request): CompletableFuture[Unit] = {\n   489\u2192    val response = new AlterConfigsResponseData()\n   490\u2192    val alterConfigsRequest = request.body[AlterConfigsRequest]\n   491\u2192    val context = new ControllerRequestContext(request.context.header.data, request.context.principal, OptionalLong.empty())\n   492\u2192    val duplicateResources = new util.HashSet[ConfigResource]\n   493\u2192    val configChanges = new util.HashMap[ConfigResource, util.Map[String, String]]()\n   494\u2192    alterConfigsRequest.data.resources.forEach { resource =>\n   495\u2192      val configResource = new ConfigResource(\n   496\u2192        ConfigResource.Type.forId(resource.resourceType), resource.resourceName())\n   497\u2192      if (configResource.`type`().equals(ConfigResource.Type.UNKNOWN)) {\n   498\u2192        response.responses().add(new OldAlterConfigsResourceResponse().\n   499\u2192          setErrorCode(UNSUPPORTED_VERSION.code()).\n   500\u2192          setErrorMessage(\"Unknown resource type \" + resource.resourceType() + \".\").\n   501\u2192          setResourceName(resource.resourceName()).\n   502\u2192          setResourceType(resource.resourceType()))\n   503\u2192      } else if (!duplicateResources.contains(configResource)) {\n   504\u2192        val configs = new util.HashMap[String, String]()\n   505\u2192        resource.configs().forEach(config => configs.put(config.name(), config.value()))\n   506\u2192        if (configChanges.put(configResource, configs) != null) {\n   507\u2192          duplicateResources.add(configResource)\n   508\u2192          configChanges.remove(configResource)\n   509\u2192          response.responses().add(new OldAlterConfigsResourceResponse().\n   510\u2192            setErrorCode(INVALID_REQUEST.code()).\n   511\u2192            setErrorMessage(\"Duplicate resource.\").\n   512\u2192            setResourceName(resource.resourceName()).\n   513\u2192            setResourceType(resource.resourceType()))\n   514\u2192        }\n   515\u2192      }\n   516\u2192    }\n   517\u2192    val iterator = configChanges.keySet().iterator()\n   518\u2192    while (iterator.hasNext) {\n   519\u2192      val resource = iterator.next()\n   520\u2192      val apiError = authorizeAlterResource(request.context, resource)\n   521\u2192      if (apiError.isFailure) {\n   522\u2192        response.responses().add(new OldAlterConfigsResourceResponse().\n   523\u2192          setErrorCode(apiError.error().code()).\n   524\u2192          setErrorMessage(apiError.message()).\n   525\u2192          setResourceName(resource.name()).\n   526\u2192          setResourceType(resource.`type`().id()))\n   527\u2192        iterator.remove()\n   528\u2192      }\n   529\u2192    }\n   530\u2192    controller.legacyAlterConfigs(context, configChanges, alterConfigsRequest.data.validateOnly)\n   531\u2192      .handle[Unit] { (controllerResults, exception) =>\n   532\u2192        if (exception != null) {\n   533\u2192          requestHelper.handleError(request, exception)\n   534\u2192        } else {\n   535\u2192          controllerResults.forEach((key, value) => response.responses().add(\n   536\u2192            new OldAlterConfigsResourceResponse().\n   537\u2192              setErrorCode(value.error().code()).\n   538\u2192              setErrorMessage(value.message()).\n   539\u2192              setResourceName(key.name()).\n   540\u2192              setResourceType(key.`type`().id())))\n   541\u2192          requestHelper.sendResponseMaybeThrottle(request, throttleMs =>\n   542\u2192            new AlterConfigsResponse(response.setThrottleTimeMs(throttleMs)))\n   543\u2192        }\n   544\u2192      }\n   545\u2192  }\n   546\u2192\n   547\u2192  def handleVote(request: RequestChannel.Request): CompletableFuture[Unit] = {\n   548\u2192    authHelper.authorizeClusterOperation(request, CLUSTER_ACTION)\n   549\u2192    handleRaftRequest(request, response => new VoteResponse(response.asInstanceOf[VoteResponseData]))\n   550\u2192  }\n   551\u2192\n   552\u2192  def handleBeginQuorumEpoch(request: RequestChannel.Request): CompletableFuture[Unit] = {\n   553\u2192    authHelper.authorizeClusterOperation(request, CLUSTER_ACTION)\n   554\u2192    handleRaftRequest(request, response => new BeginQuorumEpochResponse(response.asInstanceOf[BeginQuorumEpochResponseData]))\n   555\u2192  }\n   556\u2192\n   557\u2192  def handleEndQuorumEpoch(request: RequestChannel.Request): CompletableFuture[Unit] = {\n   558\u2192    authHelper.authorizeClusterOperation(request, CLUSTER_ACTION)\n   559\u2192    handleRaftRequest(request, response => new EndQuorumEpochResponse(response.asInstanceOf[EndQuorumEpochResponseData]))\n   560\u2192  }\n   561\u2192\n   562\u2192  def handleDescribeQuorum(request: RequestChannel.Request): CompletableFuture[Unit] = {\n   563\u2192    authHelper.authorizeClusterOperation(request, DESCRIBE)\n   564\u2192    handleRaftRequest(request, response => new DescribeQuorumResponse(response.asInstanceOf[DescribeQuorumResponseData]))\n   565\u2192  }\n   566\u2192\n   567\u2192  def handleElectLeaders(request: RequestChannel.Request): CompletableFuture[Unit] = {\n   568\u2192    authHelper.authorizeClusterOperation(request, ALTER)\n   569\u2192    val electLeadersRequest = request.body[ElectLeadersRequest]\n   570\u2192    val context = new ControllerRequestContext(request.context.header.data, request.context.principal,\n   571\u2192      requestTimeoutMsToDeadlineNs(time, electLeadersRequest.data.timeoutMs))\n   572\u2192    val future = controller.electLeaders(context, electLeadersRequest.data)\n   573\u2192    future.handle[Unit] { (responseData, exception) =>\n   574\u2192      if (exception != null) {\n   575\u2192        requestHelper.sendResponseMaybeThrottle(request, throttleMs => {\n   576\u2192          electLeadersRequest.getErrorResponse(throttleMs, exception)\n   577\u2192        })\n   578\u2192      } else {\n   579\u2192        requestHelper.sendResponseMaybeThrottle(request, throttleMs => {\n   580\u2192          new ElectLeadersResponse(responseData.setThrottleTimeMs(throttleMs))\n   581\u2192        })\n   582\u2192      }\n   583\u2192    }\n   584\u2192  }\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"188-      request.header.apiKey match {\n189:        case ApiKeys.PRODUCE => handleProduceRequest(request, requestLocal)\n190:        case ApiKeys.FETCH => handleFetchRequest(request)\n191:        case ApiKeys.LIST_OFFSETS => handleListOffsetRequest(request)\n192:        case ApiKeys.METADATA => handleTopicMetadataRequest(request)\n193:        case ApiKeys.LEADER_AND_ISR => handleLeaderAndIsrRequest(request)\n194:        case ApiKeys.STOP_REPLICA => handleStopReplicaRequest(request)\n195:        case ApiKeys.UPDATE_METADATA => handleUpdateMetadataRequest(request, requestLocal)\n196:        case ApiKeys.CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN => handleControlledShutdownRequest(request)\n197:        case ApiKeys.OFFSET_COMMIT => handleOffsetCommitRequest(request, requestLocal).exceptionally(handleError)\n198:        case ApiKeys.OFFSET_FETCH => handleOffsetFetchRequest(request).exceptionally(handleError)\n199:        case ApiKeys.FIND_COORDINATOR => handleFindCoordinatorRequest(request)\n200:        case ApiKeys.JOIN_GROUP => handleJoinGroupRequest(request, requestLocal).exceptionally(handleError)\n201:        case ApiKeys.HEARTBEAT => handleHeartbeatRequest(request).exceptionally(handleError)\n202:        case ApiKeys.LEAVE_GROUP => handleLeaveGroupRequest(request).exceptionally(handleError)\n203:        case ApiKeys.SYNC_GROUP => handleSyncGroupRequest(request, requestLocal).exceptionally(handleError)\n204:        case ApiKeys.DESCRIBE_GROUPS => handleDescribeGroupsRequest(request).exceptionally(handleError)\n205:        case ApiKeys.LIST_GROUPS => handleListGroupsRequest(request).exceptionally(handleError)\n206:        case ApiKeys.SASL_HANDSHAKE => handleSaslHandshakeRequest(request)\n207:        case ApiKeys.API_VERSIONS => handleApiVersionsRequest(request)\n\n[Showing results with pagination = limit: 20, offset: 0]"

"  1033\u2192  def getOrCreateLog(topicPartition: TopicPartition, isNew: Boolean = false, isFuture: Boolean = false,\n  1034\u2192                     topicId: Option[Uuid], targetLogDirectoryId: Option[Uuid] = Option.empty): UnifiedLog = {\n  1035\u2192    logCreationOrDeletionLock synchronized {\n  1036\u2192      val log = getLog(topicPartition, isFuture).getOrElse {\n  1037\u2192        // create the log if it has not already been created in another thread\n  1038\u2192        if (!isNew && offlineLogDirs.nonEmpty)\n  1039\u2192          throw new KafkaStorageException(s\"Can not create log for $topicPartition because log directories ${offlineLogDirs.mkString(\",\")} are offline\")\n  1040\u2192\n  1041\u2192        val logDirs: List[File] = {\n  1042\u2192          val preferredLogDir = targetLogDirectoryId.filterNot(Seq(DirectoryId.UNASSIGNED,DirectoryId.LOST).contains) match {\n  1043\u2192            case Some(targetId) if !preferredLogDirs.containsKey(topicPartition) =>\n  1044\u2192              // If partition is configured with both targetLogDirectoryId and preferredLogDirs, then\n  1045\u2192              // preferredLogDirs will be respected, otherwise targetLogDirectoryId will be respected\n  1046\u2192              directoryIds.find(_._2 == targetId).map(_._1).orNull\n  1047\u2192            case _ =>\n  1048\u2192              preferredLogDirs.get(topicPartition)\n  1049\u2192          }\n  1050\u2192\n  1051\u2192          if (isFuture) {\n  1052\u2192            if (preferredLogDir == null)\n  1053\u2192              throw new IllegalStateException(s\"Can not create the future log for $topicPartition without having a preferred log directory\")\n  1054\u2192            else if (getLog(topicPartition).get.parentDir == preferredLogDir)\n  1055\u2192              throw new IllegalStateException(s\"Can not create the future log for $topicPartition in the current log directory of this partition\")\n  1056\u2192          }\n  1057\u2192\n  1058\u2192          if (preferredLogDir != null)\n  1059\u2192            List(new File(preferredLogDir))\n  1060\u2192          else\n  1061\u2192            nextLogDirs()\n  1062\u2192        }\n  1063\u2192\n  1064\u2192        val logDirName = {\n  1065\u2192          if (isFuture)\n  1066\u2192            UnifiedLog.logFutureDirName(topicPartition)\n  1067\u2192          else\n  1068\u2192            UnifiedLog.logDirName(topicPartition)\n  1069\u2192        }\n  1070\u2192\n  1071\u2192        val logDir = logDirs\n  1072\u2192          .iterator // to prevent actually mapping the whole list, lazy map\n  1073\u2192          .map(createLogDirectory(_, logDirName))\n  1074\u2192          .find(_.isSuccess)\n  1075\u2192          .getOrElse(Failure(new KafkaStorageException(\"No log directories available. Tried \" + logDirs.map(_.getAbsolutePath).mkString(\", \"))))\n  1076\u2192          .get // If Failure, will throw\n  1077\u2192\n  1078\u2192        val config = fetchLogConfig(topicPartition.topic)\n  1079\u2192        val log = UnifiedLog(\n  1080\u2192          dir = logDir,\n  1081\u2192          config = config,\n  1082\u2192          logStartOffset = 0L,\n  1083\u2192          recoveryPoint = 0L,\n  1084\u2192          maxTransactionTimeoutMs = maxTransactionTimeoutMs,\n  1085\u2192          producerStateManagerConfig = producerStateManagerConfig,\n  1086\u2192          producerIdExpirationCheckIntervalMs = producerIdExpirationCheckIntervalMs,\n  1087\u2192          scheduler = scheduler,\n  1088\u2192          time = time,\n  1089\u2192          brokerTopicStats = brokerTopicStats,\n  1090\u2192          logDirFailureChannel = logDirFailureChannel,\n  1091\u2192          topicId = topicId,\n  1092\u2192          keepPartitionMetadataFile = keepPartitionMetadataFile,\n  1093\u2192          remoteStorageSystemEnable = remoteStorageSystemEnable)\n  1094\u2192\n  1095\u2192        if (isFuture)\n  1096\u2192          futureLogs.put(topicPartition, log)\n  1097\u2192        else\n  1098\u2192          currentLogs.put(topicPartition, log)\n  1099\u2192\n  1100\u2192        info(s\"Created log for partition $topicPartition in $logDir with properties ${config.overriddenConfigsAsLoggableString}\")\n  1101\u2192        // Remove the preferred log dir since it has already been satisfied\n  1102\u2192        preferredLogDirs.remove(topicPartition)\n  1103\u2192\n  1104\u2192        log\n  1105\u2192      }\n  1106\u2192      // When running a ZK controller, we may get a log that does not have a topic ID. Assign it here.\n  1107\u2192      if (log.topicId.isEmpty) {\n  1108\u2192        topicId.foreach(log.assignTopicId)\n  1109\u2192      }\n  1110\u2192\n  1111\u2192      // Ensure topic IDs are consistent\n  1112\u2192      topicId.foreach { topicId =>\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
