115. Task @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Investigate testing frameworks and patterns",
  "prompt": "Explore the Apache Kafka test suite to understand testing frameworks and patterns:\n\n1. **Unit Testing Framework:**\n   - Look in /workspace/core/src/test/scala and /workspace/clients/src/test for test files\n   - Identify what test framework is used (JUnit, ScalaTest, etc.)\n   - Find example test files and analyze their structure\n   - Look for test base classes or traits that tests extend\n\n2. **Integration Testing:**\n   - Look in /workspace/tests/src/main/java for integration tests\n   - Find test utilities and helper classes\n   - Look for test fixtures or test setup patterns\n   - Find cluster setup and cleanup code\n\n3. **Test Dependencies:**\n   - Look at build.gradle to see testImplementation dependencies\n   - Find JUnit versions, ScalaTest versions, mocking libraries\n   - Look for test utilities like TestUtils, MockServer, etc.\n\n4. **Test Patterns:**\n   - Find example unit tests in core/src/test\n   - Find example integration tests in tests/src\n   - Look for test naming conventions\n   - Look for mock objects and test helpers\n\n5. **Special Testing Tools:**\n   - Look for Trogdor testing framework (performance/workload testing)\n   - Look for QuickTest or similar utilities\n   - Find test constants and configuration builders\n\nKey search areas:\n- /workspace/build.gradle for test dependencies\n- /workspace/core/src/test/scala for Scala test examples\n- /workspace/clients/src/test/java for Java test examples\n- /workspace/tests/src/main/java for integration test patterns\n- /workspace/trogdor for performance testing framework",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "a6728bb23ad52caf2",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me create a detailed summary report:\n\n## Apache Kafka Test Suite - Comprehensive Analysis\n\nI've thoroughly explored the Apache Kafka test suite at version 3.9.0. Here's a comprehensive breakdown of testing frameworks, patterns, and infrastructure:\n\n---\n\n### 1. UNIT TESTING FRAMEWORK\n\n**Primary Framework: JUnit 5 (Jupiter)**\n- **Version**: 5.10.2\n- **Key annotations used**:\n  - `@Test` - marks individual test methods\n  - `@BeforeEach` - setup before each test\n  - `@AfterEach` - teardown after each test\n  - `@ParameterizedTest` - parameterized tests\n  - `@ValueSource` - simple parameter values\n  - `@MethodSource` - complex parameter sources\n  - `@Timeout` - test timeouts with thread mode control\n\n**Test Location Statistics**:\n- Java Unit Tests: 135 test files in `/workspace/clients/src/test/java`\n- Scala Unit Tests: 328 test files in `/workspace/core/src/test/scala`\n\n---\n\n### 2. TEST DEPENDENCIES\n\nFrom `/workspace/gradle/dependencies.gradle`:\n\n**Core Testing Libraries**:\n```gradle\n- junitJupiter: \"org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter:5.10.2\"\n- junitJupiterApi: \"org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter-api:5.10.2\"\n- junitPlatformSuiteEngine: \"org.junit.platform:junit-platform-suite-engine:1.10.2\"\n- junitPlatformLanucher: \"org.junit.platform:junit-platform-launcher:1.10.2\"\n```\n\n**Mocking Framework**:\n```gradle\n- Mockito: varies by Java/Scala version\n  - Java 11+: mockito-core 5.10.0 (inline strategy)\n  - Java 8 + Scala 2.13: mockito-inline 4.11.0\n  - Scala 2.12: mockito-inline 4.9.0\n- mockitoJunitJupiter: \"org.mockito:mockito-junit-jupiter:$mockitoVersion\"\n```\n\n**Assertion Libraries**:\n```gradle\n- hamcrest: \"2.2\"\n```\n\n**Property-Based Testing**:\n```gradle\n- jqwik: \"1.8.3\" - property-based testing framework\n```\n\n---\n\n### 3. UNIT TEST EXAMPLES\n\n**Java Unit Test Structure** (`NetworkClientTest.java`):\n```java\npublic class NetworkClientTest {\n    protected final int defaultRequestTimeoutMs = 1000;\n    protected final MockTime time = new MockTime();\n    protected final MockSelector selector = new MockSelector(time);\n    protected final Node node = TestUtils.singletonCluster().nodes().iterator().next();\n    \n    // Uses static imports for assertions\n    // import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;\n    // import static org.mockito.Mockito.*;\n}\n```\n\n**Scala Unit Test Structure** (`KafkaRequestHandlerTest.scala`):\n```scala\nclass KafkaRequestHandlerTest {\n  val brokerTopicStats = new BrokerTopicStats(true)\n  val time = new MockTime()\n  \n  @Test\n  def testCallbackTiming(): Unit = {\n    // test implementation\n  }\n  \n  @Test\n  def testCallbackTryCompleteActions(): Unit = {\n    // test implementation\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Parameterized Test Example** (`PlaintextProducerSendTest.scala`):\n```scala\n@ParameterizedTest\n@ValueSource(strings = Array(\"zk\", \"kraft\"))\ndef testWrongSerializer(quorum: String): Unit = {\n  // runs once for each value in strings\n}\n\n@ParameterizedTest\n@MethodSource\ndef testMethod(args: Arguments): Unit = {\n  // uses method source for complex parameters\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 4. INTEGRATION TESTING\n\n**Test Harness Hierarchy**:\n\n```\nQuorumTestHarness (base - handles ZK or KRaft setup)\n    \u2514\u2500\u2500 KafkaServerTestHarness (manages broker lifecycle)\n            \u2514\u2500\u2500 IntegrationTestHarness (producer/consumer clients)\n                    \u2514\u2500\u2500 BaseProducerSendTest / BaseConsumerTest / BaseAdminIntegrationTest\n```\n\n**Base Classes**:\n\n**QuorumTestHarness** (`/workspace/core/src/test/scala/unit/kafka/integration/KafkaServerTestHarness.scala`):\n- Sets up ZooKeeper or KRaft clusters\n- Implements `BeforeEach` and `AfterEach` lifecycle\n- Provides methods: `isZKTest()`, `isKRaftTest()`, `isBrokerTest()`\n- Trait implementations: `ZooKeeperQuorumImplementation`, `KRaftQuorumImplementation`\n\n**KafkaServerTestHarness**:\n- Manages broker lifecycle: `createBrokers()`, `shutdownServers()`\n- Configuration generation: `generateConfigs(): Seq[KafkaConfig]`\n- Helper methods: `bootstrapServers()`, `brokers: Buffer[KafkaBroker]`\n- Security configuration hooks: `configureSecurityBeforeServersStart()`, `configureSecurityAfterServersStart()`\n\n**IntegrationTestHarness** (extends `KafkaServerTestHarness`):\n- Manages producer/consumer/admin clients\n- Configuration properties: `producerConfig`, `consumerConfig`, `adminClientConfig`\n- Lifecycle: maintains buffers of clients for cleanup\n- Helper: `modifyConfigs(props: Seq[Properties]): Unit`\n- Methods: `doSetup()`, `doSuperuserSetup()` for security tests\n\n**Example Integration Test** (`PlaintextProducerSendTest.scala`):\n```scala\nclass PlaintextProducerSendTest extends BaseProducerSendTest {\n  @ParameterizedTest\n  @ValueSource(strings = Array(\"zk\", \"kraft\"))\n  def testWrongSerializer(quorum: String): Unit = {\n    val producerProps = new Properties()\n    producerProps.put(ProducerConfig.BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG, bootstrapServers())\n    val producer = registerProducer(new KafkaProducer(producerProps))\n    assertThrows(classOf[SerializationException], () => producer.send(record))\n  }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 5. TEST UTILITIES AND HELPERS\n\n**Test Utility Classes**:\n\n**`TestUtils`** (`/workspace/clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/test/TestUtils.java`):\n- File operations: `tempDir()`, `tempFile()`\n- Random data generation: `SEEDED_RANDOM`, `RANDOM`\n- Constants: `LETTERS`, `DIGITS`, `LETTERS_AND_DIGITS`\n- Cluster helpers: `singletonCluster()`, `createCluster()`\n- Client creation: `createAdminClient()`, `createConsumer()`, `createProducer()`\n- Polling utilities: `waitForCondition()` with retries\n- Message helpers: `RequestTestUtils`, `ByteBufferChannel`\n\n**Mock Objects**:\n- `MockTime` - controllable time for testing\n- `MockSelector` - network selector simulation\n- `MockClient` - HTTP client mock for admin operations\n- `MockMetadataUpdater` - metadata management mock\n- `MockMetricsReporter`, `MockSerializer`, `MockDeserializer`\n- `MockPartitioner`, `MockConsumerInterceptor`, `MockProducerInterceptor`\n\n**Admin Client Testing**:\n\n`AdminClientUnitTestEnv.java`:\n```java\npublic class AdminClientUnitTestEnv implements AutoCloseable {\n    private final Time time;\n    private final Cluster cluster;\n    private final MockClient mockClient;\n    private final KafkaAdminClient adminClient;\n    \n    // Constructor setups mock infrastructure\n    // Use try-with-resources for automatic cleanup\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 6. TEST PATTERNS AND CONVENTIONS\n\n**Naming Conventions**:\n- Unit Tests: `*Test.scala` or `*Test.java`\n- Integration Tests: `*IntegrationTest.scala` or `*IntegrationTest.java`\n- Parameterized Tests: Same naming, use `@ParameterizedTest` annotation\n- Test methods: `test<Feature>()` pattern\n\n**Common Test Patterns**:\n\n1. **Setup/Teardown with JUnit 5**:\n   ```scala\n   @BeforeEach\n   override def setUp(testInfo: TestInfo): Unit = {\n     super.setUp(testInfo)\n     // initialization\n   }\n   \n   @AfterEach\n   override def tearDown(): Unit = {\n     TestUtils.shutdownServers(_brokers)\n     super.tearDown()\n   }\n   ```\n\n2. **Try-with-resources for Auto-cleanup**:\n   ```java\n   try (AdminClientUnitTestEnv env = new AdminClientUnitTestEnv(cluster)) {\n       Admin admin = env.adminClient();\n       // test code\n   }\n   ```\n\n3. **Mocking with Mockito**:\n   ```scala\n   val apiHandler = mock(classOf[ApiRequestHandler])\n   when(apiHandler.handle(any(), any())).thenAnswer { _ =>\n     // behavior\n   }\n   verify(apiHandler, times(2)).handle(any(), any())\n   ```\n\n4. **Wait for Condition Pattern**:\n   ```java\n   TestUtils.waitForCondition(() -> condition.isMet(), \n       DEFAULT_MAX_WAIT_MS, \"Condition not met\");\n   ```\n\n5. **Timeout Control**:\n   ```scala\n   @Timeout(value = 15, unit = TimeUnit.SECONDS, \n            threadMode = Timeout.ThreadMode.SEPARATE_THREAD)\n   def testLongRunning(): Unit = { }\n   ```\n\n---\n\n### 7. INTEGRATION TESTING WITH CLUSTERS\n\n**Security Protocol Support**:\n- PLAINTEXT\n- SSL (with TrustStore/KeyStore)\n- SASL (PLAIN, SCRAM, OAUTHBEARER, GSSAPI)\n\n**Configuration Builders** (in `IntegrationTestHarness`):\n```scala\nprotected def securityProtocol: SecurityProtocol = SecurityProtocol.PLAINTEXT\nprotected def listenerName: ListenerName = ListenerName.forSecurityProtocol(securityProtocol)\nprotected def trustStoreFile: Option[File] = None\nprotected def serverSaslProperties: Option[Properties] = None\nprotected def clientSaslProperties: Option[Properties] = None\n```\n\n**Cluster Types Supported**:\n- **ZooKeeper-based**: Traditional ZK-managed clusters\n- **KRaft-based**: Kraft metadata mode (KIP-500)\n- **Hybrid**: ZK migration mode\n\n---\n\n### 8. TROGDOR - PERFORMANCE & WORKLOAD TESTING\n\n**Location**: `/workspace/trogdor`\n\n**Purpose**: Distributed performance testing and fault injection framework\n\n**Architecture**:\n- **Coordinator**: Central task manager\n- **Agents**: Run on cluster nodes, execute tasks\n- **Tasks**: Specifications in JSON format\n\n**Task Types**:\n\n1. **Workloads** (performance testing):\n   - `ProduceBenchSpec`: Measures producer latency (p50, p95, p99)\n   - `ConsumeBenchSpec`: Measures consumer latency\n   - `RoundTripWorkload`: End-to-end latency testing\n   - `ConnectionStressSpec`: Connection pool testing\n   - `SustainedConnectionSpec`: Long-duration connection testing\n\n2. **Faults** (chaos engineering):\n   - `ProcessStopFault`: SIGSTOP/SIGCONT signals\n   - `NetworkPartitionFault`: iptables-based network isolation\n   - `DegradedNetworkFault`: Latency/packet loss injection\n   - `KiboshFault`: Disk I/O manipulation\n   - `FilesUnreadableFault`: Permission changes\n\n3. **Task States**: PENDING \u2192 RUNNING \u2192 STOPPING \u2192 DONE\n\n**Task Specification Format**:\n```json\n{\n  \"class\": \"org.apache.kafka.trogdor.workload.ProduceBenchSpec\",\n  \"startMs\": 1000,\n  \"durationMs\": 30000,\n  \"producerNode\": \"node0\",\n  \"bootstrapServers\": \"localhost:9092\",\n  \"targetMessagesPerSec\": 10000,\n  \"activeTopics\": {\n    \"foo[1-3]\": {\n      \"numPartitions\": 10,\n      \"replicationFactor\": 1\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Key Features**:\n- Histogram tracking for latency percentiles\n- Payload generators (constant, Gaussian, random, timestamp-based)\n- Throughput generators with configurable patterns\n- JSON REST API for task management\n- Idempotent RPC operations\n\n---\n\n### 9. TEST CONFIGURATION & ENVIRONMENT\n\n**JVM Settings** (from `build.gradle`):\n```gradle\ndefaultMaxHeapSize = \"2g\"\ndefaultJvmArgs = [\"-Xss4m\", \"-XX:+UseParallelGC\"]\n\n// Java 16+ special handling for module access\nif (JavaVersion.current().isCompatibleWith(JavaVersion.VERSION_16))\n  defaultJvmArgs.addAll(\n    \"--add-opens=java.base/java.io=ALL-UNNAMED\",\n    \"--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED\",\n    // ... more module access configurations\n  )\n```\n\n**Test Compiler Options**:\n- Parameter annotations enabled: `-parameters` flag\n- This enables `TestInfo#getDisplayName()` with actual parameter names\n\n**Test Properties**:\n- `maxParallelForks`: Parallelization control\n- `maxScalaacThreads`: Scala compiler threads\n- `ignoreFailures`: Soft failure mode\n- `maxTestRetries`, `maxTestRetryFailures`: Flaky test handling\n- `testLoggingEvents`: Custom logging configuration\n\n---\n\n### 10. SPECIAL TESTING TOOLS\n\n**TestUtils Features**:\n- Temporary file/directory management\n- Random string/byte array generation\n- Cluster metadata creation\n- Request serialization helpers\n- Exit code mocking\n- Network receive simulation\n\n**Advanced Mocking**:\n- `DelayedReceive`: Simulates network delays\n- `MockSelector`: Mocks network I/O\n- `MockPartitioner`: Custom partition assignment\n- `CounterConsumerRebalanceListener`: Rebalance tracking\n\n---\n\n### 11. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE\n\n```\n/workspace\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 build.gradle                          # Main build config with test settings\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 gradle/\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 dependencies.gradle               # All test dependencies defined here\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 clients/src/test/java/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 org/apache/kafka/clients/         # NetworkClient, Admin tests\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 org/apache/kafka/clients/admin/   # Admin client unit tests (135 files)\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/# Consumer tests\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/# Producer tests\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 org/apache/kafka/test/            # Shared test utilities (TestUtils, MockClient, etc.)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 core/src/test/scala/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 integration/kafka/api/            # Integration tests (producer, consumer, admin)\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 integration/kafka/server/         # Server/broker integration tests\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 kafka/server/                     # Unit tests (328 files)\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 kafka/utils/                      # Testing utilities\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 trogdor/\n    \u251c\u2500\u2500 src/main/java/                    # Performance/fault injection framework\n    \u2514\u2500\u2500 README.md                         # Detailed architecture and usage\n```\n\n---\n\n### 12. KEY TEST BASE CLASSES REFERENCE\n\n| Class | Location | Purpose |\n|-------|----------|---------|\n| `QuorumTestHarness` | `core/src/test/scala/unit/kafka/integration/KafkaServerTestHarness.scala` | Base for all cluster tests (ZK or KRaft) |\n| `KafkaServerTestHarness` | Same file | Broker lifecycle management |\n| `IntegrationTestHarness` | `core/src/test/scala/integration/kafka/api/IntegrationTestHarness.scala` | Client integration tests |\n| `BaseProducerSendTest` | `core/src/test/scala/integration/kafka/api/BaseProducerSendTest.scala` | Producer test base |\n| `AdminClientUnitTestEnv` | `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/admin/AdminClientUnitTestEnv.java` | Mock admin client setup |\n| `TestUtils` | `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/test/TestUtils.java` | Shared test helpers |\n\n---\n\nThis comprehensive exploration reveals a sophisticated, multi-layered testing infrastructure combining unit testing (JUnit 5), integration testing (custom harnesses), mocking (Mockito), and performance testing (Trogdor) frameworks, with extensive support for various security models and cluster configurations.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Explore the Apache Kafka test suite to understand testing frameworks and patterns:\n\n1. **Unit Testing Framework:**\n   - Look in /workspace/core/src/test/scala and /workspace/clients/src/test for test files\n   - Identify what test framework is used (JUnit, ScalaTest, etc.)\n   - Find example test files and analyze their structure\n   - Look for test base classes or traits that tests extend\n\n2. **Integration Testing:**\n   - Look in /workspace/tests/src/main/java for integration tests\n   - Find test utilities and helper classes\n   - Look for test fixtures or test setup patterns\n   - Find cluster setup and cleanup code\n\n3. **Test Dependencies:**\n   - Look at build.gradle to see testImplementation dependencies\n   - Find JUnit versions, ScalaTest versions, mocking libraries\n   - Look for test utilities like TestUtils, MockServer, etc.\n\n4. **Test Patterns:**\n   - Find example unit tests in core/src/test\n   - Find example integration tests in tests/src\n   - Look for test naming conventions\n   - Look for mock objects and test helpers\n\n5. **Special Testing Tools:**\n   - Look for Trogdor testing framework (performance/workload testing)\n   - Look for QuickTest or similar utilities\n   - Find test constants and configuration builders\n\nKey search areas:\n- /workspace/build.gradle for test dependencies\n- /workspace/core/src/test/scala for Scala test examples\n- /workspace/clients/src/test/java for Java test examples\n- /workspace/tests/src/main/java for integration test patterns\n- /workspace/trogdor for performance testing framework",

17\u2192import org.gradle.api.JavaVersion\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192buildscript {\n    22\u2192  repositories {\n    23\u2192 non-Git files\n    29\u2192    classpath \"org.ajoberstar.grgit:grgit-core:$versions.grgit\"\n    30\u2192  }\n    31\u2192}\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192plugins {\n    34\u2192  id 'com.github.ben-manes.versions' version '0.48.0'\n    35\u2192  id 'idea'\n    36\u2192  id 'jacoco'\n    37\u2192  id 'java-library'\n    38\u2192  id 'org.owasp.dependencycheck' version '8.2.1'\n spotbugs gradle plugin, check if it already\n    43\u2192  // includes spotbugs version 4.7.4, in which case CVE-2022-42920 can\n    44\u2192  // be dropped from gradle/resources/dependencycheck-suppressions.xml\n    45\u2192  id \"com.github.spotbugs\" version '5.1.3' apply false\n    46\u2192  id 'org.scoverage' version '8.0.3' apply false\n    47\u2192  // Updating the shadow plugin version to 8.1.1 causes issue with signing and publishing the shadowed\n    48\u2192  // artifacts - see https://github.com/johnrengelman/shadow/issues/901\n    49\u2192  id 'com.github.johnrengelman.shadow' version '8.1.0' apply false\n    50\u2192  //  Spotless 6.13.0 has issue with Java 21 (see https://github.com/diffplug/spotless/pull/1920), and Spotless 6.14.0+ requires JRE 11\n    51\u2192  //  We are going to drop JDK8 support. Hence, the spotless is upgrade to newest version and be applied only if the build env is compatible with JDK 11.\n    52\u2192  //  spotless 6.15.0+ has issue in runtime with JDK8 even through we define it with `apply:false`. see https://github.com/diffplug/spotless/issues/2156 for more details\n    53\u2192  id 'com.diffplug.spotless' version \"6.14.0\" apply false\n    54\u2192}\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192ext {\n    57\u2192  gradleVersion = versions.gradle\n    58\u2192  minJavaVersion = 8\n    59\u2192  buildVersionFileName = \"kafka-version.properties\"\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192  defaultMaxHeapSize = \"2g\"\n    62\u2192  defaultJvmArgs = [\"-Xss4m\", \"-XX:+UseParallelGC\"]\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192  // \"JEP 403: Strongly Encapsulate JDK Internals\" causes some tests to fail when they try\n    65\u2192  // to access internals (often via mocking libraries). We use `--add-opens` as a workaround\n    66\u2192  // for now and we'll fix it properly (where possible) via KAFKA-13275.\n    67\u2192  if (JavaVersion.current().isCompatibleWith(JavaVersion.VERSION_16))\n    68\u2192    defaultJvmArgs.addAll(\n    69\u2192      \"--add-opens=java.base/java.io=ALL-UNNAMED\",\n    70\u2192      \"--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED\",\n    71\u2192      \"--add-opens=java.base/java.nio=ALL-UNNAMED\",\n    72\u2192      \"--add-opens=java.base/java.nio.file=ALL-UNNAMED\",\n    73\u2192      \"--add-opens=java.base/java.util=ALL-UNNAMED\",\n    74\u2192      \"--add-opens=java.base/java.util.concurrent=ALL-UNNAMED\",\n    75\u2192      \"--add-opens=java.base/java.util.regex=ALL-UNNAMED\",\n    76\u2192      \"--add-opens=java.base/java.util.stream=ALL-UNNAMED\",\n    77\u2192      \"--add-opens=java.base/java.text=ALL-UNNAMED\",\n    78\u2192      \"--add-opens=java.base/java.time=ALL-UNNAMED\",\n    79\u2192      \"--add-opens=java.security.jgss/sun.security.krb5=ALL-UNNAMED\"\n    80\u2192    )\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192  maxTestForks = project.hasProperty('maxParallelForks') ? maxParallelForks.toInteger() : Runtime.runtime.availableProcessors()\n    83\u2192  maxScalacThreads = project.hasProperty('maxScalacThreads') ? maxScalacThreads.toInteger() :\n    84\u2192      Math.min(Runtime.runtime.availableProcessors(), 8)\n    85\u2192  userIgnoreFailures = project.hasProperty('ignoreFailures') ? ignoreFailures : false\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192  userMaxTestRetries = project.hasProperty('maxTestRetries') ? maxTestRetries.toInteger() : 0\n    88\u2192  userMaxTestRetryFailures = project.hasProperty('maxTestRetryFailures') ? maxTestRetryFailures.toInteger() : 0\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192  skipSigning = project.hasProperty('skipSigning') && skipSigning.toBoolean()\n    91\u2192  shouldSign = !skipSigning && !version.endsWith(\"SNAPSHOT\")\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192  mavenUrl = project.hasProperty('mavenUrl') ? project.mavenUrl : ''\n    94\u2192  mavenUsername = project.hasProperty('mavenUsername') ? project.mavenUsername : ''\n    95\u2192  mavenPassword = project.hasProperty('mavenPassword') ? project.mavenPassword : ''\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192  userShowStandardStreams = project.hasProperty(\"showStandardStreams\") ? showStandardStreams : null\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192  userTestLoggingEvents = project.hasProperty(\"testLoggingEvents\") ? Arrays.asList(testLoggingEvents.split(\",\")) : null\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192  userEnableTestCoverage = project.hasProperty(\"enableTestCoverage\") ? enableTestCoverage : false\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192  userKeepAliveModeString = project.hasProperty(\"keepAliveMode\") ? keepAliveMode : \"daemon\"\n   104\u2192  userKeepAliveMode = KeepAliveMode.values().find(m -> m.name().toLowerCase().equals(userKeepAliveModeString))\n   105\u2192  if (userKeepAliveMode == null) {\n   106\u2192    def keepAliveValues = KeepAliveMode.values().collect(m -> m.name.toLowerCase())\n   107\u2192    throw new GradleException(\"Unexpected value for keepAliveMode property. Expected one of $keepAliveValues, but received: $userKeepAliveModeString\")\n   108\u2192  }\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192  // See README.md for details on this option and the reasoning for the default\n   111\u2192  userScalaOptimizerMode = project.hasProperty(\"scalaOptimizerMode\") ? scalaOptimizerMode : \"inline-kafka\"\n   112\u2192  def scalaOptimizerValues = [\"none\", \"method\", \"inline-kafka\", \"inline-scala\"]\n   113\u2192  if (!scalaOptimizerValues.contains(userScalaOptimizerMode))\n   114\u2192    throw new GradleException(\"Unexpected value for scalaOptimizerMode property. Expected one of $scalaOptimizerValues, but received: $userScalaOptimizerMode\")\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192  generatedDocsDir = new File(\"${project.rootDir}/docs/generated\")\n   117\u2192  repo = file(\"$rootDir/.git\").isDirectory() ? Grgit.open(currentDir: project.getRootDir()) : null\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192  commitId = determineCommitId()\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192  addParametersForTests = { name, options ->\n   122\u2192    // -parameters generates arguments with parameter names in TestInfo#getDisplayName.\n   123\u2192    // ref: https://github.com/junit-team/junit5/blob/4c0dddad1b96d4a20e92a2cd583954643ac56ac0/junit-jupiter-params/src/main/java/org/junit/jupiter/params/ParameterizedTest.java#L161-L164\n   124\u2192    if (name == \"compileTestJava\" || name == \"compileTestScala\")\n   125\u2192      options.compilerArgs << \"-parameters\"\n   126\u2192  }\n   127\u2192}\n   128\u2192\n   129\u2192allprojects {\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192  repositories {\n   132\u2192    mavenCentral()\n   133\u2192  }\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192  dependencyUpdates {\n   136\u2192    revision=\"release\"\n   137\u2192    resolutionStrategy {\n   138\u2192      componentSelection { rules ->\n   139\u2192        rules.all { ComponentSelection selection ->\n   140\u2192          boolean rejected = ['snap', 'alpha', 'beta', 'rc', 'cr', 'm'].any { qualifier ->\n   141\u2192            selection.candidate.version ==~ /(?i).*[.-]${qualifier}[.\\d-]*/\n   142\u2192          }\n   143\u2192          if (rejected) {\n   144\u2192            selection.reject('Release candidate')\n   145\u2192          }\n   146\u2192        }\n   147\u2192      }\n   148\u2192    }\n   149\u2192  }\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192  configurations.all {\n   152\u2192    // zinc is the Scala incremental compiler, it has a configuration for its own dependencies\n   153\u2192    // that are unrelated to the project dependencies, we should not change them\n   154\u2192    if (name != \"zinc\") {\n   155\u2192      resolutionStrategy {\n   156\u2192        force(\n   157\u2192          // be explicit about the javassist dependency version instead of relying on the transitive version\n   158\u2192          libs.javassist,\n   159\u2192          // ensure we have a single version in the classpath despite transitive dependencies\n   160\u2192          libs.scalaLibrary,\n   161\u2192          libs.scalaReflect,\n   162\u2192          libs.jacksonAnnotations,\n   163\u2192          // be explicit about the Netty dependency version instead of relying on the version set by\n   164\u2192          // ZooKeeper (potentially older and containing CVEs)\n   165\u2192          libs.nettyHandler,\n   166\u2192          libs.nettyTransportNativeEpoll,\n   167\u2192          // be explicit about the reload4j version instead of relying on the transitive versions\n   168\u2192          libs.reload4j\n   169\u2192        )\n   170\u2192      }\n   171\u2192    }\n   172\u2192  }\n   173\u2192  task printAllDependencies(type: DependencyReportTask) {}\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192  tasks.withType(Javadoc) {\n   176\u2192    options.charSet = 'UTF-8'\n   177\u2192    options.docEncoding = 'UTF-8'\n   178\u2192    options.encoding = 'UTF-8'\n   179\u2192    options.memberLevel = JavadocMemberLevel.PUBLIC  // Document only public members/API\n   180\u2192    // Turn off doclint for now, see https://blog.joda.org/2014/02/turning-off-doclint-in-jdk-8-javadoc.html for rationale\n   181\u2192    options.addStringOption('Xdoclint:none', '-quiet')\n   182\u2192    // Javadoc warnings should fail the build in JDK 15+ https://bugs.openjdk.org/browse/JDK-8200363\n   183\u2192    options.addBooleanOption('Werror', JavaVersion.current().isCompatibleWith(JavaVersion.VERSION_15))\n   184\u2192\n   185\u2192    // The URL structure was changed to include the locale after Java 8\n   186\u2192    if (JavaVersion.current().isJava11Compatible())\n   187\u2192      options.links \"https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/${JavaVersion.current().majorVersion}/docs/api/\"\n   188\u2192    else\n   189\u2192      options.links \"https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/\"\n   190\u2192  }\n   191\u2192}\n   192\u2192\n   193\u2192def determineCommitId() {\n   194\u2192  def takeFromHash = 16\n   195\u2192  if (project.hasProperty('commitId')) {\n   196\u2192    commitId.take(takeFromHash)\n   197\u2192  } else if (repo != null) {\n   198\u2192    repo.head().id.take(takeFromHash)\n   199\u2192  } else {\n   200\u2192    \"unknown\"\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

See the License for the\n    16\u2192 * specific language governing permissions and limitations\n    17\u2192 * under the License.\n    18\u2192 */\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192ext {\n    21\u2192  versions = [:]\n    22\u2192  libs = [:]\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192  // Available if -PscalaVersion is used. This is useful when we want to support a Scala version that has\n    25\u2192  // a higher minimum Java requirement than Kafka. This was previously the case for Scala 2.12 and Java 7.\n    26\u2192  availableScalaVersions = [ '2.12', '2.13' ]\n    27\u2192}\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192// Add Scala version\n    30\u2192def defaultScala212Version = '2.12.19'\n    31\u2192def defaultScala213Version = '2.13.14'\n    32\u2192if (hasProperty('scalaVersion')) {\n    33\u2192  if (scalaVersion == '2.12') {\n    34\u2192    versions[\"scala\"] = defaultScala212Version\n    35\u2192  } else if (scalaVersion == '2.13') {\n    36\u2192    versions[\"scala\"] = defaultScala213Version\n    37\u2192  }  else {\n    38\u2192    versions[\"scala\"] = scalaVersion\n    39\u2192  }\n    40\u2192} else {\n    41\u2192  versions[\"scala\"] = defaultScala212Version\n    42\u2192}\n    43\u2192\n    44\u2192/* Resolve base Scala version according to these patterns:\n    45\u2192 1. generally available Scala versions (such as: 2.12.y and 2.13.z) corresponding base versions will be: 2.12 and 2.13 (respectively)\n    46\u2192 2. pre-release Scala versions (i.e. milestone/rc, such as: 2.13.0-M5, 2.13.0-RC1, 2.14.0-M1, etc.) will have identical base versions;\n    47\u2192    rationale: pre-release Scala versions are not binary compatible with each other and that's the reason why libraries include the full\n    48\u2192    Scala release string in their name for pre-releases (see dependencies below with an artifact name suffix '_$versions.baseScala')\n    49\u2192*/\n    50\u2192if ( !versions.scala.contains('-') ) {\n    51\u2192  versions[\"baseScala\"] = versions.scala.substring(0, versions.scala.lastIndexOf(\".\"))\n    52\u2192} else {\n    53\u2192  versions[\"baseScala\"] = versions.scala\n    54\u2192}\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192// mockito >= 5.5 is required for Java 21 and mockito 5.x requires at least Java 11\n    57\u2192// mockito 4.9 is required for Scala 2.12 as a workaround for compiler errors due to ambiguous reference to `Mockito.spy`\n    58\u2192// since Scala 2.12 support is going away soon, this is simpler than adjusting the code.\n    59\u2192// mockito 4.11 is used with Java 8 and Scala 2.13\n    60\u2192String mockitoVersion\n    61\u2192if (scalaVersion == \"2.12\")\n    62\u2192  mockitoVersion = \"4.9.0\"\n    63\u2192else if (JavaVersion.current().isCompatibleWith(JavaVersion.VERSION_11))\n    64\u2192  mockitoVersion = \"5.10.0\"\n    65\u2192else\n    66\u2192  mockitoVersion = \"4.11.0\"\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192// mockito 4.x has two mocking strategies: subclass (mockito-core) and inline (mockito-inline).\n    69\u2192// mockito 5.x has two mocking strategies: inline (mockito-core) and subclass (mockito-subclass).\n    70\u2192// The default strategy (i.e. what `mockito-core` uses) changed to `inline` in 5.x because it works better with newer\n    71\u2192// Java versions.\n    72\u2192// We always use the `inline` strategy.\n    73\u2192String mockitoArtifactName\n    74\u2192if (mockitoVersion.startsWith(\"4.\"))\n    75\u2192  mockitoArtifactName = \"mockito-inline\"\n    76\u2192else\n    77\u2192  mockitoArtifactName = \"mockito-core\"\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192// When adding, removing or updating dependencies, please also update the LICENSE-binary file accordingly.\n    80\u2192// See https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/KAFKA-12622 for steps to verify the LICENSE-binary file is correct.\n    81\u2192versions += [\n    82\u2192  activation: \"1.1.1\",\n    83\u2192  apacheda: \"1.0.2\",\n    84\u2192  apacheds: \"2.0.0-M24\",\n    85\u2192  argparse4j: \"0.7.0\",\n    86\u2192  bcpkix: \"1.75\",\n    87\u2192  caffeine: \"2.9.3\", // 3.x supports JDK 11 and above\n    88\u2192  // when updating checkstyle, check whether the exclusion of\n    89\u2192  // CVE-2023-2976 and CVE-2020-8908 can be dropped from\n    90\u2192  // gradle/resources/dependencycheck-suppressions.xml\n    91\u2192  checkstyle: \"8.36.2\",\n    92\u2192  commonsCli: \"1.4\",\n    93\u2192  commonsIo: \"2.14.0\", // ZooKeeper dependency. 19\u2192\n    20\u2192import java.time.Duration\n    21\u2192import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.{Consumer, ConsumerConfig, KafkaConsumer}\n    22\u2192import kafka.utils.TestUtils\n    23\u2192import kafka.utils.Implicits._\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192import java.util.Properties\n    26\u2192import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.{KafkaProducer, ProducerConfig}\n    27\u2192import kafka.server.KafkaConfig\n    28\u2192import kafka.integration.KafkaServerTestHarness\n    29\u2192import org.apache.kafka.clients.admin.{Admin, AdminClientConfig}\n    30\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.network.{ListenerName, ConnectionMode}\n    31\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.{ByteArrayDeserializer, ByteArraySerializer, Deserializer, Serializer}\n    32\u2192import org.apache.kafka.coordinator.group.GroupCoordinatorConfig\n    33\u2192import org.apache.kafka.network.SocketServerConfigs\n    34\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.config.{KRaftConfigs, ReplicationConfigs}\n    35\u2192import org.junit.jupiter.api.{AfterEach, BeforeEach, TestInfo}\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192import scala.collection.mutable\n    38\u2192import scala.collection.Seq\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192/**\n    41\u2192 * A helper class for writing integration tests that involve producers, consumers, and servers\n    42\u2192 */\n    43\u2192abstract class IntegrationTestHarness extends KafkaServerTestHarness {\n    44\u2192  protected def brokerCount: Int\n    45\u2192  protected def logDirCount: Int = 1\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192  val producerConfig = new Properties\n    48\u2192  val consumerConfig = new Properties\n    49\u2192  val adminClientConfig = new Properties\n    50\u2192  val superuserClientConfig = new Properties\n    51\u2192  val serverConfig = new Properties\n    52\u2192  val controllerConfig = new Properties\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192  private val consumers = mutable.Buffer[Consumer[_, _]]()\n    55\u2192  private val producers = mutable.Buffer[KafkaProducer[_, _]]()\n    56\u2192  private val adminClients = mutable.Buffer[Admin]()\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192  protected def interBrokerListenerName: ListenerName = listenerName\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192  protected def modifyConfigs(props: Seq[Properties]): Unit = {\n    61\u2192    props.foreach(_ ++= serverConfig)\n    62\u2192  }\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192  override def generateConfigs: Seq[KafkaConfig] = {\n    65\u2192    val cfgs = TestUtils.createBrokerConfigs(brokerCount, zkConnectOrNull, interBrokerSecurityProtocol = Some(securityProtocol),\n    66\u2192      trustStoreFile = trustStoreFile, saslProperties = serverSaslProperties, logDirCount = logDirCount)\n    67\u2192    configureListeners(cfgs)\n    68\u2192    modifyConfigs(cfgs)\n    69\u2192    if (isZkMigrationTest()) {\n    70\u2192      cfgs.foreach(_.setProperty(KRaftConfigs.MIGRATION_ENABLED_CONFIG, \"true\"))\n    71\u2192    }\n    72\u2192    if (isNewGroupCoordinatorEnabled()) {\n    73\u2192      cfgs.foreach(_.setProperty(GroupCoordinatorConfig.NEW_GROUP_COORDINATOR_ENABLE_CONFIG, \"true\"))\n    74\u2192      cfgs.foreach(_.setProperty(GroupCoordinatorConfig.GROUP_COORDINATOR_REBALANCE_PROTOCOLS_CONFIG, \"classic,consumer\"))\n    75\u2192    }\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192    if(isKRaftTest()) {\n    78\u2192      cfgs.foreach(_.setProperty(KRaftConfigs.METADATA_LOG_DIR_CONFIG, TestUtils.tempDir().getAbsolutePath))\n    79\u2192    }\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192    insertControllerListenersIfNeeded(cfgs)\n    82\u2192    cfgs.map(KafkaConfig.fromProps)\n    83\u2192  }\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192  override protected def kraftControllerConfigs(testInfo: TestInfo): Seq[Properties] = {\n    86\u2192    Seq(controllerConfig)\n    87\u2192  }\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192  protected def configureListeners(props: Seq[Properties]): Unit = {\n    90\u2192    props.foreach { config =>\n    91\u2192      config.remove(ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_CONFIG)\n    92\u2192      config.setProperty(ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG, interBrokerListenerName.value)\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192      val listenerNames = Set(listenerName, interBrokerListenerName)\n    95\u2192      val listeners = listenerNames.map(listenerName => s\"${listenerName.value}://localhost:${TestUtils.RandomPort}\").mkString(\",\")\n    96\u2192      val listenerSecurityMap = listenerNames.map(listenerName => s\"${listenerName.value}:${securityProtocol.name}\").mkString(\",\")\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192      config.setProperty(SocketServerConfigs.LISTENERS_CONFIG, listeners)\n    99\u2192      config.setProperty(SocketServerConfigs.ADVERTISED_LISTENERS_CONFIG, listeners)\n   100\u2192      config.setProperty(SocketServerConfigs.LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_CONFIG, listenerSecurityMap)\n   101\u2192    }\n   102\u2192  }\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192  private def insertControllerListenersIfNeeded(props: Seq[Properties]): Unit = {\n   105\u2192    if (isKRaftTest()) {\n   106\u2192      props.foreach { config =>\n   107\u2192        // Add a security protocol for the controller endpoints, if one is not already set.\n   108\u2192        val securityPairs = config.getProperty(SocketServerConfigs.LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_CONFIG, \"\").split(\",\")\n   109\u2192        val toAdd = config.getProperty(KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG, \"\").split(\",\").filter(\n   110\u2192          e => !securityPairs.exists(_.startsWith(s\"$e:\")))\n   111\u2192        if (toAdd.nonEmpty) {\n   112\u2192          config.setProperty(SocketServerConfigs.LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_CONFIG, (securityPairs ++\n   113\u2192            toAdd.map(e => s\"$e:${controllerListenerSecurityProtocol.toString}\")).mkString(\",\"))\n   114\u2192        }\n   115\u2192      }\n   116\u2192    }\n   117\u2192  }\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192  @BeforeEach\n   120\u2192  override def setUp(testInfo: TestInfo): Unit = {\n   121\u2192    doSetup(testInfo, createOffsetsTopic = true)\n   122\u2192  }\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192  /*\n   125\u2192   * The superuser by default is set up the same as the admin.\n   126\u2192   * Some tests need a separate principal for superuser operations.\n   127\u2192   * These tests may need to override the config before creating the offset topic.\n   128\u2192   */\n   129\u2192  protected def doSuperuserSetup(testInfo: TestInfo): Unit = {\n   130\u2192    superuserClientConfig.put(AdminClientConfig.BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG, bootstrapServers())\n   131\u2192  }\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192  def doSetup(testInfo: TestInfo,\n   134\u2192              createOffsetsTopic: Boolean): Unit = {\n   135\u2192    // Generate client security properties before starting the brokers in case certs are needed\n   136\u2192    producerConfig ++= clientSecurityProps(\"producer\")\n   137\u2192    consumerConfig ++= clientSecurityProps(\"consumer\")\n   138\u2192    adminClientConfig ++= clientSecurityProps(\"adminClient\")\n   139\u2192    superuserClientConfig ++= superuserSecurityProps(\"superuserClient\")\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192    super.setUp(testInfo)\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192    producerConfig.put(ProducerConfig.BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG, bootstrapServers())\n   144\u2192    producerConfig.putIfAbsent(ProducerConfig.ACKS_CONFIG, \"-1\")\n   145\u2192    producerConfig.putIfAbsent(ProducerConfig.KEY_SERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, classOf[ByteArraySerializer].getName)\n   146\u2192    producerConfig.putIfAbsent(ProducerConfig.VALUE_SERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, classOf[ByteArraySerializer].getName)\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192    consumerConfig.put(ConsumerConfig.BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG, bootstrapServers())\n   149\u2192    consumerConfig.putIfAbsent(ConsumerConfig.AUTO_OFFSET_RESET_CONFIG, \"earliest\")\n   150\u2192    consumerConfig.putIfAbsent(ConsumerConfig.GROUP_ID_CONFIG, \"group\")\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

19\u2192\n    20\u2192import java.io.File\n    21\u2192import java.net.InetSocketAddress\n    22\u2192import java.util\n    23\u2192import java.util.{Collections, Optional, OptionalInt, Properties}\n    24\u2192import java.util.concurrent.{CompletableFuture, TimeUnit}\n    25\u2192import javax.security.auth.login.Configuration\n    26\u2192import kafka.utils.{CoreUtils, Logging, TestInfoUtils, TestUtils}\n    27\u2192import kafka.zk.{AdminZkClient, EmbeddedZookeeper, KafkaZkClient}\n    28\u2192import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.GroupProtocol\n    29\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.metrics.Metrics\n    30\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.security.JaasUtils\n    31\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.SecurityProtocol\n    32\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.{Exit, Time}\n    33\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.{DirectoryId, Uuid}\n    34\u2192import org.apache.kafka.metadata.properties.MetaPropertiesEnsemble.VerificationFlag.{REQUIRE_AT_LEAST_ONE_VALID, REQUIRE_METADATA_LOG_DIR}\n    35\u2192import org.apache.kafka.metadata.properties.{MetaProperties, MetaPropertiesEnsemble, MetaPropertiesVersion}\n    36\u2192import org.apache.kafka.metadata.storage.Formatter\n    37\u2192import org.apache.kafka.network.SocketServerConfigs\n    38\u2192import org.apache.kafka.raft.QuorumConfig\n    39\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.common.MetadataVersion\n    40\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.config.{KRaftConfigs, ServerConfigs, ServerLogConfigs}\n    41\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.fault.{FaultHandler, MockFaultHandler}\n    42\u2192import org.apache.zookeeper.client.ZKClientConfig\n    43\u2192import org.apache.zookeeper.{WatchedEvent, Watcher, ZooKeeper}\n    44\u2192import org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions._\n    45\u2192import org.junit.jupiter.api.{AfterAll, AfterEach, BeforeAll, BeforeEach, Tag, TestInfo}\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192import java.nio.file.{Files, Paths}\n    48\u2192import scala.collection.Seq\n    49\u2192import scala.compat.java8.OptionConverters._\n    50\u2192import scala.jdk.CollectionConverters._\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192trait QuorumImplementation {\n    53\u2192  def createBroker(\n    54\u2192    config: KafkaConfig,\n    55\u2192    time: Time = Time.SYSTEM,\n    56\u2192    startup: Boolean = true,\n    57\u2192    threadNamePrefix: Option[String] = None,\n    58\u2192  ): KafkaBroker\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192  def shutdown(): Unit\n    61\u2192}\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192class ZooKeeperQuorumImplementation(\n    64\u2192  val zookeeper: EmbeddedZookeeper,\n    65\u2192  val zkConnect: String,\n    66\u2192  val zkClient: KafkaZkClient,\n    67\u2192  val adminZkClient: AdminZkClient,\n    68\u2192  val log: Logging\n    69\u2192) extends QuorumImplementation {\n    70\u2192  override def createBroker(\n    71\u2192    config: KafkaConfig,\n    72\u2192    time: Time,\n    73\u2192    startup: Boolean,\n    74\u2192    threadNamePrefix: Option[String],\n    75\u2192  ): KafkaBroker = {\n    76\u2192    val server = new KafkaServer(config, time, threadNamePrefix, false)\n    77\u2192    if (startup) server.startup()\n    78\u2192    server\n    79\u2192  }\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192  override def shutdown(): Unit = {\n    82\u2192    CoreUtils.swallow(zkClient.close(), log)\n    83\u2192    CoreUtils.swallow(zookeeper.shutdown(), log)\n    84\u2192  }\n    85\u2192}\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192class KRaftQuorumImplementation(\n    88\u2192  val controllerServer: ControllerServer,\n    89\u2192  val faultHandlerFactory: FaultHandlerFactory,\n    90\u2192  val metadataDir: File,\n    91\u2192  val controllerQuorumVotersFuture: CompletableFuture[util.Map[Integer, InetSocketAddress]],\n    92\u2192  val clusterId: String,\n    93\u2192  val log: Logging,\n    94\u2192  val faultHandler: FaultHandler\n    95\u2192) extends QuorumImplementation {\n    96\u2192  override def createBroker(\n    97\u2192    config: KafkaConfig,\n    98\u2192    time: Time,\n    99\u2192    startup: Boolean,\n   100\u2192    threadNamePrefix: Option[String],\n   101\u2192  ): KafkaBroker = {\n   102\u2192    val metaPropertiesEnsemble = {\n   103\u2192      val loader = new MetaPropertiesEnsemble.Loader()\n   104\u2192      loader.addLogDirs(config.logDirs.asJava)\n   105\u2192      loader.addMetadataLogDir(config.metadataLogDir)\n   106\u2192      val ensemble = loader.load()\n   107\u2192      val copier = new MetaPropertiesEnsemble.Copier(ensemble)\n   108\u2192      ensemble.emptyLogDirs().forEach(logDir => {\n   109\u2192        copier.setLogDirProps(logDir, new MetaProperties.Builder().\n   110\u2192          setVersion(MetaPropertiesVersion.V1).\n   111\u2192          setClusterId(clusterId).\n   112\u2192          setNodeId(config.nodeId).\n   113\u2192          setDirectoryId(DirectoryId.random()).\n   114\u2192          build())\n   115\u2192      })\n   116\u2192      copier.setPreWriteHandler((logDir, _, _) => {\n   117\u2192        Files.createDirectories(Paths.get(logDir))\n   118\u2192      })\n   119\u2192      copier.writeLogDirChanges()\n   120\u2192      copier.copy()\n   121\u2192    }\n   122\u2192    metaPropertiesEnsemble.verify(Optional.of(clusterId),\n   123\u2192      OptionalInt.of(config.nodeId),\n   124\u2192      util.EnumSet.of(REQUIRE_AT_LEAST_ONE_VALID, REQUIRE_METADATA_LOG_DIR))\n   125\u2192    val sharedServer = new SharedServer(\n   126\u2192      config,\n   127\u2192      metaPropertiesEnsemble,\n   128\u2192      time,\n   129\u2192      new Metrics(),\n   130\u2192      controllerQuorumVotersFuture,\n   131\u2192      controllerQuorumVotersFuture.get().values(),\n   132\u2192      faultHandlerFactory\n   133\u2192    )\n   134\u2192    var broker: BrokerServer = null\n   135\u2192    try {\n   136\u2192      broker = new BrokerServer(sharedServer)\n   137\u2192      if (startup) broker.startup()\n   138\u2192      broker\n   139\u2192    } catch {\n   140\u2192      case e: Throwable => {\n   141\u2192        if (broker != null) CoreUtils.swallow(broker.shutdown(), log)\n   142\u2192        CoreUtils.swallow(sharedServer.stopForBroker(), log)\n   143\u2192        throw e\n   144\u2192      }\n   145\u2192    }\n   146\u2192  }\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192  override def shutdown(): Unit = {\n   149\u2192    CoreUtils.swallow(controllerServer.shutdown(), log)\n   150\u2192  }\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets\n    35\u2192import scala.annotation.nowarn\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192class PlaintextProducerSendTest extends BaseProducerSendTest {\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192  @ParameterizedTest\n    41\u2192  @ValueSource(strings = Array(\"zk\", \"kraft\"))\n    42\u2192  def testWrongSerializer(quorum: String): Unit = {\n    43\u2192    val producerProps = new Properties()\n    44\u2192    producerProps.put(ProducerConfig.BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG, bootstrapServers())\n    45\u2192    producerProps.put(ProducerConfig.KEY_SERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, \"org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringSerializer\")\n    46\u2192    producerProps.put(ProducerConfig.VALUE_SERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, \"org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringSerializer\")\n    47\u2192    val producer = registerProducer(new KafkaProducer(producerProps))\n    48\u2192    val record = new ProducerRecord[Array[Byte], Array[Byte]](topic, 0, \"key\".getBytes, \"value\".getBytes)\n    49\u2192    assertThrows(classOf[SerializationException], () => producer.send(record))\n    50\u2192  }\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192  @ParameterizedTest\n    53\u2192  @ValueSource(strings = Array(\"zk\", \"kraft\"))\n    54\u2192  def testBatchSizeZero(quorum: String): Unit = {\n    55\u2192    val producer = createProducer(\n    56\u2192      lingerMs = Int.MaxValue,\n    57\u2192      deliveryTimeoutMs = Int.MaxValue,\n    58\u2192      batchSize = 0)\n    59\u2192    sendAndVerify(producer)\n    60\u2192  }\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192  @Timeout(value = 15, unit = TimeUnit.SECONDS, threadMode = Timeout.ThreadMode.SEPARATE_THREAD)\n    63\u2192  @ParameterizedTest\n    64\u2192  @ValueSource(strings = Array(\"zk\", \"kraft\"))\n    65\u2192  def testBatchSizeZeroNoPartitionNoRecordKey(quorum: String): Unit = {\n    66\u2192    val producer = createProducer(batchSize = 0)\n    67\u2192    val numRecords = 10\n    68\u2192    try {\n    69\u2192      TestUtils.createTopicWithAdmin(admin, topic, brokers, controllerServers, 2)\n    70\u2192      val futures = for (i <- 1 to numRecords) yield {\n    71\u2192        val record = new ProducerRecord[Array[Byte], Array[Byte]](topic, null, s\"value$i\".getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8))\n    72\u2192        producer.send(record)\n    73\u2192      }\n    74\u2192      producer.flush()\n    75\u2192      val lastOffset = futures.foldLeft(0) { (offset, future) =>\n    76\u2192        val recordMetadata = future.get\n    77\u2192        assertEquals(topic, recordMetadata.topic)\n    78\u2192        offset + 1\n    79\u2192      }\n    80\u2192      assertEquals(numRecords, lastOffset)\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. Create a {@link Cluster} manually or use {@link org.apache.kafka.test.TestUtils} methods to\n    35\u2192 * easily create a simple cluster.\n    36\u2192 * <p>\n    37\u2192 * To use in a test, create an instance and prepare its {@link #kafkaClient() MockClient} with the expected responses\n    38\u2192 * for the {@link Admin}. Then, use the {@link #adminClient() AdminClient} in the test, which will then use the MockClient\n    39\u2192 * and receive the responses you provided.\n    40\u2192 *\n    41\u2192 * Since {@link #kafkaClient() MockClient} is not thread-safe,\n    42\u2192 * users should be wary of calling its methods after the {@link #adminClient() AdminClient} is instantiated.\n    43\u2192 *\n    44\u2192 * <p>\n    45\u2192 * When finished, be sure to {@link #close() close} the environment object.\n    46\u2192 */\n    47\u2192public class AdminClientUnitTestEnv implements AutoCloseable {\n    48\u2192    private final Time time;\n    49\u2192    private final Cluster cluster;\n    50\u2192    private final MockClient mockClient;\n    51\u2192    private final KafkaAdminClient adminClient;\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192    public AdminClientUnitTestEnv(Cluster cluster, String... vals) {\n    54\u2192        this(Time.SYSTEM, cluster, vals);\n    55\u2192    }\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192 AdminClientUnitTestEnv(Time time, Cluster cluster) {\n    62\u2192        this(time, cluster, clientConfigs());\n    63\u2192    }\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192    public AdminClientUnitTestEnv(Time time, Cluster cluster, Map<String, Object> config) {\n    66\u2192        this(time, cluster, config, Collections.emptyMap());\n    67\u2192    }\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192    public AdminClientUnitTestEnv(Time time, Cluster cluster, Map<String, Object> config, Map<Node, Long> unreachableNodes) {\n    70\u2192        this.time = time;\n    71\u2192        this.cluster = cluster;\n    72\u2192        AdminClientConfig adminClientConfig = new AdminClientConfig(config);\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192        AdminMetadataManager metadataManager = new AdminMetadataManager(new LogContext(),\n    75\u2192                adminClientConfig.getLong(AdminClientConfig.RETRY_BACKOFF_MS_CONFIG),\n    76\u2192                adminClientConfig.getLong(AdminClientConfig.METADATA_MAX_AGE_CONFIG), false);\n    77\u2192        this.mockClient = new MockClient(time, new MockClient.MockMetadataUpdater() {\n    78\u2192   84\u2192            public boolean isUpdateNeeded() {\n    85\u2192                return false;\n    86\u2192            }\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192            @Override\n    89\u2192            public void update(Time time, MockClient.MetadataUpdate update) {\n    90\u2192                throw new UnsupportedOperationException();\n    91\u2192            }\n    92\u2192        });\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192        metadataManager.update(cluster, time.milliseconds());\n    95\u2192        unreachableNodes.forEach(mockClient::setUnreachable);\n    96\u2192        this.adminClient = KafkaAdminClient.createInternal(adminClientConfig, metadataManager, mockClient, time);\n    97\u2192    }\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192    public Time time() {\n   100\u2192        return time;\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. scala.jdk.CollectionConverters._\n    43\u2192import scala.util.Using\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192/**\n    46\u2192 * A test harness that brings up some number of broker nodes\n    47\u2192 */\n    48\u2192abstract class KafkaServerTestHarness extends QuorumTestHarness {\n    49\u2192  var instanceConfigs: Seq[KafkaConfig] = _\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192  private val _brokers = new mutable.ArrayBuffer[KafkaBroker]\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192  /**\n    54\u2192   * Get the list of brokers, which could be either BrokerServer objects or KafkaServer objects.\n    55\u2192   */\n    56\u2192  def brokers: mutable.Buffer[KafkaBroker] = _brokers\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192  /**\n    59\u2192   * Get the list of brokers, as instances of KafkaServer.\n    60\u2192   * This method should only be used when dealing with brokers that use ZooKeeper.\n    61\u2192   */\n    62\u2192  def servers: mutable.Buffer[KafkaServer] = {\n    63\u2192    checkIsZKTest()\n    64\u2192    _brokers.asInstanceOf[mutable.Buffer[KafkaServer]]\n    65\u2192  }\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192  var alive: Array[Boolean] = _\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192  /**\n    70\u2192   * Implementations must override this method to return a set of KafkaConfigs. This method will be invoked for every\n    71\u2192   * test and should not reuse previous configurations unless they select their ports randomly when servers are started.\n    72\u2192   */\n    73\u2192  def generateConfigs: Seq[KafkaConfig]\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192  /**\n    76\u2192   * Override this in case ACLs or security credentials must be set before `servers` are started.\n    77\u2192   *\n    78\u2192   * This is required in some cases because of the topic creation in the setup of `IntegrationTestHarness`. If the ACLs\n    79\u2192   * are only set later, tests may fail. The failure could manifest itself as a cluster action\n    80\u2192   * authorization exception when processing an update metadata request (controller -> broker) or in more obscure\n    81\u2192   * ways (e.g. __consumer_offsets topic replication fails because the metadata cache has no brokers as a previous\n    82\u2192   * update metadata request failed due to an authorization exception).\n    83\u2192   *\n    84\u2192   * The default implementation of this method is a no-op.\n    85\u2192   */\n    86\u2192  def configureSecurityBeforeServersStart(testInfo: TestInfo): Unit = {}\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192  /**\n    89\u2192   * Override this in case Tokens or security credentials needs to be created after `servers` are started.\n    90\u2192   * The default implementation of this method is a no-op.\n    91\u2192   */\n    92\u2192  def configureSecurityAfterServersStart(): Unit = {}\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192  def configs: Seq[KafkaConfig] = {\n    95\u2192    if (instanceConfigs == null)\n    96\u2192      instanceConfigs = generateConfigs\n    97\u2192    instanceConfigs\n    98\u2192  }\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192  def serverForId(id: Int): Option[KafkaServer] = servers.find(s => s.config.brokerId == id)\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192  def boundPort(server: KafkaServer): Int = server.boundPort(listenerName)\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192  def bootstrapServers(listenerName: ListenerName = listenerName): String = {\n   105\u2192    TestUtils.bootstrapServers(_brokers, listenerName)\n   106\u2192  }\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192  protected def securityProtocol: SecurityProtocol = SecurityProtocol.PLAINTEXT\n   109\u2192  protected def listenerName: ListenerName = ListenerName.forSecurityProtocol(securityProtocol)\n   110\u2192  protected def trustStoreFile: Option[File] = None\n   111\u2192  protected def serverSaslProperties: Option[Properties] = None\n   112\u2192  protected def clientSaslProperties: Option[Properties] = None\n   113\u2192  protected def brokerTime(brokerId: Int): Time = Time.SYSTEM\n   114\u2192\n   115\u2192  @BeforeEach\n   116\u2192  override def setUp(testInfo: TestInfo): Unit = {\n   117\u2192    super.setUp(testInfo)\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192    if (configs.isEmpty)\n   120\u2192      throw new KafkaException(\"Must supply at least one server config.\")\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192    // default implementation is a no-op, it is overridden by subclasses if required\n   123\u2192    configureSecurityBeforeServersStart(testInfo)\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192    createBrokers(startup = true)\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192    // default implementation is a no-op, it is overridden by subclasses if required\n   129\u2192    configureSecurityAfterServersStart()\n   130\u2192  }\n   131\u2192\n   132\u2192  @AfterEach\n   133\u2192  override def tearDown(): Unit = {\n   134\u2192    TestUtils.shutdownServers(_brokers)\n   135\u2192    super.tearDown()\n   136\u2192  }\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192  def recreateBrokers(reconfigure: Boolean = false, startup: Boolean = false): Unit = {\n   139\u2192    // The test must be allowed to fail and be torn down if an exception is raised here.\n   140\u2192    if (reconfigure) {\n   141\u2192      instanceConfigs = null\n   142\u2192    }\n   143\u2192    if (configs.isEmpty)\n   144\u2192      throw new KafkaException(\"Must supply at least one server config.\")\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192    TestUtils.shutdownServers(_brokers, deleteLogDirs = false)\n   147\u2192    _brokers.clear()\n   148\u2192    util.Arrays.fill(alive, false)\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192    createBrokers(startup)\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

org.apache.kafka.test.TestUtils;\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192import java.util.ArrayList;\n    32\u2192import java.util.Collections;\n    33\u2192import java.util.HashMap;\n    34\u2192import java.util.HashSet;\n    35\u2192import java.util.Iterator;\n    36\u2192import java.util.List;\n    37\u2192import java.util.Map;\n    38\u2192import java.util.Queue;\n    39\u2192import java.util.Set;\n    40\u2192import java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture;\n    41\u2192import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentLinkedDeque;\n    42\u2192import java.util.function.Consumer;\n    43\u2192import java.util.stream.Collectors;\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192/**\n    46\u2192 * A mock network client for use testing code\n    47\u2192 */\n    48\u2192public class MockClient implements KafkaClient {\n    49\u2192    public static final RequestMatcher ALWAYS_TRUE = body -> true;\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192    private static class FutureResponse {\n    52\u2192        private final Node node;\n    53\u2192        private final RequestMatcher requestMatcher;\n    54\u2192        private final AbstractResponse responseBody;\n    55\u2192        private final boolean disconnected;\n    56\u2192        private final boolean isUnsupportedRequest;\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192        public FutureResponse(Node node,\n    59\u2192                              RequestMatcher requestMatcher,\n    60\u2192                              AbstractResponse responseBody,\n    61\u2192                              boolean disconnected,\n    62\u2192                              boolean isUnsupportedRequest) {\n    63\u2192            this.node = node;\n    64\u2192            this.requestMatcher = requestMatcher;\n    65\u2192            this.responseBody = responseBody;\n    66\u2192            this.disconnected = disconnected;\n    67\u2192            this.isUnsupportedRequest = isUnsupportedRequest;\n    68\u2192        }\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192    }\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192    private int correlation;\n    73\u2192    private Runnable wakeupHook;\n    74\u2192    private final Time time;\n    75\u2192    private final MockMetadataUpdater metadataUpdater;\n    76\u2192    private final Map<String, ConnectionState> connections = new HashMap<>();\n    77\u2192    private final Map<Node, Long> pendingAuthenticationErrors = new HashMap<>();\n    78\u2192    private final Map<Node, AuthenticationException> authenticationErrors = new HashMap<>();\n    79\u2192    // Use concurrent queue for requests so that requests may be queried from a different thread\n    80\u2192    private final Queue<ClientRequest> requests = new ConcurrentLinkedDeque<>();\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider
