"     1\u2192/**\n     2\u2192 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n     3\u2192 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with\n     4\u2192 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n     5\u2192 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n     6\u2192 * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n     7\u2192 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     8\u2192 *\n     9\u2192 *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n    10\u2192 *\n    11\u2192 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    12\u2192 * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    13\u2192 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    14\u2192 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    15\u2192 * limitations under the License.\n    16\u2192 */\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192package kafka.server\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192import com.yammer.metrics.core.Meter\n    21\u2192import kafka.network.RequestChannel\n    22\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.memory.MemoryPool\n    23\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.network.{ClientInformation, ListenerName}\n    24\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.protocol.ApiKeys\n    25\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.{RequestContext, RequestHeader}\n    26\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.{KafkaPrincipal, SecurityProtocol}\n    27\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.{BufferSupplier, MockTime, Time}\n    28\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.log.remote.storage.RemoteStorageMetrics\n    29\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.metrics.KafkaYammerMetrics\n    30\u2192import org.apache.kafka.storage.log.metrics.BrokerTopicMetrics\n    31\u2192import org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.{assertEquals, assertFalse, assertTrue}\n    32\u2192import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test\n    33\u2192import org.junit.jupiter.params.ParameterizedTest\n    34\u2192import org.junit.jupiter.params.provider.ValueSource\n    35\u2192import org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers\n    36\u2192import org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.any\n    37\u2192import org.mockito.Mockito.{mock, times, verify, when}\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192import java.net.InetAddress\n    40\u2192import java.nio.ByteBuffer\n    41\u2192import java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture\n    42\u2192import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicInteger\n    43\u2192import java.util.stream.Collectors\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192class KafkaRequestHandlerTest {\n    46\u2192  val brokerTopicStats = new BrokerTopicStats(true)\n    47\u2192  val topic = \"topic\"\n    48\u2192  val topic2 = \"topic2\"\n    49\u2192  val brokerTopicMetrics: BrokerTopicMetrics = brokerTopicStats.topicStats(topic)\n    50\u2192  val allTopicMetrics: BrokerTopicMetrics = brokerTopicStats.allTopicsStats\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192  @Test\n    53\u2192  def testCallbackTiming(): Unit = {\n    54\u2192    val time = new MockTime()\n    55\u2192    val startTime = time.nanoseconds()\n    56\u2192    val metrics = new RequestChannel.Metrics(None)\n    57\u2192    val requestChannel = new RequestChannel(10, \"\", time, metrics)\n    58\u2192    val apiHandler = mock(classOf[ApiRequestHandler])\n    59\u2192    try {\n    60\u2192      val handler = new KafkaRequestHandler(0, 0, mock(classOf[Meter]), new AtomicInteger(1), requestChannel, apiHandler, time)\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192      val request = makeRequest(time, metrics)\n    63\u2192      requestChannel.sendRequest(request)\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192      when(apiHandler.handle(ArgumentMatchers.eq(request), any())).thenAnswer { _ =>\n    66\u2192        time.sleep(2)\n    67\u2192        // Prepare the callback.\n    68\u2192        val callback = KafkaRequestHandler.wrapAsyncCallback(\n    69\u2192          (_: RequestLocal, ms: Int) => {\n    70\u2192            time.sleep(ms)\n    71\u2192            handler.stop()\n    72\u2192          },\n    73\u2192          RequestLocal.NoCaching)\n    74\u2192        // Execute the callback asynchronously.\n    75\u2192        CompletableFuture.runAsync(() => callback(1))\n    76\u2192        request.apiLocalCompleteTimeNanos = time.nanoseconds\n    77\u2192      }\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192      handler.run()\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192      assertEquals(startTime, request.requestDequeueTimeNanos)\n    82\u2192      assertEquals(startTime + 2000000, request.apiLocalCompleteTimeNanos)\n    83\u2192      assertEquals(Some(startTime + 2000000), request.callbackRequestDequeueTimeNanos)\n    84\u2192      assertEquals(Some(startTime + 3000000), request.callbackRequestCompleteTimeNanos)\n    85\u2192    } finally {\n    86\u2192      metrics.close()\n    87\u2192    }\n    88\u2192  }\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192  @Test\n    91\u2192  def testCallbackTryCompleteActions(): Unit = {\n    92\u2192    val time = new MockTime()\n    93\u2192    val metrics = mock(classOf[RequestChannel.Metrics])\n    94\u2192    val apiHandler = mock(classOf[ApiRequestHandler])\n    95\u2192    val requestChannel = new RequestChannel(10, \"\", time, metrics)\n    96\u2192    val handler = new KafkaRequestHandler(0, 0, mock(classOf[Meter]), new AtomicInteger(1), requestChannel, apiHandler, time)\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192    var handledCount = 0\n    99\u2192    var tryCompleteActionCount = 0\n   100\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
147. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/trogdor/README.md"
}
Output
"     1\u2192Trogdor\n     2\u2192========================================\n     3\u2192Trogdor is a test framework for Apache Kafka.\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192Trogdor can run benchmarks and other workloads.  Trogdor can also inject faults in order to stress test the system.\n     6\u2192\n     7\u2192Quickstart\n     8\u2192=========================================================\n     9\u2192First, we want to start a single-node Kafka cluster with a ZooKeeper and a broker.\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192Running ZooKeeper:\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192```\n    14\u2192 ./bin/zookeeper-server-start.sh ./config/zookeeper.properties &> /tmp/zookeeper.log &\n    15\u2192```\n    16\u2192Running Kafka:\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192```\n    19\u2192./bin/kafka-server-start.sh ./config/server.properties &> /tmp/kafka.log &\n    20\u2192```\n    21\u2192Then, we want to run a Trogdor Agent, plus a Trogdor Coordinator.\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192To run the Trogdor Agent:\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192```\n    26\u2192./bin/trogdor.sh agent -c ./config/trogdor.conf -n node0 &> /tmp/trogdor-agent.log &\n    27\u2192```\n    28\u2192To run the Trogdor Coordinator:\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192```\n    31\u2192./bin/trogdor.sh coordinator -c ./config/trogdor.conf -n node0 &> /tmp/trogdor-coordinator.log &\n    32\u2192```\n    33\u2192Let's confirm that all of the daemons are running:\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192```\n    36\u2192jps\n    37\u2192116212 Coordinator\n    38\u2192115188 QuorumPeerMain\n    39\u2192116571 Jps\n    40\u2192115420 Kafka\n    41\u2192115694 Agent\n    42\u2192```\n    43\u2192Now, we can submit a test job to Trogdor.\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192```\n    46\u2192./bin/trogdor.sh client createTask -t localhost:8889 -i produce0 --spec ./tests/spec/simple_produce_bench.json\n    47\u2192Sent CreateTaskRequest for task produce0.\n    48\u2192```\n    49\u2192We can run showTask to see what the task's status is:\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192```\n    52\u2192./bin/trogdor.sh client showTask -t localhost:8889 -i produce0\n    53\u2192Task bar of type org.apache.kafka.trogdor.workload.ProduceBenchSpec is DONE. FINISHED at 2019-01-09T20:38:22.039-08:00 after 6s\n    54\u2192```\n    55\u2192To see the results, we use showTask with --show-status:\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192```\n    58\u2192./bin/trogdor.sh client showTask -t localhost:8889 -i produce0 --show-status\n    59\u2192Task bar of type org.apache.kafka.trogdor.workload.ProduceBenchSpec is DONE. FINISHED at 2019-01-09T20:38:22.039-08:00 after 6s\n    60\u2192Status: {\n    61\u2192  \"totalSent\" : 50000,\n    62\u2192  \"averageLatencyMs\" : 17.83388,\n    63\u2192  \"p50LatencyMs\" : 12,\n    64\u2192  \"p95LatencyMs\" : 75,\n    65\u2192  \"p99LatencyMs\" : 96,\n    66\u2192  \"transactionsCommitted\" : 0\n    67\u2192}\n    68\u2192```\n    69\u2192Trogdor Architecture\n    70\u2192========================================\n    71\u2192Trogdor has a single coordinator process which manages multiple agent processes.  Each agent process is responsible for a single cluster node.\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192The Trogdor coordinator manages tasks.  A task is anything we might want to do on a cluster, such as running a benchmark, injecting a fault, or running a workload.  In order to implement each task, the coordinator creates workers on one or more agent nodes.\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192The Trogdor agent process implements the tasks.  For example, when running a workload, the agent process is the process which produces and consumes messages.\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192Both the coordinator and the agent expose a REST interface that accepts objects serialized via JSON.  There is also a command-line program which makes it easy to send messages to either one without manually crafting the JSON message body.\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192All Trogdor RPCs are idempotent except the shutdown requests.  Sending an idempotent RPC twice in a row has the same effect as sending the RPC once.\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192Tasks\n    82\u2192========================================\n    83\u2192Tasks are described by specifications containing:\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192* A \"class\" field describing the task type.  This contains a full Java class name.\n    86\u2192* A \"startMs\" field describing when the task should start.  This is given in terms of milliseconds since the UNIX epoch.\n    87\u2192* A \"durationMs\" field describing how long the task should last.  This is given in terms of milliseconds.\n    88\u2192* Other fields which are task-specific.\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192The task specification is usually written as JSON.  For example, this task specification describes a network partition between nodes 1 and 2, and 3:\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192    {\n    93\u2192        \"class\": \"org.apache.kafka.trogdor.fault.NetworkPartitionFaultSpec\",\n    94\u2192        \"startMs\": 1000,\n    95\u2192        \"durationMs\": 30000,\n    96\u2192        \"partitions\": [[\"node1\", \"node2\"], [\"node3\"]]\n    97\u2192    }\n    98\u2192    \n    99\u2192This task runs a simple ProduceBench test on a cluster with one producer node, 5 topics, and 10,000 messages per second. \n   100\u2192The keys are generated sequentially and the configured partitioner (DefaultPartitioner) is used. \n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192    {\n   103\u2192        \"class\": \"org.apache.kafka.trogdor.workload.ProduceBenchSpec\",\n   104\u2192        \"durationMs\": 10000000,\n   105\u2192        \"producerNode\": \"node0\",\n   106\u2192        \"bootstrapServers\": \"localhost:9092\",\n   107\u2192        \"targetMessagesPerSec\": 10000,\n   108\u2192        \"maxMessages\": 50000,\n   109\u2192        \"activeTopics\": {\n   110\u2192            \"foo[1-3]\": {\n   111\u2192                \"numPartitions\": 10,\n   112\u2192                \"replicationFactor\": 1\n   113\u2192            }\n   114\u2192        },\n   115\u2192        \"inactiveTopics\": {\n   116\u2192             \"foo[4-5]\": {\n   117\u2192                 \"numPartitions\": 10,\n   118\u2192                 \"replicationFactor\": 1\n   119\u2192             }\n   120\u2192        },\n   121\u2192        \"keyGenerator\": {\n   122\u2192             \"type\": \"sequential\",\n   123\u2192             \"size\": 8,\n   124\u2192             \"offset\": 1\n   125\u2192        },\n   126\u2192        \"useConfiguredPartitioner\": true\n   127\u2192     }\n   128\u2192\n   129\u2192Tasks are submitted to the coordinator.  Once the coordinator determines that it is time for the task to start, it creates workers on agent processes.  The workers run until the task is done.\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192Task specifications are immutable; they do not change after the task has been created.\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192Tasks can be in several states:\n   134\u2192* PENDING, when task is waiting to execute,\n   135\u2192* RUNNING, when the task is running,\n   136\u2192* STOPPING, when the task is in the process of stopping,\n   137\u2192* DONE, when the task is done.\n   138\u2192\n   139\u2192Tasks that are DONE also have an error field which will be set if the task failed.\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192Workloads\n   142\u2192========================================\n   143\u2192Trogdor can run several workloads.  Workloads perform operations on the cluster and measure their performance.  Workloads fail when the operations cannot be performed.\n   144\u2192\n   145\u2192### ProduceBench\n   146\u2192ProduceBench starts a Kafka producer on a single agent node, producing to several partitions.  The workload measures the average produce latency, as well as the median, 95th percentile, and 99th percentile latency.\n   147\u2192It can be configured to use a transactional producer which can commit transactions based on a set time interval or number of messages.\n   148\u2192\n   149\u2192### RoundTripWorkload\n   150\u2192RoundTripWorkload tests both production and consumption.  The workload starts a Kafka producer and consumer on a single node.  The consumer will read back the messages that were produced by the producer.\n   151\u2192\n   152\u2192### ConsumeBench\n   153\u2192ConsumeBench starts one or more Kafka consumers on a single agent node. Depending on the passed in configuration (see ConsumeBenchSpec), the consumers either subscribe to a set of topics (leveraging consumer group functionality and dynamic partition assignment) or manually assign partitions to themselves.\n   154\u2192The workload measures the average consume latency, as well as the median, 95th percentile, and 99th percentile latency.\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192Faults\n   157\u2192========================================\n   158\u2192Trogdor can run several faults which deliberately break something in the cluster.\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192### ProcessStopFault\n   161\u2192ProcessStopFault stops a process by sending it a SIGSTOP signal.  When the fault ends, the process is resumed with SIGCONT.\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192### NetworkPartitionFault\n   164\u2192NetworkPartitionFault sets up an artificial network partition between one or more sets of nodes.  Currently, this is implemented using iptables.  The iptables rules are set up on the outbound traffic from the affected nodes.  Therefore, the affected nodes should still be reachable from outside the cluster.\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192External Processes\n   167\u2192========================================\n   168\u2192Trogdor supports running arbitrary commands in external processes. This is a generic way to run any configurable command in the Trogdor framework - be it a Python program, bash script, docker image, etc.\n   169\u2192\n   170\u2192### ExternalCommandWorker\n   171\u2192ExternalCommandWorker starts an external command defined by the ExternalCommandSpec. It essentially allows you to run any command on any Trogdor agent node.\n   172\u2192The worker communicates with the external process via its stdin, stdout and stderr in a JSON protocol. It uses stdout for any actionable communication and only logs what it sees in stderr.\n   173\u2192On startup the worker will first send a message describing the workload to the external process in this format:\n   174\u2192```\n   175\u2192{\"id\":<task ID string>, \"workload\":<configured workload JSON object>}\n   176\u2192```\n   177\u2192and will then listen for messages from the external process, again in a JSON format.\n   178\u2192Said JSON can contain the following fields:\n   179\u2192- status: If the object contains this field, the status of the worker will be set to the given value.\n   180\u2192- error: If the object contains this field, the error of the worker will be set to the given value. Once an error occurs, the external process will be terminated.\n   181\u2192- log: If the object contains this field, a log message will be issued with this text.\n   182\u2192An example:\n   183\u2192```json\n   184\u2192{\"log\": \"Finished successfully.\", \"status\": {\"p99ProduceLatency\": \"100ms\", \"messagesSent\": 10000}}\n   185\u2192```\n   186\u2192\n   187\u2192Exec Mode\n   188\u2192========================================\n   189\u2192Sometimes, you just want to run a test quickly on a single node.  In this case, you can use \"exec mode.\"  This mode allows you to run a single Trogdor Agent without a Coordinator.\n   190\u2192\n   191\u2192When using exec mode, you must pass in a Task specification to use.  The Agent will try to start this task.\n   192\u2192\n   193\u2192For example:\n   194\u2192\n   195\u2192```\n   196\u2192./bin/trogdor.sh agent -n node0 -c ./config/trogdor.conf --exec ./tests/spec/simple_produce_bench.json\n   197\u2192```\n   198\u2192When using exec mode, the Agent will exit once the task is complete.\n   199\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
148. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "find /workspace/clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients -name \"*Test.java\" | wc -l",
  "description": "Count Java test files"
}
Output
"135"
149. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "find /workspace/core/src/test/scala -name \"*Test.scala\" | wc -l",

151. Task @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Investigate Kafka configuration system and management",
  "prompt": "Explore the Apache Kafka configuration system to understand how broker configs are defined, validated, and managed:\n\n1. **Configuration Registry:**\n   - Find KafkaConfig class in /workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala\n   - Look for ConfigDef or similar configuration definition classes\n   - Find where all broker configurations are registered\n   - Look for config validation logic\n\n2. **Configuration Definition Pattern:**\n   - Look for ConfigDef class (likely in clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/)\n   - Find how config types are defined (INT, STRING, BOOLEAN, etc.)\n   - Look for validation methods and config validators\n   - Find how defaults are specified\n\n3. **Dynamic Config Updates:**\n   - Search for DynamicConfigManager or similar\n   - Look for which configs support dynamic updates (without broker restart)\n   - Find the mechanism for applying dynamic changes\n   - Look for ConfigResourceType definitions\n\n4. **Config Validation:**\n   - Find ValidatorImpl or Validator classes\n   - Look for config value validation patterns\n   - Find error handling for invalid configs\n   - Look for range validators, list validators, etc.\n\n5. **Configuration Files and Loading:**\n   - Look at server.properties loading\n   - Find PropertiesUtils or similar loading utilities\n   - Look for how overrides are handled\n   - Find how defaults are applied\n\nKey files to explore:\n- /workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala\n- /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/ConfigDef.java\n- /workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/*Config.scala\n- Files with \"Config\" in the name in /workspace/core/src/main",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "a84caeffcecebd13f",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive report with all the findings:\n\n## Apache Kafka Configuration System - Comprehensive Exploration Report\n\nI've completed a thorough exploration of the Apache Kafka configuration system. Here's a detailed breakdown of how broker configs are defined, validated, and managed:\n\n---\n\n## 1. CONFIGURATION REGISTRY\n\n### Primary Location\n- **File**: `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala`\n- **Key Class**: `KafkaConfig` extends `AbstractKafkaConfig`\n\n### Configuration Definition\nThe entire broker configuration is centrally defined through `AbstractKafkaConfig.CONFIG_DEF`, which merges multiple ConfigDef objects from specialized configuration classes:\n\n```\nAbstractKafkaConfig.CONFIG_DEF = merged configuration of:\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 RemoteLogManagerConfig.configDef()\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 ZkConfigs.CONFIG_DEF\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 ServerConfigs.CONFIG_DEF\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 KRaftConfigs.CONFIG_DEF\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 SocketServerConfigs.CONFIG_DEF\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 ReplicationConfigs.CONFIG_DEF\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 GroupCoordinatorConfig configs\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 CleanerConfig.CONFIG_DEF\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 LogConfig.SERVER_CONFIG_DEF\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 TransactionLogConfigs.CONFIG_DEF\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 QuorumConfig.CONFIG_DEF\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 MetricConfigs.CONFIG_DEF\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 QuotaConfigs.CONFIG_DEF\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 BrokerSecurityConfigs.CONFIG_DEF\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 DelegationTokenManagerConfigs.CONFIG_DEF\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 PasswordEncoderConfigs.CONFIG_DEF\n```\n\n**File**: `/workspace/server/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/config/AbstractKafkaConfig.java`\n\n### Configuration Access\n```scala\nobject KafkaConfig {\n  val configDef = AbstractKafkaConfig.CONFIG_DEF  // All broker configs\n  def configNames: Seq[String] = configDef.names.asScala.toBuffer.sorted\n  def defaultValues: Map[String, _] = configDef.defaultValues.asScala\n  def configKeys: Map[String, ConfigKey] = configDef.configKeys.asScala\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 2. CONFIGURATION DEFINITION PATTERN\n\n### ConfigDef Class\n**File**: `/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/ConfigDef.java`\n\nThe `ConfigDef` class is the core framework for defining configurations with rich metadata:\n\n```java\npublic class ConfigDef {\n    private final Map<String, ConfigKey> configKeys;\n    \n    public ConfigDef define(String name, Type type, Object defaultValue, \n                           Validator validator, Importance importance, \n                           String documentation, String group, int orderInGroup,\n                           Width width, String displayName, List<String> dependents,\n                           Recommender recommender)\n}\n```\n\n### Configuration Types (Enum Type)\n- **BOOLEAN**: Boolean values (true/false)\n- **STRING**: String values\n- **INT**: 32-bit integer values\n- **SHORT**: 16-bit integer values\n- **LONG**: 64-bit integer values\n- **DOUBLE**: Double precision floating-point values\n- **LIST**: Comma-separated list of strings\n- **CLASS**: Java class names (loaded dynamically)\n- **PASSWORD**: Sensitive string data (hidden in logs)\n\n### Example Definition (ServerConfigs)\n**File**: `/workspace/server/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/config/ServerConfigs.java`\n\n```java\npublic static final String BROKER_ID_CONFIG = \"broker.id\";\npublic static final int BROKER_ID_DEFAULT = -1;\npublic static final String BROKER_ID_DOC = \"The broker id for this server...\";\n\npublic static final ConfigDef CONFIG_DEF = new ConfigDef()\n    .define(BROKER_ID_CONFIG, INT, BROKER_ID_DEFAULT, HIGH, BROKER_ID_DOC)\n    .define(MESSAGE_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG, INT, LogConfig.DEFAULT_MAX_MESSAGE_BYTES, \n            atLeast(0), HIGH, MESSAGE_MAX_BYTES_DOC)\n    .define(COMPRESSION_TYPE_CONFIG, STRING, LogConfig.DEFAULT_COMPRESSION_TYPE,\n            ConfigDef.ValidString.in(BrokerCompressionType.names().toArray(new String[0])), \n            HIGH, COMPRESSION_TYPE_DOC);\n```\n\n---\n\n## 3. VALIDATION FRAMEWORK\n\n### Validator Interface\n```java\npublic interface Validator {\n    void ensureValid(String name, Object value) throws ConfigException;\n}\n```\n\n### Built-in Validators\n\n#### Range Validator\n```java\npublic static class Range implements Validator {\n    public static Range atLeast(Number min)      // min <= value\n    public static Range between(Number min, Number max)  // min <= value <= max\n    \n    public void ensureValid(String name, Object o) {\n        Number n = (Number) o;\n        if (min != null && n.doubleValue() < min.doubleValue())\n            throw new ConfigException(name, o, \"Value must be at least \" + min);\n        if (max != null && n.doubleValue() > max.doubleValue())\n            throw new ConfigException(name, o, \"Value must be no more than \" + max);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n#### String Validators\n```java\npublic static class ValidString implements Validator\npublic static class CaseInsensitiveValidString implements Validator  \npublic static class ValidList implements Validator\npublic static class NonEmptyString implements Validator\npublic static class NonEmptyStringWithoutControlChars implements Validator\npublic static class NonNullValidator implements Validator\n```\n\n#### List Size Validator\n```java\npublic static class ListSize implements Validator {\n    public static ListSize atMostOfSize(final int maxSize)\n}\n```\n\n#### Composite & Lambda Validators\n```java\npublic static class CompositeValidator implements Validator {\n    public static CompositeValidator of(Validator... validators)\n}\n\npublic static class LambdaValidator implements Validator {\n    public static LambdaValidator with(BiConsumer<String, Object> ensureValid,\n                                      Supplier<String> toStringFunction)\n}\n```\n\n### Validation Process\n**File**: `/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/AbstractConfig.java`\n\n```java\npublic AbstractConfig(ConfigDef definition, Map<?, ?> originals, ...) {\n    Map<String, Object> originalMap = Utils.castToStringObjectMap(originals);\n    this.originals = resolveConfigVariables(configProviderProps, originalMap);\n    this.values = definition.parse(this.originals);  // Type parsing + validation\n    Map<String, Object> configUpdates = postProcessParsedConfig(...);\n    this.values.putAll(configUpdates);\n    definition.parse(this.values);  // Re-validate after post-processing\n}\n```\n\nType parsing and validator execution:\n```java\nObject parseValue(ConfigKey key, Object value, boolean isSet) {\n    Object parsedValue;\n    if (isSet) {\n        parsedValue = parseType(key.name, value, key.type);  // Type conversion\n    } else if (NO_DEFAULT_VALUE.equals(key.defaultValue)) {\n        throw new ConfigException(\"Missing required configuration...\");\n    } else {\n        parsedValue = key.defaultValue;\n    }\n    if (key.validator != null) {\n        key.validator.ensureValid(key.name, parsedValue);  // Validation\n    }\n    return parsedValue;\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 4. DYNAMIC CONFIG UPDATES\n\n### Dynamic Config Management\n**File**: `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/DynamicBrokerConfig.scala`\n\nDynamic configurations are stored at multiple levels with precedence order:\n\n```scala\n/**\n * Order of precedence for broker configs:\n * 1. DYNAMIC_BROKER_CONFIG: /configs/brokers/{brokerId} (ZK)\n * 2. DYNAMIC_DEFAULT_BROKER_CONFIG: /configs/brokers/<default> (ZK)\n * 3. STATIC_BROKER_CONFIG: server.properties (static)\n * 4. DEFAULT_CONFIG: KafkaConfig defaults\n */\n```\n\n### Reconfigurable Components\n**File**: `/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/Reconfigurable.java`\n\nTwo types of reconfigurable components:\n\n```scala\ntrait Reconfigurable {\n    def reconfigurableConfigs(): java.util.Set[String]\n    def validateReconfiguration(configs: java.util.Map[String, _]): Unit\n    def reconfigure(configs: java.util.Map[String, _]): Unit\n}\n\ntrait BrokerReconfigurable {\n    def reconfigurableConfigs: Set[String]\n    def validateReconfiguration(newConfig: KafkaConfig): Unit\n    def reconfigure(oldConfig: KafkaConfig, newConfig: KafkaConfig): Unit\n}\n```\n\n### Dynamic Config Update Handlers\n**File**: `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/DynamicBrokerConfig.scala` (lines 260-295)\n\nRegistered reconfigurable components:\n- `DynamicLogConfig` - Log configuration updates\n- `DynamicListenerConfig` - Listener/SSL configuration updates\n- `DynamicBrokerThreadPool` - Thread pool size updates\n- `SocketServer` - Socket server configuration updates\n- `DynamicClientQuotaCallback` - Quota updates\n- `DynamicMetricsReporters` - Metrics reporter updates\n- `DynamicProducerStateManagerConfig` - Producer state config updates\n- `DynamicRemoteLogConfig` - Remote log manager updates\n\n### Dynamic Config Classes\n\n#### AllDynamicConfigs\n```scala\nobject DynamicBrokerConfig {\n    private[server] val DynamicSecurityConfigs = SslConfigs.RECONFIGURABLE_CONFIGS.asScala\n    \n    val AllDynamicConfigs = DynamicSecurityConfigs ++\n        LogCleaner.ReconfigurableConfigs ++\n        DynamicLogConfig.ReconfigurableConfigs ++\n        DynamicThreadPool.ReconfigurableConfigs ++\n        Set(MetricConfigs.METRIC_REPORTER_CLASSES_CONFIG) ++\n        DynamicListenerConfig.ReconfigurableConfigs ++\n        SocketServer.ReconfigurableConfigs ++\n        DynamicProducerStateManagerConfig ++\n        DynamicRemoteLogConfig.ReconfigurableConfigs\n}\n```\n\n#### DynamicConfig.Broker\n**File**: `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/DynamicConfig.scala` (lines 35-54)\n\n```scala\nobject DynamicConfig {\n    object Broker {\n        private val brokerConfigs = {\n            val configs = QuotaConfigs.brokerQuotaConfigs()\n            \n            // Filter and define all dynamic configurations\n            KafkaConfig.configKeys\n                .filter { case (configName, _) => AllDynamicConfigs.contains(configName) }\n                .foreach { case (_, config) => configs.define(config) }\n            configs\n        }\n        \n        val nonDynamicProps: Set[String] = KafkaConfig.configNames.toSet -- brokerConfigs.names.asScala\n        \n        def configKeys: util.Map[String, ConfigDef.ConfigKey] = brokerConfigs.configKeys\n        def names: util.Set[String] = brokerConfigs.names\n        def validate(props: Properties): util.Map[String, AnyRef] = ...\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Validation of Dynamic Updates\n**File**: `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/DynamicBrokerConfig.scala` (lines 142-155)\n\n```scala\ndef validateConfigs(props: Properties, perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Unit = {\n    def checkInvalidProps(invalidPropNames: Set[String], errorMessage: String): Unit = {\n        if (invalidPropNames.nonEmpty)\n            throw new ConfigException(s\"$errorMessage: $invalidPropNames\")\n    }\n    \n    checkInvalidProps(nonDynamicConfigs(props), \"Cannot update these configs dynamically\")\n    checkInvalidProps(securityConfigsWithoutListenerPrefix(props),\n        \"These security configs can be dynamically updated only per-listener using the listener prefix\")\n    validateConfigTypes(props)\n    \n    if (!perBrokerConfig) {\n        checkInvalidProps(perBrokerConfigs(props),\n            \"Cannot update these configs at default cluster level, broker id must be specified\")\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Dynamic Update Process\n**File**: `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/DynamicBrokerConfig.scala` (lines 573-608)\n\n```scala\nprivate def processReconfiguration(newProps: Map[String, String], validateOnly: Boolean, \n                                  doLog: Boolean = false): (KafkaConfig, List[BrokerReconfigurable]) = {\n    val newConfig = new KafkaConfig(newProps.asJava, doLog)\n    val (changeMap, deletedKeySet) = updatedConfigs(newConfig.originalsFromThisConfig, currentConfig.originals)\n    \n    if (changeMap.nonEmpty || deletedKeySet.nonEmpty) {\n        try {\n            val customConfigs = new util.HashMap[String, Object](newConfig.originalsFromThisConfig)\n            newConfig.valuesFromThisConfig.keySet.forEach(k => customConfigs.remove(k))\n            \n            // Validate and reconfigure Reconfigurable components\n            reconfigurables.forEach { reconfigurable =>\n                if (needsReconfiguration(reconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs, changeMap.keySet, deletedKeySet))\n                    processReconfigurable(reconfigurable, changeMap.keySet, \n                                        newConfig.valuesFromThisConfig, customConfigs, validateOnly)\n            }\n            \n            // Validate BrokerReconfigurable components (reconfigure after config update)\n            val brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate = mutable.Buffer[BrokerReconfigurable]()\n            brokerReconfigurables.forEach { reconfigurable =>\n                if (needsReconfiguration(reconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs.asJava, \n                                        changeMap.keySet, deletedKeySet)) {\n                    reconfigurable.validateReconfiguration(newConfig)\n                    if (!validateOnly)\n                        brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate += reconfigurable\n                }\n            }\n            \n            (newConfig, brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate.toList)\n        } catch {\n            case e: Exception =>\n                if (!validateOnly)\n                    error(s\"Failed to update broker configuration with configs : \" +\n                          s\"${ConfigUtils.configMapToRedactedString(newConfig.originalsFromThisConfig, KafkaConfig.configDef)}\", e)\n                throw new ConfigException(\"Invalid dynamic configuration\", e)\n        }\n    } else {\n        (currentConfig, List.empty)\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Config Synonyms and Precedence\n**File**: `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/DynamicBrokerConfig.scala` (lines 122-140)\n\nSome configurations have multiple names (synonyms):\n```scala\ndef brokerConfigSynonyms(name: String, matchListenerOverride: Boolean): List[String] = {\n    name match {\n        case ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG | ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG =>\n            List(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG, ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG)\n        case ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_JITTER_MILLIS_CONFIG | ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_JITTER_HOURS_CONFIG =>\n            List(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_JITTER_MILLIS_CONFIG, ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_JITTER_HOURS_CONFIG)\n        case ServerLogConfigs.LOG_FLUSH_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG =>\n            List(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_FLUSH_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG, ServerLogConfigs.LOG_FLUSH_SCHEDULER_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG)\n        case ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG | ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MINUTES_CONFIG | ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG =>\n            List(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG, ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MINUTES_CONFIG, ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG)\n        case ListenerConfigRegex(baseName) if matchListenerOverride =>\n            val mechanismConfig = ListenerMechanismConfigs.find(baseName.endsWith)\n            List(name, mechanismConfig.getOrElse(baseName))\n        case _ => List(name)\n    }\n}\n```\n\nListener-specific configs override base configs:\n- Pattern: `listener.name.{listenerName}.{configName}`\n- Example: `listener.name.PLAINTEXT.ssl.keystore.location`\n\n---\n\n## 5. CONFIGURATION FILES AND LOADING\n\n### Server Properties File\n**File**: `/workspace/config/server.properties`\n\nExample broker configuration structure:\n```properties\n# General\nbroker.id=0\n\n# Socket Server Settings\nlisteners=PLAINTEXT://:9092\nadvertised.listeners=PLAINTEXT://your.host.name:9092\nnum.network.threads=3\nnum.io.threads=8\nsocket.send.buffer.bytes=102400\nsocket.receive.buffer.bytes=102400\nsocket.request.max.bytes=104857600\n\n# Log Configuration\nlog.dirs=/tmp/kafka-logs\nnum.partitions=1\nnum.recovery.threads.per.data.dir=1\n\n# Internal Topics\noffsets.topic.replication.factor=1\ntransaction.state.log.replication.factor=1\n\n# Replication\ndefault.replication.factor=3\n\n# Log Retention\nlog.retention.hours=168\nlog.retention.check.interval.ms=300000\n\n# ZooKeeper\nzookeeper.connect=localhost:2181\nzookeeper.connection.timeout.ms=18000\n\n# Group Coordinator\ngroup.initial.rebalance.delay.ms=0\n```\n\n### KafkaConfig Creation\n**File**: `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala` (lines 97-111)\n\n```scala\ndef fromProps(props: Properties): KafkaConfig = fromProps(props, true)\n\ndef fromProps(props: Properties, doLog: Boolean): KafkaConfig =\n    new KafkaConfig(props, doLog)\n\ndef fromProps(defaults: Properties, overrides: Properties): KafkaConfig =\n    fromProps(defaults, overrides, true)\n\ndef fromProps(defaults: Properties, overrides: Properties, doLog: Boolean): KafkaConfig = {\n    val props = new Properties()\n    props ++= defaults\n    props ++= overrides\n    fromProps(props, doLog)\n}\n```\n\n### Configuration Parsing\n```scala\nclass KafkaConfig private(doLog: Boolean, val props: util.Map[_, _])\n    extends AbstractKafkaConfig(KafkaConfig.configDef, props, \n                               Utils.castToStringObjectMap(props), doLog) \n```\n\nThe `AbstractKafkaConfig` constructor:\n1. Resolves config variables (ConfigProvider)\n2. Parses all configs using `ConfigDef.parse()` (type conversion + validation)\n3. Post-processes parsed config\n4. Re-validates after post-processing\n5. Logs configuration (if `doLog=true`)\n\n### Synonym Handling\n**File**: `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala` (lines 162-172)\n\n```scala\ndef populateSynonyms(input: util.Map[_, _]): util.Map[Any, Any] = {\n    val output = new util.HashMap[Any, Any](input)\n    val brokerId = output.get(ServerConfigs.BROKER_ID_CONFIG)\n    val nodeId = output.get(KRaftConfigs.NODE_ID_CONFIG)\n    \n    if (brokerId == null && nodeId != null) {\n        output.put(ServerConfigs.BROKER_ID_CONFIG, nodeId)\n    } else if (brokerId != null && nodeId == null) {\n        output.put(KRaftConfigs.NODE_ID_CONFIG, brokerId)\n    }\n    output\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 6. CONFIGURATION VALIDATION\n\n### Static Validation\nHappens during broker startup in `KafkaConfig.validateValues()`:\n\n**File**: `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala` (lines 855-1093)\n\nExamples of validations:\n- broker.id must be >= 0\n- log.dirs must not be empty\n- listener configurations must be valid\n- security configurations must be consistent\n- KRaft-specific validations (process.roles, quorum settings)\n- Inter-broker protocol version compatibility\n- Listener name consistency\n\n### Dynamic Validation\nHappens when dynamic configs are updated via AdminClient:\n\n```scala\nprivate def removeInvalidConfigs(props: Properties, perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Unit = {\n    try {\n        validateConfigTypes(props)\n        props.asScala\n    } catch {\n        case e: Exception =>\n            val invalidProps = props.asScala.filter { case (k, v) =>\n                val props1 = new Properties\n                props1.put(k, v)\n                try {\n                    validateConfigTypes(props1)\n                    false\n                } catch {\n                    case _: Exception => true\n                }\n            }\n            invalidProps.keys.foreach(props.remove)\n            error(s\"Dynamic config contains invalid values in: ${invalidProps.keys}, these configs will be ignored\", e)\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Per-Broker vs Cluster-Level Configs\n```scala\nprivate val ClusterLevelListenerConfigs = Set(\n    SocketServerConfigs.MAX_CONNECTIONS_CONFIG, \n    SocketServerConfigs.MAX_CONNECTION_CREATION_RATE_CONFIG, \n    SocketServerConfigs.NUM_NETWORK_THREADS_CONFIG\n)\n\nprivate val PerBrokerConfigs = (DynamicSecurityConfigs ++ \n    DynamicListenerConfig.ReconfigurableConfigs).diff(ClusterLevelListenerConfigs)\n```\n\nPer-broker configs can only be set at broker level (e.g., via `/configs/brokers/{brokerId}`)\nCluster-level configs can be set at default level (e.g., via `/configs/brokers/<default>`)\n\n---\n\n## 7. PASSWORD ENCRYPTION FOR DYNAMIC CONFIGS\n\n**File**: `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/DynamicBrokerConfig.scala` (lines 218-222, 376-405, 407-442, 444-468)\n\nDynamic configuration passwords are encrypted:\n\n```scala\nprivate def toPersistentProps(configProps: Properties, perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Properties = {\n    val props = configProps.clone().asInstanceOf[Properties]\n    \n    def encodePassword(configName: String, value: String): Unit = {\n        if (value != null) {\n            if (!perBrokerConfig)\n                throw new ConfigException(\"Password config can be defined only at broker level\")\n            props.setProperty(configName, passwordEncoder.encode(new Password(value)))\n        }\n    }\n    \n    configProps.asScala.forKeyValue { (name, value) =>\n        if (isPasswordConfig(name))\n            encodePassword(name, value)\n    }\n    props\n}\n\nprivate[server] def fromPersistentProps(persistentProps: Properties,\n                                       perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Properties = {\n    val props = persistentProps.clone().asInstanceOf[Properties]\n    \n    removeInvalidConfigs(props, perBrokerConfig)\n    \n    def decodePassword(configName: String, value: String): Unit = {\n        if (value != null) {\n            try {\n                props.setProperty(configName, passwordEncoder.decode(value).value)\n            } catch {\n                case e: Exception =>\n                    error(s\"Dynamic password config $configName could not be decoded, ignoring.\", e)\n                    props.remove(configName)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n    \n    props.asScala.forKeyValue { (name, value) =>\n        if (isPasswordConfig(name))\n            decodePassword(name, value)\n    }\n    props\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 8. CONFIGURATION METADATA\n\nEach configuration has metadata:\n\n```java\npublic static class ConfigKey {\n    public final String name;              // e.g., \"broker.id\"\n    public final Type type;                // e.g., Type.INT\n    public final String documentation;    // Help text\n    public final Object defaultValue;     // Default value\n    public final Validator validator;     // Validation logic\n    public final Importance importance;   // HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW\n    public final String group;            // Configuration group\n    public final int orderInGroup;        // Order in UI\n    public final Width width;             // UI width (NONE, SHORT, MEDIUM, LONG)\n    public final String displayName;      // Display name for UI\n    public final List<String> dependents; // Configs that depend on this one\n    public final Recommender recommender; // Suggests valid values based on other configs\n    public final boolean internal;        // Internal/hidden from documentation\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\nThe Kafka configuration system is sophisticated and well-structured:\n\n1. **Central Definition**: All configs defined in modular ConfigDef classes\n2. **Type-Safe**: Strong typing with conversion and validation\n3. **Flexible Validation**: Extensible validator framework with built-in validators\n4. **Dynamic Updates**: Supports updating configs without broker restart\n5. **Precedence Management**: Clear order of precedence for multiple config sources\n6. **Security**: Password encryption for sensitive dynamic configs\n7. **Rich Metadata**: Detailed configuration metadata for documentation and UI\n8. **Reconfigurable Components**: Clean interface for components to react to config changes\n9. **Synonyms**: Support for configuration aliases with proper precedence handling\n10. **Comprehensive Validation**: Both static (startup) and dynamic validation with detailed error messages",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Explore the Apache Kafka configuration system to understand how broker configs are defined, validated, and managed:\n\n1. **Configuration Registry:**\n   - Find KafkaConfig class in /workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala\n   - Look for ConfigDef or similar configuration definition classes\n   - Find where all broker configurations are registered\n   - Look for config validation logic\n\n2. **Configuration Definition Pattern:**\n   - Look for ConfigDef class (likely in clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/)\n   - Find how config types are defined (INT, STRING, BOOLEAN, etc.)\n   - Look for validation methods and config validators\n   - Find how defaults are specified\n\n3. **Dynamic Config Updates:**\n   - Search for DynamicConfigManager or similar\n   - Look for which configs support dynamic updates (without broker restart)\n   - Find the mechanism for applying dynamic changes\n   - Look for ConfigResourceType definitions\n\n4. **Config Validation:**\n   - Find ValidatorImpl or Validator classes\n   - Look for config value validation patterns\n   - Find error handling for invalid configs\n   - Look for range validators, list validators, etc.\n\n5. **Configuration Files and Loading:**\n   - Look at server.properties loading\n   - Find PropertiesUtils or similar loading utilities\n   - Look for how overrides are handled\n   - Find how defaults are applied\n\nKey files to explore:\n- /workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala\n- /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/ConfigDef.java\n- /workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/*Config.scala\n- Files with \"Config\" in the name in /workspace/core/src/main",

getInt(ZkConfigs.ZK_SESSION_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n   225\u2192  val zkConnectionTimeoutMs: Int =\n   226\u2192 getBoolean(ZkConfigs.ZK_ENABLE_SECURE_ACLS_CONFIG)\n   228\u2192  val zkMaxInFlightRequests: Int = getInt(ZkConfigs.ZK_MAX_IN_FLIGHT_REQUESTS_CONFIG)\n   229\u2192\n   230\u2192  private val String): Boolean = {\n   241\u2192    // Use the system property if it exists and the Kafka config value was defaulted rather than actually provided\n   242\u2192    // Need to translate any system property value from true/false (String) to true/false (Boolean)\n   243\u2192    val actuallyProvided = originals.containsKey(propKey)\n   244\u2192    if (actuallyProvided) getBoolean(propKey) else }\n   253\u2192\n   254\u2192  private def zkStringConfigOrSystemPropertyWithDefaultValue(propKey: String): String = {\n   255\u2192    // Use the system property if it exists and the Kafka config value was defaulted rather than actually provided\n   256\u2192    val actuallyProvided = originals.containsKey(propKey)\n   257\u2192    if (actuallyProvided) getString(propKey) else {\n   258\u2192      KafkaConfig.zooKeeperClientProperty(zkClientConfigViaSystemProperties, propKey) match {\n   259\u2192        case Some(v) => v\n   260\u2192        case _ => getString(propKey) // not specified so use the default value\n   261\u2192      }\n   262\u2192    }\n   263\u2192  }\n   264\u2192\n   265\u2192  private def zkOptionalStringConfigOrSystemProperty(propKey: String): Option[String] = {\n   266\u2192    Option(getString(propKey)).orElse {\n   267\u2192      KafkaConfig.zooKeeperClientProperty(zkClientConfigViaSystemProperties, propKey)\n   268\u2192    }\n   269\u2192  }\n   270\u2192  private def zkPasswordConfigOrSystemProperty(propKey: String): Option[Password] = {\n   271\u2192    Option(getPassword(propKey)).orElse {\n   272\u2192      KafkaConfig.zooKeeperClientProperty(zkClientConfigViaSystemProperties, propKey).map(new Password(_))\n   273\u2192    }\n   274\u2192  }\n   275\u2192  private def zkListConfigOrSystemProperty(propKey: String): Option[util.List[String]] = {\n   276\u2192    Option(getList(propKey)).orElse {\n   277\u2192      KafkaConfig.zooKeeperClientProperty(zkClientConfigViaSystemProperties, propKey).map { sysProp =>\n   278\u2192        sysProp.split(\"\\\\s*,\\\\s*\").toBuffer.asJava\n   279\u2192      }\n   280\u2192    }\n   281\u2192  }\n   282\u2192\n   283\u2192  val zkSslClientEnable = zkBooleanConfigOrSystemPropertyWithDefaultValue(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_CLIENT_ENABLE_CONFIG)\n   284\u2192  val zkClientCnxnSocketClassName = zkOptionalStringConfigOrSystemProperty(ZkConfigs.ZK_CLIENT_CNXN_SOCKET_CONFIG)\n   285\u2192  val zkSslKeyStoreLocation = zkOptionalStringConfigOrSystemProperty(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_KEY_STORE_LOCATION_CONFIG)\n   286\u2192  val zkSslKeyStorePassword = zkPasswordConfigOrSystemProperty(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_KEY_STORE_PASSWORD_CONFIG)\n   287\u2192  val zkSslKeyStoreType = zkOptionalStringConfigOrSystemProperty(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_KEY_STORE_TYPE_CONFIG)\n   288\u2192  val zkSslTrustStoreLocation = zkOptionalStringConfigOrSystemProperty(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_TRUST_STORE_LOCATION_CONFIG)\n   289\u2192  val zkSslTrustStorePassword = zkPasswordConfigOrSystemProperty(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_TRUST_STORE_PASSWORD_CONFIG)\n   290\u2192  val zkSslTrustStoreType = zkOptionalStringConfigOrSystemProperty(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_TRUST_STORE_TYPE_CONFIG)\n   291\u2192  val ZkSslProtocol = zkStringConfigOrSystemPropertyWithDefaultValue(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_PROTOCOL_CONFIG)\n   292\u2192  val ZkSslEnabledProtocols = zkListConfigOrSystemProperty(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_ENABLED_PROTOCOLS_CONFIG)\n   293\u2192  val ZkSslCipherSuites = zkListConfigOrSystemProperty(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_CIPHER_SUITES_CONFIG)\n   294\u2192  val ZkSslEndpointIdentificationAlgorithm = {\n   295\u2192    // Use the system property if it exists and the Kafka config value was defaulted rather than actually provided\n   296\u2192    // Need to translate any system property value from true/false to HTTPS/<blank>\n   297\u2192    val kafkaProp = ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_ENDPOINT_IDENTIFICATION_ALGORITHM_CONFIG\n   298\u2192    val actuallyProvided = originals.containsKey(kafkaProp)\n   299\u2192    if (actuallyProvided)\n   300\u2192      getString(kafkaProp)\n   301\u2192    else {\n   302\u2192      KafkaConfig.zooKeeperClientProperty(zkClientConfigViaSystemProperties, kafkaProp) match {\n   303\u2192        case Some(\"true\") => \"HTTPS\"\n   304\u2192        case Some(_) => \"\"\n   305\u2192        case None => getString(kafkaProp) // not specified so use the default value\n   306\u2192      }\n   307\u2192    }\n   308\u2192  }\n   309\u2192  val ZkSslCrlEnable = zkBooleanConfigOrSystemPropertyWithDefaultValue(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_CRL_ENABLE_CONFIG)\n   310\u2192  val ZkSslOcspEnable = zkBooleanConfigOrSystemPropertyWithDefaultValue(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_OCSP_ENABLE_CONFIG)\n   311\u2192  /** ********* General Configuration ***********/\n   312\u2192  val brokerIdGenerationEnable: Boolean = getBoolean(ServerConfigs.BROKER_ID_GENERATION_ENABLE_CONFIG)\n   313\u2192  val maxReservedBrokerId: Int = getInt(ServerConfigs.RESERVED_BROKER_MAX_ID_CONFIG)\n   314\u2192  private[server] var _brokerId: Int = getInt(ServerConfigs.BROKER_ID_CONFIG)\n   315\u2192  def brokerId: Int = _brokerId\n   316\u2192  private[server] var _nodeId: Int = getInt(KRaftConfigs.NODE_ID_CONFIG)\n   317\u2192  def nodeId: Int = _nodeId\n   318\u2192  val initialRegistrationTimeoutMs: Int = getInt(KRaftConfigs.INITIAL_BROKER_REGISTRATION_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n   319\u2192  val brokerHeartbeatIntervalMs: Int = getInt(KRaftConfigs.BROKER_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG)\n   320\u2192  val brokerSessionTimeoutMs: Int = getInt(KRaftConfigs.BROKER_SESSION_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n   321\u2192\n   322\u2192  def requiresZookeeper: Boolean = processRoles.isEmpty\n   323\u2192  def usesSelfManagedQuorum: Boolean = processRoles.nonEmpty\n   324\u2192\n   325\u2192  val migrationEnabled: Boolean = getBoolean(KRaftConfigs.MIGRATION_ENABLED_CONFIG)\n   326\u2192  val migrationMetadataMinBatchSize: Int = getInt(KRaftConfigs.MIGRATION_METADATA_MIN_BATCH_SIZE_CONFIG)\n   327\u2192\n   328\u2192  val elrEnabled: Boolean = getBoolean(KRaftConfigs.ELR_ENABLED_CONFIG)\n   329\u2192\n   330\u2192  private def parseProcessRoles(): Set[ProcessRole] = {\n   331\u2192    val roles = getList(KRaftConfigs.PROCESS_ROLES_CONFIG).asScala.map {\n   332\u2192      case \"broker\" => ProcessRole.BrokerRole\n   333\u2192      case \"controller\" => ProcessRole.ControllerRole\n   334\u2192      case role => throw new ConfigException(s\"Unknown process role '$role'\" +\n   335\u2192        \" (only 'broker' and 'controller' are allowed roles)\")\n   336\u2192    }\n   337\u2192\n   338\u2192    val distinctRoles: Set[ProcessRole] = roles.toSet\n   339\u2192\n   340\u2192    if (distinctRoles.size != roles.size) {\n   341\u2192      throw new ConfigException(s\"Duplicate role names found in `${KRaftConfigs.PROCESS_ROLES_CONFIG}`: $roles\")\n   342\u2192    }\n   343\u2192\n   344\u2192    distinctRoles\n   345\u2192  }\n   346\u2192\n   347\u2192  def isKRaftCombinedMode: Boolean = {\n   348\u2192    processRoles == Set(ProcessRole.BrokerRole, ProcessRole.ControllerRole)\n   349\u2192  }\n   350\u2192\n   351\u2192  def metadataLogDir: String = {\n   352\u2192    Option(getString(KRaftConfigs.METADATA_LOG_DIR_CONFIG)) match {\n   353\u2192      case Some(dir) => dir\n   354\u2192      case None => logDirs.head\n   355\u2192    }\n   356\u2192  }\n   357\u2192\n   358\u2192  def metadataLogSegmentBytes = getInt(KRaftConfigs.METADATA_LOG_SEGMENT_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   359\u2192  def metadataLogSegmentMillis = getLong(KRaftConfigs.METADATA_LOG_SEGMENT_MILLIS_CONFIG)\n   360\u2192  def metadataRetentionBytes = getLong(KRaftConfigs.METADATA_MAX_RETENTION_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   361\u2192  def metadataRetentionMillis = getLong(KRaftConfigs.METADATA_MAX_RETENTION_MILLIS_CONFIG)\n   362\u2192  def metadataNodeIDConfig = getInt(KRaftConfigs.NODE_ID_CONFIG)\n   363\u2192  def metadataLogSegmentMinBytes = getInt(KRaftConfigs.METADATA_LOG_SEGMENT_MIN_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   364\u2192  val serverMaxStartupTimeMs = getLong(KRaftConfigs.SERVER_MAX_STARTUP_TIME_MS_CONFIG)\n   365\u2192\n   366\u2192  def backgroundThreads = getInt(ServerConfigs.BACKGROUND_THREADS_CONFIG)\n   367\u2192  def numIoThreads = getInt(ServerConfigs.NUM_IO_THREADS_CONFIG)\n   368\u2192  def messageMaxBytes = getInt(ServerConfigs.MESSAGE_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   369\u2192  val requestTimeoutMs = getInt(ServerConfigs.REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n   370\u2192  val connectionSetupTimeoutMs = getLong(ServerConfigs.SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n   371\u2192  val connectionSetupTimeoutMaxMs = getLong(ServerConfigs.SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MAX_MS_CONFIG)\n   372\u2192\n   373\u2192  def getNumReplicaAlterLogDirsThreads: Int = {\n   374\u2192    val numThreads: Integer = Option(getInt(ServerConfigs.NUM_REPLICA_ALTER_LOG_DIRS_THREADS_CONFIG)).getOrElse(logDirs.size)\n   375\u2192    numThreads\n   376\u2192  }\n   377\u2192\n   378\u2192  /************* Metadata Configuration ***********/\n   379\u2192  val metadataSnapshotMaxNewRecordBytes = getLong(KRaftConfigs.METADATA_SNAPSHOT_MAX_NEW_RECORD_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   380\u2192  val metadataSnapshotMaxIntervalMs = getLong(KRaftConfigs.METADATA_SNAPSHOT_MAX_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG)\n   381\u2192  val metadataMaxIdleIntervalNs: Option[Long] = {\n   382\u2192    val value = TimeUnit.NANOSECONDS.convert(getInt(KRaftConfigs.METADATA_MAX_IDLE_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG).toLong, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)\n   383\u2192    if (value > 0) Some(value) else None\n   384\u2192  }\n   385\u2192\n   386\u2192  /************* Authorizer Configuration ***********/\n   387\u2192  def createNewAuthorizer(): Option[Authorizer] = {\n   388\u2192    val className = getString(ServerConfigs.AUTHORIZER_CLASS_NAME_CONFIG)\n   389\u2192    if (className == null || className.isEmpty)\n   390\u2192      None\n   391\u2192    else {\n   392\u2192      Some(AuthorizerUtils.createAuthorizer(className))\n   393\u2192    }\n   394\u2192  }\n   395\u2192\n   396\u2192  val earlyStartListeners: Set[ListenerName] = {\n   397\u2192    val listenersSet = listeners.map(_.listenerName).toSet\n   398\u2192    val controllerListenersSet = controllerListeners.map(_.listenerName).toSet\n   399\u2192    Option(getString(ServerConfigs.EARLY_START_LISTENERS_CONFIG)) match {\n   400\u2192      case None => controllerListenersSet\n   401\u2192      case Some(str) =>\n   402\u2192        str.split(\",\").map(_.trim()).filterNot(_.isEmpty).map { str =>\n   403\u2192          val listenerName = new ListenerName(str)\n   404\u2192          if (!listenersSet.contains(listenerName) && !controllerListenersSet.contains(listenerName))\n   405\u2192            throw new ConfigException(s\"${ServerConfigs.EARLY_START_LISTENERS_CONFIG} contains \" +\n   406\u2192              s\"listener ${listenerName.value()}, but this is not contained in \" +\n   407\u2192              s\"${SocketServerConfigs.LISTENERS_CONFIG} or ${KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG}\")\n   408\u2192          listenerName\n   409\u2192        }.toSet\n   410\u2192    }\n   411\u2192  }\n   412\u2192\n   413\u2192  /** ********* Socket Server Configuration ***********/\n   414\u2192  val socketSendBufferBytes = getInt(SocketServerConfigs.SOCKET_SEND_BUFFER_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   415\u2192  val socketReceiveBufferBytes = getInt(SocketServerConfigs.SOCKET_RECEIVE_BUFFER_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   416\u2192  val socketRequestMaxBytes = getInt(SocketServerConfigs.SOCKET_REQUEST_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   417\u2192  val socketListenBacklogSize = getInt(SocketServerConfigs.SOCKET_LISTEN_BACKLOG_SIZE_CONFIG)\n   418\u2192  val maxConnectionsPerIp = getInt(SocketServerConfigs.MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP_CONFIG)\n   419\u2192  val maxConnectionsPerIpOverrides: Map[String, Int] =\n   420\u2192    getMap(SocketServerConfigs.MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP_OVERRIDES_CONFIG, getString(SocketServerConfigs.MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP_OVERRIDES_CONFIG)).map { case (k, v) => (k, v.toInt)}\n   421\u2192  def maxConnections = getInt(SocketServerConfigs.MAX_CONNECTIONS_CONFIG)\n   422\u2192  def maxConnectionCreationRate = getInt(SocketServerConfigs.MAX_CONNECTION_CREATION_RATE_CONFIG)\n   423\u2192  val connectionsMaxIdleMs = getLong(SocketServerConfigs.CONNECTIONS_MAX_IDLE_MS_CONFIG)\n   424\u2192  val failedAuthenticationDelayMs = getInt(SocketServerConfigs.FAILED_AUTHENTICATION_DELAY_MS_CONFIG)\n   425\u2192  val queuedMaxRequests = getInt(SocketServerConfigs.QUEUED_MAX_REQUESTS_CONFIG)\n   426\u2192  val queuedMaxBytes = getLong(SocketServerConfigs.QUEUED_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   427\u2192  def numNetworkThreads = getInt(SocketServerConfigs.NUM_NETWORK_THREADS_CONFIG)\n   428\u2192\n   429\u2192  /***************** rack configuration **************/\n   430\u2192  val rack = Option(getString(ServerConfigs.BROKER_RACK_CONFIG))\n   431\u2192  val replicaSelectorClassName = Option(getString(ReplicationConfigs.REPLICA_SELECTOR_CLASS_CONFIG))\n   432\u2192\n   433\u2192  /** getInt(ServerLogConfigs.NUM_PARTITIONS_CONFIG)\n   436\u2192  val logDirs: Seq[String] = Csv.parseCsvList(Option(getString(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_DIRS_CONFIG)).getOrElse(getString(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_DIR_CONFIG))).asScala\n   437\u2192  def logSegmentBytes = getInt(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_SEGMENT_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   438\u2192  def logFlushIntervalMessages = getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_FLUSH_INTERVAL_MESSAGES_CONFIG)\n   439\u2192  val logCleanerThreads = getInt(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_FLUSH_OFFSET_CHECKPOINT_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG).toLong\n   443\u2192  val logFlushStartOffsetCheckpointIntervalMs = getInt(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_FLUSH_START_OFFSET_CHECKPOINT_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG).toLong\n   444\u2192  val logCleanupIntervalMs = getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG)\n   445\u2192  def logCleanupPolicy = getList(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_CLEANUP_POLICY_CONFIG)\n   446\u2192\n   447\u2192  def logRetentionBytes = getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   448\u2192  val logCleanerDedupeBufferSize = getLong(CleanerConfig.LOG_CLEANER_DEDUPE_BUFFER_SIZE_PROP)\n   449\u2192  val logCleanerDedupeBufferLoadFactor = getDouble(CleanerConfig.LOG_CLEANER_DEDUPE_BUFFER_LOAD_FACTOR_PROP)\n   450\u2192  val logCleanerIoBufferSize = getInt(CleanerConfig.LOG_CLEANER_IO_BUFFER_SIZE_PROP)\n   451\u2192  val logCleanerIoMaxBytesPerSecond = getDouble(CleanerConfig.LOG_CLEANER_IO_MAX_BYTES_PER_SECOND_PROP)\n   452\u2192  def logCleanerDeleteRetentionMs = getLong(CleanerConfig.LOG_CLEANER_DELETE_RETENTION_MS_PROP)\n   453\u2192  def logCleanerMinCompactionLagMs = getLong(CleanerConfig.LOG_CLEANER_MIN_COMPACTION_LAG_MS_PROP)\n   454\u2192  def def logCleanerMinCleanRatio = getDouble(CleanerConfig.LOG_CLEANER_MIN_CLEAN_RATIO_PROP)\n   457\u2192  val logCleanerEnable = getBoolean(CleanerConfig.LOG_CLEANER_ENABLE_PROP)\n   458\u2192  def logIndexSizeMaxBytes = getInt(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_INDEX_SIZE_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   459\u2192  def logIndexIntervalBytes = getInt(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_INDEX_INTERVAL_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   460\u2192  def logDeleteDelayMs = getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_DELETE_DELAY_MS_CONFIG)\n   461\u2192  def logRollTimeMillis: java.lang.Long = Option(getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG)).getOrElse(60 * 60 * 1000L * getInt(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG))\n   462\u2192  def logRollTimeJitterMillis:
