logFlushIntervalMs: java.lang.Long = Option(getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_FLUSH_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG)).getOrElse(getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_FLUSH_SCHEDULER_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG))\n   464\u2192  def minInSyncReplicas = getInt(ServerLogConfigs.MIN_IN_SYNC_REPLICAS_CONFIG)\n   465\u2192  def logPreAllocateEnable: java.lang.Boolean = getBoolean(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_PRE_ALLOCATE_CONFIG)\n   466\u2192  def logInitialTaskDelayMs: java.lang.Long = Option(getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_INITIAL_TASK_DELAY_MS_CONFIG)).getOrElse(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_INITIAL_TASK_DELAY_MS_DEFAULT)\n   467\u2192\n   468\u2192  // We keep the user-provided String as `MetadataVersion.fromVersionString` can choose a slightly different version (eg if `0.10.0`\n   469\u2192  // is passed, `0.10.0-IV0` may be picked)\n   470\u2192  @nowarn(\"cat=deprecation\")\n   471\u2192  private val logMessageFormatVersionString = getString(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_CONFIG)\n   472\u2192\n   473\u2192  /* See `TopicConfig.MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_CONFIG` for details */\n   474\u2192  @deprecated(\"3.0\")\n   475\u2192  lazy val logMessageFormatVersion =\n   476\u2192    if (LogConfig.shouldIgnoreMessageFormatVersion(interBrokerProtocolVersion))\n   477\u2192      MetadataVersion.fromVersionString(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_DEFAULT)\n   478\u2192    else MetadataVersion.fromVersionString(logMessageFormatVersionString)\n   479\u2192\n   480\u2192  def logMessageTimestampType = TimestampType.forName(getString(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_TYPE_CONFIG))\n   481\u2192\n   482\u2192  /* See `TopicConfig.MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_DIFFERENCE_MAX_MS_CONFIG` for details */\n   483\u2192  @deprecated(\"3.6\")\n   484\u2192  def logMessageTimestampDifferenceMaxMs: Long = getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_DIFFERENCE_MAX_MS_CONFIG)\n   485\u2192\n   486\u2192  // In the transition period before logMessageTimestampDifferenceMaxMs is removed, to maintain backward compatibility,\n   487\u2192  // we are using its value if logMessageTimestampBeforeMaxMs default value hasn't changed.\n   488\u2192  // See `TopicConfig.MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_CONFIG` for deprecation details\n   489\u2192  @nowarn(\"cat=deprecation\")\n   490\u2192  def logMessageTimestampBeforeMaxMs: Long = {\n   491\u2192    val messageTimestampBeforeMaxMs: Long = getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_BEFORE_MAX_MS_CONFIG)\n   492\u2192    if (messageTimestampBeforeMaxMs != ServerLogConfigs.LOG_MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_DIFFERENCE_MAX_MS_DEFAULT) {\n   493\u2192      messageTimestampBeforeMaxMs\n   494\u2192    } else {\n   495\u2192      logMessageTimestampDifferenceMaxMs\n   496\u2192    }\n   497\u2192  }\n   498\u2192\n   499\u2192  // In the transition period before logMessageTimestampDifferenceMaxMs is removed, to maintain backward compatibility,\n   500\u2192  // we are using its value if logMessageTimestampAfterMaxMs default value hasn't changed.\n   501\u2192  // See `TopicConfig.MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_CONFIG` for deprecation details\n   502\u2192  @nowarn(\"cat=deprecation\")\n   503\u2192  def logMessageTimestampAfterMaxMs: Long = {\n   504\u2192    val messageTimestampAfterMaxMs: Long = getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_AFTER_MAX_MS_CONFIG)\n   505\u2192    if (messageTimestampAfterMaxMs != Long.MaxValue) {\n   506\u2192      messageTimestampAfterMaxMs\n   507\u2192    } else {\n   508\u2192      logMessageTimestampDifferenceMaxMs\n   509\u2192    }\n   510\u2192  }\n   511\u2192\n   512\u2192  def logMessageDownConversionEnable: Boolean = getBoolean(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_MESSAGE_DOWNCONVERSION_ENABLE_CONFIG)\n   513\u2192\n   514\u2192  def logDirFailureTimeoutMs: Long = getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_DIR_FAILURE_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n   515\u2192\n   516\u2192  /** ********* Replication configuration ***********/\n   517\u2192  val controllerSocketTimeoutMs: Int = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.CONTROLLER_SOCKET_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n   518\u2192  val defaultReplicationFactor: Int = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.DEFAULT_REPLICATION_FACTOR_CONFIG)\n   519\u2192  val replicaLagTimeMaxMs = getLong(ReplicationConfigs.REPLICA_LAG_TIME_MAX_MS_CONFIG)\n   520\u2192  val replicaSocketTimeoutMs = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.REPLICA_SOCKET_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n   521\u2192  val replicaSocketReceiveBufferBytes = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.REPLICA_SOCKET_RECEIVE_BUFFER_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   522\u2192  val replicaFetchMaxBytes = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.REPLICA_FETCH_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   523\u2192  val replicaFetchWaitMaxMs = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.REPLICA_FETCH_WAIT_MAX_MS_CONFIG)\n   524\u2192  val replicaFetchMinBytes = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.REPLICA_FETCH_MIN_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   525\u2192  val replicaFetchResponseMaxBytes = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.REPLICA_FETCH_RESPONSE_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   526\u2192  val replicaFetchBackoffMs = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.REPLICA_FETCH_BACKOFF_MS_CONFIG)\n   527\u2192  def numReplicaFetchers = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.NUM_REPLICA_FETCHERS_CONFIG)\n   528\u2192  val replicaHighWatermarkCheckpointIntervalMs = getLong(ReplicationConfigs.REPLICA_HIGH_WATERMARK_CHECKPOINT_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG)\n   529\u2192  val fetchPurgatoryPurgeIntervalRequests = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.FETCH_PURGATORY_PURGE_INTERVAL_REQUESTS_CONFIG)\n   530\u2192  val producerPurgatoryPurgeIntervalRequests = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.PRODUCER_PURGATORY_PURGE_INTERVAL_REQUESTS_CONFIG)\n   531\u2192  val deleteRecordsPurgatoryPurgeIntervalRequests = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.DELETE_RECORDS_PURGATORY_PURGE_INTERVAL_REQUESTS_CONFIG)\n   532\u2192  val autoLeaderRebalanceEnable = getBoolean(ReplicationConfigs.AUTO_LEADER_REBALANCE_ENABLE_CONFIG)\n   533\u2192  val leaderImbalancePerBrokerPercentage = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.LEADER_IMBALANCE_PER_BROKER_PERCENTAGE_CONFIG)\n   534\u2192  val leaderImbalanceCheckIntervalSeconds: Long = getLong(ReplicationConfigs.LEADER_IMBALANCE_CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS_CONFIG)\n   535\u2192  val uncleanLeaderElectionCheckIntervalMs: Long = getLong(ReplicationConfigs.UNCLEAN_LEADER_ELECTION_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG)\n   536\u2192  def uncleanLeaderElectionEnable: java.lang.Boolean = getBoolean(ReplicationConfigs.UNCLEAN_LEADER_ELECTION_ENABLE_CONFIG)\n   537\u2192\n   538\u2192  // We keep the user-provided String as `MetadataVersion.fromVersionString` can choose a slightly different version (eg if `0.10.0`\n   539\u2192  // is passed, `0.10.0-IV0` may be picked)\n   540\u2192  val interBrokerProtocolVersionString = getString(ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION_CONFIG)\n   541\u2192  val interBrokerProtocolVersion = if (processRoles.isEmpty) {\n   542\u2192    MetadataVersion.fromVersionString(interBrokerProtocolVersionString)\n   543\u2192  } else {\n   544\u2192    if (originals.containsKey(ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION_CONFIG)) {\n   545\u2192      // A user-supplied IBP was given\n   546\u2192      val configuredVersion = MetadataVersion.fromVersionString(interBrokerProtocolVersionString)\n   547\u2192      if (!configuredVersion.isKRaftSupported) {\n   548\u2192        throw new ConfigException(s\"A non-KRaft version $interBrokerProtocolVersionString given for ${ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION_CONFIG}. \" +\n   549\u2192          s\"The minimum version is ${MetadataVersion.MINIMUM_KRAFT_VERSION}\")\n   550\u2192      } else {\n   551\u2192        warn(s\"${ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION_CONFIG} is deprecated in KRaft mode as of 3.3 and will only \" +\n   552\u2192          s\"be read when first upgrading from a KRaft prior to 3.3. See kafka-storage.sh help for details on setting \" +\n   553\u2192          s\"the metadata.version for a new KRaft cluster.\")\n   554\u2192      }\n   555\u2192    }\n   556\u2192    // In KRaft mode, we pin this value to the minimum KRaft-supported version. This prevents inadvertent usage of\n   557\u2192    // the static IBP config in broker components running in KRaft mode\n   558\u2192    MetadataVersion.MINIMUM_KRAFT_VERSION\n   559\u2192  }\n   560\u2192\n   561\u2192  /** ********* Controlled shutdown configuration ***********/\n   562\u2192  val controlledShutdownMaxRetries = getInt(ServerConfigs.CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_MAX_RETRIES_CONFIG)\n   563\u2192  val controlledShutdownRetryBackoffMs = getLong(ServerConfigs.CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_RETRY_BACKOFF_MS_CONFIG)\n   564\u2192  val controlledShutdownEnable = getBoolean(ServerConfigs.CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_ENABLE_CONFIG)\n   565\u2192\n   566\u2192  /** ********* Feature configuration ***********/\n   567\u2192  def isFeatureVersioningSupported = interBrokerProtocolVersion.isFeatureVersioningSupported\n   568\u2192\n   569\u2192  /** New group coordinator configs */\n   570\u2192  val groupCoordinatorRebalanceProtocols = {\n   571\u2192    val protocols = getList(GroupCoordinatorConfig.GROUP_COORDINATOR_REBALANCE_PROTOCOLS_CONFIG)\n   572\u2192      .asScala.map(_.toUpperCase).map(GroupType.valueOf).toSet\n   573\u2192    if (!protocols.contains(GroupType.CLASSIC)) {\n   574\u2192      throw new ConfigException(s\"Disabling the '${GroupType.CLASSIC}' protocol is not supported.\")\n   575\u2192    }\n   576\u2192    if (protocols.contains(GroupType.CONSUMER)) {\n   577\u2192      if (processRoles.isEmpty) {\n   578\u2192        throw new ConfigException(s\"The new '${GroupType.CONSUMER}' rebalance protocol is only supported in KRaft cluster.\")\n   579\u2192      }\n   580\u2192      warn(s\"The new '${GroupType.CONSUMER}' rebalance protocol is enabled along with the new group coordinator. \" +\n   581\u2192        \"This is part of the preview of KIP-848 and MUST NOT be used in production.\")\n   582\u2192    }\n   583\u2192    protocols\n   584\u2192  }\n   585\u2192  // The new group coordinator is enabled in two cases: 1) The internal configuration to enable\n   586\u2192  // it is explicitly set; or 2) the consumer rebalance protocol is enabled.\n   587\u2192  val isNewGroupCoordinatorEnabled = getBoolean(GroupCoordinatorConfig.NEW_GROUP_COORDINATOR_ENABLE_CONFIG) ||\n   588\u2192    groupCoordinatorRebalanceProtocols.contains(GroupType.CONSUMER)\n   589\u2192\n   590\u2192  /** ********* Transaction management configuration ***********/\n   591\u2192  val transactionalIdExpirationMs = getInt(TransactionStateManagerConfigs.TRANSACTIONAL_ID_EXPIRATION_MS_CONFIG)\n   592\u2192  val transactionMaxTimeoutMs = getInt(TransactionStateManagerConfigs.TRANSACTIONS_MAX_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n   593\u2192  val transactionTopicMinISR = getInt(TransactionLogConfigs.TRANSACTIONS_TOPIC_MIN_ISR_CONFIG)\n   594\u2192  val transactionsLoadBufferSize = getInt(TransactionLogConfigs.TRANSACTIONS_LOAD_BUFFER_SIZE_CONFIG)\n   595\u2192  val transactionTopicReplicationFactor = getShort(TransactionLogConfigs.TRANSACTIONS_TOPIC_REPLICATION_FACTOR_CONFIG)\n   596\u2192  val transactionTopicPartitions = getInt(TransactionLogConfigs.TRANSACTIONS_TOPIC_PARTITIONS_CONFIG)\n   597\u2192  val transactionTopicSegmentBytes = getInt(TransactionLogConfigs.TRANSACTIONS_TOPIC_SEGMENT_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   598\u2192  val transactionAbortTimedOutTransactionCleanupIntervalMs = getInt(TransactionStateManagerConfigs.TRANSACTIONS_ABORT_TIMED_OUT_TRANSACTION_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG)\n   599\u2192  val transactionRemoveExpiredTransactionalIdCleanupIntervalMs = getInt(TransactionStateManagerConfigs.TRANSACTIONS_REMOVE_EXPIRED_TRANSACTIONAL_ID_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG)\n   600\u2192\n   601\u2192  def transactionPartitionVerificationEnable = getBoolean(TransactionLogConfigs.TRANSACTION_PARTITION_VERIFICATION_ENABLE_CONFIG)\n   602\u2192\n   603\u2192  def producerIdExpirationMs = getInt(TransactionLogConfigs.PRODUCER_ID_EXPIRATION_MS_CONFIG)\n   604\u2192  val producerIdExpirationCheckIntervalMs = getInt(TransactionLogConfigs.PRODUCER_ID_EXPIRATION_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG)\n   605\u2192\n   606\u2192  /** ********* Metric Configuration **************/\n   607\u2192  val metricNumSamples = getInt(MetricConfigs.METRIC_NUM_SAMPLES_CONFIG)\n   608\u2192  val metricSampleWindowMs = getLong(MetricConfigs.METRIC_SAMPLE_WINDOW_MS_CONFIG)\n   609\u2192  val metricRecordingLevel = getString(MetricConfigs.METRIC_RECORDING_LEVEL_CONFIG)\n   610\u2192\n   611\u2192  /** ********* Kafka Client Telemetry Metrics Configuration ***********/\n   612\u2192  val clientTelemetryMaxBytes: Int = getInt(MetricConfigs.CLIENT_TELEMETRY_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   613\u2192\n   614\u2192  /** ********* SSL/SASL Configuration **************/\n   615\u2192  // Security configs may be overridden for listeners, so it is not safe to use the base values\n   616\u2192  // Hence the base SSL/SASL configs are not fields of KafkaConfig, listener configs should be\n   617\u2192  // retrieved using KafkaConfig#valuesWithPrefixOverride\n   618\u2192  private def saslEnabledMechanisms(listenerName: ListenerName): Set[String] = {\n   619\u2192    val value = valuesWithPrefixOverride(listenerName.configPrefix).get(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG)\n   620\u2192    if (value != null)\n   621\u2192      value.asInstanceOf[util.List[String]].asScala.toSet\n   622\u2192    else\n   623\u2192      Set.empty[String]\n   624\u2192  }\n   625\u2192\n   626\u2192  def interBrokerListenerName = getInterBrokerListenerNameAndSecurityProtocol._1\n   627\u2192  def interBrokerSecurityProtocol = getInterBrokerListenerNameAndSecurityProtocol._2\n   628\u2192  def controlPlaneListenerName = getControlPlaneListenerNameAndSecurityProtocol.map { case (listenerName, _) => listenerName }\n   629\u2192  def controlPlaneSecurityProtocol = getControlPlaneListenerNameAndSecurityProtocol.map { case (_, securityProtocol) => securityProtocol }\n   630\u2192  def saslMechanismInterBrokerProtocol = getString(BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_MECHANISM_INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_CONFIG)\n   631\u2192  val saslInterBrokerHandshakeRequestEnable = interBrokerProtocolVersion.isSaslInterBrokerHandshakeRequestEnabled\n   632\u2192\n   633\u2192  /** ********* DelegationToken Configuration **************/\n   634\u2192  val delegationTokenSecretKey = Option(getPassword(DelegationTokenManagerConfigs.DELEGATION_TOKEN_SECRET_KEY_CONFIG))\n   635\u2192    .getOrElse(getPassword(DelegationTokenManagerConfigs.DELEGATION_TOKEN_SECRET_KEY_ALIAS_CONFIG))\n   636\u2192  val tokenAuthEnabled = delegationTokenSecretKey != null && delegationTokenSecretKey.value.nonEmpty\n   637\u2192  val delegationTokenMaxLifeMs = getLong(DelegationTokenManagerConfigs.DELEGATION_TOKEN_MAX_LIFETIME_CONFIG)\n   638\u2192  val delegationTokenExpiryTimeMs = getLong(DelegationTokenManagerConfigs.DELEGATION_TOKEN_EXPIRY_TIME_MS_CONFIG)\n   639\u2192  val delegationTokenExpiryCheckIntervalMs = getLong(DelegationTokenManagerConfigs.DELEGATION_TOKEN_EXPIRY_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG)\n   640\u2192\n   641\u2192  /** ********* Password encryption configuration for dynamic configs *********/\n   642\u2192  def passwordEncoderSecret = Option(getPassword(PasswordEncoderConfigs.PASSWORD_ENCODER_SECRET_CONFIG))\n   643\u2192  def passwordEncoderOldSecret = Option(getPassword(PasswordEncoderConfigs.PASSWORD_ENCODER_OLD_SECRET_CONFIG))\n   644\u2192  def passwordEncoderCipherAlgorithm = getString(PasswordEncoderConfigs.PASSWORD_ENCODER_CIPHER_ALGORITHM_CONFIG)\n   645\u2192  def passwordEncoderKeyFactoryAlgorithm = getString(PasswordEncoderConfigs.PASSWORD_ENCODER_KEYFACTORY_ALGORITHM_CONFIG)\n   646\u2192  def passwordEncoderKeyLength = getInt(PasswordEncoderConfigs.PASSWORD_ENCODER_KEY_LENGTH_CONFIG)\n   647\u2192  def passwordEncoderIterations = getInt(PasswordEncoderConfigs.PASSWORD_ENCODER_ITERATIONS_CONFIG)\n   648\u2192\n   649\u2192  /** ********* Quota Configuration **************/\n   650\u2192  val numQuotaSamples = getInt(QuotaConfigs.NUM_QUOTA_SAMPLES_CONFIG)\n   651\u2192  val quotaWindowSizeSeconds = getInt(QuotaConfigs.QUOTA_WINDOW_SIZE_SECONDS_CONFIG)\n   652\u2192  val numReplicationQuotaSamples = getInt(QuotaConfigs.NUM_REPLICATION_QUOTA_SAMPLES_CONFIG)\n   653\u2192  val replicationQuotaWindowSizeSeconds = getInt(QuotaConfigs.REPLICATION_QUOTA_WINDOW_SIZE_SECONDS_CONFIG)\n   654\u2192  val numAlterLogDirsReplicationQuotaSamples = getInt(QuotaConfigs.NUM_ALTER_LOG_DIRS_REPLICATION_QUOTA_SAMPLES_CONFIG)\n   655\u2192  val alterLogDirsReplicationQuotaWindowSizeSeconds = getInt(QuotaConfigs.ALTER_LOG_DIRS_REPLICATION_QUOTA_WINDOW_SIZE_SECONDS_CONFIG)\n   656\u2192  val numControllerQuotaSamples = getInt(QuotaConfigs.NUM_CONTROLLER_QUOTA_SAMPLES_CONFIG)\n   657\u2192  val controllerQuotaWindowSizeSeconds = getInt(QuotaConfigs.CONTROLLER_QUOTA_WINDOW_SIZE_SECONDS_CONFIG)\n   658\u2192\n   659\u2192  /** ********* Fetch Configuration **************/\n   660\u2192  val maxIncrementalFetchSessionCacheSlots = getInt(ServerConfigs.MAX_INCREMENTAL_FETCH_SESSION_CACHE_SLOTS_CONFIG)\n   661\u2192  val fetchMaxBytes = getInt(ServerConfigs.FETCH_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   662\u2192\n   663\u2192  /** ********* Request Limit Configuration ***********/\n   664\u2192  val maxRequestPartitionSizeLimit = getInt(ServerConfigs.MAX_REQUEST_PARTITION_SIZE_LIMIT_CONFIG)\n   665\u2192\n   666\u2192  val deleteTopicEnable = getBoolean(ServerConfigs.DELETE_TOPIC_ENABLE_CONFIG)\n   667\u2192  def compressionType = getString(ServerConfigs.COMPRESSION_TYPE_CONFIG)\n   668\u2192\n   669\u2192  def gzipCompressionLevel = getInt(ServerConfigs.COMPRESSION_GZIP_LEVEL_CONFIG)\n   670\u2192  def lz4CompressionLevel = getInt(ServerConfigs.COMPRESSION_LZ4_LEVEL_CONFIG)\n   671\u2192  def zstdCompressionLevel = getInt(ServerConfigs.COMPRESSION_ZSTD_LEVEL_CONFIG)\n   672\u2192\n   673\u2192  /** ********* Raft Quorum Configuration *********/\n   674\u2192  val quorumVoters = getList(QuorumConfig.QUORUM_VOTERS_CONFIG)\n   675\u2192  val quorumBootstrapServers = getList(QuorumConfig.QUORUM_BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG)\n   676\u2192  val quorumElectionTimeoutMs = getInt(QuorumConfig.QUORUM_ELECTION_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n   677\u2192  val quorumFetchTimeoutMs = getInt(QuorumConfig.QUORUM_FETCH_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n   678\u2192  val quorumElectionBackoffMs = getInt(QuorumConfig.QUORUM_ELECTION_BACKOFF_MAX_MS_CONFIG)\n   679\u2192  val quorumLingerMs = getInt(QuorumConfig.QUORUM_LINGER_MS_CONFIG)\n   680\u2192  val quorumRequestTimeoutMs = getInt(QuorumConfig.QUORUM_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n   681\u2192  val quorumRetryBackoffMs = getInt(QuorumConfig.QUORUM_RETRY_BACKOFF_MS_CONFIG)\n   682\u2192\n   683\u2192  /** Internal Configurations **/\n   684\u2192  val unstableApiVersionsEnabled = getBoolean(ServerConfigs.UNSTABLE_API_VERSIONS_ENABLE_CONFIG)\n   685\u2192  val unstableFeatureVersionsEnabled = getBoolean(ServerConfigs.UNSTABLE_FEATURE_VERSIONS_ENABLE_CONFIG)\n   686\u2192\n   687\u2192  def addReconfigurable(reconfigurable: Reconfigurable): Unit = {\n   688\u2192    dynamicConfig.addReconfigurable(reconfigurable)\n   689\u2192  }\n   690\u2192\n   691\u2192  def removeReconfigurable(reconfigurable: Reconfigurable): Unit = {\n   692\u2192    dynamicConfig.removeReconfigurable(reconfigurable)\n   693\u2192  }\n   694\u2192\n   695\u2192  def logRetentionTimeMillis: Long = {\n   696\u2192    val millisInMinute = 60L * 1000L\n   697\u2192    val millisInHour = 60L * millisInMinute\n   698\u2192\n   699\u2192    val millis: java.lang.Long =\n   700\u2192      Option(getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG)).getOrElse(\n   701\u2192        Option(getInt(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MINUTES_CONFIG)) match {\n   702\u2192          case Some(mins) => millisInMinute * mins\n   703\u2192          case None => getInt(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG) * millisInHour\n   704\u2192        })\n   705\u2192\n   706\u2192    if (millis < 0) return -1\n   707\u2192    millis\n   708\u2192  }\n   709\u2192\n   710\u2192  private def getMap(propName: String, propValue: String): Map[String, String] = {\n   711\u2192    try {\n   712\u2192      Csv.parseCsvMap(propValue).asScala\n   713\u2192    } catch {\n   714\u2192      case e: Exception => throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Error parsing configuration property '%s': %s\".format(propName, e.getMessage))\n   715\u2192    }\n   716\u2192  }\n   717\u2192\n   718\u2192  def listeners: Seq[EndPoint] =\n   719\u2192    CoreUtils.listenerListToEndPoints(getString(SocketServerConfigs.LISTENERS_CONFIG), effectiveListenerSecurityProtocolMap)\n   720\u2192\n   721\u2192  def controllerListenerNames: Seq[String] = {\n   722\u2192    val value = Option(getString(KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG)).getOrElse(\"\")\n   723\u2192    if (value.isEmpty) {\n   724\u2192      Seq.empty\n   725\u2192    } else {\n   726\u2192      value.split(\",\")\n   727\u2192    }\n   728\u2192  }\n   729\u2192\n   730\u2192  def controllerListeners: Seq[EndPoint] =\n   731\u2192    listeners.filter(l => controllerListenerNames.contains(l.listenerName.value()))\n   732\u2192\n   733\u2192  def saslMechanismControllerProtocol: String = getString(KRaftConfigs.SASL_MECHANISM_CONTROLLER_PROTOCOL_CONFIG)\n   734\u2192\n   735\u2192  def controlPlaneListener: Option[EndPoint] = {\n   736\u2192    controlPlaneListenerName.map { listenerName =>\n   737\u2192      listeners.filter(endpoint => endpoint.listenerName.value() == listenerName.value()).head\n   738\u2192    }\n   739\u2192  }\n   740\u2192\n   741\u2192  def dataPlaneListeners: Seq[EndPoint] = {\n   742\u2192    listeners.filterNot { listener =>\n   743\u2192      val name = listener.listenerName.value()\n   744\u2192      name.equals(getString(SocketServerConfigs.CONTROL_PLANE_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG)) ||\n   745\u2192        controllerListenerNames.contains(name)\n   746\u2192    }\n   747\u2192  }\n   748\u2192\n   749\u2192  def effectiveAdvertisedControllerListeners: Seq[EndPoint] = {\n   750\u2192    val controllerAdvertisedListeners = advertisedListeners.filter(l => controllerListenerNames.contains(l.listenerName.value()))\n   751\u2192    val controllerListenersValue = controllerListeners\n   752\u2192\n   753\u2192    controllerListenerNames.flatMap { name =>\n   754\u2192      controllerAdvertisedListeners\n   755\u2192        .find(endpoint => endpoint.listenerName.equals(ListenerName.normalised(name)))\n   756\u2192        .orElse(controllerListenersValue.find(endpoint => endpoint.listenerName.equals(ListenerName.normalised(name))))\n   757\u2192    }\n   758\u2192  }\n   759\u2192\n   760\u2192  def effectiveAdvertisedBrokerListeners: Seq[EndPoint] = {\n   761\u2192    advertisedListeners.filter(l => {\n   762\u2192      if (!controllerListenerNames.contains(l.listenerName.value())) {\n   763\u2192        true\n   764\u2192      } else if (migrationEnabled && Some(l.listenerName.value()).equals(controlPlaneListener.map(_.listenerName.value()))) {\n   765\u2192        // KAFKA-17788: during ZK migration, always include control.plane.listener.name\n   766\u2192        // in advertisedBrokerListeners.\n   767\u2192        true\n   768\u2192      } else {\n   769\u2192        false\n   770\u2192      }\n   771\u2192    })\n   772\u2192  }\n   773\u2192\n   774\u2192  // Use advertised listeners if defined, fallback to listeners otherwise\n   775\u2192  private def advertisedListeners: Seq[EndPoint] = {\n   776\u2192    val advertisedListenersProp = getString(SocketServerConfigs.ADVERTISED_LISTENERS_CONFIG)\n   777\u2192    if (advertisedListenersProp != null) {\n   778\u2192      CoreUtils.listenerListToEndPoints(advertisedListenersProp, effectiveListenerSecurityProtocolMap, requireDistinctPorts=false)\n   779\u2192    } else {\n   780\u2192      listeners\n   781\u2192    }\n   782\u2192  }\n   783\u2192\n   784\u2192  private def getInterBrokerListenerNameAndSecurityProtocol: (ListenerName, SecurityProtocol) = {\n   785\u2192    Option(getString(ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG)) match {\n   786\u2192      case Some(_) if originals.containsKey(ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_CONFIG) =>\n   787\u2192        throw new ConfigException(s\"Only one of ${ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG} and \" +\n   788\u2192          s\"${ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_CONFIG} should be set.\")\n   789\u2192      case Some(name) =>\n   790\u2192        val listenerName = ListenerName.normalised(name)\n   791\u2192        val securityProtocol = effectiveListenerSecurityProtocolMap.getOrElse(listenerName,\n   792\u2192          throw new ConfigException(s\"Listener with name ${listenerName.value} defined in \" +\n   793\u2192            s\"${ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG} not found in ${SocketServerConfigs.LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_CONFIG}.\"))\n   794\u2192        (listenerName, securityProtocol)\n   795\u2192      case None =>\n   796\u2192        val securityProtocol = getSecurityProtocol(getString(ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_CONFIG),\n   797\u2192          ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_CONFIG)\n   798\u2192        (ListenerName.forSecurityProtocol(securityProtocol), securityProtocol)\n   799\u2192    }\n   800\u2192  }\n   801\u2192\n   802\u2192  private def getControlPlaneListenerNameAndSecurityProtocol: Option[(ListenerName, SecurityProtocol)] = {\n   803\u2192    Option(getString(SocketServerConfigs.CONTROL_PLANE_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG)) match {\n   804\u2192      case Some(name) =>\n   805\u2192        val listenerName = ListenerName.normalised(name)\n   806\u2192        val securityProtocol = effectiveListenerSecurityProtocolMap.getOrElse(listenerName,\n   807\u2192          throw new ConfigException(s\"Listener with ${listenerName.value} defined in \" +\n   808\u2192            s\"${SocketServerConfigs.CONTROL_PLANE_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG} not found in ${SocketServerConfigs.LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_CONFIG}.\"))\n   809\u2192        Some(listenerName, securityProtocol)\n   810\u2192\n   811\u2192      case None => None\n   812\u2192   }\n   813\u2192  }\n   814\u2192\n   815\u2192  private def getSecurityProtocol(protocolName: String, configName: String): SecurityProtocol = {\n   816\u2192    try SecurityProtocol.forName(protocolName)\n   817\u2192    catch {\n   818\u2192      case _: IllegalArgumentException =>\n   819\u2192        throw new ConfigException(s\"Invalid security protocol `$protocolName` defined in $configName\")\n   820\u2192    }\n   821\u2192  }\n   822\u2192\n   823\u2192  def effectiveListenerSecurityProtocolMap: Map[ListenerName, SecurityProtocol] = {\n   824\u2192    val mapValue = getMap(SocketServerConfigs.LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_CONFIG, getString(SocketServerConfigs.LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_CONFIG))\n   825\u2192      .map { case (listenerName, protocolName) =>\n   826\u2192        ListenerName.normalised(listenerName) -> getSecurityProtocol(protocolName, SocketServerConfigs.LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_CONFIG)\n   827\u2192      }\n   828\u2192    if (usesSelfManagedQuorum && !originals.containsKey(SocketServerConfigs.LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_CONFIG)) {\n   829\u2192      // Nothing was specified explicitly for listener.security.protocol.map, so we are using the default value,\n   830\u2192      // and we are using KRaft.\n   831\u2192      // Add PLAINTEXT mappings for controller listeners as long as there is no SSL or SASL_{PLAINTEXT,SSL} in use\n   832\u2192      def isSslOrSasl(name: String): Boolean = name.equals(SecurityProtocol.SSL.name) || name.equals(SecurityProtocol.SASL_SSL.name) || name.equals(SecurityProtocol.SASL_PLAINTEXT.name)\n   833\u2192      // check controller listener names (they won't appear in listeners when process.roles=broker)\n   834\u2192      // as well as listeners for occurrences of SSL or SASL_*\n   835\u2192      if (controllerListenerNames.exists(isSslOrSasl) ||\n   836\u2192        Csv.parseCsvList(getString(SocketServerConfigs.LISTENERS_CONFIG)).asScala.exists(listenerValue => isSslOrSasl(EndPoint.parseListenerName(listenerValue)))) {\n   837\u2192        mapValue // don't add default mappings since we found something that is SSL or SASL_*\n   838\u2192      } else {\n   839\u2192        // add the PLAINTEXT mappings for all controller listener names that are not explicitly PLAINTEXT\n   840\u2192        mapValue ++ controllerListenerNames.filterNot(SecurityProtocol.PLAINTEXT.name.equals(_)).map(\n   841\u2192          new ListenerName(_) -> SecurityProtocol.PLAINTEXT)\n   842\u2192      }\n   843\u2192    } else {\n   844\u2192      mapValue\n   845\u2192    }\n   846\u2192  }\n   847\u2192\n   848\u2192  // Topic IDs are used with all self-managed quorum clusters and ZK cluster with IBP greater than or equal to 2.8\n   849\u2192  def usesTopicId: Boolean =\n   850\u2192    usesSelfManagedQuorum || interBrokerProtocolVersion.isTopicIdsSupported\n   851\u2192\n   852\u2192  validateValues()\n   853\u2192\n   854\u2192  @nowarn(\"cat=deprecation\")\n   855\u2192  private def validateValues(): Unit = {\n   856\u2192    if (nodeId != brokerId) {\n   857\u2192      throw new ConfigException(s\"You must set `${KRaftConfigs.NODE_ID_CONFIG}` to the same value as `${ServerConfigs.BROKER_ID_CONFIG}`.\")\n   858\u2192    }\n   859\u2192    if (requiresZookeeper) {\n   860\u2192      if (zkConnect == null) {\n   861\u2192        throw new ConfigException(s\"Missing required configuration `${ZkConfigs.ZK_CONNECT_CONFIG}` which has no default value.\")\n   862\u2192      }\n   863\u2192      if (brokerIdGenerationEnable) {\n   864\u2192        if (migrationEnabled) {\n   865\u2192          require(brokerId >= 0, \"broker.id generation is incompatible with ZooKeeper migration. Please stop using it before enabling migration (set broker.id to a value greater or equal to 0).\")\n   866\u2192        }\n   867\u2192        require(brokerId >= -1 && brokerId <= maxReservedBrokerId, \"broker.id must be greater than or equal to -1 and not greater than reserved.broker.max.id\")\n   868\u2192      } else {\n   869\u2192        require(brokerId >= 0, \"broker.id must be greater than or equal to 0\")\n   870\u2192      }\n   871\u2192    } else {\n   872\u2192      // KRaft-based metadata quorum\n   873\u2192      if (nodeId < 0) {\n   874\u2192        throw new ConfigException(s\"Missing configuration `${KRaftConfigs.NODE_ID_CONFIG}` which is required \" +\n   875\u2192          s\"when `process.roles` is defined (i.e. when running in KRaft mode).\")\n   876\u2192      }\n   877\u2192      if (migrationEnabled) {\n   878\u2192        if (zkConnect == null) {\n   879\u2192          throw new ConfigException(s\"If using `${KRaftConfigs.MIGRATION_ENABLED_CONFIG}` in KRaft mode, `${ZkConfigs.ZK_CONNECT_CONFIG}` must also be set.\")\n   880\u2192        }\n   881\u2192      }\n   882\u2192    }\n   883\u2192    require(logRollTimeMillis >= 1, \"log.roll.ms must be greater than or equal to 1\")\n   884\u2192    require(logRollTimeJitterMillis >= 0, \"log.roll.jitter.ms must be greater than or equal to 0\")\n   885\u2192    require(logRetentionTimeMillis >= 1 || logRetentionTimeMillis == -1, \"log.retention.ms must be unlimited (-1) or, greater than or equal to 1\")\n   886\u2192    require(logDirs.nonEmpty, \"At least one log directory must be defined via log.dirs or log.dir.\")\n   887\u2192    require(logCleanerDedupeBufferSize / logCleanerThreads > 1024 * 1024, \"log.cleaner.dedupe.buffer.size must be at least 1MB per cleaner thread.\")\n   888\u2192    require(replicaFetchWaitMaxMs <= replicaSocketTimeoutMs, \"replica.socket.timeout.ms should always be at least replica.fetch.wait.max.ms\" +\n   889\u2192      \" to prevent unnecessary socket timeouts\")\n   890\u2192    require(replicaFetchWaitMaxMs <= replicaLagTimeMaxMs, \"replica.fetch.wait.max.ms should always be less than or equal to replica.lag.time.max.ms\" +\n   891\u2192      \" to prevent frequent changes in ISR\")\n   892\u2192\n   893\u2192    val advertisedBrokerListenerNames = effectiveAdvertisedBrokerListeners.map(_.listenerName).toSet\n   894\u2192\n   895\u2192    // validate KRaft-related configs\n   896\u2192    val voterIds = QuorumConfig.parseVoterIds(quorumVoters)\n   897\u2192    def validateQuorumVotersAndQuorumBootstrapServerForKRaft(): Unit = {\n   898\u2192      if (voterIds.isEmpty && quorumBootstrapServers.isEmpty) {\n   899\u2192        throw new ConfigException(\n   900\u2192          s\"\"\"If using ${KRaftConfigs.PROCESS_ROLES_CONFIG}, either ${QuorumConfig.QUORUM_BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG} must\n   901\u2192          |contain the set of bootstrap controllers or ${QuorumConfig.QUORUM_VOTERS_CONFIG} must contain a parseable\n   902\u2192          |set of controllers.\"\"\".stripMargin.replace(\"\\n\", \" \")\n   903\u2192        )\n   904\u2192      }\n   905\u2192    }\n   906\u2192    def validateQuorumVotersAndQuorumBootstrapServerForMigration(): Unit = {\n   907\u2192      if (voterIds.isEmpty && quorumBootstrapServers.isEmpty) {\n   908\u2192        throw new ConfigException(\n   909\u2192          s\"\"\"If using ${KRaftConfigs.MIGRATION_ENABLED_CONFIG}, either ${QuorumConfig.QUORUM_BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG} must\n   910\u2192          |contain the set of bootstrap controllers or ${QuorumConfig.QUORUM_VOTERS_CONFIG} must contain a parseable\n   911\u2192          |set of controllers.\"\"\".stripMargin.replace(\"\\n\", \" \")\n   912\u2192        )\n   913\u2192      }\n   914\u2192    }\n   915\u2192    def validateControlPlaneListenerEmptyForKRaft(): Unit = {\n   916\u2192      require(controlPlaneListenerName.isEmpty,\n   917\u2192        s\"${SocketServerConfigs.CONTROL_PLANE_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG} is not supported in KRaft mode.\")\n   918\u2192    }\n   919\u2192    def validateControllerQuorumVotersMustContainNodeIdForKRaftController(): Unit = {\n   920\u2192      require(voterIds.isEmpty || voterIds.contains(nodeId),\n   921\u2192        s\"If ${KRaftConfigs.PROCESS_ROLES_CONFIG} contains the 'controller' role, the node id $nodeId must be included in the set of voters ${QuorumConfig.QUORUM_VOTERS_CONFIG}=${voterIds.asScala.toSet}\")\n   922\u2192    }\n   923\u2192    def validateAdvertisedControllerListenersNonEmptyForKRaftController(): Unit = {\n   924\u2192      require(effectiveAdvertisedControllerListeners.nonEmpty,\n   925\u2192        s\"${KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG} must contain at least one value appearing in the '${SocketServerConfigs.LISTENERS_CONFIG}' configuration when running the KRaft controller role\")\n   926\u2192    }\n   927\u2192    def validateControllerListenerNamesMustAppearInListenersForKRaftController(): Unit = {\n   928\u2192      val listenerNameValues = listeners.map(_.listenerName.value).toSet\n   929\u2192      require(controllerListenerNames.forall(cln => listenerNameValues.contains(cln)),\n   930\u2192        s\"${KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG} must only contain values appearing in the '${SocketServerConfigs.LISTENERS_CONFIG}' configuration when running the KRaft controller role\")\n   931\u2192    }\n   932\u2192    def validateAdvertisedBrokerListenersNonEmptyForBroker(): Unit = {\n   933\u2192      require(advertisedBrokerListenerNames.nonEmpty,\n   934\u2192        \"There must be at least one broker advertised listener.\" + (\n   935\u2192          if (processRoles.contains(ProcessRole.BrokerRole)) s\" Perhaps all listeners appear in ${KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG}?\" else \"\"))\n   936\u2192    }\n   937\u2192    if (processRoles == Set(ProcessRole.BrokerRole)) {\n   938\u2192      // KRaft broker-only\n   939\u2192      validateQuorumVotersAndQuorumBootstrapServerForKRaft()\n   940\u2192      validateControlPlaneListenerEmptyForKRaft()\n   941\u2192      // nodeId must not appear in controller.quorum.voters\n   942\u2192      require(!voterIds.contains(nodeId),\n   943\u2192        s\"If ${KRaftConfigs.PROCESS_ROLES_CONFIG} contains just the 'broker' role, the node id $nodeId must not be included in the set of voters ${QuorumConfig.QUORUM_VOTERS_CONFIG}=${voterIds.asScala.toSet}\")\n   944\u2192      // controller.listener.names must be non-empty...\n   945\u2192      require(controllerListenerNames.nonEmpty,\n   946\u2192        s\"${KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG} must contain at least one value when running KRaft with just the broker role\")\n   947\u2192      // controller.listener.names are forbidden in listeners...\n   948\u2192      require(controllerListeners.isEmpty,\n   949\u2192        s\"${KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG} must not contain a value appearing in the '${SocketServerConfigs.LISTENERS_CONFIG}' configuration when running KRaft with just the broker role\")\n   950\u2192      // controller.listener.names must all appear in listener.security.protocol.map\n   951\u2192      controllerListenerNames.foreach { name =>\n   952\u2192        val listenerName = ListenerName.normalised(name)\n   953\u2192        if (!effectiveListenerSecurityProtocolMap.contains(listenerName)) {\n   954\u2192          throw new ConfigException(s\"Controller listener with name ${listenerName.value} defined in \" +\n   955\u2192            s\"${KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG} not found in ${SocketServerConfigs.LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_CONFIG}  (an explicit security mapping for each controller listener is required if ${SocketServerConfigs.LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_CONFIG} is non-empty, or if there are security protocols other than PLAINTEXT in use)\")\n   956\u2192        }\n   957\u2192      }\n   958\u2192      // warn that only the first controller listener is used if there is more than one\n   959\u2192      if (controllerListenerNames.size > 1) {\n   960\u2192        warn(s\"${KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG} has multiple entries; only the first will be used since ${KRaftConfigs.PROCESS_ROLES_CONFIG}=broker: ${controllerListenerNames.asJava}\")\n   961\u2192      }\n   962\u2192    } else if (processRoles == Set(ProcessRole.ControllerRole)) {\n   963\u2192      // KRaft controller-only\n   964\u2192      validateQuorumVotersAndQuorumBootstrapServerForKRaft()\n   965\u2192      validateControlPlaneListenerEmptyForKRaft()\n   966\u2192      // listeners should only contain listeners also enumerated in the controller listener\n   967\u2192      require(\n   968\u2192        effectiveAdvertisedControllerListeners.size == listeners.size,\n   969\u2192        s\"The ${SocketServerConfigs.LISTENERS_CONFIG} config must only contain KRaft controller listeners from ${KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG} when ${KRaftConfigs.PROCESS_ROLES_CONFIG}=controller\"\n   970\u2192      )\n   971\u2192      validateControllerQuorumVotersMustContainNodeIdForKRaftController()\n   972\u2192      validateAdvertisedControllerListenersNonEmptyForKRaftController()\n   973\u2192      validateControllerListenerNamesMustAppearInListenersForKRaftController()\n   974\u2192    } else if (isKRaftCombinedMode) {\n   975\u2192      // KRaft combined broker and controller\n   976\u2192      validateQuorumVotersAndQuorumBootstrapServerForKRaft()\n   977\u2192      validateControlPlaneListenerEmptyForKRaft()\n   978\u2192      validateControllerQuorumVotersMustContainNodeIdForKRaftController()\n   979\u2192      validateAdvertisedControllerListenersNonEmptyForKRaftController()\n   980\u2192      validateControllerListenerNamesMustAppearInListenersForKRaftController()\n   981\u2192    } else {\n   982\u2192      // ZK-based\n   983\u2192      if (migrationEnabled) {\n   984\u2192        require(brokerId >= 0,\n   985\u2192          \"broker.id generation is incompatible with ZooKeeper migration. Please stop using it before enabling migration (set broker.id to a value greater or equal to 0).\")\n   986\u2192        validateQuorumVotersAndQuorumBootstrapServerForMigration()\n   987\u2192        require(controllerListenerNames.nonEmpty,\n   988\u2192          s\"${KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG} must not be empty when running in ZooKeeper migration mode: ${controllerListenerNames.asJava}\")\n   989\u2192        require(interBrokerProtocolVersion.isMigrationSupported, s\"Cannot enable ZooKeeper migration without setting \" +\n   990\u2192          s\"'${ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION_CONFIG}' to 3.4 or higher\")\n   991\u2192        if (logDirs.size > 1) {\n   992\u2192          require(interBrokerProtocolVersion.isDirectoryAssignmentSupported,\n   993\u2192            s\"Cannot enable ZooKeeper migration with multiple log directories (aka JBOD) without setting \" +\n   994\u2192            s\"'${ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION_CONFIG}' to ${MetadataVersion.IBP_3_7_IV2} or higher\")\n   995\u2192        }\n   996\u2192      } else {\n   997\u2192        // controller listener names must be empty when not in KRaft mode\n   998\u2192        require(controllerListenerNames.isEmpty,\n   999\u2192          s\"${KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG} must be empty when not running in KRaft mode: ${controllerListenerNames.asJava}\")\n  1000\u2192      }\n  1001\u2192    }\n  1002\u2192\n  1003\u2192    val listenerNames = listeners.map(_.listenerName).toSet\n  1004\u2192    if (processRoles.isEmpty || processRoles.contains(ProcessRole.BrokerRole)) {\n  1005\u2192      // validations for all broker setups (i.e. ZooKeeper and KRaft broker-only and KRaft co-located)\n  1006\u2192      validateAdvertisedBrokerListenersNonEmptyForBroker()\n  1007\u2192      require(advertisedBrokerListenerNames.contains(interBrokerListenerName),\n  1008\u2192        s\"${ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG} must be a listener name defined in ${SocketServerConfigs.ADVERTISED_LISTENERS_CONFIG}. \" +\n  1009\u2192          s\"The valid options based on currently configured listeners are ${advertisedBrokerListenerNames.map(_.value).mkString(\",\")}\")\n  1010\u2192      require(advertisedBrokerListenerNames.subsetOf(listenerNames),\n  1011\u2192        s\"${SocketServerConfigs.ADVERTISED_LISTENERS_CONFIG} listener names must be equal to or a subset of the ones defined in ${SocketServerConfigs.LISTENERS_CONFIG}. \" +\n  1012\u2192          s\"Found ${advertisedBrokerListenerNames.map(_.value).mkString(\",\")}. The valid options based on the current configuration \" +\n  1013\u2192          s\"are ${listenerNames.map(_.value).mkString(\",\")}\"\n  1014\u2192      )\n  1015\u2192    }\n  1016\u2192\n  1017\u2192    require(!effectiveAdvertisedBrokerListeners.exists(endpoint => endpoint.host==\"0.0.0.0\"),\n  1018\u2192      s\"${SocketServerConfigs.ADVERTISED_LISTENERS_CONFIG} cannot use the nonroutable meta-address 0.0.0.0. \"+\n  1019\u2192      s\"Use a routable IP address.\")\n  1020\u2192\n  1021\u2192    require(!effectiveAdvertisedControllerListeners.exists(endpoint => endpoint.host==\"0.0.0.0\"),\n  1022\u2192      s\"${SocketServerConfigs.ADVERTISED_LISTENERS_CONFIG} cannot use the nonroutable meta-address 0.0.0.0. \"+\n  1023\u2192      s\"Use a routable IP address.\")\n  1024\u2192\n  1025\u2192    // validate control.plane.listener.name config\n  1026\u2192    if (controlPlaneListenerName.isDefined) {\n  1027\u2192      require(advertisedBrokerListenerNames.contains(controlPlaneListenerName.get),\n  1028\u2192        s\"${SocketServerConfigs.CONTROL_PLANE_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG} must be a listener name defined in ${SocketServerConfigs.ADVERTISED_LISTENERS_CONFIG}. \" +\n  1029\u2192        s\"The valid options based on currently configured listeners are ${advertisedBrokerListenerNames.map(_.value).mkString(\",\")}\")\n  1030\u2192      // controlPlaneListenerName should be different from interBrokerListenerName\n  1031\u2192      require(!controlPlaneListenerName.get.value().equals(interBrokerListenerName.value()),\n  1032\u2192        s\"${SocketServerConfigs.CONTROL_PLANE_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG}, when defined, should have a different value from the inter broker listener name. \" +\n  1033\u2192        s\"Currently they both have the value ${controlPlaneListenerName.get}\")\n  1034\u2192    }\n  1035\u2192\n  1036\u2192    val messageFormatVersion = new MessageFormatVersion(logMessageFormatVersionString, interBrokerProtocolVersionString)\n  1037\u2192    if (messageFormatVersion.shouldWarn)\n  1038\u2192      warn(createBrokerWarningMessage)\n  1039\u2192\n  1040\u2192    val recordVersion = logMessageFormatVersion.highestSupportedRecordVersion\n  1041\u2192    require(interBrokerProtocolVersion.highestSupportedRecordVersion().value >= recordVersion.value,\n  1042\u2192      s\"log.message.format.version $logMessageFormatVersionString can only be used when inter.broker.protocol.version \" +\n  1043\u2192      s\"is set to version ${MetadataVersion.minSupportedFor(recordVersion).shortVersion} or higher\")\n  1044\u2192\n  1045\u2192    if (groupCoordinatorConfig.offsetTopicCompressionType == CompressionType.ZSTD)\n  1046\u2192      require(interBrokerProtocolVersion.highestSupportedRecordVersion().value >= IBP_2_1_IV0.highestSupportedRecordVersion().value,\n  1047\u2192        \"offsets.topic.compression.codec zstd can only be used when inter.broker.protocol.version \" +\n  1048\u2192        s\"is set to version ${IBP_2_1_IV0.shortVersion} or higher\")\n  1049\u2192\n  1050\u2192    val interBrokerUsesSasl = interBrokerSecurityProtocol == SecurityProtocol.SASL_PLAINTEXT || interBrokerSecurityProtocol == SecurityProtocol.SASL_SSL\n  1051\u2192    require(!interBrokerUsesSasl || saslInterBrokerHandshakeRequestEnable || saslMechanismInterBrokerProtocol == SaslConfigs.GSSAPI_MECHANISM,\n  1052\u2192      s\"Only GSSAPI mechanism is supported for inter-broker communication with SASL when inter.broker.protocol.version is set to $interBrokerProtocolVersionString\")\n  1053\u2192    require(!interBrokerUsesSasl || saslEnabledMechanisms(interBrokerListenerName).contains(saslMechanismInterBrokerProtocol),\n  1054\u2192      s\"${BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_MECHANISM_INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_CONFIG} must be included in ${BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_ENABLED_MECHANISMS_CONFIG} when SASL is used for inter-broker communication\")\n  1055\u2192    require(queuedMaxBytes <= 0 || queuedMaxBytes >= socketRequestMaxBytes,\n  1056\u2192      s\"${SocketServerConfigs.QUEUED_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG} must be larger or equal to ${SocketServerConfigs.SOCKET_REQUEST_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG}\")\n  1057\u2192\n  1058\u2192    if (maxConnectionsPerIp == 0)\n  1059\u2192      require(maxConnectionsPerIpOverrides.nonEmpty, s\"${SocketServerConfigs.MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP_CONFIG} can be set to zero only if\" +\n  1060\u2192        s\" ${SocketServerConfigs.MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP_OVERRIDES_CONFIG} property is set.\")\n  1061\u2192\n  1062\u2192    val invalidAddresses = maxConnectionsPerIpOverrides.keys.filterNot(address => Utils.validHostPattern(address))\n  1063\u2192    if (invalidAddresses.nonEmpty)\n  1064\u2192      throw new IllegalArgumentException(s\"${SocketServerConfigs.MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP_OVERRIDES_CONFIG} contains invalid addresses : ${invalidAddresses.mkString(\",\")}\")\n  1065\u2192\n  1066\u2192    if (connectionsMaxIdleMs >= 0)\n  1067\u2192      require(failedAuthenticationDelayMs < connectionsMaxIdleMs,\n  1068\u2192        s\"${SocketServerConfigs.FAILED_AUTHENTICATION_DELAY_MS_CONFIG}=$failedAuthenticationDelayMs should always be less than\" +\n  1069\u2192        s\" ${SocketServerConfigs.CONNECTIONS_MAX_IDLE_MS_CONFIG}=$connectionsMaxIdleMs to prevent failed\" +\n  1070\u2192        s\" authentication responses from timing out\")\n  1071\u2192\n  1072\u2192    val principalBuilderClass = getClass(BrokerSecurityConfigs.PRINCIPAL_BUILDER_CLASS_CONFIG)\n  1073\u2192    require(principalBuilderClass != null, s\"${BrokerSecurityConfigs.PRINCIPAL_BUILDER_CLASS_CONFIG} must be non-null\")\n  1074\u2192    require(classOf[KafkaPrincipalSerde].isAssignableFrom(principalBuilderClass),\n  1075\u2192      s\"${BrokerSecurityConfigs.PRINCIPAL_BUILDER_CLASS_CONFIG} must implement KafkaPrincipalSerde\")\n  1076\u2192\n  1077\u2192\n  1078\u2192    if (originals.containsKey(GroupCoordinatorConfig.OFFSET_COMMIT_REQUIRED_ACKS_CONFIG)) {\n  1079\u2192      warn(s\"${GroupCoordinatorConfig.OFFSET_COMMIT_REQUIRED_ACKS_CONFIG} is deprecated and it will be removed in Apache Kafka 4.0.\")\n  1080\u2192    }\n  1081\u2192  }\n  1082\u2192\n  1083\u2192  /**\n  1084\u2192   * Validate some configurations for new MetadataVersion. A new MetadataVersion can take place when\n  1085\u2192   * a FeatureLevelRecord for \"metadata.version\" is read from the cluster metadata.\n  1086\u2192   */\n  1087\u2192  def validateWithMetadataVersion(metadataVersion: MetadataVersion): Unit = {\n  1088\u2192    if (processRoles.contains(ProcessRole.BrokerRole) && logDirs.size > 1) {\n  1089\u2192      require(metadataVersion.isDirectoryAssignmentSupported,\n  1090\u2192        s\"Multiple log directories (aka JBOD) are not supported in the current MetadataVersion ${metadataVersion}. \" +\n  1091\u2192          s\"Need ${MetadataVersion.IBP_3_7_IV2} or higher\")\n  1092\u2192    }\n  1093\u2192  }\n  1094\u2192\n  1095\u2192  /**\n  1096\u2192   * Copy the subset of properties that are relevant to Logs. The individual properties\n  1097\u2192   * are listed here since the names are slightly different in each Config class...\n  1098\u2192   */\n  1099\u2192  @nowarn(\"cat=deprecation\")\n  1100\u2192  def extractLogConfigMap: java.util.Map[String, Object] = {\n  1101\u2192    val logProps = new java.util.HashMap[String, Object]()\n  1102\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.SEGMENT_BYTES_CONFIG, logSegmentBytes)\n  1103\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.SEGMENT_MS_CONFIG, logRollTimeMillis)\n  1104\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.SEGMENT_JITTER_MS_CONFIG, logRollTimeJitterMillis)\n  1105\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.SEGMENT_INDEX_BYTES_CONFIG, logIndexSizeMaxBytes)\n  1106\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.FLUSH_MESSAGES_INTERVAL_CONFIG, logFlushIntervalMessages)\n  1107\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.FLUSH_MS_CONFIG, logFlushIntervalMs)\n  1108\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.RETENTION_BYTES_CONFIG, logRetentionBytes)\n  1109\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.RETENTION_MS_CONFIG, logRetentionTimeMillis: java.lang.Long)\n  1110\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.MAX_MESSAGE_BYTES_CONFIG, messageMaxBytes)\n  1111\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.INDEX_INTERVAL_BYTES_CONFIG, logIndexIntervalBytes)\n  1112\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.DELETE_RETENTION_MS_CONFIG, logCleanerDeleteRetentionMs)\n  1113\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.MIN_COMPACTION_LAG_MS_CONFIG, logCleanerMinCompactionLagMs)\n  1114\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.MAX_COMPACTION_LAG_MS_CONFIG, logCleanerMaxCompactionLagMs)\n  1115\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.FILE_DELETE_DELAY_MS_CONFIG, logDeleteDelayMs)\n  1116\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.MIN_CLEANABLE_DIRTY_RATIO_CONFIG, logCleanerMinCleanRatio)\n  1117\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.CLEANUP_POLICY_CONFIG, logCleanupPolicy)\n  1118\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.MIN_IN_SYNC_REPLICAS_CONFIG, minInSyncReplicas)\n  1119\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.COMPRESSION_TYPE_CONFIG, compressionType)\n  1120\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.COMPRESSION_GZIP_LEVEL_CONFIG, gzipCompressionLevel)\n  1121\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.COMPRESSION_LZ4_LEVEL_CONFIG, lz4CompressionLevel)\n  1122\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.COMPRESSION_ZSTD_LEVEL_CONFIG, zstdCompressionLevel)\n  1123\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.UNCLEAN_LEADER_ELECTION_ENABLE_CONFIG, uncleanLeaderElectionEnable)\n  1124\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.PREALLOCATE_CONFIG, logPreAllocateEnable)\n  1125\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_CONFIG, logMessageFormatVersion.version)\n  1126\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_TYPE_CONFIG, logMessageTimestampType.name)\n  1127\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_DIFFERENCE_MAX_MS_CONFIG, logMessageTimestampDifferenceMaxMs: java.lang.Long)\n  1128\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_BEFORE_MAX_MS_CONFIG, logMessageTimestampBeforeMaxMs: java.lang.Long)\n  1129\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_AFTER_MAX_MS_CONFIG, logMessageTimestampAfterMaxMs: java.lang.Long)\n  1130\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.MESSAGE_DOWNCONVERSION_ENABLE_CONFIG, logMessageDownConversionEnable: java.lang.Boolean)\n  1131\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.LOCAL_LOG_RETENTION_MS_CONFIG, remoteLogManagerConfig.logLocalRetentionMs: java.lang.Long)\n  1132\u2192    logProps.put(TopicConfig.LOCAL_LOG_RETENTION_BYTES_CONFIG, remoteLogManagerConfig.logLocalRetentionBytes: java.lang.Long)\n  1133\u2192    logProps\n  1134\u2192  }\n  1135\u2192\n  1136\u2192  @nowarn(\"cat=deprecation\")\n  1137\u2192  private def createBrokerWarningMessage: String = {\n  1138\u2192    s\"Broker configuration ${ServerLogConfigs.LOG_MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_CONFIG} with value $logMessageFormatVersionString is ignored \" +\n  1139\u2192      s\"because the inter-broker protocol version `$interBrokerProtocolVersionString` is greater or equal than 3.0. \" +\n  1140\u2192      \"This configuration is deprecated and it will be removed in Apache Kafka 4.0.\"\n  1141\u2192  }\n  1142\u2192}\n  1143\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
153. Glob @ -
Input
{
  "pattern": "/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/ConfigDef.java"
}
Output
"/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/ConfigDef.java"
154. Glob @ -
Input
{
  "pattern": "/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/*Config.scala"
}
Output
"/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/DynamicBrokerConfig.scala\n/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/DynamicConfig.scala\n/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala"
155. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "find /workspace/core/src/main -name \"*Config.scala\" -type f | head -20",
  "description": "Find Config-related Scala files in core"
}

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n     3\u2192 * contributor license agreements. For each configuration, you can specify\n    41\u2192 * the name, the type, the default value, the documentation, the group information, the order in the group,\n    42\u2192 * the width of the configuration value and the name suitable for display in the UI.\n    43\u2192 *\n    44\u2192 * You can provide special validation logic used for single configuration validation by overriding {@link Validator}.\n    45\u2192 *\n    46\u2192 * Moreover, you can specify the dependents of a configuration. The valid values and visibility of a configuration\n    47\u2192 * may change according to the values of other configurations. You can override {@link Recommender} to get valid\n    48\u2192 * values and set visibility of a configuration given the current configuration values.\n    49\u2192 *\n    50\u2192 * <p/>\n    51\u2192 * To use the class:\n    52\u2192 * <p/>\n    53\u2192 * <pre>\n    54\u2192 * ConfigDef defs = new ConfigDef();\n    55\u2192 *\n    56\u2192 * // check {@link #define(String, Type, Object, Importance, String)} for more details.\n    57\u2192 * defs.define(&quot;config_with_default&quot;, Type.STRING, &quot;default string value&quot;, Importance.High, &quot;Configuration with default value.&quot;);\n    58\u2192 * // check {@link #define(String, Type, Object, Validator, Importance, String)} for more details.\n    59\u2192 * defs.define(&quot;config_with_validator&quot;, Type.INT, 42, Range.atLeast(0), Importance.High, &quot;Configuration with user provided validator.&quot;);\n    60\u2192 * // check {@link #define(String, Type, Importance, String, String, int, Width, String, List) define(String, Type, Importance, String, String, int, Width, String, List&lt;String&gt;)} for more details.\n    61\u2192 * defs.define(&quot;config_with_dependents&quot;, Type.INT, Importance.LOW, &quot;Configuration with dependents.&quot;, &quot;group&quot;, 1, Width.SHORT, &quot;Config With Dependents&quot;, Arrays.asList(&quot;config_with_default&quot;,&quot;config_with_validator&quot;));\n    62\u2192 *\n    63\u2192 * Map&lt;String, String&gt; props = new HashMap&lt;&gt;();\n    64\u2192 * props.put(&quot;config_with_default&quot;, &quot;some value&quot;);\n    65\u2192 * props.put(&quot;config_with_dependents&quot;, &quot;some other value&quot;);\n    66\u2192 *\n    67\u2192 * Map&lt;String, Object&gt; configs = defs.parse(props);\n    68\u2192 * // will return &quot;some value&quot;\n    69\u2192 * String someConfig = (String) configs.get(&quot;config_with_default&quot;);\n    70\u2192 * // will return default value of 42\n    71\u2192 * int anotherConfig = (Integer) configs.get(&quot;config_with_validator&quot;);\n    72\u2192 *\n    73\u2192 * // To validate the full configuration, use:\n    74\u2192 * List&lt;ConfigValue&gt; configValues = defs.validate(props);\n    75\u2192 * // The {@link ConfigValue} contains updated configuration information given the current configuration values.\n    76\u2192 * </pre>\n    77\u2192 * <p/>\n    78\u2192 * This class can be used standalone or in combination with {@link AbstractConfig} which provides some additional\n    79\u2192 * functionality for accessing configs.\n    80\u2192 */\n    81\u2192public class ConfigDef {\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192    private static final Pattern COMMA_WITH_WHITESPACE = Pattern.compile(\"\\\\s*,\\\\s*\");\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192    /**\n    86\u2192     * A unique Java object which represents the lack of a default value.\n    87\u2192     */\n    88\u2192    public static final Object NO_DEFAULT_VALUE = new Object();\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192    private final Map<String, ConfigKey> configKeys;\n    91\u2192    private final List<String> groups;\n    92\u2192    private Set<String> configsWithNoParent;\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192    public ConfigDef() {\n    95\u2192        configKeys = new LinkedHashMap<>();\n    96\u2192        groups = new LinkedList<>();\n    97\u2192        configsWithNoParent = null;\n    98\u2192    }\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192    public ConfigDef(ConfigDef base) {\n   101\u2192        configKeys = new LinkedHashMap<>(base.configKeys);\n   102\u2192        groups = new LinkedList<>(base.groups);\n   103\u2192        // It is not safe to copy this from the parent because we may subsequently add to the set of configs and\n   104\u2192        // invalidate this\n   105\u2192        configsWithNoParent = null;\n   106\u2192    }\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192    /**\n   109\u2192     * Returns unmodifiable set of properties names defined in this {@linkplain ConfigDef}\n   110\u2192     *\n   111\u2192     * @return new unmodifiable {@link Set} instance containing the keys\n   112\u2192     */\n   113\u2192    public Set<String> names() {\n   114\u2192        return Collections.unmodifiableSet(configKeys.keySet());\n   115\u2192    }\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192    public Map<String, Object> defaultValues() {\n   118\u2192        Map<String, Object> defaultValues = new HashMap<>();\n   119\u2192        for (ConfigKey key : configKeys.values()) {\n   120\u2192            if (key.defaultValue != NO_DEFAULT_VALUE)\n   121\u2192                defaultValues.put(key.name, key.defaultValue);\n   122\u2192        }\n   123\u2192        return defaultValues;\n   124\u2192    }\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192    public ConfigDef define(ConfigKey key) {\n   127\u2192        if (configKeys.containsKey(key.name)) {\n   128\u2192            throw new ConfigException(\"Configuration \" + key.name + \" is defined twice.\");\n   129\u2192        }\n   130\u2192        if (key.group != null && !groups.contains(key.group)) {\n   131\u2192            groups.add(key.group);\n   132\u2192        }\n   133\u2192        configKeys.put(key.name, key);\n   134\u2192        return this;\n   135\u2192    }\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192    /**\n   138\u2192     * Define a new configuration\n   139\u2192     * @param name          the name of the config parameter\n   140\u2192     * @param type          the type of the config\n   141\u2192     * @param defaultValue  the default value to use if this config isn't present\n   142\u2192     * @param validator     the validator to use in checking the correctness of the config\n   143\u2192     * @param importance    the importance of this config\n   144\u2192     * @param documentation the documentation string for the config\n   145\u2192     * @param group         the group this config belongs to\n   146\u2192     * @param orderInGroup  the order of this config in the group\n   147\u2192     * @param width         the width of the config\n   148\u2192     * @param displayName   the name suitable for display\n   149\u2192     * @param dependents    the configurations that are dependents of this configuration\n   150\u2192     * @param recommender   the recommender provides valid values given the parent configuration values\n   151\u2192     * @return This ConfigDef so you can chain calls\n   152\u2192     */\n   153\u2192    public ConfigDef define(String name, Type type, Object defaultValue, Validator validator, Importance importance, String documentation,\n   154\u2192                            String group, int orderInGroup, Width width, String displayName, List<String> dependents, Recommender recommender) {\n   155\u2192        return define(new ConfigKey(name, type, defaultValue, validator, importance, documentation, group, orderInGroup, width, displayName, dependents, recommender, false, null));\n   156\u2192    }\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192    /**\n   159\u2192     * Define a new configuration\n   160\u2192     * @param name               the name of the config parameter\n   161\u2192     * @param type               the type of the config\n   162\u2192     * @param defaultValue       the default value to use if this config isn't present\n   163\u2192     * @param validator          the validator to use in checking the correctness of the config\n   164\u2192     * @param importance         the importance of this config\n   165\u2192     * @param documentation      the documentation string for the config\n   166\u2192     * @param group              the group this config belongs to\n   167\u2192     * @param orderInGroup       the order of this config in the group\n   168\u2192     * @param width              the width of the config\n   169\u2192     * @param displayName        the name suitable for display\n   170\u2192     * @param dependents         the configurations that are dependents of this configuration\n   171\u2192     * @param recommender        the recommender provides valid values given the parent configuration values\n   172\u2192     * @param alternativeString  the string which will be used to override the string of defaultValue\n   173\u2192     * @return This ConfigDef so you can chain calls\n   174\u2192     */\n   175\u2192    public ConfigDef define(String name, Type type, Object defaultValue, Validator validator, Importance importance, String documentation,\n   176\u2192                            String group, int orderInGroup, Width width, String displayName, List<String> dependents, Recommender recommender,\n   177\u2192                            String alternativeString) {\n   178\u2192        return define(new ConfigKey(name, type, defaultValue, validator, importance, documentation, group, orderInGroup, width, displayName, dependents, recommender, false, alternativeString));\n   179\u2192    }\n   180\u2192\n   181\u2192    /**\n   182\u2192     * Define a new configuration with no custom recommender\n   183\u2192     * @param name          the name of the config parameter\n   184\u2192     * @param type          the type of the config\n   185\u2192     * @param defaultValue  the default value to use if this config isn't present\n   186\u2192     * @param validator     the validator to use in checking the correctness of the config\n   187\u2192     * @param importance    the importance of this config\n   188\u2192     * @param documentation the documentation string for the config\n   189\u2192     * @param group         the group this config belongs to\n   190\u2192     * @param orderInGroup  the order of this config in the group\n   191\u2192     * @param width         the width of the config\n   192\u2192     * @param displayName   the name suitable for display\n   193\u2192     * @param dependents    the configurations that are dependents of this configuration\n   194\u2192     * @return This ConfigDef so you can chain calls\n   195\u2192     */\n   196\u2192    public ConfigDef define(String name, Type type, Object defaultValue, Validator validator, Importance importance, String documentation,\n   197\u2192                            String group, int orderInGroup, Width width, String displayName, List<String> dependents) {\n   198\u2192        return define(name, type, defaultValue, validator, importance, documentation, group, orderInGroup, width, displayName, dependents, null);\n   199\u2192    }\n   200\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. "     1\u2192/**\n     2\u2192  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n     3\u2192  * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with\n     4\u2192  * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n     5\u2192  * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n     6\u2192  * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n     7\u2192  * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     8\u2192  *\n     9\u2192  * http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n    10\u2192  *\n    11\u2192  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    12\u2192  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    13\u2192  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    14\u2192  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    15\u2192  * limitations under the License.\n    16\u2192  */\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192package kafka.server\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192import java.util\n    21\u2192import java.util.{Collections, Properties}\n    22\u2192import java.util.concurrent.CopyOnWriteArrayList\n    23\u2192import java.util.concurrent.locks.ReentrantReadWriteLock\n    24\u2192import kafka.cluster.EndPoint\n    25\u2192import kafka.log.{LogCleaner, LogManager}\n    26\u2192import kafka.network.{DataPlaneAcceptor, SocketServer}\n    27\u2192import kafka.server.DynamicBrokerConfig._\n    28\u2192import kafka.utils.{CoreUtils, Logging}\n    29\u2192import kafka.utils.Implicits._\n    30\u2192import kafka.zk.{AdminZkClient, KafkaZkClient}\n    31\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.Reconfigurable\n    32\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.config.internals.BrokerSecurityConfigs\n    33\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.config.{AbstractConfig, ConfigDef, ConfigException, SaslConfigs, SslConfigs, TopicConfig}\n    34\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.metrics.{JmxReporter, Metrics, MetricsReporter}\n    35\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.config.types.Password\n    36\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.network.{ListenerName, ListenerReconfigurable}\n    37\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.security.authenticator.LoginManager\n    38\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.{ConfigUtils, Utils}\n    39\u2192import org.apache.kafka.coordinator.transaction.TransactionLogConfigs\n    40\u2192import org.apache.kafka.network.SocketServerConfigs\n    41\u2192import org.apache.kafka.security.PasswordEncoder\n    42\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.ProcessRole\n    43\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.config.{ConfigType, ReplicationConfigs, ServerConfigs, ServerLogConfigs, ServerTopicConfigSynonyms, ZooKeeperInternals}\n    44\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.log.remote.storage.RemoteLogManagerConfig\n    45\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.metrics.{ClientMetricsReceiverPlugin, MetricConfigs}\n    46\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.telemetry.ClientTelemetry\n    47\u2192import org.apache.kafka.storage.internals.log.{LogConfig, ProducerStateManagerConfig}\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192import scala.annotation.nowarn\n    50\u2192import scala.collection._\n    51\u2192import scala.jdk.CollectionConverters._\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192/**\n    54\u2192  * Dynamic broker configurations are stored in ZooKeeper and may be defined at two levels:\n    55\u2192  * <ul>\n    56\u2192  *   <li>Per-broker configs persisted at <tt>/configs/brokers/{brokerId}</tt>: These can be described/altered\n    57\u2192  *       using AdminClient using the resource name brokerId.</li>\n    58\u2192  *   <li>Cluster-wide defaults persisted at <tt>/configs/brokers/&lt;default&gt;</tt>: These can be described/altered\n    59\u2192  *       using AdminClient using an empty resource name.</li>\n    60\u2192  * </ul>\n    61\u2192  * The order of precedence for broker configs is:\n    62\u2192  * <ol>\n    63\u2192  *   <li>DYNAMIC_BROKER_CONFIG: stored in ZK at /configs/brokers/{brokerId}</li>\n    64\u2192  *   <li>DYNAMIC_DEFAULT_BROKER_CONFIG: stored in ZK at /configs/brokers/&lt;default&gt;</li>\n    65\u2192  *   <li>STATIC_BROKER_CONFIG: properties that broker is started up with, typically from server.properties file</li>\n    66\u2192  *   <li>DEFAULT_CONFIG: Default configs defined in KafkaConfig</li>\n    67\u2192  * </ol>\n    68\u2192  * Log configs use topic config overrides if defined and fallback to broker defaults using the order of precedence above.\n    69\u2192  * Topic config overrides may use a different config name from the default broker config.\n    70\u2192  * See [[org.apache.kafka.storage.internals.log.LogConfig#TopicConfigSynonyms]] for the mapping.\n    71\u2192  * <p>\n    72\u2192  * AdminClient returns all config synonyms in the order of precedence when configs are described with\n    73\u2192  * <code>includeSynonyms</code>. In addition to configs that may be defined with the same name at different levels,\n    74\u2192  * some configs have additional synonyms.\n    75\u2192  * </p>\n    76\u2192  * <ul>\n    77\u2192  *   <li>Listener configs may be defined using the prefix <tt>listener.name.{listenerName}.{configName}</tt>. These may be\n    78\u2192  *       configured as dynamic or static broker configs. Listener configs have higher precedence than the base configs\n    79\u2192  *       that don't specify the listener name. Listeners without a listener config use the base config. Base configs\n    80\u2192  *       may be defined only as STATIC_BROKER_CONFIG or DEFAULT_CONFIG and cannot be updated dynamically.<li>\n    81\u2192  *   <li>Some configs may be defined using multiple properties. For example, <tt>log.roll.ms</tt> and\n    82\u2192  *       <tt>log.roll.hours</tt> refer to the same config that may be defined in milliseconds or hours. The order of\n    83\u2192  *       precedence of these synonyms is described in the docs of these configs in [[kafka.server.KafkaConfig]].</li>\n    84\u2192  * </ul>\n    85\u2192  *\n    86\u2192  */\n    87\u2192object DynamicBrokerConfig {\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192  private[server] val DynamicSecurityConfigs = SslConfigs.RECONFIGURABLE_CONFIGS.asScala\n    90\u2192  private[server] val DynamicProducerStateManagerConfig = Set(TransactionLogConfigs.PRODUCER_ID_EXPIRATION_MS_CONFIG, TransactionLogConfigs.TRANSACTION_PARTITION_VERIFICATION_ENABLE_CONFIG)\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192  val AllDynamicConfigs = DynamicSecurityConfigs ++\n    93\u2192    LogCleaner.ReconfigurableConfigs ++\n    94\u2192    DynamicLogConfig.ReconfigurableConfigs ++\n    95\u2192    DynamicThreadPool.ReconfigurableConfigs ++\n    96\u2192    Set(MetricConfigs.METRIC_REPORTER_CLASSES_CONFIG) ++\n    97\u2192    DynamicListenerConfig.ReconfigurableConfigs ++\n    98\u2192    SocketServer.ReconfigurableConfigs ++\n    99\u2192    DynamicProducerStateManagerConfig ++\n   100\u2192    DynamicRemoteLogConfig.ReconfigurableConfigs\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192  private val ClusterLevelListenerConfigs = Set(SocketServerConfigs.MAX_CONNECTIONS_CONFIG, SocketServerConfigs.MAX_CONNECTION_CREATION_RATE_CONFIG, SocketServerConfigs.NUM_NETWORK_THREADS_CONFIG)\n   103\u2192  private val PerBrokerConfigs = (DynamicSecurityConfigs ++ DynamicListenerConfig.ReconfigurableConfigs).diff(\n   104\u2192    ClusterLevelListenerConfigs)\n   105\u2192  private val ListenerMechanismConfigs = Set(SaslConfigs.SASL_JAAS_CONFIG,\n   106\u2192    SaslConfigs.SASL_LOGIN_CALLBACK_HANDLER_CLASS,\n   107\u2192    SaslConfigs.SASL_LOGIN_CLASS,\n   108\u2192    BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_SERVER_CALLBACK_HANDLER_CLASS_CONFIG,\n   109\u2192    BrokerSecurityConfigs.CONNECTIONS_MAX_REAUTH_MS_CONFIG)\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192  private val ReloadableFileConfigs = Set(SslConfigs.SSL_KEYSTORE_LOCATION_CONFIG, SslConfigs.SSL_TRUSTSTORE_LOCATION_CONFIG)\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192  private val ListenerConfigRegex = \"\"\"listener\\.name\\.[^.]*\\.(.*)\"\"\".r\n   114\u2192\n   115\u2192  private val DynamicPasswordConfigs = {\n   116\u2192    val passwordConfigs = KafkaConfig.configKeys.filter(_._2.`type` == ConfigDef.Type.PASSWORD).keySet\n   117\u2192    AllDynamicConfigs.intersect(passwordConfigs)\n   118\u2192  }\n   119\u2192\n   120\u2192  def isPasswordConfig(name: String): Boolean = DynamicBrokerConfig.DynamicPasswordConfigs.exists(name.endsWith)\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192  def brokerConfigSynonyms(name: String, matchListenerOverride: Boolean): List[String] = {\n   123\u2192    name match {\n   124\u2192      case ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG | ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG =>\n   125\u2192        List(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG, ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG)\n   126\u2192      case ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_JITTER_MILLIS_CONFIG | ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_JITTER_HOURS_CONFIG =>\n   127\u2192        List(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_JITTER_MILLIS_CONFIG, ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_JITTER_HOURS_CONFIG)\n   128\u2192      case ServerLogConfigs.LOG_FLUSH_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG => // KafkaLogConfigs.LOG_FLUSH_SCHEDULER_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG is used as default\n   129\u2192        List(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_FLUSH_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG, ServerLogConfigs.LOG_FLUSH_SCHEDULER_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG)\n   130\u2192      case ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG | ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MINUTES_CONFIG | ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG =>\n   131\u2192        List(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG, ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MINUTES_CONFIG, ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG)\n   132\u2192      case ListenerConfigRegex(baseName) if matchListenerOverride =>\n   133\u2192        // `ListenerMechanismConfigs` are specified as listenerPrefix.mechanism.<configName>\n   134\u2192        // and other listener configs are specified as listenerPrefix.<configName>\n   135\u2192        // Add <configName> as a synonym in both cases.\n   136\u2192        val mechanismConfig = ListenerMechanismConfigs.find(baseName.endsWith)\n   137\u2192        List(name, mechanismConfig.getOrElse(baseName))\n   138\u2192      case _ => List(name)\n   139\u2192    }\n   140\u2192  }\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192  def validateConfigs(props: Properties, perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Unit = {\n   143\u2192    def checkInvalidProps(invalidPropNames: Set[String], errorMessage: String): Unit = {\n   144\u2192      if (invalidPropNames.nonEmpty)\n   145\u2192        throw new ConfigException(s\"$errorMessage: $invalidPropNames\")\n   146\u2192    }\n   147\u2192    checkInvalidProps(nonDynamicConfigs(props), \"Cannot update these configs dynamically\")\n   148\u2192    checkInvalidProps(securityConfigsWithoutListenerPrefix(props),\n   149\u2192      \"These security configs can be dynamically updated only per-listener using the listener prefix\")\n   150\u2192    validateConfigTypes(props)\n   151\u2192    if (!perBrokerConfig) {\n   152\u2192      checkInvalidProps(perBrokerConfigs(props),\n   153\u2192        \"Cannot update these configs at default cluster level, broker id must be specified\")\n   154\u2192    }\n   155\u2192  }\n   156\u2192\n   157\u2192  private def perBrokerConfigs(props: Properties): Set[String] = {\n   158\u2192    val configNames = props.asScala.keySet\n   159\u2192    def perBrokerListenerConfig(name: String): Boolean = {\n   160\u2192      name match {\n   161\u2192        case ListenerConfigRegex(baseName) => !ClusterLevelListenerConfigs.contains(baseName)\n   162\u2192        case _ => false\n   163\u2192      }\n   164\u2192    }\n   165\u2192    configNames.intersect(PerBrokerConfigs) ++ configNames.filter(perBrokerListenerConfig)\n   166\u2192  }\n   167\u2192\n   168\u2192  private def nonDynamicConfigs(props: Properties): Set[String] = {\n   169\u2192    props.asScala.keySet.intersect(DynamicConfig.Broker.nonDynamicProps)\n   170\u2192  }\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192  private def securityConfigsWithoutListenerPrefix(props: Properties): Set[String] = {\n   173\u2192    DynamicSecurityConfigs.filter(props.containsKey)\n   174\u2192  }\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192  private def validateConfigTypes(props: Properties): Unit = {\n   177\u2192    val baseProps = new Properties\n   178\u2192    props.asScala.foreach {\n   179\u2192      case (ListenerConfigRegex(baseName), v) => baseProps.put(baseName, v)\n   180\u2192      case (k, v) => baseProps.put(k, v)\n   181\u2192    }\n   182\u2192    DynamicConfig.Broker.validate(baseProps)\n   183\u2192  }\n   184\u2192\n   185\u2192  private[server] def dynamicConfigUpdateModes: util.Map[String, String] = {\n   186\u2192    AllDynamicConfigs.map { name =>\n   187\u2192      val mode = if (PerBrokerConfigs.contains(name)) \"per-broker\" else \"cluster-wide\"\n   188\u2192      name -> mode\n   189\u2192    }.toMap.asJava\n   190\u2192  }\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192  private[server] def resolveVariableConfigs(propsOriginal: Properties): Properties = {\n   193\u2192    val props = new Properties\n   194\u2192    val config = new AbstractConfig(new ConfigDef(), propsOriginal, Utils.castToStringObjectMap(propsOriginal), false)\n   195\u2192    config.originals.forEach { (key, value) =>\n   196\u2192      if (!key.startsWith(AbstractConfig.CONFIG_PROVIDERS_CONFIG)) {\n   197\u2192        props.put(key, value)\n   198\u2192      }\n   199\u2192    }\n   200\u2192    props\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

Input

  "file_path": "/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/DynamicConfig.scala",
