}
Output
"     1\u2192/**\n     2\u2192  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n     3\u2192  * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with\n     4\u2192  * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n     5\u2192  * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n     6\u2192  * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n     7\u2192  * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     8\u2192  *\n     9\u2192  * http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n    10\u2192  *\n    11\u2192  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    12\u2192  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    13\u2192  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    14\u2192  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    15\u2192  * limitations under the License.\n    16\u2192  */\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192package kafka.server\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192import kafka.server.DynamicBrokerConfig.AllDynamicConfigs\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192import java.net.{InetAddress, UnknownHostException}\n    23\u2192import java.util.Properties\n    24\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef\n    25\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.config.{QuotaConfigs, ZooKeeperInternals}\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192import java.util\n    28\u2192import scala.jdk.CollectionConverters._\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192/**\n    31\u2192  * Class used to hold dynamic configs. These are configs which have no physical manifestation in the server.properties\n    32\u2192  * and can only be set dynamically.\n    33\u2192  */\n    34\u2192object DynamicConfig {\n    35\u2192    object Broker {\n    36\u2192      private val brokerConfigs = {\n    37\u2192        val configs = QuotaConfigs.brokerQuotaConfigs()\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192        // Filter and define all dynamic configurations\n    40\u2192        KafkaConfig.configKeys\n    41\u2192          .filter { case (configName, _) => AllDynamicConfigs.contains(configName) }\n    42\u2192          .foreach { case (_, config) => configs.define(config) }\n    43\u2192        configs\n    44\u2192      }\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192    // In order to avoid circular reference, all DynamicBrokerConfig's variables which are initialized by `DynamicConfig.Broker` should be moved to `DynamicConfig.Broker`.\n    47\u2192    // Otherwise, those variables of DynamicBrokerConfig will see intermediate state of `DynamicConfig.Broker`, because `brokerConfigs` is created by `DynamicBrokerConfig.AllDynamicConfigs`\n    48\u2192    val nonDynamicProps: Set[String] = KafkaConfig.configNames.toSet -- brokerConfigs.names.asScala\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192    def configKeys: util.Map[String, ConfigDef.ConfigKey] = brokerConfigs.configKeys\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192    def names: util.Set[String] = brokerConfigs.names\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192    def validate(props: Properties): util.Map[String, AnyRef] = DynamicConfig.validate(brokerConfigs, props, customPropsAllowed = true)\n    55\u2192  }\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192  object Client {\n    58\u2192    private val clientConfigs = QuotaConfigs.userAndClientQuotaConfigs()\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192    def configKeys: util.Map[String, ConfigDef.ConfigKey] = clientConfigs.configKeys\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192    def names: util.Set[String] = clientConfigs.names\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192    def validate(props: Properties): util.Map[String, AnyRef] = DynamicConfig.validate(clientConfigs, props, customPropsAllowed = false)\n    65\u2192  }\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192  object User {\n    68\u2192    private val userConfigs = QuotaConfigs.scramMechanismsPlusUserAndClientQuotaConfigs()\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192    def configKeys: util.Map[String, ConfigDef.ConfigKey] = userConfigs.configKeys\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192    def names: util.Set[String] = userConfigs.names\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192    def validate(props: Properties): util.Map[String, AnyRef] = DynamicConfig.validate(userConfigs, props, customPropsAllowed = false)\n    75\u2192  }\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192  object Ip {\n    78\u2192    private val ipConfigs = QuotaConfigs.ipConfigs()\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192    def configKeys: util.Map[String, ConfigDef.ConfigKey] = ipConfigs.configKeys\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192    def names: util.Set[String] = ipConfigs.names\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192    def validate(props: Properties): util.Map[String, AnyRef] = DynamicConfig.validate(ipConfigs, props, customPropsAllowed = false)\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192    def isValidIpEntity(ip: String): Boolean = {\n    87\u2192      if (ip != ZooKeeperInternals.DEFAULT_STRING) {\n    88\u2192        try {\n    89\u2192          InetAddress.getByName(ip)\n    90\u2192        } catch {\n    91\u2192          case _: UnknownHostException => return false\n    92\u2192        }\n    93\u2192      }\n    94\u2192      true\n    95\u2192    }\n    96\u2192  }\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192  object ClientMetrics {\n    99\u2192    private val clientConfigs = org.apache.kafka.server.metrics.ClientMetricsConfigs.configDef()\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192    def names: util.Set[String] = clientConfigs.names\n   102\u2192  }\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192  private def validate(configDef: ConfigDef, props: Properties, customPropsAllowed: Boolean) = {\n   105\u2192    // Validate Names\n   106\u2192    val names = configDef.names()\n   107\u2192    val propKeys = props.keySet.asScala.map(_.asInstanceOf[String])\n   108\u2192    if (!customPropsAllowed) {\n   109\u2192      val unknownKeys = propKeys.filterNot(names.contains(_))\n   110\u2192      require(unknownKeys.isEmpty, s\"Unknown Dynamic Configuration: $unknownKeys.\")\n   111\u2192    }\n   112\u2192    val propResolved = DynamicBrokerConfig.resolveVariableConfigs(props)\n   113\u2192    // ValidateValues\n   114\u2192    configDef.parse(propResolved)\n   115\u2192  }\n   116\u2192}\n   117\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

documentation string for the config\n   422\u2192     * @param alternativeString The string which will be used to override the string of defaultValue\n   423\u2192     * @return This ConfigDef so you can chain calls\n   424\u2192     */\n   425\u2192    public ConfigDef define(String name, Type type, Object defaultValue, Importance importance, String documentation, String alternativeString) {\n   426\u2192        return define(name, type, defaultValue, null, importance, documentation, null, -1, Width.NONE,\n   427\u2192                name, Collections.emptyList(), null, alternativeString);\n   428\u2192    }\n   429\u2192\n   430\u2192    /**\n   431\u2192     * Define a new configuration with no default value and no special validation logic\n   432\u2192     * @param name          The name of the config parameter\n   433\u2192     * @param type          The type of the config\n   434\u2192     * @param importance    The importance of this config: is this something you will likely need to change.\n   435\u2192     * @param documentation The documentation string for the config\n   436\u2192     * @return This ConfigDef so you can chain calls\n   437\u2192     */\n   438\u2192    public ConfigDef define(String name, Type type, Importance importance, String documentation) {\n   439\u2192        return define(name, type, NO_DEFAULT_VALUE, null, importance, documentation);\n   440\u2192    }\n   441\u2192\n   442\u2192    /**\n   443\u2192     * Define a new internal configuration. Internal configuration won't show up in the docs and aren't\n   444\u2192     * intended for general use.\n   445\u2192     * @param name              The name of the config parameter\n   446\u2192     * @param type              The type of the config\n   447\u2192     * @param defaultValue      The default value to use if this config isn't present\n   448\u2192     * @param importance        The importance of this config (i.e. is this something you will likely need to change?)\n   449\u2192     * @return This ConfigDef so you can chain calls\n   450\u2192     */\n   451\u2192    public ConfigDef defineInternal(final String name, final Type type, final Object defaultValue, final Importance importance) {\n   452\u2192        return define(new ConfigKey(name, type, defaultValue, null, importance, \"\", \"\", -1, Width.NONE, name, Collections.emptyList(), null, true, null));\n   453\u2192    }\n   454\u2192\n   455\u2192    /**\n   456\u2192     * Define a new internal configuration. Internal configuration won't show up in the docs and aren't\n   457\u2192     * intended for general use.\n   458\u2192     * @param name              The name of the config parameter\n   459\u2192 validator to use in checking the correctness of the config\n   462\u2192     * @param importance        The importance of this config (i.e. is this something you will likely need to change?)\n   463\u2192     * @param documentation     The documentation string for the config\n   464\u2192     * @return This ConfigDef so you can chain calls\n   465\u2192     */\n   466\u2192    public ConfigDef defineInternal(final String name, final Type type, final Object defaultValue, final Validator validator, final Importance importance, final String documentation) {\n   467\u2192        return define(new ConfigKey(name, type, defaultValue, validator, importance, documentation, \"\", -1, Width.NONE, name, Collections.emptyList(), null, true, null));\n   468\u2192    }\n   469\u2192\n configKeys() {\n   475\u2192        return configKeys;\n   476\u2192    }\n   477\u2192\n   478\u2192    /**\n   479\u2192     * Get the groups for the configuration\n   480\u2192     * @return a list of group names\n   481\u2192     */\n   482\u2192    public List<String> groups() {\n   483\u2192        return groups;\n   484\u2192    }\n   485\u2192\n   486\u2192    /**\n   487\u2192     * Add java.util.stream.Collectors;\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192/**\n    42\u2192 * A convenient base class for configurations to extend.\n    43\u2192 * <p>\n    44\u2192 * This class holds both the original configuration that was provided as well as the parsed\n    45\u2192 */\n    46\u2192public class AbstractConfig {\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192    private final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(getClass());\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192    /**\n    51\u2192     * Configs for which values have been requested, used to detect unused configs.\n    52\u2192     * This set must be concurrent modifiable and iterable. It will be modified\n    53\u2192     * when directly accessed or as a result of RecordingMap access.\n    54\u2192     */\n    55\u2192    private final Set<String> used = ConcurrentHashMap.newKeySet();\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192    /* the original values passed in by the user */\n    58\u2192    private final Map<String, ?> originals;\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192    /* the parsed values */\n    61\u2192    private final Map<String, Object> values;\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192    private final ConfigDef definition;\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192    public static final String AUTOMATIC_CONFIG_PROVIDERS_PROPERTY = \"org.apache.kafka.automatic.config.providers\";\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192    public static final String CONFIG_PROVIDERS_CONFIG = \"config.providers\";\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192    private static final String CONFIG_PROVIDERS_PARAM = \".param.\";\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192    /**\n    72\u2192     * Construct a configuration with a ConfigDef and the configuration properties, which can include properties\n    73\u2192     * for zero or more {@link ConfigProvider} that will be used to resolve variables in configuration property\n    74\u2192     * values.\n    75\u2192     * <p>\n    76\u2192     * The originals is a name-value pair configuration properties and optional config provider configs. The\n    77\u2192     * value of the configuration can be a variable as defined below or the actual value. This constructor will\n    78\u2192     * first instantiate the ConfigProviders using the config provider configs, then it will find all the\n    79\u2192     * variables in the values of the originals configurations, attempt to resolve the variables using the named\n    80\u2192     * ConfigProviders, and then parse and validate the configurations.\n    81\u2192     * <p>\n    82\u2192     * ConfigProvider configs can be passed either as configs in the originals map or in the separate\n    83\u2192     * configProviderProps map. If config providers properties are passed in the configProviderProps any config\n    84\u2192     * provider properties in originals map will be ignored. If ConfigProvider properties are not provided, the\n    85\u2192     * constructor will skip the variable substitution step and will simply validate and parse the supplied\n    86\u2192     * configuration.\n    87\u2192     * <p>\n    88\u2192     * The \"{@code config.providers}\" configuration property and all configuration properties that begin with the\n    89\u2192     * \"{@code config.providers.}\" prefix are reserved. The \"{@code config.providers}\" configuration property\n    90\u2192     * specifies the names of the config providers, and properties that begin with the \"{@code config.providers..}\"\n    91\u2192     * prefix correspond to the properties for that named provider. For example, the \"{@code config.providers..class}\"\n    92\u2192     * property specifies the name of the {@link ConfigProvider} implementation class that should be used for\n    93\u2192     * the provider.\n    94\u2192     * <p>\n    95\u2192     * The keys for ConfigProvider configs in both originals and configProviderProps will start with the above\n    96\u2192     * mentioned \"{@code config.providers.}\" prefix.\n    97\u2192     * <p>\n    98\u2192     * Variables have the form \"${providerName:[path:]key}\", where \"providerName\" is the name of a ConfigProvider,\n    99\u2192     * \"path\" is an optional string, and \"key\" is a required string. This variable is resolved by passing the \"key\"\n   100\u2192     * and optional \"path\" to a ConfigProvider with the specified name, and the result from the ConfigProvider is\n   101\u2192     * then used in place of the variable. Variables that cannot be resolved by the AbstractConfig constructor will\n   102\u2192     * be left unchanged in the configuration.\n   103\u2192     *\n   104\u2192     * @param definition          the definition of the configurations; may not be null\n   105\u2192     * @param originals           the configuration properties plus any optional config provider properties;\n   106\u2192     * @param configProviderProps the map of properties of config providers which will be instantiated by\n   107\u2192     *                            the constructor to resolve any variables in {@code originals}; may be null or empty\n   108\u2192     * @param doLog               whether the configurations should be logged\n   109\u2192     */\n   110\u2192    @SuppressWarnings({\"this-escape\"})\n   111\u2192    public AbstractConfig(ConfigDef definition, Map<?, ?> originals, Map<String, ?> configProviderProps, boolean doLog) {\n   112\u2192        Map<String, Object> originalMap = Utils.castToStringObjectMap(originals);\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192        this.originals = resolveConfigVariables(configProviderProps, originalMap);\n   115\u2192        this.values = definition.parse(this.originals);\n   116\u2192        Map<String, Object> configUpdates = postProcessParsedConfig(Collections.unmodifiableMap(this.values));\n   117\u2192        this.values.putAll(configUpdates);\n   118\u2192        definition.parse(this.values);\n   119\u2192        this.definition = definition;\n   120\u2192        if (doLog)\n   121\u2192            logAll();\n   122\u2192    }\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192    /**\n   125\u2192     * Construct a configuration with a ConfigDef and the configuration properties,\n   126\u2192     * which can include properties for zero or more {@link ConfigProvider}\n   127\u2192     * that will be used to resolve variables in configuration property values.\n   128\u2192     *\n   129\u2192     * @param definition the definition of the configurations; may not be null\n   130\u2192     * @param originals  the configuration properties plus any optional config provider properties; may not be null\n   131\u2192     */\n   132\u2192    public AbstractConfig(ConfigDef definition, Map<?, ?> originals) {\n   133\u2192        this(definition, originals, Collections.emptyMap(), true);\n   134\u2192    }\n   135\u2192\n   136\u2192    /**\n   137\u2192     * Construct a configuration with a ConfigDef and the configuration properties,\n   138\u2192     * which can include properties for zero or more {@link ConfigProvider}\n   139\u2192     * that will be used to resolve variables in configuration property values.\n   140\u2192     *\n   141\u2192     * @param definition the definition of the configurations; may not be null\n   142\u2192     * @param originals  the configuration properties plus any optional config provider properties; may not be null\n   143\u2192     * @param doLog      whether the configurations should be logged\n   144\u2192     */\n   145\u2192    public AbstractConfig(ConfigDef definition, Map<?, ?> originals, boolean doLog) {\n   146\u2192        this(definition, originals, Collections.emptyMap(), doLog);\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192    }\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192    /**\n   151\u2192     * Called directly after user configs got parsed (and thus default values got set).\n   152\u2192     * This allows to change default values for \"secondary defaults\" if required.\n   153\u2192     *\n   154\u2192     * @param parsedValues unmodifiable map of current configuration\n   155\u2192     * @return a map of updates that should be applied to the configuration (will be validated to prevent bad updates)\n   156\u2192     */\n   157\u2192    protected Map<String, Object> postProcessParsedConfig(Map<String, Object> parsedValues) {\n   158\u2192        return Collections.emptyMap();\n   159\u2192    }\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192    protected Object get(String key) {\n   162\u2192        if (!values.containsKey(key))\n   163\u2192            throw new ConfigException(String.format(\"Unknown configuration '%s'\", key));\n   164\u2192        used.add(key);\n   165\u2192        return values.get(key);\n   166\u2192    }\n   167\u2192\n   168\u2192    public void ignore(String key) {\n   169\u2192        used.add(key);\n   170\u2192    }\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192    public Short getShort(String key) {\n   173\u2192        return (Short) get(key);\n   174\u2192    }\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192    public Integer getInt(String key) {\n   177\u2192        return (Integer) get(key);\n   178\u2192    }\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192    public Long getLong(String key) {\n   181\u2192        return (Long) get(key);\n   182\u2192    }\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192    public Double getDouble(String key) {\n   185\u2192        return (Double) get(key);\n   186\u2192    }\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n   189\u2192    public List<String> getList(String key) {\n   190\u2192        return (List<String>) get(key);\n   191\u2192    }\n   192\u2192\n   193\u2192    public Boolean getBoolean(String key) {\n   194\u2192        return (Boolean) get(key);\n   195\u2192    }\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192    public String getString(String key) {\n   198\u2192        final String res = (String) get(key);\n   199\u2192        return res == null ? res : res.trim();\n   200\u2192    }\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. {
  "file_path": "/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/ConfigDef.java",

Output
"   500\u2192        SaslConfigs.addClientSaslSupport(this);\n   501\u2192        return this;\n   502\u2192    }\n   503\u2192\n   504\u2192    /**\n   505\u2192     * Parse and validate configs against this configuration definition. The input is a map of configs. It is expected\n   506\u2192     * that the keys of the map are strings, but the values can either be strings or they may already be of the\n   507\u2192     * appropriate type (int, string, etc). This will work equally well with either java.util.Properties instances or a\n   508\u2192     * programmatically constructed map.\n   509\u2192     *\n   510\u2192     * @param props The configs to parse and validate.\n   511\u2192     * @return Parsed and validated configs. The key will be the config name and the value will be the value parsed into\n   512\u2192     * the appropriate type (int, string, etc).\n   513\u2192     */\n   514\u2192    public Map<String, Object> parse(Map<?, ?> props) {\n   515\u2192        // Check all configurations are defined\n   516\u2192        List<String> undefinedConfigKeys = undefinedDependentConfigs();\n   517\u2192        if (!undefinedConfigKeys.isEmpty()) {\n   518\u2192            String joined = undefinedConfigKeys.stream().map(String::toString).collect(Collectors.joining(\",\"));\n   519\u2192            throw new ConfigException(\"Some configurations in are referred in the dependents, but not defined: \" + joined);\n   520\u2192        }\n   521\u2192        // parse all known keys\n   522\u2192        Map<String, Object> values = new HashMap<>();\n   523\u2192        for (ConfigKey key : configKeys.values())\n   524\u2192            values.put(key.name, parseValue(key, props.get(key.name), props.containsKey(key.name)));\n   525\u2192        return values;\n   526\u2192    }\n   527\u2192\n   528\u2192    Object parseValue(ConfigKey key, Object value, boolean isSet) {\n   529\u2192        Object parsedValue;\n   530\u2192        if (isSet) {\n   531\u2192            parsedValue = parseType(key.name, value, key.type);\n   532\u2192        // props map doesn't contain setting, the key is required because no default value specified - its an error\n   533\u2192        } else if (NO_DEFAULT_VALUE.equals(key.defaultValue)) {\n   534\u2192            throw new ConfigException(\"Missing required configuration \\\"\" + key.name + \"\\\" which has no default value.\");\n   535\u2192        } else {\n   536\u2192            // otherwise assign setting its default value\n   537\u2192            parsedValue = key.defaultValue;\n   538\u2192        }\n   539\u2192        if (key.validator != null) {\n   540\u2192            key.validator.ensureValid(key.name, parsedValue);\n   541\u2192        }\n   542\u2192        return parsedValue;\n   543\u2192    }\n   544\u2192\n   545\u2192    /**\n   546\u2192     * Validate the current configuration values with the configuration definition.\n   547\u2192     * @param props the current configuration values\n   548\u2192     * @return List of Config, each Config contains the updated configuration information given\n   549\u2192     * the current configuration values.\n   550\u2192     */\n   551\u2192    public List<ConfigValue> validate(Map<String, String> props) {\n   552\u2192        return new ArrayList<>(validateAll(props).values());\n   553\u2192    }\n   554\u2192\n   555\u2192    public Map<String, ConfigValue> validateAll(Map<String, String> props) {\n   556\u2192        Map<String, ConfigValue> configValues = new HashMap<>();\n   557\u2192        for (String name: configKeys.keySet()) {\n   558\u2192            configValues.put(name, new ConfigValue(name));\n   559\u2192        }\n   560\u2192\n   561\u2192        List<String> undefinedConfigKeys = undefinedDependentConfigs();\n   562\u2192        for (String undefinedConfigKey: undefinedConfigKeys) {\n   563\u2192            ConfigValue undefinedConfigValue = new ConfigValue(undefinedConfigKey);\n   564\u2192            undefinedConfigValue.addErrorMessage(undefinedConfigKey + \" is referred in the dependents, but not defined.\");\n   565\u2192            undefinedConfigValue.visible(false);\n   566\u2192            configValues.put(undefinedConfigKey, undefinedConfigValue);\n   567\u2192        }\n   568\u2192\n   569\u2192        Map<String, Object> parsed = parseForValidate(props, configValues);\n   570\u2192        return validate(parsed, configValues);\n   571\u2192    }\n   572\u2192\n   573\u2192    // package accessible for testing\n   574\u2192    Map<String, Object> parseForValidate(Map<String, String> props, Map<String, ConfigValue> configValues) {\n   575\u2192        Map<String, Object> parsed = new HashMap<>();\n   576\u2192        Set<String> configsWithNoParent = getConfigsWithNoParent();\n   577\u2192        for (String name: configsWithNoParent) {\n   578\u2192            parseForValidate(name, props, parsed, configValues);\n   579\u2192        }\n   580\u2192        return parsed;\n   581\u2192    }\n   582\u2192\n   583\u2192\n   584\u2192    private Map<String, ConfigValue> validate(Map<String, Object> parsed, Map<String, ConfigValue> configValues) {\n   585\u2192        Set<String> configsWithNoParent = getConfigsWithNoParent();\n   586\u2192        for (String name: configsWithNoParent) {\n   587\u2192            validate(name, parsed, configValues);\n   588\u2192        }\n   589\u2192        return configValues;\n   590\u2192    }\n   591\u2192\n   592\u2192    private List<String> undefinedDependentConfigs() {\n   593\u2192        Set<String> undefinedConfigKeys = new HashSet<>();\n   594\u2192        for (ConfigKey configKey : configKeys.values()) {\n   595\u2192            for (String dependent: configKey.dependents) {\n   596\u2192                if (!configKeys.containsKey(dependent)) {\n   597\u2192                    undefinedConfigKeys.add(dependent);\n   598\u2192                }\n   599\u2192            }\n   600\u2192        }\n   601\u2192        return new ArrayList<>(undefinedConfigKeys);\n   602\u2192    }\n   603\u2192\n   604\u2192    // package accessible for testing\n   605\u2192    Set<String> getConfigsWithNoParent() {\n   606\u2192        if (this.configsWithNoParent != null) {\n   607\u2192            return this.configsWithNoParent;\n   608\u2192        }\n   609\u2192        Set<String> configsWithParent = new HashSet<>();\n   610\u2192\n   611\u2192        for (ConfigKey configKey: configKeys.values()) {\n   612\u2192            List<String> dependents = configKey.dependents;\n   613\u2192            configsWithParent.addAll(dependents);\n   614\u2192        }\n   615\u2192\n   616\u2192        Set<String> configs = new HashSet<>(configKeys.keySet());\n   617\u2192        configs.removeAll(configsWithParent);\n   618\u2192        this.configsWithNoParent = configs;\n   619\u2192        return configs;\n   620\u2192    }\n   621\u2192\n   622\u2192    private void parseForValidate(String name, Map<String, String> props, Map<String, Object> parsed, Map<String, ConfigValue> configs) {\n   623\u2192        if (!configKeys.containsKey(name)) {\n   624\u2192            return;\n   625\u2192        }\n   626\u2192        ConfigKey key = configKeys.get(name);\n   627\u2192        ConfigValue config = configs.get(name);\n   628\u2192\n   629\u2192        Object value = null;\n   630\u2192        if (props.containsKey(key.name)) {\n   631\u2192            try {\n   632\u2192                value = parseType(key.name, props.get(key.name), key.type);\n   633\u2192            } catch (ConfigException e) {\n   634\u2192                config.addErrorMessage(e.getMessage());\n   635\u2192            }\n   636\u2192        } else if (NO_DEFAULT_VALUE.equals(key.defaultValue)) {\n   637\u2192            config.addErrorMessage(\"Missing required configuration \\\"\" + key.name + \"\\\" which has no default value.\");\n   638\u2192        } else {\n   639\u2192            value = key.defaultValue;\n   640\u2192        }\n   641\u2192\n   642\u2192        if (key.validator != null) {\n   643\u2192            try {\n   644\u2192                key.validator.ensureValid(key.name, value);\n   645\u2192            } catch (ConfigException e) {\n   646\u2192                config.addErrorMessage(e.getMessage());\n   647\u2192            }\n   648\u2192        }\n   649\u2192        config.value(value);\n   650\u2192        parsed.put(name, value);\n   651\u2192        for (String dependent: key.dependents) {\n   652\u2192            parseForValidate(dependent, props, parsed, configs);\n   653\u2192        }\n   654\u2192    }\n   655\u2192\n   656\u2192    private void validate(String name, Map<String, Object> parsed, Map<String, ConfigValue> configs) {\n   657\u2192        if (!configKeys.containsKey(name)) {\n   658\u2192            return;\n   659\u2192        }\n   660\u2192        ConfigKey key = configKeys.get(name);\n   661\u2192        ConfigValue value = configs.get(name);\n   662\u2192        if (key.recommender != null) {\n   663\u2192            try {\n   664\u2192                List<Object> recommendedValues = key.recommender.validValues(name, parsed);\n   665\u2192                List<Object> originalRecommendedValues = value.recommendedValues();\n   666\u2192                if (!originalRecommendedValues.isEmpty()) {\n   667\u2192                    Set<Object> originalRecommendedValueSet = new HashSet<>(originalRecommendedValues);\n   668\u2192                    recommendedValues.removeIf(o -> !originalRecommendedValueSet.contains(o));\n   669\u2192                }\n   670\u2192                value.recommendedValues(recommendedValues);\n   671\u2192                value.visible(key.recommender.visible(name, parsed));\n   672\u2192            } catch (ConfigException e) {\n   673\u2192                value.addErrorMessage(e.getMessage());\n   674\u2192            }\n   675\u2192        }\n   676\u2192\n   677\u2192        configs.put(name, value);\n   678\u2192        for (String dependent: key.dependents) {\n   679\u2192            validate(dependent, parsed, configs);\n   680\u2192        }\n   681\u2192    }\n   682\u2192\n   683\u2192    /**\n   684\u2192     * Parse a value according to its expected type.\n   685\u2192     * @param name  The config name\n   686\u2192     * @param value The config value\n   687\u2192     * @param type  The expected type\n   688\u2192     * @return The parsed object\n   689\u2192     */\n   690\u2192    public static Object parseType(String name, Object value, Type type) {\n   691\u2192        try {\n   692\u2192            if (value == null) return null;\n   693\u2192\n   694\u2192            String trimmed = null;\n   695\u2192            if (value instanceof String)\n   696\u2192                trimmed = ((String) value).trim();\n   697\u2192\n   698\u2192            switch (type) {\n   699\u2192                case BOOLEAN:\n   700\u2192                    if (value instanceof String) {\n   701\u2192                        if (trimmed.equalsIgnoreCase(\"true\"))\n   702\u2192                            return true;\n   703\u2192                        else if (trimmed.equalsIgnoreCase(\"false\"))\n   704\u2192                            return false;\n   705\u2192                        else\n   706\u2192                            throw new ConfigException(name, value, \"Expected value to be either true or false\");\n   707\u2192                    } else if (value instanceof Boolean)\n   708\u2192                        return value;\n   709\u2192                    else\n   710\u2192                        throw new ConfigException(name, value, \"Expected value to be either true or false\");\n   711\u2192                case PASSWORD:\n   712\u2192                    if (value instanceof Password)\n   713\u2192                        return value;\n   714\u2192                    else if (value instanceof String)\n   715\u2192                        return new Password(trimmed);\n   716\u2192                    else\n   717\u2192                        throw new ConfigException(name, value, \"Expected value to be a string, but it was a \" + value.getClass().getName());\n   718\u2192                case STRING:\n   719\u2192                    if (value instanceof String)\n   720\u2192                        return trimmed;\n   721\u2192                    else\n   722\u2192                        throw new ConfigException(name, value, \"Expected value to be a string, but it was a \" + value.getClass().getName());\n   723\u2192                case INT:\n   724\u2192                    if (value instanceof Integer) {\n   725\u2192                        return value;\n   726\u2192                    } else if (value instanceof String) {\n   727\u2192                        return Integer.parseInt(trimmed);\n   728\u2192                    } else {\n   729\u2192                        throw new ConfigException(name, value, \"Expected value to be a 32-bit integer, but it was a \" + value.getClass().getName());\n   730\u2192                    }\n   731\u2192                case SHORT:\n   732\u2192                    if (value instanceof Short) {\n   733\u2192                        return value;\n   734\u2192                    } else if (value instanceof String) {\n   735\u2192                        return Short.parseShort(trimmed);\n   736\u2192                    } else {\n   737\u2192                        throw new ConfigException(name, value, \"Expected value to be a 16-bit integer (short), but it was a \" + value.getClass().getName());\n   738\u2192                    }\n   739\u2192                case LONG:\n   740\u2192                    if (value instanceof Integer)\n   741\u2192                        return ((Integer) value).longValue();\n   742\u2192                    if (value instanceof Long)\n   743\u2192                        return value;\n   744\u2192                    else if (value instanceof String)\n   745\u2192                        return Long.parseLong(trimmed);\n   746\u2192                    else\n   747\u2192                        throw new ConfigException(name, value, \"Expected value to be a 64-bit integer (long), but it was a \" + value.getClass().getName());\n   748\u2192                case DOUBLE:\n   749\u2192                    if (value instanceof Number)\n   750\u2192                        return ((Number) value).doubleValue();\n   751\u2192                    else if (value instanceof String)\n   752\u2192                        return Double.parseDouble(trimmed);\n   753\u2192                    else\n   754\u2192                        throw new ConfigException(name, value, \"Expected value to be a double, but it was a \" + value.getClass().getName());\n   755\u2192                case LIST:\n   756\u2192                    if (value instanceof List)\n   757\u2192                        return value;\n   758\u2192                    else if (value instanceof String)\n   759\u2192                        if (trimmed.isEmpty())\n   760\u2192                            return Collections.emptyList();\n   761\u2192                        else\n   762\u2192                            return Arrays.asList(COMMA_WITH_WHITESPACE.split(trimmed, -1));\n   763\u2192                    else\n   764\u2192                        throw new ConfigException(name, value, \"Expected a comma separated list.\");\n   765\u2192                case CLASS:\n   766\u2192                    if (value instanceof Class)\n   767\u2192                        return value;\n   768\u2192                    else if (value instanceof String) {\n   769\u2192                        return Utils.loadClass(trimmed, Object.class);\n   770\u2192                    } else\n   771\u2192                        throw new ConfigException(name, value, \"Expected a Class instance or class name.\");\n   772\u2192                default:\n   773\u2192                    throw new IllegalStateException(\"Unknown type.\");\n   774\u2192            }\n   775\u2192        } catch (NumberFormatException e) {\n   776\u2192            throw new ConfigException(name, value, \"Not a number of type \" + type);\n   777\u2192        } catch (ClassNotFoundException e) {\n   778\u2192            throw new ConfigException(name, value, \"Class \" + value + \" could not be found.\");\n   779\u2192        }\n   780\u2192    }\n   781\u2192\n   782\u2192    public static String convertToString(Object parsedValue, Type type) {\n   783\u2192        if (parsedValue == null) {\n   784\u2192            return null;\n   785\u2192        }\n   786\u2192\n   787\u2192        if (type == null) {\n   788\u2192            return parsedValue.toString();\n   789\u2192        }\n   790\u2192\n   791\u2192        switch (type) {\n   792\u2192            case BOOLEAN:\n   793\u2192            case SHORT:\n   794\u2192            case INT:\n   795\u2192            case LONG:\n   796\u2192            case DOUBLE:\n   797\u2192            case STRING:\n   798\u2192            case PASSWORD:\n   799\u2192                return parsedValue.toString();\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

Values must be provided as a Number object, as a\n   867\u2192         * Double object or as a String being a valid Double value, otherwise a {@link ConfigException} is thrown.\n   868\u2192         */\n   869\u2192        DOUBLE,\n   870\u2192        /**\n   871\u2192         * Used for list values. When the value is provided as a String it must use commas to separate the\n   873\u2192         * different entries (for example: <code>first-entry, second-entry</code>) and an empty String maps to an empty List.\n   874\u2192         */\n   875\u2192        LIST,\n   876\u2192        /**\n   877\u2192         * Used for values that implement a Kafka interface. Values must be provided as a\n   885\u2192         * String object, otherwise a {@link ConfigException} is thrown.\n   886\u2192         */\n   887\u2192        PASSWORD;\n   888\u2192\n   889\u2192        /**\n   890\u2192         * Whether this type contains sensitive data such as a password or key.\n   891\u2192         * @return true if the type is {@link #PASSWORD}\n   892\u2192         */\n   893\u2192        public boolean isSensitive() {\n   894\u2192            return this == PASSWORD;\n   895\u2192        }\n   896\u2192    }\n   897\u2192\n   898\u2192    /**\n   899\u2192     * The importance level for a configuration\n   900\u2192     */\n   901\u2192    public enum Importance {\n   902\u2192        HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW\n   903\u2192    }\n   904\u2192\n   905\u2192    /**\n   906\u2192     * The width of a configuration value\n   907\u2192     */\n   908\u2192    public enum Width {\n   909\u2192        NONE, SHORT, MEDIUM, LONG\n   910\u2192    }\n   911\u2192\n   912\u2192    /**\n   913\u2192     * This is used by the {@link #validate(Map)} to get valid values for a configuration given the current\n   914\u2192     * configuration values in order to perform full configuration validation and visibility modification.\n   915\u2192     * In case that there are dependencies between configurations, the valid values and visibility\n   916\u2192     * for a configuration may change given the values of other configurations.\n   917\u2192     */\n   918\u2192    public interface Recommender {\n   919\u2192\n   920\u2192        /**\n   921\u2192         * The valid values for the configuration given the current configuration values.\n   922\u2192         * @param name The name of the configuration\n   923\u2192         * @param parsedConfig The parsed configuration values\n   924\u2192         * @return The list of valid values. To function properly, the returned objects should have the type\n   925\u2192         * defined for the configuration using the recommender.\n   926\u2192         */\n   927\u2192        List<Object> validValues(String name, Map<String, Object> parsedConfig);\n   928\u2192\n   929\u2192        /**\n   930\u2192         * Set the visibility of the configuration given the current configuration values.\n   931\u2192         * @param name The name of the configuration\n   932\u2192         * @param parsedConfig The parsed configuration values\n   933\u2192         * @return The visibility of the configuration\n   934\u2192         */\n   935\u2192        boolean visible(String name, Map<String, Object> parsedConfig);\n   936\u2192    }\n   937\u2192\n   938\u2192    /**\n   939\u2192     * Validation logic the user may provide to perform single configuration validation.\n   940\u2192     */\n   941\u2192    public interface Validator {\n   942\u2192        /**\n   943\u2192         * Perform single configuration validation.\n   944\u2192         * @param name The name of the configuration\n   945\u2192         * @param value The value of the configuration\n   946\u2192         * @throws ConfigException if the value is invalid.\n   947\u2192         */\n   948\u2192        void ensureValid(String name, Object value);\n   949\u2192    }\n   950\u2192\n   951\u2192    /**\n   952\u2192     * Validation logic for numeric ranges\n   953\u2192     */\n   954\u2192    public static class Range implements Validator {\n   955\u2192        private final Number min;\n   956\u2192        private final Number max;\n   957\u2192\n   958\u2192        /**\n   959\u2192         *  A numeric range with inclusive upper bound and inclusive lower bound\n   960\u2192         * @param min  the lower bound\n   961\u2192         * @param max  the upper bound\n   962\u2192         */\n   963\u2192        private Range(Number min, Number max) {\n   964\u2192            this.min = min;\n   965\u2192            this.max = max;\n   966\u2192        }\n   967\u2192\n   968\u2192        /**\n   969\u2192         * A numeric range that checks only the lower bound\n   970\u2192         *\n   971\u2192         * @param min The minimum acceptable value\n   972\u2192         */\n   973\u2192        public static Range atLeast(Number min) {\n   974\u2192            return new Range(min, null);\n   975\u2192        }\n   976\u2192\n   977\u2192        /**\n   978\u2192         * A numeric range that checks both the upper (inclusive) and lower bound\n   979\u2192         */\n   980\u2192        public static Range between(Number min, Number max) {\n   981\u2192            return new Range(min, max);\n   982\u2192        }\n   983\u2192\n   984\u2192        public void ensureValid(String name, Object o) {\n   985\u2192            if (o == null)\n   986\u2192                throw new ConfigException(name, null, \"Value must be non-null\");\n   987\u2192            Number n = (Number) o;\n   988\u2192            if (min != null && n.doubleValue() < min.doubleValue())\n   989\u2192                throw new ConfigException(name, o, \"Value must be at least \" + min);\n   990\u2192            if (max != null && n.doubleValue() > max.doubleValue())\n   991\u2192                throw new ConfigException(name, o, \"Value must be no more than \" + max);\n   992\u2192        }\n   993\u2192\n   994\u2192        public String toString() {\n   995\u2192            if (min == null && max == null)\n   996\u2192                return \"[...]\";\n   997\u2192            else if (min == null)\n   998\u2192                return \"[...,\" + max + \"]\";\n   999\u2192            else if (max == null)\n  1000\u2192                return \"[\" + min + \",...]\";\n  1001\u2192            else\n  1002\u2192                return \"[\" + min + \",...,\" + max + \"]\";\n  1003\u2192        }\n  1004\u2192    }\n  1005\u2192\n  1006\u2192    public static class ValidList implements Validator {\n  1007\u2192\n  1008\u2192        final ValidString validString;\n  1009\u2192\n  1010\u2192        private ValidList(List<String> validStrings) {\n  1011\u2192            this.validString = new ValidString(validStrings);\n  1012\u2192        }\n  1013\u2192\n  1014\u2192        public static ValidList in(String... validStrings) {\n  1015\u2192            return new ValidList(Arrays.asList(validStrings));\n  1016\u2192        }\n  1017\u2192\n  1018\u2192        @Override\n  1019\u2192        public void ensureValid(final String name, final Object value) {\n  1020\u2192            @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n  1021\u2192            List<String> values = (List<String>) value;\n  1022\u2192            for (String string : values) {\n  1023\u2192                validString.ensureValid(name, string);\n  1024\u2192            }\n  1025\u2192        }\n  1026\u2192\n  1027\u2192        public String toString() {\n  1028\u2192            return validString.toString();\n  1029\u2192        }\n  1030\u2192    }\n  1031\u2192\n  1032\u2192    public static class ValidString implements Validator {\n  1033\u2192        final List<String> validStrings;\n  1034\u2192\n  1035\u2192        private ValidString(List<String> validStrings) {\n  1036\u2192            this.validStrings = validStrings;\n  1037\u2192 "   941\u2192    public interface Validator {\n   942\u2192        /**\n   943\u2192         * Perform single configuration validation.\n   944\u2192         * @param name The name of the configuration\n   945\u2192         * @param value The value of the configuration\n   946\u2192         * @throws ConfigException if the value is invalid.\n   947\u2192         */\n   948\u2192        void ensureValid(String name, Object value);\n   949\u2192    }\n   950\u2192\n   951\u2192    /**\n   952\u2192     * Validation logic for numeric ranges\n   953\u2192     */\n   954\u2192    public static class Range implements Validator {\n   955\u2192        private final Number min;\n   956\u2192        private final Number max;\n   957\u2192\n   958\u2192        /**\n   959\u2192         *  A numeric range with inclusive upper bound and inclusive lower bound\n   960\u2192         * @param min  the lower bound\n   961\u2192         * @param max  the upper bound\n   962\u2192         */\n   963\u2192        private Range(Number min, Number max) {\n   964\u2192            this.min = min;\n   965\u2192            this.max = max;\n   966\u2192        }\n   967\u2192\n   968\u2192        /**\n   969\u2192         * A numeric range that checks only the lower bound\n   970\u2192         *\n   971\u2192         * @param min The minimum acceptable value\n   972\u2192         */\n   973\u2192        public static Range atLeast(Number min) {\n   974\u2192            return new Range(min, null);\n   975\u2192        }\n   976\u2192\n   977\u2192        /**\n   978\u2192         * A numeric range that checks both the upper (inclusive) and lower bound\n   979\u2192         */\n   980\u2192        public static Range between(Number min, Number max) {\n   981\u2192            return new Range(min, max);\n   982\u2192        }\n   983\u2192\n   984\u2192        public void ensureValid(String name, Object o) {\n   985\u2192            if (o == null)\n   986\u2192                throw new ConfigException(name, null, \"Value must be non-null\");\n   987\u2192            Number n = (Number) o;\n   988\u2192            if (min != null && n.doubleValue() < min.doubleValue())\n   989\u2192                throw new ConfigException(name, o, \"Value must be at least \" + min);\n   990\u2192            if (max != null && n.doubleValue() > max.doubleValue())\n   991\u2192                throw new ConfigException(name, o, \"Value must be no more than \" + max);\n   992\u2192        }\n   993\u2192\n   994\u2192        public String toString() {\n   995\u2192            if (min == null && max == null)\n   996\u2192                return \"[...]\";\n   997\u2192            else if (min == null)\n   998\u2192                return \"[...,\" + max + \"]\";\n   999\u2192            else if (max == null)\n  1000\u2192                return \"[\" + min + \",...]\";\n  1001\u2192            else\n  1002\u2192                return \"[\" + min + \",...,\" + max + \"]\";\n  1003\u2192        }\n  1004\u2192    }\n  1005\u2192\n  1006\u2192    public static class ValidList implements Validator {\n  1007\u2192\n  1008\u2192        final ValidString validString;\n  1009\u2192\n  1010\u2192        private ValidList(List<String> validStrings) {\n  1011\u2192            this.validString = new ValidString(validStrings);\n  1012\u2192        }\n  1013\u2192\n  1014\u2192        public static ValidList in(String... validStrings) {\n  1015\u2192            return new ValidList(Arrays.asList(validStrings));\n  1016\u2192        }\n  1017\u2192\n  1018\u2192        @Override\n  1019\u2192        public void ensureValid(final String name, final Object value) {\n  1020\u2192            @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n  1021\u2192            List<String>   }\n  1026\u2192\n  1027\u2192        public String toString() {\n  1028\u2192            return validString.toString();\n  1029\u2192        }\n  1030\u2192    }\n  1031\u2192\n  1032\u2192 1036\u2192            this.validStrings = validStrings;\n  1037\u2192        }\n  1038\u2192\n  1039\u2192        public static ValidString in(String... validStrings) {\n  1040\u2192            return         String s = (String) o;\n  1046\u2192            if (!validStrings.contains(s)) {\n  1047\u2192                throw new ConfigException(name, o, \"String must be one of: \" + String.join(\", String.join(\", \", validStrings) + \"]\";\n  1054\u2192        }\n  1055\u2192    }\n  1056\u2192\n  1057\u2192    public static class CaseInsensitiveValidString implements Validator {\n  1058\u2192\n  1059\u2192        final Set<String> validStrings;\n  1060\u2192\n  1061\u2192        private CaseInsensitiveValidString(List<String> validStrings) {\n  1062\u2192            this.validStrings = validStrings.stream()\n  1063\u2192                .map(s -> s.toUpperCase(Locale.ROOT))\n  1064\u2192                .collect(Collectors.toSet());\n  1065\u2192        }\n  1066\u2192\n  1067\u2192        public static CaseInsensitiveValidString in(String... validStrings) {\n  1068\u2192            return new CaseInsensitiveValidString(Arrays.asList(validStrings));\n  1069\u2192        }\n  1070\u2192\n  1071\u2192        @Override\n  1072\u2192        public void ensureValid(String name, Object o) {\n  1073\u2192            String s = (String) o;\n  1074\u2192            if (s == null || !validStrings.contains(s.toUpperCase(Locale.ROOT))) {\n  1075\u2192                throw new ConfigException(name, o, \"String must be one of (case insensitive): \" + String.join(\", \", validStrings));\n  1076\u2192            }\n  1077\u2192        }\n  1078\u2192\n  1079\u2192        public String toString() {\n  1080\u2192            return \"(case insensitive) [\" + String.join(\", \", validStrings) + \"]\";\n  1081\u2192        }\n  1082\u2192    }\n  1083\u2192\n  1084\u2192    public static class NonNullValidator implements Validator {\n  1085\u2192        @Override\n  1086\u2192        public void ensureValid(String name, Object value) {\n  1087\u2192            if (value == null) {\n  1088\u2192                // Pass in the string null to avoid the spotbugs warning\n  1089\u2192                throw new ConfigException(name, \"null\", \"entry must be non null\");\n  1090\u2192            }\n  1091\u2192        }\n  1092\u2192\n  1093\u2192 Validator {\n  1099\u2192        BiConsumer<String, Object> ensureValid;\n  1100\u2192        Supplier<String> toStringFunction;\n  1101\u2192\n  1102\u2192        private LambdaValidator(BiConsumer<String, Object> ensureValid,\n  1103\u2192                                Supplier<String> toStringFunction) {\n  1104\u2192            this.ensureValid = ensureValid;\n  1105\u2192            this.toStringFunction = toStringFunction;\n  1106\u2192        }\n  1107\u2192\n  1108\u2192        public static LambdaValidator with(BiConsumer<String, Object> ensureValid,\n  1109\u2192                                           Supplier<String> toStringFunction) {\n  1110\u2192            return new LambdaValidator(ensureValid, toStringFunction);\n  1111\u2192        }\n  1112\u2192\n  1113\u2192        @Override\n  1114\u2192        public void ensureValid(String name, Object value) {\n  1115\u2192            ensureValid.accept(name, value);\n  1116\u2192        }\n  1117\u2192\n  1118\u2192        @Override\n  1119\u2192        public String toString() {\n  1120\u2192            return toStringFunction.get();\n  1121\u2192        }\n  1122\u2192    }\n  1123\u2192\n  1124\u2192    public static class CompositeValidator implements Validator {\n  1125\u2192        private final List<Validator> validators;\n  1126\u2192\n  1127\u2192        private CompositeValidator(List<Validator> validators) {\n  1128\u2192            this.validators = Collections.unmodifiableList(validators);\n  1129\u2192        }\n  1130\u2192\n  1131\u2192        public static CompositeValidator of(Validator... validators) {\n  1132\u2192            return new CompositeValidator(Arrays.asList(validators));\n  1133\u2192        }\n  1134\u2192\n  1135\u2192        @Override\n  1136\u2192        public void ensureValid(String name, Object value) {\n  1137\u2192            for (Validator validator: validators) {\n  1138\u2192                validator.ensureValid(name, value);\n  1139\u2192            }\n  1140\u2192        }\n  1141\u2192\n  1142\u2192        @Override\n  1143\u2192        public String toString() {\n  1144\u2192            if (validators == null) return \"\";\n  1145\u2192            StringBuilder desc = new StringBuilder();\n  1146\u2192            for (Validator v: validators) {\n  1147\u2192                if (desc.length() > 0) {\n  1148\u2192                    desc.append(',').append(' ');\n  1149\u2192                }\n  1150\u2192                desc.append(v);\n  1151\u2192            }\n  1152\u2192            return desc.toString();\n  1153\u2192        }\n  1154\u2192    }\n  1155\u2192\n  1156\u2192    public static class NonEmptyString implements Validator {\n  1157\u2192\n  1158\u2192        @Override\n  1159\u2192        public void ensureValid(String name, Object o) {\n  1160\u2192            String s = (String) o;\n  1161\u2192            if (s != null && s.isEmpty()) {\n  1162\u2192                throw new ConfigException(name, o, \"String must be non-empty\");\n  1163\u2192            }\n  1164\u2192        }\n  1165\u2192\n  1166\u2192        @Override\n  1167\u2192        public String toString() {\n  1168\u2192            return \"non-empty string\";\n  1169\u2192        }\n  1170\u2192    }\n  1171\u2192\n  1172\u2192    public static class NonEmptyStringWithoutControlChars implements Validator {\n  1173\u2192\n  1174\u2192        public static NonEmptyStringWithoutControlChars nonEmptyStringWithoutControlChars() {\n  1175\u2192            return new NonEmptyStringWithoutControlChars();\n  1176\u2192        }\n  1177\u2192\n  1178\u2192        @Override\n  1179\u2192        public void ensureValid(String name, Object value) {\n  1180\u2192            String s = (String) value;\n  1181\u2192\n  1182\u2192            if (s == null) {\n  1183\u2192                // This can happen during creation of the config object due to no default value being defined for the\n  1184\u2192                // name configuration - a missing name parameter is caught when checking for mandatory parameters,\n  1185\u2192                // thus we can ok a null value here\n  1186\u2192                return;\n  1187\u2192            } else if (s.isEmpty()) {\n  1188\u2192                throw new ConfigException(name, value, \"String may not be empty\");\n  1189\u2192            }\n  1190\u2192\n  1191\u2192            // Check name string for illegal characters\n  1192\u2192            ArrayList<Integer> foundIllegalCharacters = new ArrayList<>();\n  1193\u2192\n  1194\u2192            for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {\n  1195\u2192                if (Character.isISOControl(s.codePointAt(i))) {\n  1196\u2192                    foundIllegalCharacters.add(s.codePointAt(i));\n  1197\u2192                }\n  1198\u2192            }\n  1199\u2192\n  1200\u2192            if (!foundIllegalCharacters.isEmpty()) {\n  1201\u2192                throw new ConfigException(name, value, \"String may not contain control sequences but had the following ASCII chars: \" +\n  1202\u2192                        foundIllegalCharacters.stream().map(Object::toString).collect(Collectors.joining(\", \")));\n  1203\u2192            }\n  1204\u2192        }\n  1205\u2192\n  1206\u2192        public String toString() {\n  1207\u2192            return \"non-empty string without ISO control characters\";\n  1208\u2192        }\n  1209\u2192    }\n  1210\u2192\n  1211\u2192    public static class ListSize implements Validator {\n  1212\u2192        final int maxSize;\n  1213\u2192\n  1214\u2192        private ListSize(final int maxSize) {\n  1215\u2192            this.maxSize = maxSize;\n  1216\u2192        }\n  1217\u2192\n  1218\u2192        public static ListSize atMostOfSize(final int maxSize) {\n  1219\u2192            return new ListSize(maxSize);\n  1220\u2192        }\n  1221\u2192\n  1222\u2192        @Override\n  1223\u2192        public void ensureValid(final String name, final Object value) {\n  1224\u2192            @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n  1225\u2192            List<String> values = (List<String>) value;\n  1226\u2192            if (values.size() > maxSize) {\n  1227\u2192                throw new ConfigException(name, value, \"exceeds maximum list size of [\" + maxSize + \"].\");\n  1228\u2192            }\n  1229\u2192        }\n  1230\u2192\n  1231\u2192        @Override\n  1232\u2192        public String toString() {\n  1233\u2192            return \"List containing maximum of \" + maxSize + \" elements\";\n  1234\u2192        }\n  1235\u2192    }\n  1236\u2192\n  1237\u2192    public static class ConfigKey {\n  1238\u2192        public final String name;\n  1239\u2192        public final Type type;\n  1240\u2192        public final String documentation;\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would 18\u2192\n    19\u2192import org.apache.kafka.clients.CommonClientConfigs;\n    20\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef;\n    21\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.LONG;\n    34\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.STRING;\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192public class ServerConfigs {\n    37\u2192    /** ********* General Configuration ***********/\n    38\u2192    public static final String RESERVED_BROKER_MAX_ID_CONFIG = \"reserved.broker.max.id\";\n    39\u2192    public static final int RESERVED_BROKER_MAX_ID_DEFAULT = 1000;\n    40\u2192    public static final String RESERVED_BROKER_MAX_ID_DOC = \"Max number that can be used for a broker.id\";\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192    public static final String BROKER_ID_GENERATION_ENABLE_CONFIG = \"broker.id.generation.enable\";\n    43\u2192    public static final boolean BROKER_ID_GENERATION_ENABLE_DEFAULT = true;\n    44\u2192    public static final String BROKER_ID_GENERATION_ENABLE_DOC = \"Enable automatic broker id generation on the server. When enabled the value configured for \" + RESERVED_BROKER_MAX_ID_CONFIG + \" should be reviewed.\";\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192    public static final String BROKER_ID_CONFIG = \"broker.id\";\n    48\u2192    public static final int BROKER_ID_DEFAULT = -1;\n    49\u2192    public static final String BROKER_ID_DOC = \"The broker id for this server. If unset, a unique broker id will be generated.\" +\n    50\u2192            \"To avoid conflicts between ZooKeeper generated broker id's and user configured broker id's, generated broker ids \" +\n    51\u2192            \"start from \" + RESERVED_BROKER_MAX_ID_CONFIG + \" + 1.\";\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192    public static final String MESSAGE_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG = \"message.max.bytes\";\n    54\u2192    public static final String MESSAGE_MAX_BYTES_DOC = TopicConfig.MAX_MESSAGE_BYTES_DOC +\n    55\u2192            \"This can be set per topic with the topic level <code>\" + TopicConfig.MAX_MESSAGE_BYTES_CONFIG + \"</code> config.\";\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192    public static final String NUM_IO_THREADS_CONFIG = \"num.io.threads\";\n    58\u2192    public static final int NUM_IO_THREADS_DEFAULT = 8;\n    59\u2192    public static final String NUM_IO_THREADS_DOC = \"The number of threads that the server uses for processing requests, which may include disk I/O\";\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192    public static final String BACKGROUND_THREADS_CONFIG = \"background.threads\";\n    62\u2192    public static final int BACKGROUND_THREADS_DEFAULT = 10;\n    63\u2192    public static final String BACKGROUND_THREADS_DOC = \"The number of threads to use for various background processing tasks\";\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192    public static final String NUM_REPLICA_ALTER_LOG_DIRS_THREADS_CONFIG = \"num.replica.alter.log.dirs.threads\";\n    66\u2192    public static final String NUM_REPLICA_ALTER_LOG_DIRS_THREADS_DOC = \"The number of threads that can move replicas between log directories, which may include disk I/O\";\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192    public static final String REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG = CommonClientConfigs.REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG;\n    69\u2192    public static final int REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_DEFAULT = 30000;\n    70\u2192    public static final String REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_DOC = CommonClientConfigs.REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_DOC;\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192    public static final String SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG = CommonClientConfigs.SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG;\n    73\u2192    public static final long DEFAULT_SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MS = CommonClientConfigs.DEFAULT_SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MS;\n    74\u2192    public static final String SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MS_DOC = CommonClientConfigs.SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MS_DOC;\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192    public static final String SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MAX_MS_CONFIG = CommonClientConfigs.SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MAX_MS_CONFIG;\n    77\u2192    public static final long SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MAX_MS = CommonClientConfigs.DEFAULT_SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MAX_MS;\n    78\u2192    public static final String SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MAX_MS_DOC = CommonClientConfigs.SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MAX_MS_DOC;\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192    public static final String DELETE_TOPIC_ENABLE_CONFIG = \"delete.topic.enable\";\n    81\u2192    public static final boolean DELETE_TOPIC_ENABLE_DEFAULT = true;\n    82\u2192    public static final String DELETE_TOPIC_ENABLE_DOC = \"Enables delete topic. Delete topic through the admin tool will have no effect if this config is turned off\";\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192    public static final String COMPRESSION_TYPE_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.COMPRESSION_TYPE_CONFIG);\n    85\u2192    public static final String COMPRESSION_TYPE_DOC = \"Specify the final compression type for a given topic. This configuration accepts the standard compression codecs \" +\n    86\u2192            \"('gzip', 'snappy', 'lz4', 'zstd'). It additionally accepts 'uncompressed' which is equivalent to no compression; and \" +\n    87\u2192            \"'producer' which means retain the original compression codec set by the producer.\";\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192    public static final String COMPRESSION_GZIP_LEVEL_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.COMPRESSION_GZIP_LEVEL_CONFIG);\n    90\u2192    public static final String COMPRESSION_GZIP_LEVEL_DOC = \"The compression level to use if \" + COMPRESSION_TYPE_CONFIG + \" is set to 'gzip'.\";\n    91\u2192    public static final String COMPRESSION_LZ4_LEVEL_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.COMPRESSION_LZ4_LEVEL_CONFIG);\n    92\u2192    public static final String COMPRESSION_LZ4_LEVEL_DOC = \"The compression level to use if \" + COMPRESSION_TYPE_CONFIG + \" is set to 'lz4'.\";\n    93\u2192    public static final String COMPRESSION_ZSTD_LEVEL_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.COMPRESSION_ZSTD_LEVEL_CONFIG);\n    94\u2192    public static final String COMPRESSION_ZSTD_LEVEL_DOC = \"The compression level to use if \" + COMPRESSION_TYPE_CONFIG + \" is set to 'zstd'.\";\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192    /***************** rack configuration *************/\n    97\u2192    public static final String BROKER_RACK_CONFIG = \"broker.rack\";\n    98\u2192    public static final String BROKER_RACK_DOC = \"Rack of the broker. This will be used in rack aware replication assignment for fault tolerance. Examples: <code>RACK1</code>, <code>us-east-1d</code>\";\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192    /** ********* Controlled shutdown configuration ***********/\n   101\u2192    public static final String CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_MAX_RETRIES_CONFIG = \"controlled.shutdown.max.retries\";\n   102\u2192    public static final int CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_MAX_RETRIES_DEFAULT = 3;\n   103\u2192    public static final String CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_MAX_RETRIES_DOC = \"Controlled shutdown can fail for multiple reasons. This determines the number of retries when such failure happens\";\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192    public static final String CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_RETRY_BACKOFF_MS_CONFIG = \"controlled.shutdown.retry.backoff.ms\";\n   106\u2192    public static final int CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_RETRY_BACKOFF_MS_DEFAULT = 5000;\n   107\u2192    public static final String CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_RETRY_BACKOFF_MS_DOC = \"Before each retry, the system needs time to recover from the state that caused the previous failure (Controller fail over, replica lag etc). This config determines the amount of time to wait before retrying.\";\n   108\u2192\n   109\u2192    public static final String CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_ENABLE_CONFIG = \"controlled.shutdown.enable\";\n   110\u2192    public static final boolean CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_ENABLE_DEFAULT = true;\n   111\u2192    public static final String CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_ENABLE_DOC = \"Enable controlled shutdown of the server.\";\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192    /** ********* Fetch Configuration **************/\n   114\u2192    public static final String MAX_INCREMENTAL_FETCH_SESSION_CACHE_SLOTS_CONFIG = \"max.incremental.fetch.session.cache.slots\";\n   115\u2192    public static final int MAX_INCREMENTAL_FETCH_SESSION_CACHE_SLOTS_DEFAULT = 1000;\n   116\u2192    public static final String MAX_INCREMENTAL_FETCH_SESSION_CACHE_SLOTS_DOC = \"The maximum number of total incremental fetch sessions that we will maintain. FetchSessionCache is sharded into 8 shards and the limit is equally divided among all shards. Sessions are allocated to each shard in round-robin. Only entries within a shard are considered eligible for eviction.\";\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192    public static final String FETCH_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG = \"fetch.max.bytes\";\n   119\u2192    public static final int FETCH_MAX_BYTES_DEFAULT = 55 * 1024 * 1024;\n   120\u2192    public static final String FETCH_MAX_BYTES_DOC = \"The maximum number of bytes we will return for a fetch request. Must be at least 1024.\";\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192    /** ********* Request Limit Configuration **************/\n   123\u2192    public static final String MAX_REQUEST_PARTITION_SIZE_LIMIT_CONFIG = \"max.request.partition.size.limit\";\n   124\u2192    public static final int MAX_REQUEST_PARTITION_SIZE_LIMIT_DEFAULT = 2000;\n   125\u2192    public static final String MAX_REQUEST_PARTITION_SIZE_LIMIT_DOC = \"The maximum number of partitions can be served in one request.\";\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192    /** Internal Configurations **/\n   128\u2192    public static final String UNSTABLE_API_VERSIONS_ENABLE_CONFIG = \"unstable.api.versions.enable\";\n   129\u2192    public static final String UNSTABLE_FEATURE_VERSIONS_ENABLE_CONFIG = \"unstable.feature.versions.enable\";\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192    /************* Authorizer Configuration ***********/\n   132\u2192    public static final String AUTHORIZER_CLASS_NAME_CONFIG = \"authorizer.class.name\";\n   133\u2192    public static final String AUTHORIZER_CLASS_NAME_DEFAULT = \"\";\n   134\u2192    public static final String AUTHORIZER_CLASS_NAME_DOC = \"The fully qualified name of a class that implements <code>\" +\n   135\u2192           Authorizer.class.getName() + \"</code> interface, which is used by the broker for authorization.\";\n   136\u2192    public static final String EARLY_START_LISTENERS_CONFIG = \"early.start.listeners\";\n   137\u2192    public static final String EARLY_START_LISTENERS_DOC = \"A comma-separated list of listener names which may be started before the authorizer has finished \" +\n   138\u2192            \"initialization. This is useful when the authorizer is dependent on the cluster itself for bootstrapping, as is the case for \" +\n   139\u2192            \"the StandardAuthorizer (which stores ACLs in the metadata log.) By default, all listeners included in controller.listener.names \" +\n   140\u2192            \"will also be early start listeners. A listener should not appear in this list if it accepts external traffic.\";\n   141\u2192    public static final ConfigDef CONFIG_DEF =  new ConfigDef()\n   142\u2192            .define(BROKER_ID_GENERATION_ENABLE_CONFIG, BOOLEAN, BROKER_ID_GENERATION_ENABLE_DEFAULT, MEDIUM, BROKER_ID_GENERATION_ENABLE_DOC)\n   143\u2192            .define(RESERVED_BROKER_MAX_ID_CONFIG, INT, RESERVED_BROKER_MAX_ID_DEFAULT, atLeast(0), MEDIUM, RESERVED_BROKER_MAX_ID_DOC)\n   144\u2192            .define(BROKER_ID_CONFIG, INT, BROKER_ID_DEFAULT, HIGH, BROKER_ID_DOC)\n   145\u2192            .define(MESSAGE_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG, INT, LogConfig.DEFAULT_MAX_MESSAGE_BYTES, atLeast(0), HIGH, MESSAGE_MAX_BYTES_DOC)\n   146\u2192            .define(NUM_IO_THREADS_CONFIG, INT, NUM_IO_THREADS_DEFAULT, atLeast(1), HIGH, NUM_IO_THREADS_DOC)\n   147\u2192            .define(NUM_REPLICA_ALTER_LOG_DIRS_THREADS_CONFIG, INT, null, HIGH, NUM_REPLICA_ALTER_LOG_DIRS_THREADS_DOC)\n   148\u2192            .define(BACKGROUND_THREADS_CONFIG, INT, BACKGROUND_THREADS_DEFAULT, atLeast(1), HIGH, BACKGROUND_THREADS_DOC)\n   149\u2192            .define(REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG, INT, REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_DEFAULT, HIGH, REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_DOC)\n   150\u2192            .define(SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG, LONG, DEFAULT_SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MS, MEDIUM, SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MS_DOC)\n   151\u2192            .define(SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MAX_MS_CONFIG, LONG, SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MAX_MS, MEDIUM, SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MAX_MS_DOC)\n   152\u2192            /************* Authorizer Configuration ***********/\n   153\u2192            .define(AUTHORIZER_CLASS_NAME_CONFIG, STRING, AUTHORIZER_CLASS_NAME_DEFAULT, new ConfigDef.NonNullValidator(), LOW, AUTHORIZER_CLASS_NAME_DOC)\n   154\u2192            .define(EARLY_START_LISTENERS_CONFIG, STRING, null,  HIGH, EARLY_START_LISTENERS_DOC)\n   155\u2192            /************ Rack Configuration ******************/\n   156\u2192            .define(BROKER_RACK_CONFIG, STRING, null, MEDIUM, BROKER_RACK_DOC)\n   157\u2192            /** ********* Controlled shutdown configuration ***********/\n   158\u2192            .define(CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_MAX_RETRIES_CONFIG, INT, CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_MAX_RETRIES_DEFAULT, MEDIUM, CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_MAX_RETRIES_DOC)\n   159\u2192            .define(CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_RETRY_BACKOFF_MS_CONFIG, LONG, CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_RETRY_BACKOFF_MS_DEFAULT, MEDIUM, CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_RETRY_BACKOFF_MS_DOC)\n   160\u2192            .define(CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_ENABLE_CONFIG, BOOLEAN, CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_ENABLE_DEFAULT, MEDIUM, CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_ENABLE_DOC)\n   161\u2192            .define(DELETE_TOPIC_ENABLE_CONFIG, BOOLEAN, DELETE_TOPIC_ENABLE_DEFAULT, HIGH, DELETE_TOPIC_ENABLE_DOC)\n   162\u2192            .define(COMPRESSION_TYPE_CONFIG, STRING, LogConfig.DEFAULT_COMPRESSION_TYPE, ConfigDef.ValidString.in(BrokerCompressionType.names().toArray(new String[0])), HIGH, COMPRESSION_TYPE_DOC)\n   163\u2192            .define(COMPRESSION_GZIP_LEVEL_CONFIG, INT, CompressionType.GZIP.defaultLevel(), CompressionType.GZIP.levelValidator(), MEDIUM, COMPRESSION_GZIP_LEVEL_DOC)\n   164\u2192            .define(COMPRESSION_LZ4_LEVEL_CONFIG, INT, CompressionType.LZ4.defaultLevel(), CompressionType.LZ4.levelValidator(), MEDIUM, COMPRESSION_LZ4_LEVEL_DOC)\n   165\u2192            .define(COMPRESSION_ZSTD_LEVEL_CONFIG, INT, CompressionType.ZSTD.defaultLevel(), CompressionType.ZSTD.levelValidator(), MEDIUM, COMPRESSION_ZSTD_LEVEL_DOC)\n   166\u2192            /** ********* Fetch Configuration **************/\n   167\u2192            .define(MAX_INCREMENTAL_FETCH_SESSION_CACHE_SLOTS_CONFIG, INT, MAX_INCREMENTAL_FETCH_SESSION_CACHE_SLOTS_DEFAULT, atLeast(0), MEDIUM, MAX_INCREMENTAL_FETCH_SESSION_CACHE_SLOTS_DOC)\n   168\u2192            .define(FETCH_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG, INT, FETCH_MAX_BYTES_DEFAULT, atLeast(1024), MEDIUM, FETCH_MAX_BYTES_DOC)\n   169\u2192\n   170\u2192            /** ********* Request Limit Configuration ***********/\n   171\u2192            .define(MAX_REQUEST_PARTITION_SIZE_LIMIT_CONFIG, INT, MAX_REQUEST_PARTITION_SIZE_LIMIT_DEFAULT, atLeast(1), MEDIUM, MAX_REQUEST_PARTITION_SIZE_LIMIT_DOC)\n   172\u2192            /** Internal Configurations **/\n   173\u2192            // This indicates whether unreleased APIs should be advertised by this node.\n   174\u2192            .defineInternal(UNSTABLE_API_VERSIONS_ENABLE_CONFIG, BOOLEAN, false, HIGH)\n   175\u2192            // This indicates whether unreleased MetadataVersions should be enabled on this node.\n   176\u2192            .defineInternal(UNSTABLE_FEATURE_VERSIONS_ENABLE_CONFIG, BOOLEAN, false, HIGH);\n   177\u2192}\n   178\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.
