"   200\u2192    props\n   201\u2192  }\n   202\u2192}\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192class DynamicBrokerConfig(private val kafkaConfig: KafkaConfig) extends Logging {\n   205\u2192\n   206\u2192  private[server] val staticBrokerConfigs = ConfigDef.convertToStringMapWithPasswordValues(kafkaConfig.originalsFromThisConfig).asScala\n   207\u2192  private[server] val staticDefaultConfigs = ConfigDef.convertToStringMapWithPasswordValues(KafkaConfig.defaultValues.asJava).asScala\n   208\u2192  private val dynamicBrokerConfigs = mutable.Map[String, String]()\n   209\u2192  private val dynamicDefaultConfigs = mutable.Map[String, String]()\n   210\u2192\n   211\u2192  // Use COWArrayList to prevent concurrent modification exception when an item is added by one thread to these\n   212\u2192  // collections, while another thread is iterating over them.\n   213\u2192  private[server] val reconfigurables = new CopyOnWriteArrayList[Reconfigurable]()\n   214\u2192  private val brokerReconfigurables = new CopyOnWriteArrayList[BrokerReconfigurable]()\n   215\u2192  private val lock = new ReentrantReadWriteLock\n   216\u2192  private var metricsReceiverPluginOpt: Option[ClientMetricsReceiverPlugin] = _\n   217\u2192  private var currentConfig: KafkaConfig = _\n   218\u2192  private val dynamicConfigPasswordEncoder = if (kafkaConfig.processRoles.isEmpty) {\n   219\u2192    maybeCreatePasswordEncoder(kafkaConfig.passwordEncoderSecret)\n   220\u2192  } else {\n   221\u2192    Some(PasswordEncoder.NOOP)\n   222\u2192  }\n   223\u2192\n   224\u2192  private[server] def initialize(zkClientOpt: Option[KafkaZkClient], clientMetricsReceiverPluginOpt: Option[ClientMetricsReceiverPlugin]): Unit = {\n   225\u2192    currentConfig = new KafkaConfig(kafkaConfig.props, false)\n   226\u2192    metricsReceiverPluginOpt = clientMetricsReceiverPluginOpt\n   227\u2192\n   228\u2192    zkClientOpt.foreach { zkClient =>\n   229\u2192      val adminZkClient = new AdminZkClient(zkClient)\n   230\u2192      updateDefaultConfig(adminZkClient.fetchEntityConfig(ConfigType.BROKER, ZooKeeperInternals.DEFAULT_STRING), false)\n   231\u2192      val props = adminZkClient.fetchEntityConfig(ConfigType.BROKER, kafkaConfig.brokerId.toString)\n   232\u2192      val brokerConfig = maybeReEncodePasswords(props, adminZkClient)\n   233\u2192      updateBrokerConfig(kafkaConfig.brokerId, brokerConfig)\n   234\u2192    }\n   235\u2192  }\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192  /**\n   238\u2192   * Clear all cached values. This is used to clear state on broker shutdown to avoid\n   239\u2192   * exceptions in tests when broker is restarted. These fields are re-initialized when\n   240\u2192   * broker starts up.\n   241\u2192   */\n   242\u2192  private[server] def clear(): Unit = {\n   243\u2192    dynamicBrokerConfigs.clear()\n   244\u2192    dynamicDefaultConfigs.clear()\n   245\u2192    reconfigurables.clear()\n   246\u2192    brokerReconfigurables.clear()\n   247\u2192  }\n   248\u2192\n   249\u2192  /**\n   250\u2192   * Add reconfigurables to be notified when a dynamic broker config is updated.\n   251\u2192   *\n   252\u2192   * `Reconfigurable` is the public API used by configurable plugins like metrics reporter\n   253\u2192   * and quota callbacks. These are reconfigured before `KafkaConfig` is updated so that\n   254\u2192   * the update can be aborted if `reconfigure()` fails with an exception.\n   255\u2192   *\n   256\u2192   * `BrokerReconfigurable` is used for internal reconfigurable classes. These are\n   257\u2192   * reconfigured after `KafkaConfig` is updated so that they can access `KafkaConfig`\n   258\u2192   * directly. They are provided both old and new configs.\n   259\u2192   */\n   260\u2192  def addReconfigurables(kafkaServer: KafkaBroker): Unit = {\n   261\u2192    kafkaServer.authorizer match {\n   262\u2192      case Some(authz: Reconfigurable) => addReconfigurable(authz)\n   263\u2192      case _ =>\n   264\u2192    }\n   265\u2192    addReconfigurable(kafkaServer.kafkaYammerMetrics)\n   266\u2192    addReconfigurable(new DynamicMetricsReporters(kafkaConfig.brokerId, kafkaServer.config, kafkaServer.metrics, kafkaServer.clusterId))\n   267\u2192    addReconfigurable(new DynamicClientQuotaCallback(kafkaServer.quotaManagers, kafkaServer.config))\n   268\u2192\n   269\u2192    addBrokerReconfigurable(new BrokerDynamicThreadPool(kafkaServer))\n   270\u2192    addBrokerReconfigurable(new DynamicLogConfig(kafkaServer.logManager, kafkaServer))\n   271\u2192    addBrokerReconfigurable(new DynamicListenerConfig(kafkaServer))\n   272\u2192    addBrokerReconfigurable(kafkaServer.socketServer)\n   273\u2192    addBrokerReconfigurable(new DynamicProducerStateManagerConfig(kafkaServer.logManager.producerStateManagerConfig))\n   274\u2192    addBrokerReconfigurable(new DynamicRemoteLogConfig(kafkaServer))\n   275\u2192  }\n   276\u2192\n   277\u2192  /**\n   278\u2192   * Add reconfigurables to be notified when a dynamic controller config is updated.\n   279\u2192   */\n   280\u2192  def addReconfigurables(controller: ControllerServer): Unit = {\n   281\u2192    controller.authorizer match {\n   282\u2192      case Some(authz: Reconfigurable) => addReconfigurable(authz)\n   283\u2192      case _ =>\n   284\u2192    }\n   285\u2192    if (!kafkaConfig.processRoles.contains(ProcessRole.BrokerRole)) {\n   286\u2192      // only add these if the controller isn't also running the broker role\n   287\u2192      // because these would already be added via the broker in that case\n   288\u2192      addReconfigurable(controller.kafkaYammerMetrics)\n   289\u2192      addReconfigurable(new DynamicMetricsReporters(kafkaConfig.nodeId, controller.config, controller.metrics, controller.clusterId))\n   290\u2192    }\n   291\u2192    addReconfigurable(new DynamicClientQuotaCallback(controller.quotaManagers, controller.config))\n   292\u2192    addBrokerReconfigurable(new ControllerDynamicThreadPool(controller))\n   293\u2192    // TODO: addBrokerReconfigurable(new DynamicListenerConfig(controller))\n   294\u2192    addBrokerReconfigurable(controller.socketServer)\n   295\u2192  }\n   296\u2192\n   297\u2192  def addReconfigurable(reconfigurable: Reconfigurable): Unit = CoreUtils.inWriteLock(lock) {\n   298\u2192    verifyReconfigurableConfigs(reconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs.asScala)\n   299\u2192    reconfigurables.add(reconfigurable)\n   300\u2192  }\n   301\u2192\n   302\u2192  def addBrokerReconfigurable(reconfigurable: BrokerReconfigurable): Unit = CoreUtils.inWriteLock(lock) {\n   303\u2192    verifyReconfigurableConfigs(reconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs)\n   304\u2192    brokerReconfigurables.add(reconfigurable)\n   305\u2192  }\n   306\u2192\n   307\u2192  def removeReconfigurable(reconfigurable: Reconfigurable): Unit = CoreUtils.inWriteLock(lock) {\n   308\u2192    reconfigurables.remove(reconfigurable)\n   309\u2192  }\n   310\u2192\n   311\u2192  private def verifyReconfigurableConfigs(configNames: Set[String]): Unit = CoreUtils.inWriteLock(lock) {\n   312\u2192    val nonDynamic = configNames.intersect(DynamicConfig.Broker.nonDynamicProps)\n   313\u2192    require(nonDynamic.isEmpty, s\"Reconfigurable contains non-dynamic configs $nonDynamic\")\n   314\u2192  }\n   315\u2192\n   316\u2192  // Visibility for testing\n   317\u2192  private[server] def currentKafkaConfig: KafkaConfig = CoreUtils.inReadLock(lock) {\n   318\u2192    currentConfig\n   319\u2192  }\n   320\u2192\n   321\u2192  private[server] def currentDynamicBrokerConfigs: Map[String, String] = CoreUtils.inReadLock(lock) {\n   322\u2192    dynamicBrokerConfigs.clone()\n   323\u2192  }\n   324\u2192\n   325\u2192  private[server] def currentDynamicDefaultConfigs: Map[String, String] = CoreUtils.inReadLock(lock) {\n   326\u2192    dynamicDefaultConfigs.clone()\n   327\u2192  }\n   328\u2192\n   329\u2192  private[server] def clientMetricsReceiverPlugin: Option[ClientMetricsReceiverPlugin] = CoreUtils.inReadLock(lock) {\n   330\u2192    metricsReceiverPluginOpt\n   331\u2192  }\n   332\u2192\n   333\u2192  private[server] def updateBrokerConfig(brokerId: Int, persistentProps: Properties, doLog: Boolean = true): Unit = CoreUtils.inWriteLock(lock) {\n   334\u2192    try {\n   335\u2192      val props = fromPersistentProps(persistentProps, perBrokerConfig = true)\n   336\u2192      dynamicBrokerConfigs.clear()\n   337\u2192      dynamicBrokerConfigs ++= props.asScala\n   338\u2192      updateCurrentConfig(doLog)\n   339\u2192    } catch {\n   340\u2192      case e: Exception => error(s\"Per-broker configs of $brokerId could not be applied: ${persistentProps.keys()}\", e)\n   341\u2192    }\n   342\u2192  }\n   343\u2192\n   344\u2192  private[server] def updateDefaultConfig(persistentProps: Properties, doLog: Boolean = true): Unit = CoreUtils.inWriteLock(lock) {\n   345\u2192    try {\n   346\u2192      val props = fromPersistentProps(persistentProps, perBrokerConfig = false)\n   347\u2192      dynamicDefaultConfigs.clear()\n   348\u2192      dynamicDefaultConfigs ++= props.asScala\n   349\u2192      updateCurrentConfig(doLog)\n   350\u2192    } catch {\n   351\u2192      case e: Exception => error(s\"Cluster default configs could not be applied: ${persistentProps.keys()}\", e)\n   352\u2192    }\n   353\u2192  }\n   354\u2192\n   355\u2192  /**\n   356\u2192   * All config updates through ZooKeeper are triggered through actual changes in values stored in ZooKeeper.\n   357\u2192   * For some configs like SSL keystores and truststores, we also want to reload the store if it was modified\n   358\u2192   * in-place, even though the actual value of the file path and password haven't changed. This scenario alone\n   359\u2192   * is handled here when a config update request using admin client is processed by ZkAdminManager. If any of\n   360\u2192   * the SSL configs have changed, then the update will not be done here, but will be handled later when ZK\n   361\u2192   * changes are processed. At the moment, only listener configs are considered for reloading.\n   362\u2192   */\n   363\u2192  private[server] def reloadUpdatedFilesWithoutConfigChange(newProps: Properties): Unit = CoreUtils.inWriteLock(lock) {\n   364\u2192    reconfigurables.asScala\n   365\u2192      .filter(reconfigurable => ReloadableFileConfigs.exists(reconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs.contains))\n   366\u2192      .foreach {\n   367\u2192        case reconfigurable: ListenerReconfigurable =>\n   368\u2192          val kafkaProps = validatedKafkaProps(newProps, perBrokerConfig = true)\n   369\u2192          val newConfig = new KafkaConfig(kafkaProps.asJava, false)\n   370\u2192          processListenerReconfigurable(reconfigurable, newConfig, Collections.emptyMap(), validateOnly = false, reloadOnly = true)\n   371\u2192        case reconfigurable =>\n   372\u2192          trace(s\"Files will not be reloaded without config change for $reconfigurable\")\n   373\u2192      }\n   374\u2192  }\n   375\u2192\n   376\u2192  private def maybeCreatePasswordEncoder(secret: Option[Password]): Option[PasswordEncoder] = {\n   377\u2192   secret.map { secret =>\n   378\u2192     PasswordEncoder.encrypting(secret,\n   379\u2192        kafkaConfig.passwordEncoderKeyFactoryAlgorithm,\n   380\u2192        kafkaConfig.passwordEncoderCipherAlgorithm,\n   381\u2192        kafkaConfig.passwordEncoderKeyLength,\n   382\u2192        kafkaConfig.passwordEncoderIterations)\n   383\u2192    }\n   384\u2192  }\n   385\u2192\n   386\u2192  private def passwordEncoder: PasswordEncoder = {\n   387\u2192    dynamicConfigPasswordEncoder.getOrElse(throw new ConfigException(\"Password encoder secret not configured\"))\n   388\u2192  }\n   389\u2192\n   390\u2192  private[server] def toPersistentProps(configProps: Properties, perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Properties = {\n   391\u2192    val props = configProps.clone().asInstanceOf[Properties]\n   392\u2192\n   393\u2192    def encodePassword(configName: String, value: String): Unit = {\n   394\u2192      if (value != null) {\n   395\u2192        if (!perBrokerConfig)\n   396\u2192          throw new ConfigException(\"Password config can be defined only at broker level\")\n   397\u2192        props.setProperty(configName, passwordEncoder.encode(new Password(value)))\n   398\u2192      }\n   399\u2192    }\n   400\u2192    configProps.asScala.forKeyValue { (name, value) =>\n   401\u2192      if (isPasswordConfig(name))\n   402\u2192        encodePassword(name, value)\n   403\u2192    }\n   404\u2192    props\n   405\u2192  }\n   406\u2192\n   407\u2192  private[server] def fromPersistentProps(persistentProps: Properties,\n   408\u2192                                          perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Properties = {\n   409\u2192    val props = persistentProps.clone().asInstanceOf[Properties]\n   410\u2192\n   411\u2192    // Remove all invalid configs from `props`\n   412\u2192    removeInvalidConfigs(props, perBrokerConfig)\n   413\u2192    def removeInvalidProps(invalidPropNames: Set[String], errorMessage: String): Unit = {\n   414\u2192      if (invalidPropNames.nonEmpty) {\n   415\u2192        invalidPropNames.foreach(props.remove)\n   416\u2192        error(s\"$errorMessage: $invalidPropNames\")\n   417\u2192      }\n   418\u2192    }\n   419\u2192    removeInvalidProps(nonDynamicConfigs(props), \"Non-dynamic configs configured in ZooKeeper will be ignored\")\n   420\u2192    removeInvalidProps(securityConfigsWithoutListenerPrefix(props),\n   421\u2192      \"Security configs can be dynamically updated only using listener prefix, base configs will be ignored\")\n   422\u2192    if (!perBrokerConfig)\n   423\u2192      removeInvalidProps(perBrokerConfigs(props), \"Per-broker configs defined at default cluster level will be ignored\")\n   424\u2192\n   425\u2192    def decodePassword(configName: String, value: String): Unit = {\n   426\u2192      if (value != null) {\n   427\u2192        try {\n   428\u2192          props.setProperty(configName, passwordEncoder.decode(value).value)\n   429\u2192        } catch {\n   430\u2192          case e: Exception =>\n   431\u2192            error(s\"Dynamic password config $configName could not be decoded, ignoring.\", e)\n   432\u2192            props.remove(configName)\n   433\u2192        }\n   434\u2192      }\n   435\u2192    }\n   436\u2192\n   437\u2192    props.asScala.forKeyValue { (name, value) =>\n   438\u2192      if (isPasswordConfig(name))\n   439\u2192        decodePassword(name, value)\n   440\u2192    }\n   441\u2192    props\n   442\u2192  }\n   443\u2192\n   444\u2192  // If the secret has changed, password.encoder.old.secret contains the old secret that was used\n   445\u2192  // to encode the configs in ZK. Decode passwords using the old secret and update ZK with values\n   446\u2192  // encoded using the current secret. Ignore any errors during decoding since old secret may not\n   447\u2192  // have been removed during broker restart.\n   448\u2192  private def maybeReEncodePasswords(persistentProps: Properties, adminZkClient: AdminZkClient): Properties = {\n   449\u2192    val props = persistentProps.clone().asInstanceOf[Properties]\n   450\u2192    if (props.asScala.keySet.exists(isPasswordConfig)) {\n   451\u2192      maybeCreatePasswordEncoder(kafkaConfig.passwordEncoderOldSecret).foreach { passwordDecoder =>\n   452\u2192        persistentProps.asScala.forKeyValue { (configName, value) =>\n   453\u2192          if (isPasswordConfig(configName) && value != null) {\n   454\u2192            val decoded = try {\n   455\u2192              Some(passwordDecoder.decode(value).value)\n   456\u2192            } catch {\n   457\u2192              case _: Exception =>\n   458\u2192                debug(s\"Dynamic password config $configName could not be decoded using old secret, new secret will be used.\")\n   459\u2192                None\n   460\u2192            }\n   461\u2192            decoded.foreach(value => props.put(configName, passwordEncoder.encode(new Password(value))))\n   462\u2192          }\n   463\u2192        }\n   464\u2192        adminZkClient.changeBrokerConfig(Some(kafkaConfig.brokerId), props)\n   465\u2192      }\n   466\u2192    }\n   467\u2192    props\n   468\u2192  }\n   469\u2192\n   470\u2192  /**\n   471\u2192   * Validate the provided configs `propsOverride` and return the full Kafka configs with\n   472\u2192   * the configured defaults and these overrides.\n   473\u2192   *\n   474\u2192   * Note: The caller must acquire the read or write lock before invoking this method.\n   475\u2192   */\n   476\u2192  private def validatedKafkaProps(propsOverride: Properties, perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Map[String, String] = {\n   477\u2192    val propsResolved = DynamicBrokerConfig.resolveVariableConfigs(propsOverride)\n   478\u2192    validateConfigs(propsResolved, perBrokerConfig)\n   479\u2192    val newProps = mutable.Map[String, String]()\n   480\u2192    newProps ++= staticBrokerConfigs\n   481\u2192    if (perBrokerConfig) {\n   482\u2192      overrideProps(newProps, dynamicDefaultConfigs)\n   483\u2192      overrideProps(newProps, propsResolved.asScala)\n   484\u2192    } else {\n   485\u2192      overrideProps(newProps, propsResolved.asScala)\n   486\u2192      overrideProps(newProps, dynamicBrokerConfigs)\n   487\u2192    }\n   488\u2192    newProps\n   489\u2192  }\n   490\u2192\n   491\u2192  private[server] def validate(props: Properties, perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Unit = CoreUtils.inReadLock(lock) {\n   492\u2192    val newProps = validatedKafkaProps(props, perBrokerConfig)\n   493\u2192    processReconfiguration(newProps, validateOnly = true)\n   494\u2192  }\n   495\u2192\n   496\u2192  private def removeInvalidConfigs(props: Properties, perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Unit = {\n   497\u2192    try {\n   498\u2192      validateConfigTypes(props)\n   499\u2192      props.asScala\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
172. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "find /workspace -name \"server.properties\" | head -3",
  "description": "Find server.properties example files"
}
Output
"/workspace/config/server.properties\n/workspace/config/kraft/server.properties\n/workspace/docker/examples/fixtures/file-input/server.properties"
173. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "find /workspace -path \"*/config/*\" -name \"*.java\" | grep -E \"ReplicationConfigs|ServerLogConfigs|KRaftConfigs\" | head -5",
  "description": "Find additional config definition files"
}
Output
"/workspace/server/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/config/ReplicationConfigs.java\n/workspace/server/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/config/KRaftConfigs.java\n/workspace/server-common/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/config/ServerLogConfigs.java"
174. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/config/server.properties",
  "limit": 150
}
Output
"     1\u2192# Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n     2\u2192# contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with\n     3\u2192# this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n     4\u2192# The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n     5\u2192# (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n     6\u2192# the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192#\n     8\u2192#    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192#\n    10\u2192# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192# distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192# limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192#\n    17\u2192# This configuration file is intended for use in ZK-based mode, where Apache ZooKeeper is required.\n    18\u2192# See kafka.server.KafkaConfig for additional details and defaults\n    19\u2192#\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192############################# Server Basics #############################\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192# The id of the broker. This must be set to a unique integer for each broker.\n    24\u2192broker.id=0\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192############################# Socket Server Settings #############################\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192# The address the socket server listens on. If not configured, the host name will be equal to the value of\n    29\u2192# java.net.InetAddress.getCanonicalHostName(), with PLAINTEXT listener name, and port 9092.\n    30\u2192#   FORMAT:\n    31\u2192#     listeners = listener_name://host_name:port\n    32\u2192#   EXAMPLE:\n    33\u2192#     listeners = PLAINTEXT://your.host.name:9092\n    34\u2192#listeners=PLAINTEXT://:9092\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192# Listener name, hostname and port the broker will advertise to clients.\n    37\u2192# If not set, it uses the value for \"listeners\".\n    38\u2192#advertised.listeners=PLAINTEXT://your.host.name:9092\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192# Maps listener names to security protocols, the default is for them to be the same. See the config documentation for more details\n    41\u2192#listener.security.protocol.map=PLAINTEXT:PLAINTEXT,SSL:SSL,SASL_PLAINTEXT:SASL_PLAINTEXT,SASL_SSL:SASL_SSL\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192# The number of threads that the server uses for receiving requests from the network and sending responses to the network\n    44\u2192num.network.threads=3\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192# The number of threads that the server uses for processing requests, which may include disk I/O\n    47\u2192num.io.threads=8\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192# The send buffer (SO_SNDBUF) used by the socket server\n    50\u2192socket.send.buffer.bytes=102400\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192# The receive buffer (SO_RCVBUF) used by the socket server\n    53\u2192socket.receive.buffer.bytes=102400\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192# The maximum size of a request that the socket server will accept (protection against OOM)\n    56\u2192socket.request.max.bytes=104857600\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192############################# Log Basics #############################\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192# A comma separated list of directories under which to store log files\n    62\u2192log.dirs=/tmp/kafka-logs\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192# The default number of log partitions per topic. More partitions allow greater\n    65\u2192# parallelism for consumption, but this will also result in more files across\n    66\u2192# the brokers.\n    67\u2192num.partitions=1\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192# The number of threads per data directory to be used for log recovery at startup and flushing at shutdown.\n    70\u2192# This value is recommended to be increased for installations with data dirs located in RAID array.\n    71\u2192num.recovery.threads.per.data.dir=1\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192############################# Internal Topic Settings  #############################\n    74\u2192# The replication factor for the group metadata internal topics \"__consumer_offsets\" and \"__transaction_state\"\n    75\u2192# For anything other than development testing, a value greater than 1 is recommended to ensure availability such as 3.\n    76\u2192offsets.topic.replication.factor=1\n    77\u2192transaction.state.log.replication.factor=1\n    78\u2192transaction.state.log.min.isr=1\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192############################# Log Flush Policy #############################\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192# Messages are immediately written to the filesystem but by default we only fsync() to sync\n    83\u2192# the OS cache lazily. The following configurations control the flush of data to disk.\n    84\u2192# There are a few important trade-offs here:\n    85\u2192#    1. Durability: Unflushed data may be lost if you are not using replication.\n    86\u2192#    2. Latency: Very large flush intervals may lead to latency spikes when the flush does occur as there will be a lot of data to flush.\n    87\u2192#    3. Throughput: The flush is generally the most expensive operation, and a small flush interval may lead to excessive seeks.\n    88\u2192# The settings below allow one to configure the flush policy to flush data after a period of time or\n    89\u2192# every N messages (or both). This can be done globally and overridden on a per-topic basis.\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192# The number of messages to accept before forcing a flush of data to disk\n    92\u2192#log.flush.interval.messages=10000\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192# The maximum amount of time a message can sit in a log before we force a flush\n    95\u2192#log.flush.interval.ms=1000\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192############################# Log Retention Policy #############################\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192# The following configurations control the disposal of log segments. The policy can\n   100\u2192# be set to delete segments after a period of time, or after a given size has accumulated.\n   101\u2192# A segment will be deleted whenever *either* of these criteria are met. Deletion always happens\n   102\u2192# from the end of the log.\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192# The minimum age of a log file to be eligible for deletion due to age\n   105\u2192log.retention.hours=168\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192# A size-based retention policy for logs. Segments are pruned from the log unless the remaining\n   108\u2192# segments drop below log.retention.bytes. Functions independently of log.retention.hours.\n   109\u2192#log.retention.bytes=1073741824\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192# The maximum size of a log segment file. When this size is reached a new log segment will be created.\n   112\u2192#log.segment.bytes=1073741824\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192# The interval at which log segments are checked to see if they can be deleted according\n   115\u2192# to the retention policies\n   116\u2192log.retention.check.interval.ms=300000\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192############################# Zookeeper #############################\n   119\u2192\n   120\u2192# Zookeeper connection string (see zookeeper docs for details).\n   121\u2192# This is a comma separated host:port pairs, each corresponding to a zk\n   122\u2192# server. e.g. \"127.0.0.1:3000,127.0.0.1:3001,127.0.0.1:3002\".\n   123\u2192# You can also append an optional chroot string to the urls to specify the\n   124\u2192# root directory for all kafka znodes.\n   125\u2192zookeeper.connect=localhost:2181\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192# Timeout in ms for connecting to zookeeper\n   128\u2192zookeeper.connection.timeout.ms=18000\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192############################# Group Coordinator Settings #############################\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192# The following configuration specifies the time, in milliseconds, that the GroupCoordinator will delay the initial consumer rebalance.\n   134\u2192# The rebalance will be further delayed by the value of group.initial.rebalance.delay.ms as new members join the group, up to a maximum of max.poll.interval.ms.\n   135\u2192# The default value for this is 3 seconds.\n   136\u2192# We override this to 0 here as it makes for a better out-of-the-box experience for development and testing.\n   137\u2192# However, in production environments the default value of 3 seconds is more suitable as this will help to avoid unnecessary, and potentially expensive, rebalances during application startup.\n   138\u2192group.initial.rebalance.delay.ms=0\n   139\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
175. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/server/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/config/KRaftConfigs.java",
  "limit": 150
}
Output
"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n     3\u2192 * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n     4\u2192 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n     5\u2192 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n     6\u2192 * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n     7\u2192 * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     8\u2192 *\n     9\u2192 *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n    10\u2192 *\n    11\u2192 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    12\u2192 * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    13\u2192 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    14\u2192 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    15\u2192 * limitations under the License.\n    16\u2192 */\n    17\u2192package org.apache.kafka.server.config;\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef;\n    20\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.config.SaslConfigs;\n    21\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.record.Records;\n    22\u2192import org.apache.kafka.storage.internals.log.LogConfig;\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192import java.util.Collections;\n    25\u2192import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit;\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Importance.HIGH;\n    28\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Importance.LOW;\n    29\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Importance.MEDIUM;\n    30\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Range.atLeast;\n    31\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.BOOLEAN;\n    32\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.INT;\n    33\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.LIST;\n    34\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.LONG;\n    35\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.STRING;\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192public class KRaftConfigs {\n    38\u2192    /** KRaft mode configs */\n    39\u2192    public static final String PROCESS_ROLES_CONFIG = \"process.roles\";\n    40\u2192    public static final String PROCESS_ROLES_DOC = \"The roles that this process plays: 'broker', 'controller', or 'broker,controller' if it is both. \" +\n    41\u2192            \"This configuration is only applicable for clusters in KRaft (Kafka Raft) mode (instead of ZooKeeper). Leave this config undefined or empty for ZooKeeper clusters.\";\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192    public static final String INITIAL_BROKER_REGISTRATION_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG = \"initial.broker.registration.timeout.ms\";\n    44\u2192    public static final int INITIAL_BROKER_REGISTRATION_TIMEOUT_MS_DEFAULT = 60000;\n    45\u2192    public static final String INITIAL_BROKER_REGISTRATION_TIMEOUT_MS_DOC = \"When initially registering with the controller quorum, the number of milliseconds to wait before declaring failure and exiting the broker process.\";\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192    public static final String BROKER_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG = \"broker.heartbeat.interval.ms\";\n    48\u2192    public static final int BROKER_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS_DEFAULT = 2000;\n    49\u2192    public static final String BROKER_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS_DOC = \"The length of time in milliseconds between broker heartbeats. Used when running in KRaft mode.\";\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192    public static final String BROKER_SESSION_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG = \"broker.session.timeout.ms\";\n    52\u2192    public static final int BROKER_SESSION_TIMEOUT_MS_DEFAULT = 9000;\n    53\u2192    public static final String BROKER_SESSION_TIMEOUT_MS_DOC = \"The length of time in milliseconds that a broker lease lasts if no heartbeats are made. Used when running in KRaft mode.\";\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192    public static final String NODE_ID_CONFIG = \"node.id\";\n    57\u2192    public static final int EMPTY_NODE_ID = -1;\n    58\u2192    public static final String NODE_ID_DOC = \"The node ID associated with the roles this process is playing when <code>process.roles</code> is non-empty. \" +\n    59\u2192            \"This is required configuration when running in KRaft mode.\";\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192    public static final String METADATA_LOG_DIR_CONFIG = \"metadata.log.dir\";\n    62\u2192    public static final String METADATA_LOG_DIR_DOC = \"This configuration determines where we put the metadata log for clusters in KRaft mode. \" +\n    63\u2192            \"If it is not set, the metadata log is placed in the first log directory from log.dirs.\";\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192    public static final String METADATA_SNAPSHOT_MAX_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG = \"metadata.log.max.snapshot.interval.ms\";\n    66\u2192    public static final long METADATA_SNAPSHOT_MAX_INTERVAL_MS_DEFAULT = TimeUnit.HOURS.toMillis(1);\n    67\u2192    public static final String METADATA_SNAPSHOT_MAX_NEW_RECORD_BYTES_CONFIG = \"metadata.log.max.record.bytes.between.snapshots\";\n    68\u2192    public static final int METADATA_SNAPSHOT_MAX_NEW_RECORD_BYTES = 20 * 1024 * 1024;\n    69\u2192    public static final String METADATA_SNAPSHOT_MAX_NEW_RECORD_BYTES_DOC = \"This is the maximum number of bytes in the log between the latest \" +\n    70\u2192            \"snapshot and the high-watermark needed before generating a new snapshot. The default value is \" +\n    71\u2192            METADATA_SNAPSHOT_MAX_NEW_RECORD_BYTES + \". To generate snapshots based on the time elapsed, see the <code>\" +\n    72\u2192            METADATA_SNAPSHOT_MAX_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG + \"</code> configuration. The Kafka node will generate a snapshot when \" +\n    73\u2192            \"either the maximum time interval is reached or the maximum bytes limit is reached.\";\n    74\u2192    public static final String METADATA_SNAPSHOT_MAX_INTERVAL_MS_DOC = \"This is the maximum number of milliseconds to wait to generate a snapshot \" +\n    75\u2192            \"if there are committed records in the log that are not included in the latest snapshot. A value of zero disables \" +\n    76\u2192            \"time based snapshot generation. The default value is \" + METADATA_SNAPSHOT_MAX_INTERVAL_MS_DEFAULT + \". To generate \" +\n    77\u2192            \"snapshots based on the number of metadata bytes, see the <code>\" + METADATA_SNAPSHOT_MAX_NEW_RECORD_BYTES_CONFIG + \"</code> \" +\n    78\u2192            \"configuration. The Kafka node will generate a snapshot when either the maximum time interval is reached or the \" +\n    79\u2192            \"maximum bytes limit is reached.\";\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192    public static final String CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG = \"controller.listener.names\";\n    82\u2192    public static final String CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_DOC = \"A comma-separated list of the names of the listeners used by the controller. This is required \" +\n    83\u2192            \"if running in KRaft mode. When communicating with the controller quorum, the broker will always use the first listener in this list.\\n \" +\n    84\u2192            \"Note: The ZooKeeper-based controller should not set this configuration.\";\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192    public static final String SASL_MECHANISM_CONTROLLER_PROTOCOL_CONFIG = \"sasl.mechanism.controller.protocol\";\n    87\u2192    public static final String SASL_MECHANISM_CONTROLLER_PROTOCOL_DOC = \"SASL mechanism used for communication with controllers. Default is GSSAPI.\";\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192    public static final String METADATA_LOG_SEGMENT_MIN_BYTES_CONFIG = \"metadata.log.segment.min.bytes\";\n    90\u2192    public static final String METADATA_LOG_SEGMENT_MIN_BYTES_DOC = \"Override the minimum size for a single metadata log file. This should be used for testing only.\";\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192    public static final String METADATA_LOG_SEGMENT_BYTES_CONFIG = \"metadata.log.segment.bytes\";\n    93\u2192    public static final String METADATA_LOG_SEGMENT_BYTES_DOC = \"The maximum size of a single metadata log file.\";\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192    public static final String METADATA_LOG_SEGMENT_MILLIS_CONFIG = \"metadata.log.segment.ms\";\n    96\u2192    public static final String METADATA_LOG_SEGMENT_MILLIS_DOC = \"The maximum time before a new metadata log file is rolled out (in milliseconds).\";\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192    public static final String METADATA_MAX_RETENTION_BYTES_CONFIG = \"metadata.max.retention.bytes\";\n    99\u2192    public static final int METADATA_MAX_RETENTION_BYTES_DEFAULT = 100 * 1024 * 1024;\n   100\u2192    public static final String METADATA_MAX_RETENTION_BYTES_DOC = \"The maximum combined size of the metadata log and snapshots before deleting old \" +\n   101\u2192            \"snapshots and log files. Since at least one snapshot must exist before any logs can be deleted, this is a soft limit.\";\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192    public static final String METADATA_MAX_RETENTION_MILLIS_CONFIG = \"metadata.max.retention.ms\";\n   104\u2192    public static final String METADATA_MAX_RETENTION_MILLIS_DOC = \"The number of milliseconds to keep a metadata log file or snapshot before \" +\n   105\u2192            \"deleting it. Since at least one snapshot must exist before any logs can be deleted, this is a soft limit.\";\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192    public static final String METADATA_MAX_IDLE_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG = \"metadata.max.idle.interval.ms\";\n   108\u2192    public static final int METADATA_MAX_IDLE_INTERVAL_MS_DEFAULT = 500;\n   109\u2192    public static final String METADATA_MAX_IDLE_INTERVAL_MS_DOC = \"This configuration controls how often the active \" +\n   110\u2192            \"controller should write no-op records to the metadata partition. If the value is 0, no-op records \" +\n   111\u2192            \"are not appended to the metadata partition. The default value is \" + METADATA_MAX_IDLE_INTERVAL_MS_DEFAULT;\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192    public static final String SERVER_MAX_STARTUP_TIME_MS_CONFIG = \"server.max.startup.time.ms\";\n   114\u2192    public static final long SERVER_MAX_STARTUP_TIME_MS_DEFAULT = Long.MAX_VALUE;\n   115\u2192    public static final String SERVER_MAX_STARTUP_TIME_MS_DOC = \"The maximum number of milliseconds we will wait for the server to come up. \" +\n   116\u2192            \"By default there is no limit. This should be used for testing only.\";\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192    /** ZK to KRaft Migration configs */\n   119\u2192    public static final String MIGRATION_ENABLED_CONFIG = \"zookeeper.metadata.migration.enable\";\n   120\u2192    public static final String MIGRATION_ENABLED_DOC = \"Enable ZK to KRaft migration\";\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192    public static final String MIGRATION_METADATA_MIN_BATCH_SIZE_CONFIG = \"zookeeper.metadata.migration.min.batch.size\";\n   123\u2192    public static final int MIGRATION_METADATA_MIN_BATCH_SIZE_DEFAULT = 200;\n   124\u2192 eligible leader replicas configs */\n   127\u2192    public static final String ELR_ENABLED_CONFIG = \"eligible.leader.replicas.enable\";\n   128\u2192    public static final String ELR_ENABLED_DOC = \"Enable the Eligible leader replicas\";\n   129\u2192    public static final ConfigDef CONFIG_DEF =  new ConfigDef()\n   130\u2192            .define(METADATA_SNAPSHOT_MAX_NEW_RECORD_BYTES_CONFIG, METADATA_SNAPSHOT_MAX_INTERVAL_MS_DEFAULT, atLeast(0), HIGH, METADATA_SNAPSHOT_MAX_INTERVAL_MS_DOC)\n   132\u2192            .define(PROCESS_ROLES_CONFIG, LIST, Collections.emptyList(), ConfigDef.ValidList.in(\"broker\", \"controller\"), HIGH, PROCESS_ROLES_DOC)\n   133\u2192            .define(NODE_ID_CONFIG, INT, EMPTY_NODE_ID, null, HIGH, NODE_ID_DOC)\n   134\u2192            .define(INITIAL_BROKER_REGISTRATION_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG, INT, INITIAL_BROKER_REGISTRATION_TIMEOUT_MS_DEFAULT, null, MEDIUM, INITIAL_BROKER_REGISTRATION_TIMEOUT_MS_DOC)\n   135\u2192            .define(BROKER_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG, INT, BROKER_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS_DEFAULT, null, MEDIUM, BROKER_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS_DOC)\n   136\u2192            .define(BROKER_SESSION_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG, INT, BROKER_SESSION_TIMEOUT_MS_DEFAULT, null, MEDIUM, BROKER_SESSION_TIMEOUT_MS_DOC)\n   137\u2192            .define(CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG, STRING, null, null, HIGH, CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_DOC)\n   138\u2192            .define(SASL_MECHANISM_CONTROLLER_PROTOCOL_CONFIG, STRING, SaslConfigs.DEFAULT_SASL_MECHANISM, null, HIGH, .defineInternal(METADATA_LOG_SEGMENT_MIN_BYTES_CONFIG, INT, 8 * 1024 * 1024, atLeast(Records.LOG_OVERHEAD), HIGH, METADATA_LOG_SEGMENT_MIN_BYTES_DOC)\n   142\u2192 52\u2192\n    53\u2192    public static final String REPLICA_SOCKET_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG = \"replica.socket.timeout.ms\";\n    54\u2192    public static final int REPLICA_SOCKET_TIMEOUT_MS_DEFAULT = 30 * This value should always be less than the \" +\n    71\u2192            \"replica.lag.time.max.ms at all times to prevent frequent shrinking of ISR for low throughput topics\";\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192    public static final String REPLICA_FETCH_MIN_BYTES_CONFIG = \"replica.fetch.min.bytes\";\n    74\u2192    public static final int REPLICA_FETCH_MIN_BYTES_DEFAULT = 1;\n    75\u2192    public static final String REPLICA_FETCH_MIN_BYTES_DOC = \"Minimum bytes expected for each fetch response. "   500\u2192    } catch {\n   501\u2192      case e: Exception =>\n   502\u2192        val invalidProps = props.asScala.filter { case (k, v) =>\n   503\u2192          val props1 = new Properties\n   504\u2192          props1.put(k, v)\n   505\u2192          try {\n   506\u2192            validateConfigTypes(props1)\n   507\u2192            false\n   508\u2192          } catch {\n   509\u2192            case _: Exception => true\n   510\u2192          }\n   511\u2192        }\n   512\u2192        invalidProps.keys.foreach(props.remove)\n   513\u2192        val configSource = if (perBrokerConfig) \"broker\" else \"default cluster\"\n   514\u2192        error(s\"Dynamic $configSource config contains invalid values in: ${invalidProps.keys}, these configs will be ignored\", e)\n   515\u2192    }\n   516\u2192  }\n   517\u2192\n   518\u2192  private[server] def maybeReconfigure(reconfigurable: Reconfigurable, oldConfig: KafkaConfig, newConfig: util.Map[String, _]): Unit = {\n   519\u2192    if (reconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs.asScala.exists(key => oldConfig.originals.get(key) != newConfig.get(key)))\n   520\u2192      reconfigurable.reconfigure(newConfig)\n   521\u2192  }\n   522\u2192\n   523\u2192  /**\n   524\u2192   * Returns the change in configurations between the new props and current props by returning a\n   525\u2192   * map of the changed configs, as well as the set of deleted keys\n   526\u2192   */\n   527\u2192  private def updatedConfigs(newProps: java.util.Map[String, _],\n   528\u2192                             currentProps: java.util.Map[String, _]): (mutable.Map[String, _], Set[String]) = {\n   529\u2192    val changeMap = newProps.asScala.filter {\n   530\u2192      case (k, v) => v != currentProps.get(k)\n   531\u2192    }\n   532\u2192    val deletedKeySet = currentProps.asScala.filter {\n   533\u2192      case (k, _) => !newProps.containsKey(k)\n   534\u2192    }.keySet\n   535\u2192    (changeMap, deletedKeySet)\n   536\u2192  }\n   537\u2192\n   538\u2192  /**\n   539\u2192    * Updates values in `props` with the new values from `propsOverride`. Synonyms of updated configs\n   540\u2192    * are removed from `props` to ensure that the config with the higher precedence is applied. For example,\n   541\u2192    * if `log.roll.ms` was defined in server.properties and `log.roll.hours` is configured dynamically,\n   542\u2192    * `log.roll.hours` from the dynamic configuration will be used and `log.roll.ms` will be removed from\n   543\u2192    * `props` (even though `log.roll.hours` is secondary to `log.roll.ms`).\n   544\u2192    */\n   545\u2192  private def overrideProps(props: mutable.Map[String, String], propsOverride: mutable.Map[String, String]): Unit = {\n   546\u2192    propsOverride.forKeyValue { (k, v) =>\n   547\u2192      // Remove synonyms of `k` to ensure the right precedence is applied. But disable `matchListenerOverride`\n   548\u2192      // so that base configs corresponding to listener configs are not removed. Base configs should not be removed\n   549\u2192      // since they may be used by other listeners. It is ok to retain them in `props` since base configs cannot be\n   550\u2192      // dynamically updated and listener-specific configs have the higher precedence.\n   551\u2192      brokerConfigSynonyms(k, matchListenerOverride = false).foreach(props.remove)\n   552\u2192      props.put(k, v)\n   553\u2192    }\n   554\u2192  }\n   555\u2192\n   556\u2192  private def updateCurrentConfig(doLog: Boolean): Unit = {\n   557\u2192    val newProps = mutable.Map[String, String]()\n   558\u2192    newProps ++= staticBrokerConfigs\n   559\u2192    overrideProps(newProps, dynamicDefaultConfigs)\n   560\u2192    overrideProps(newProps, dynamicBrokerConfigs)\n   561\u2192\n   562\u2192    val oldConfig = currentConfig\n   563\u2192    val (newConfig, brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate) = processReconfiguration(newProps, validateOnly = false, doLog)\n   564\u2192    if (newConfig ne currentConfig) {\n   565\u2192      currentConfig = newConfig\n   566\u2192      kafkaConfig.updateCurrentConfig(newConfig)\n   567\u2192\n   568\u2192      // Process BrokerReconfigurable updates after current config is updated\n   569\u2192      brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate.foreach(_.reconfigure(oldConfig, newConfig))\n   570\u2192    }\n   571\u2192  }\n   572\u2192\n   573\u2192  private def processReconfiguration(newProps: Map[String, String], validateOnly: Boolean, doLog: Boolean = false): (KafkaConfig, List[BrokerReconfigurable]) = {\n   574\u2192    val newConfig = new KafkaConfig(newProps.asJava, doLog)\n   575\u2192    val (changeMap, deletedKeySet) = updatedConfigs(newConfig.originalsFromThisConfig, currentConfig.originals)\n   576\u2192    if (changeMap.nonEmpty || deletedKeySet.nonEmpty) {\n   577\u2192      try {\n   578\u2192        val customConfigs = new util.HashMap[String, Object](newConfig.originalsFromThisConfig) // non-Kafka configs\n   579\u2192        newConfig.valuesFromThisConfig.keySet.forEach(k => customConfigs.remove(k))\n   580\u2192        reconfigurables.forEach {\n   581\u2192          case listenerReconfigurable: ListenerReconfigurable =>\n   582\u2192            processListenerReconfigurable(listenerReconfigurable, newConfig, customConfigs, validateOnly, reloadOnly = false)\n   583\u2192          case reconfigurable =>\n   584\u2192            if (needsReconfiguration(reconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs, changeMap.keySet, deletedKeySet))\n   585\u2192              processReconfigurable(reconfigurable, changeMap.keySet, newConfig.valuesFromThisConfig, customConfigs, validateOnly)\n   586\u2192        }\n   587\u2192\n   588\u2192        // BrokerReconfigurable updates are processed after config is updated. Only do the validation here.\n   589\u2192        val brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate = mutable.Buffer[BrokerReconfigurable]()\n   590\u2192        brokerReconfigurables.forEach { reconfigurable =>\n   591\u2192          if (needsReconfiguration(reconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs.asJava, changeMap.keySet, deletedKeySet)) {\n   592\u2192            reconfigurable.validateReconfiguration(newConfig)\n   593\u2192            if (!validateOnly)\n   594\u2192              brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate += reconfigurable\n   595\u2192          }\n   596\u2192        }\n   597\u2192        (newConfig, brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate.toList)\n   598\u2192      } catch {\n   599\u2192        case e: Exception =>\n   600\u2192          if (!validateOnly)\n   601\u2192            error(s\"Failed to update broker configuration with configs : \" +\n   602\u2192                  s\"${ConfigUtils.configMapToRedactedString(newConfig.originalsFromThisConfig, KafkaConfig.configDef)}\", e)\n   603\u2192          throw new ConfigException(\"Invalid dynamic configuration\", e)\n   604\u2192      }\n   605\u2192    }\n   606\u2192    else\n   607\u2192      (currentConfig, List.empty)\n   608\u2192  }\n   609\u2192\n   610\u2192  private def needsReconfiguration(reconfigurableConfigs: util.Set[String], updatedKeys: Set[String], deletedKeys: Set[String]): Boolean = {\n   611\u2192    reconfigurableConfigs.asScala.intersect(updatedKeys).nonEmpty ||\n   612\u2192      reconfigurableConfigs.asScala.intersect(deletedKeys).nonEmpty\n   613\u2192  }\n   614\u2192\n   615\u2192  private def processListenerReconfigurable(listenerReconfigurable: ListenerReconfigurable,\n   616\u2192                                            newConfig: KafkaConfig,\n   617\u2192                                            customConfigs: util.Map[String, Object],\n   618\u2192                                            validateOnly: Boolean,\n   619\u2192                                            reloadOnly:  Boolean): Unit = {\n   620\u2192    val listenerName = listenerReconfigurable.listenerName\n   621\u2192    val oldValues = currentConfig.valuesWithPrefixOverride(listenerName.configPrefix)\n   622\u2192    val newValues = newConfig.valuesFromThisConfigWithPrefixOverride(listenerName.configPrefix)\n   623\u2192    val (changeMap, deletedKeys) = updatedConfigs(newValues, oldValues)\n   624\u2192    val updatedKeys = changeMap.keySet\n   625\u2192    val configsChanged = needsReconfiguration(listenerReconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs, updatedKeys, deletedKeys)\n   626\u2192    // if `reloadOnly`, reconfigure if configs haven't changed. Otherwise reconfigure if configs have changed\n   627\u2192    if (reloadOnly != configsChanged)\n   628\u2192      processReconfigurable(listenerReconfigurable, updatedKeys, newValues, customConfigs, validateOnly)\n   629\u2192  }\n   630\u2192\n   631\u2192  private def processReconfigurable(reconfigurable: Reconfigurable,\n   632\u2192                                    updatedConfigNames: Set[String],\n   633\u2192                                    allNewConfigs: util.Map[String, _],\n   634\u2192                                    newCustomConfigs: util.Map[String, Object],\n   635\u2192                                    validateOnly: Boolean): Unit = {\n   636\u2192    val newConfigs = new util.HashMap[String, Object]\n   637\u2192    allNewConfigs.forEach((k, v) => newConfigs.put(k, v.asInstanceOf[AnyRef]))\n   638\u2192    newConfigs.putAll(newCustomConfigs)\n   639\u2192    try {\n   640\u2192      reconfigurable.validateReconfiguration(newConfigs)\n   641\u2192    } catch {\n   642\u2192      case e: ConfigException => throw e\n   643\u2192      case _: Exception =>\n   644\u2192        throw new ConfigException(s\"Validation of dynamic config update of $updatedConfigNames failed with class ${reconfigurable.getClass}\")\n   645\u2192    }\n   646\u2192\n   647\u2192    if (!validateOnly) {\n   648\u2192      info(s\"Reconfiguring $reconfigurable, updated configs: $updatedConfigNames \" +\n   649\u2192           s\"custom configs: ${ConfigUtils.configMapToRedactedString(newCustomConfigs, KafkaConfig.configDef)}\")\n   650\u2192      reconfigurable.reconfigure(newConfigs)\n   651\u2192    }\n   652\u2192  }\n   653\u2192}\n   654\u2192\n   655\u2192trait BrokerReconfigurable {\n   656\u2192\n   657\u2192  def reconfigurableConfigs: Set[String]\n   658\u2192\n   659\u2192  def validateReconfiguration(newConfig: KafkaConfig): Unit\n   660\u2192\n   661\u2192  def reconfigure(oldConfig: KafkaConfig, newConfig: KafkaConfig): Unit\n   662\u2192}\n   663\u2192\n   664\u2192object DynamicLogConfig {\n   665\u2192  /**\n   666\u2192   * The log configurations that are non-reconfigurable. This set contains the names you\n   667\u2192   * would use when setting a dynamic configuration on a topic, which are different than the\n   668\u2192   * corresponding broker configuration names.\n   669\u2192   *\n   670\u2192   * For now, message.format.version is not reconfigurable, since we need to check that\n   671\u2192   * the version is supported on all brokers in the cluster.\n   672\u2192   */\n   673\u2192  val NonReconfigrableLogConfigs: Set[String] = Set(TopicConfig.MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_CONFIG)\n   674\u2192\n   675\u2192  /**\n   676\u2192   * The broker configurations pertaining to logs that are reconfigurable. This set contains\n   677\u2192   * the names you would use when setting a static or dynamic broker configuration (not topic\n   678\u2192   * configuration).\n   679\u2192   */\n   680\u2192  val ReconfigurableConfigs: Set[String] =\n   681\u2192    ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.TOPIC_CONFIG_SYNONYMS.asScala.\n   682\u2192      filterNot(s => NonReconfigrableLogConfigs.contains(s._1)).values.toSet\n   683\u2192}\n   684\u2192\n   685\u2192class DynamicLogConfig(logManager: LogManager, server: KafkaBroker) extends BrokerReconfigurable with Logging {\n   686\u2192\n   687\u2192  override def reconfigurableConfigs: Set[String] = {\n   688\u2192    DynamicLogConfig.ReconfigurableConfigs\n   689\u2192  }\n   690\u2192\n   691\u2192  override def validateReconfiguration(newConfig: KafkaConfig): Unit = {\n   692\u2192    // For update of topic config overrides, only config names and types are validated\n   693\u2192    // Names and types have already been validated. For consistency with topic config\n   694\u2192    // validation, no additional validation is performed.\n   695\u2192\n   696\u2192    def validateLogLocalRetentionMs(): Unit = {\n   697\u2192      val logRetentionMs = newConfig.logRetentionTimeMillis\n   698\u2192      val logLocalRetentionMs: java.lang.Long = newConfig.remoteLogManagerConfig.logLocalRetentionMs\n   699\u2192      if (logRetentionMs != -1L && logLocalRetentionMs != -2L) {\n   700\u2192        if (logLocalRetentionMs == -1L) {\n   701\u2192          throw new ConfigException(RemoteLogManagerConfig.LOG_LOCAL_RETENTION_MS_PROP, logLocalRetentionMs,\n   702\u2192            s\"Value must not be -1 as ${ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG} value is set as $logRetentionMs.\")\n   703\u2192        }\n   704\u2192        if (logLocalRetentionMs > logRetentionMs) {\n   705\u2192          throw new ConfigException(RemoteLogManagerConfig.LOG_LOCAL_RETENTION_MS_PROP, logLocalRetentionMs,\n   706\u2192            s\"Value must not be more than ${ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG} property value: $logRetentionMs\")\n   707\u2192        }\n   708\u2192      }\n   709\u2192    }\n   710\u2192\n   711\u2192    def validateLogLocalRetentionBytes(): Unit = {\n   712\u2192      val logRetentionBytes = newConfig.logRetentionBytes\n   713\u2192      val logLocalRetentionBytes: java.lang.Long = newConfig.remoteLogManagerConfig.logLocalRetentionBytes\n   714\u2192      if (logRetentionBytes > -1 && logLocalRetentionBytes != -2) {\n   715\u2192        if (logLocalRetentionBytes == -1) {\n   716\u2192          throw new ConfigException(RemoteLogManagerConfig.LOG_LOCAL_RETENTION_BYTES_PROP, logLocalRetentionBytes,\n   717\u2192            s\"Value must not be -1 as ${ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_BYTES_CONFIG} value is set as $logRetentionBytes.\")\n   718\u2192        }\n   719\u2192        if (logLocalRetentionBytes > logRetentionBytes) {\n   720\u2192          throw new ConfigException(RemoteLogManagerConfig.LOG_LOCAL_RETENTION_BYTES_PROP, logLocalRetentionBytes,\n   721\u2192            s\"Value must not be more than ${ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_BYTES_CONFIG} property value: $logRetentionBytes\")\n   722\u2192        }\n   723\u2192      }\n   724\u2192    }\n   725\u2192\n   726\u2192    validateLogLocalRetentionMs()\n   727\u2192    validateLogLocalRetentionBytes()\n   728\u2192  }\n   729\u2192\n   730\u2192  private def updateLogsConfig(newBrokerDefaults: Map[String, Object]): Unit = {\n   731\u2192    logManager.brokerConfigUpdated()\n   732\u2192    logManager.allLogs.foreach { log =>\n   733\u2192      val props = mutable.Map.empty[Any, Any]\n   734\u2192      props ++= newBrokerDefaults\n   735\u2192      props ++= log.config.originals.asScala.filter { case (k, _) =>\n   736\u2192        log.config.overriddenConfigs.contains(k)\n   737\u2192      }\n   738\u2192\n   739\u2192      val logConfig = new LogConfig(props.asJava, log.config.overriddenConfigs)\n   740\u2192      log.updateConfig(logConfig)\n   741\u2192    }\n   742\u2192  }\n   743\u2192\n   744\u2192  override def reconfigure(oldConfig: KafkaConfig, newConfig: KafkaConfig): Unit = {\n   745\u2192    val originalLogConfig = logManager.currentDefaultConfig\n   746\u2192    val originalUncleanLeaderElectionEnable = originalLogConfig.uncleanLeaderElectionEnable\n   747\u2192    val newBrokerDefaults = new util.HashMap[String, Object](newConfig.extractLogConfigMap)\n   748\u2192    val originalLogConfigMap = originalLogConfig.originals()\n   749\u2192    DynamicLogConfig.NonReconfigrableLogConfigs.foreach(k => {\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
180. Bash @ -

{
  "command": "find /workspace -name \"Reconfigurable.java\" | head -3",
  "description": "Find Reconfigurable interface"
}
Output

182. Task @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Document process for adding new broker config",
  "prompt": "Explore how to add a new broker configuration parameter to Apache Kafka. Look at concrete examples and trace the complete process:\n\n1. **Define the Configuration:**\n   - Look at ServerConfigs, SocketServerConfigs, LogConfigs to see examples\n   - Find where config names are declared (public static final String)\n   - Find where config defaults are declared\n   - Find where documentation is written\n   - Look at the define() calls to see the pattern\n\n2. **Add Configuration Definition:**\n   - Look for how .define() is called in ConfigDef\n   - See how validators are specified\n   - See how importance levels are set\n   - Find examples with different validators\n\n3. **Integrate with ConfigDef:**\n   - Find how configs are merged in AbstractKafkaConfig\n   - Look for how new ConfigDef classes are incorporated\n   - See how existing configs reference each other\n\n4. **Create KafkaConfig Fields:**\n   - Look at KafkaConfig.scala to see the pattern\n   - Find how config values are accessed via properties\n   - Look for lazy val declarations\n\n5. **Dynamic Config Updates (if needed):**\n   - Find how to mark a config as dynamically reconfigurable\n   - Look at DynamicBrokerConfig for adding to AllDynamicConfigs\n   - Find the Reconfigurable component that should handle the update\n\n6. **Testing the New Config:**\n   - Look for KafkaConfigTest examples\n   - Find how validation is tested\n   - Find how dynamic updates are tested\n\nSpecific files to examine for examples:\n- /workspace/server/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/config/ServerConfigs.java\n- /workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/SocketServerConfigs.scala\n- /workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala\n- /workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/DynamicBrokerConfig.scala\n- Test files for config testing",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "aa45548d5234aee32",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me create a detailed exploration report:\n\n## Complete Guide: Adding a New Broker Configuration Parameter to Apache Kafka\n\nBased on my exploration of the Apache Kafka 3.9.0 codebase, here's a detailed step-by-step process for adding a new broker configuration parameter:\n\n---\n\n### **STEP 1: Define the Configuration Constants**\n\n**Location:** Choose the appropriate config class based on the parameter domain:\n- `/workspace/server/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/config/ServerConfigs.java` - General server configs\n- `/workspace/server-common/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/config/ServerLogConfigs.java` - Log-related configs\n- `/workspace/server/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/config/ReplicationConfigs.java` - Replication configs\n- `/workspace/server/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/network/SocketServerConfigs.java` - Socket/Network configs\n\n**Pattern to follow:**\n\n```java\n// Configuration name constant\npublic static final String YOUR_CONFIG_NAME = \"your.config.name\";\n\n// Default value constant\npublic static final int/String/boolean YOUR_CONFIG_NAME_DEFAULT = <default_value>;\n\n// Documentation constant\npublic static final String YOUR_CONFIG_NAME_DOC = \"Human-readable description of the config...\";\n```\n\n**Example from ServerConfigs.java (lines 38-40):**\n```java\npublic static final String RESERVED_BROKER_MAX_ID_CONFIG = \"reserved.broker.max.id\";\npublic static final int RESERVED_BROKER_MAX_ID_DEFAULT = 1000;\npublic static final String RESERVED_BROKER_MAX_ID_DOC = \"Max number that can be used for a broker.id\";\n```\n\n**Example from ServerLogConfigs.java (lines 32-34):**\n```java\npublic static final String NUM_PARTITIONS_CONFIG = \"num.partitions\";\npublic static final int NUM_PARTITIONS_DEFAULT = 1;\npublic static final String NUM_PARTITIONS_DOC = \"The default number of log partitions per topic\";\n```\n\n---\n\n### **STEP 2: Add Configuration Definition to ConfigDef**\n\n**Pattern:** Use the `CONFIG_DEF.define()` method chain with proper validators and importance levels.\n\n**Basic Structure:**\n```\n.define(CONFIG_NAME, TYPE, DEFAULT_VALUE, [VALIDATOR], IMPORTANCE, DOCUMENTATION)\n```\n\n**Type Options:**\n- `INT` - Integer values\n- `LONG` - Long values\n- `STRING` - String values\n- `BOOLEAN` - Boolean values\n- `CLASS` - Class type\n- `DOUBLE` - Double values\n\n**Validator Options:**\n\n1. **Range Validators (from `ConfigDef.Range`):**\n   ```java\n   import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Range.atLeast;\n   \n   .define(MY_CONFIG, INT, MY_DEFAULT, atLeast(0), HIGH, MY_DOC)  // value >= 0\n   .define(MY_CONFIG, INT, MY_DEFAULT, atLeast(1), HIGH, MY_DOC)  // value >= 1\n   ```\n\n2. **String Validators:**\n   ```java\n   // Valid string options\n   ConfigDef.ValidString.in(option1, option2, option3)\n   \n   // Example from ServerConfigs.java (line 162):\n   .define(COMPRESSION_TYPE_CONFIG, STRING, LogConfig.DEFAULT_COMPRESSION_TYPE, \n           ConfigDef.ValidString.in(BrokerCompressionType.names().toArray(new String[0])), \n           HIGH, COMPRESSION_TYPE_DOC)\n   ```\n\n3. **Custom Validators:**\n   ```java\n   // Example from ReplicationConfigs.java (line 174):\n   .define(INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION_CONFIG, STRING, \n           INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION_DEFAULT, \n           new MetadataVersionValidator(), \n           MEDIUM, INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION_DOC)\n   \n   .define(AUTHORIZER_CLASS_NAME_CONFIG, STRING, \n           AUTHORIZER_CLASS_NAME_DEFAULT, \n           new ConfigDef.NonNullValidator(), \n           LOW, AUTHORIZER_CLASS_NAME_DOC)\n   ```\n\n**Importance Levels (from `ConfigDef.Importance`):**\n- `HIGH` - Critical configuration\n- `MEDIUM` - Important but not critical\n- `LOW` - Optional configuration\n\n**Complete Example from ServerConfigs.java (lines 143-148):**\n```java\npublic static final ConfigDef CONFIG_DEF =  new ConfigDef()\n    .define(RESERVED_BROKER_MAX_ID_CONFIG, INT, RESERVED_BROKER_MAX_ID_DEFAULT, \n            atLeast(0), MEDIUM, RESERVED_BROKER_MAX_ID_DOC)\n    .define(BROKER_ID_CONFIG, INT, BROKER_ID_DEFAULT, HIGH, BROKER_ID_DOC)\n    .define(MESSAGE_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG, INT, LogConfig.DEFAULT_MAX_MESSAGE_BYTES, \n            atLeast(0), HIGH, MESSAGE_MAX_BYTES_DOC)\n    .define(NUM_IO_THREADS_CONFIG, INT, NUM_IO_THREADS_DEFAULT, \n            atLeast(1), HIGH, NUM_IO_THREADS_DOC)\n    .define(BACKGROUND_THREADS_CONFIG, INT, BACKGROUND_THREADS_DEFAULT, \n            atLeast(1), HIGH, BACKGROUND_THREADS_DOC);\n```\n\n---\n\n### **STEP 3: Integrate ConfigDef with AbstractKafkaConfig**\n\n**Location:** `/workspace/server/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/config/AbstractKafkaConfig.java`\n\n**How it works:** The `CONFIG_DEF` is merged from multiple config classes using `Utils.mergeConfigs()`.\n\n**From AbstractKafkaConfig.java (lines 45-68):**\n```java\npublic static final ConfigDef CONFIG_DEF = Utils.mergeConfigs(Arrays.asList(\n    RemoteLogManagerConfig.configDef(),\n    ZkConfigs.CONFIG_DEF,\n    ServerConfigs.CONFIG_DEF,        // Your new config goes here\n    KRaftConfigs.CONFIG_DEF,\n    SocketServerConfigs.CONFIG_DEF,\n    ReplicationConfigs.CONFIG_DEF,\n    // ... other configs\n));\n```\n\n**No action needed** if you added your config to an existing config class that's already merged. If you created a NEW config class, add it to this list.\n\n---\n\n### **STEP 4: Create KafkaConfig Field Accessors**\n\n**Location:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala`\n\n**Pattern:** Add a lazy val or property that retrieves the config value using getter methods.\n\n**Example from KafkaConfig.scala (lines 366-371):**\n```scala\ndef backgroundThreads = getInt(ServerConfigs.BACKGROUND_THREADS_CONFIG)\ndef numIoThreads = getInt(ServerConfigs.NUM_IO_THREADS_CONFIG)\ndef messageMaxBytes = getInt(ServerConfigs.MESSAGE_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG)\nval requestTimeoutMs = getInt(ServerConfigs.REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\nval connectionSetupTimeoutMs = getLong(ServerConfigs.SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\nval connectionSetupTimeoutMaxMs = getLong(ServerConfigs.SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MAX_MS_CONFIG)\n```\n\n**Getter Method Options:**\n- `getInt(configName)` - Get Integer value\n- `getLong(configName)` - Get Long value\n- `getBoolean(configName)` - Get Boolean value\n- `getString(configName)` - Get String value\n- `getList(configName)` - Get List value\n- `getDouble(configName)` - Get Double value\n\n**Using Option for nullable configs:**\n```scala\nval rack = Option(getString(ServerConfigs.BROKER_RACK_CONFIG))\nval replicaSelectorClassName = Option(getString(ReplicationConfigs.REPLICA_SELECTOR_CLASS_CONFIG))\n```\n\n---\n\n### **STEP 5: Mark as Dynamically Reconfigurable (if applicable)**\n\nIf your config can be updated without restarting the broker:\n\n**Location:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/DynamicBrokerConfig.scala`\n\n**Option A: Add to AllDynamicConfigs collection (line 92-100):**\n```scala\nval AllDynamicConfigs = DynamicSecurityConfigs ++\n    LogCleaner.ReconfigurableConfigs ++\n    DynamicLogConfig.ReconfigurableConfigs ++\n    DynamicThreadPool.ReconfigurableConfigs ++\n    Set(MetricConfigs.METRIC_REPORTER_CLASSES_CONFIG) ++\n    DynamicListenerConfig.ReconfigurableConfigs ++\n    SocketServer.ReconfigurableConfigs ++\n    DynamicProducerStateManagerConfig ++\n    DynamicRemoteLogConfig.ReconfigurableConfigs\n```\n\n**Option B: Create a specific Dynamic handler class:**\n\n**Example - DynamicThreadPool object (lines 769-803):**\n```scala\nobject DynamicThreadPool {\n  val ReconfigurableConfigs = Set(\n    ServerConfigs.NUM_IO_THREADS_CONFIG,\n    ReplicationConfigs.NUM_REPLICA_FETCHERS_CONFIG,\n    ServerLogConfigs.NUM_RECOVERY_THREADS_PER_DATA_DIR_CONFIG,\n    ServerConfigs.BACKGROUND_THREADS_CONFIG)\n\n  def validateReconfiguration(currentConfig: KafkaConfig, newConfig: KafkaConfig): Unit = {\n    newConfig.values.forEach { (k, v) =>\n      if (ReconfigurableConfigs.contains(k)) {\n        val newValue = v.asInstanceOf[Int]\n        val oldValue = getValue(currentConfig, k)\n        if (newValue != oldValue) {\n          if (newValue <= 0)\n            throw new ConfigException(s\"Dynamic thread count update failed for $k=$v\")\n          // Custom validation logic\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  def getValue(config: KafkaConfig, name: String): Int = {\n    name match {\n      case ServerConfigs.NUM_IO_THREADS_CONFIG => config.numIoThreads\n      case ReplicationConfigs.NUM_REPLICA_FETCHERS_CONFIG => config.numReplicaFetchers\n      case _ => throw new IllegalStateException(s\"Unexpected config $name\")\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Example - BrokerDynamicThreadPool handler (lines 821-841):**\n```scala\nclass BrokerDynamicThreadPool(server: KafkaBroker) extends BrokerReconfigurable {\n\n  override def reconfigurableConfigs: Set[String] = {\n    DynamicThreadPool.ReconfigurableConfigs\n  }\n\n  override def validateReconfiguration(newConfig: KafkaConfig): Unit = {\n    DynamicThreadPool.validateReconfiguration(server.config, newConfig)\n  }\n\n  override def reconfigure(oldConfig: KafkaConfig, newConfig: KafkaConfig): Unit = {\n    if (newConfig.numIoThreads != oldConfig.numIoThreads)\n      server.dataPlaneRequestHandlerPool.resizeThreadPool(newConfig.numIoThreads)\n    if (newConfig.numReplicaFetchers != oldConfig.numReplicaFetchers)\n      server.replicaManager.resizeFetcherThreadPool(newConfig.numReplicaFetchers)\n    // Apply other reconfigurations\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Key Components of BrokerReconfigurable trait (lines 655-662):**\n```scala\ntrait BrokerReconfigurable {\n  def reconfigurableConfigs: Set[String]  // Set of configs this handler manages\n  def validateReconfiguration(newConfig: KafkaConfig): Unit  // Validate changes\n  def reconfigure(oldConfig: KafkaConfig, newConfig: KafkaConfig): Unit  // Apply changes\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### **STEP 6: Register Reconfigurable Components**\n\n**Location:** `/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/DynamicBrokerConfig.scala` (lines 260-275)\n\nThe `addReconfigurables` method registers which components should be notified of config changes:\n\n```scala\ndef addReconfigurables(kafkaServer: KafkaServer): Unit = {\n    kafkaServer.authorizer match {\n      case Some(authz: Reconfigurable) => addReconfigurable(authz)\n      case _ =>\n    }\n    addReconfigurable(kafkaServer.kafkaYammerMetrics)\n    addReconfigurable(new DynamicMetricsReporters(kafkaConfig.brokerId, kafkaServer.config, kafkaServer.metrics, kafkaServer.clusterId))\n    addReconfigurable(new DynamicClientQuotaCallback(kafkaServer.quotaManagers, kafkaServer.config))\n\n    addBrokerReconfigurable(new BrokerDynamicThreadPool(kafkaServer))\n    addBrokerReconfigurable(new DynamicLogConfig(kafkaServer.logManager, kafkaServer))\n    addBrokerReconfigurable(new DynamicListenerConfig(kafkaServer))\n    addBrokerReconfigurable(kafkaServer.socketServer)\n    // Register your reconfigurable handler here\n    addBrokerReconfigurable(new YourDynamicConfigHandler(kafkaServer))\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### **STEP 7: Write Tests**\n\n**Location:** `/workspace/core/src/test/scala/unit/kafka/server/`\n\n**Example Test Pattern from DynamicBrokerConfigTest.scala (lines 135-200):**\n```scala\n@Test\ndef testUpdateDynamicThreadPool(): Unit = {\n  val origProps = TestUtils.createBrokerConfig(0, null, port = 8181)\n  origProps.put(ServerConfigs.NUM_IO_THREADS_CONFIG, \"4\")\n  origProps.put(SocketServerConfigs.NUM_NETWORK_THREADS_CONFIG, \"2\")\n\n  val config = KafkaConfig(origProps)\n  val serverMock = Mockito.mock(classOf[KafkaBroker])\n  val handlerPoolMock = Mockito.mock(classOf[KafkaRequestHandlerPool])\n\n  Mockito.when(serverMock.config).thenReturn(config)\n  Mockito.when(serverMock.dataPlaneRequestHandlerPool).thenReturn(handlerPoolMock)\n\n  config.dynamicConfig.initialize(None, None)\n  config.dynamicConfig.addBrokerReconfigurable(new BrokerDynamicThreadPool(serverMock))\n\n  val props = new Properties()\n  props.put(ServerConfigs.NUM_IO_THREADS_CONFIG, \"8\")\n  config.dynamicConfig.updateDefaultConfig(props)\n  \n  assertEquals(8, config.numIoThreads)\n  Mockito.verify(handlerPoolMock).resizeThreadPool(newSize = 8)\n}\n```\n\n**Test Configuration Tests from KafkaConfigTest.scala (lines 57-89):**\n```scala\n@Test\ndef testLogRetentionTimeHoursProvided(): Unit = {\n  val props = TestUtils.createBrokerConfig(0, TestUtils.MockZkConnect, port = 8181)\n  props.setProperty(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG, \"1\")\n\n  val cfg = KafkaConfig.fromProps(props)\n  assertEquals(60L * 60L * 1000L, cfg.logRetentionTimeMillis)\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### **Key Configuration Patterns in Kafka**\n\n**1. Configuration Name Precedence (from DynamicBrokerConfig.scala lines 54-67):**\n```\n1. DYNAMIC_BROKER_CONFIG (ZK at /configs/brokers/{brokerId})\n2. DYNAMIC_DEFAULT_BROKER_CONFIG (ZK at /configs/brokers/<default>)\n3. STATIC_BROKER_CONFIG (server.properties)\n4. DEFAULT_CONFIG (hardcoded defaults)\n```\n\n**2. Validation Approach:**\n- Type validation done by ConfigDef at parse time\n- Range validation with `atLeast()`, `between()` validators\n- Custom validation in dynamic handlers during reconfiguration\n\n**3. Synonym Configurations (lines 122-140):**\nSome configs have multiple names (synonyms) for backwards compatibility:\n```scala\ndef brokerConfigSynonyms(name: String, matchListenerOverride: Boolean): List[String] = {\n  name match {\n    case ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG | ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG =>\n      List(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG, ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG)\n    case _ => List(name)\n  }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### **Summary Checklist**\n\n- [ ] Define config constants (NAME, DEFAULT, DOC) in appropriate config class\n- [ ] Add `.define()` entry to CONFIG_DEF with type, default, validator, importance\n- [ ] Ensure ConfigDef is merged in AbstractKafkaConfig (auto if using existing class)\n- [ ] Add getter method in KafkaConfig.scala\n- [ ] If dynamic: Add to AllDynamicConfigs or create specific Dynamic handler\n- [ ] Register handler in addReconfigurables() if needed\n- [ ] Write validation logic in handler class\n- [ ] Write unit tests for static and dynamic reconfiguration\n- [ ] Test integration with DynamicBrokerConfig framework\n\nThis process follows the established patterns throughout Kafka's configuration system and ensures proper integration with the dynamic configuration update mechanism.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Explore how to add a new broker configuration parameter to Apache Kafka. Look at concrete examples and trace the complete process:\n\n1. **Define the Configuration:**\n   - Look at ServerConfigs, SocketServerConfigs, LogConfigs to see examples\n   - Find where config names are declared (public static final String)\n   - Find where config defaults are declared\n   - Find where documentation is written\n   - Look at the define() calls to see the pattern\n\n2. **Add Configuration Definition:**\n   - Look for how .define() is called in ConfigDef\n   - See how validators are specified\n   - See how importance levels are set\n   - Find examples with different validators\n\n3. **Integrate with ConfigDef:**\n   - Find how configs are merged in AbstractKafkaConfig\n   - Look for how new ConfigDef classes are incorporated\n   - See how existing configs reference each other\n\n4. **Create KafkaConfig Fields:**\n   - Look at KafkaConfig.scala to see the pattern\n   - Find how config values are accessed via properties\n   - Look for lazy val declarations\n\n5. **Dynamic Config Updates (if needed):**\n   - Find how to mark a config as dynamically reconfigurable\n   - Look at DynamicBrokerConfig for adding to AllDynamicConfigs\n   - Find the Reconfigurable component that should handle the update\n\n6. **Testing the New Config:**\n   - Look for KafkaConfigTest examples\n   - Find how validation is tested\n   - Find how dynamic updates are tested\n\nSpecific files to examine for examples:\n- /workspace/server/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/config/ServerConfigs.java\n- /workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/SocketServerConfigs.scala\n- /workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala\n- /workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/DynamicBrokerConfig.scala\n- Test files for config testing",

This is useful when the authorizer is dependent on the cluster itself for bootstrapping, as is the case for \" +\n   139\u2192            \"the StandardAuthorizer (which stores ACLs in the metadata log.) .define(MESSAGE_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG, INT, LogConfig.DEFAULT_MAX_MESSAGE_BYTES, atLeast(0), HIGH, MESSAGE_MAX_BYTES_DOC)\n   146\u2192            .define(NUM_IO_THREADS_CONFIG, INT, NUM_IO_THREADS_DEFAULT, atLeast(1), HIGH, NUM_IO_THREADS_DOC)\n   147\u2192            .define(NUM_REPLICA_ALTER_LOG_DIRS_THREADS_CONFIG, INT, null, HIGH, NUM_REPLICA_ALTER_LOG_DIRS_THREADS_DOC)\n EARLY_START_LISTENERS_DOC)\n   155\u2192            /************ Rack Configuration ******************/\n   156\u2192            .define(BROKER_RACK_CONFIG, STRING, null, MEDIUM, BROKER_RACK_DOC)\n   157\u2192            /** ********* Controlled shutdown configuration ***********/\n   158\u2192            .define(CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_MAX_RETRIES_CONFIG, INT, CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_MAX_RETRIES_DEFAULT, MEDIUM, CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_MAX_RETRIES_DOC)\n   159\u2192            .define(CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_RETRY_BACKOFF_MS_CONFIG, LONG, CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_RETRY_BACKOFF_MS_DEFAULT, MEDIUM, CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_RETRY_BACKOFF_MS_DOC)\n   160\u2192            .define(CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_ENABLE_CONFIG, BOOLEAN, CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_ENABLE_DEFAULT, MEDIUM, CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_ENABLE_DOC)\n   161\u2192            .define(DELETE_TOPIC_ENABLE_CONFIG, BOOLEAN, DELETE_TOPIC_ENABLE_DEFAULT, HIGH, DELETE_TOPIC_ENABLE_DOC)\n   162\u2192            .define(COMPRESSION_TYPE_CONFIG, STRING, LogConfig.DEFAULT_COMPRESSION_TYPE, ConfigDef.ValidString.in(BrokerCompressionType.names().toArray(new String[0])), HIGH, COMPRESSION_TYPE_DOC)\n   163\u2192            .define(COMPRESSION_GZIP_LEVEL_CONFIG, INT, CompressionType.GZIP.defaultLevel(), CompressionType.GZIP.levelValidator(), MEDIUM, COMPRESSION_GZIP_LEVEL_DOC)\n   164\u2192            .define(COMPRESSION_LZ4_LEVEL_CONFIG, INT, CompressionType.LZ4.defaultLevel(), CompressionType.LZ4.levelValidator(), MEDIUM, COMPRESSION_LZ4_LEVEL_DOC)\n   165\u2192 Configuration **************/\n   167\u2192            .define(MAX_INCREMENTAL_FETCH_SESSION_CACHE_SLOTS_CONFIG, INT, MAX_INCREMENTAL_FETCH_SESSION_CACHE_SLOTS_DEFAULT, atLeast(0), MEDIUM, MAX_INCREMENTAL_FETCH_SESSION_CACHE_SLOTS_DOC)\n   168\u2192            .define(FETCH_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG, INT, FETCH_MAX_BYTES_DEFAULT, atLeast(1024), MEDIUM, FETCH_MAX_BYTES_DOC)\n   169\u2192\n   170\u2192            /** ********* Request Limit Configuration ***********/\n   171\u2192            .define(MAX_REQUEST_PARTITION_SIZE_LIMIT_CONFIG, INT, MAX_REQUEST_PARTITION_SIZE_LIMIT_DEFAULT, atLeast(1), MEDIUM, MAX_REQUEST_PARTITION_SIZE_LIMIT_DOC)\n   172\u2192            /** Internal Configurations **/\n   173\u2192            // This indicates whether unreleased APIs should be advertised by this node.\n   174\u2192            .defineInternal(UNSTABLE_API_VERSIONS_ENABLE_CONFIG, BOOLEAN, false, HIGH)\n   175\u2192            // This indicates whether unreleased MetadataVersions should be enabled on this node.\n   176\u2192            .defineInternal(UNSTABLE_FEATURE_VERSIONS_ENABLE_CONFIG, BOOLEAN, false, HIGH);\n   177\u2192}\n
