setZooKeeperClientProperty(clientConfig: ZKClientConfig, kafkaPropName: String, kafkaPropValue: Any): Unit = {\n    72\u2192    clientConfig.setProperty(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_CONFIG_TO_SYSTEM_PROPERTY_MAP.get(kafkaPropName),\n    73\u2192      kafkaPropName match {\n    74\u2192        case ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_ENDPOINT_IDENTIFICATION_ALGORITHM_CONFIG => (kafkaPropValue.toString.toUpperCase == \"HTTPS\").toString\n    75\u2192        case ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_ENABLED_PROTOCOLS_CONFIG | kafkaPropValue.toString\n    78\u2192        }\n    79\u2192        case _ => kafkaPropValue.toString\n    80\u2192    })\n    81\u2192  }\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192  // For ZooKeeper TLS client authentication to be enabled the client must (at a minimum) configure itself as using TLS\n    84\u2192  // with both a client connection socket and a key store location explicitly set.\n    85\u2192  private[kafka] def zkTlsClientAuthEnabled(zkClientConfig: ZKClientConfig): Boolean = {\n    86\u2192    zooKeeperClientProperty(zkClientConfig, ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_CLIENT_ENABLE_CONFIG).contains(\"true\") &&\n    87\u2192      zooKeeperClientProperty(zkClientConfig, ZkConfigs.ZK_CLIENT_CNXN_SOCKET_CONFIG).isDefined &&\n    88\u2192      zooKeeperClientProperty(zkClientConfig, ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_KEY_STORE_LOCATION_CONFIG).isDefined\n    89\u2192  }\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192  val configDef = AbstractKafkaConfig.CONFIG_DEF\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192  def configNames: Seq[String] = configDef.names.asScala.toBuffer.sorted\n    94\u2192  private[server] def defaultValues: Map[String, _] = configDef.defaultValues.asScala\n    95\u2192  private[server] def configKeys: Map[String, ConfigKey] = configDef.configKeys.asScala\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192  def fromProps(props: Properties): KafkaConfig =\n    98\u2192    fromProps(props, true)\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192  def fromProps(props: Properties, doLog: Boolean): KafkaConfig =\n   101\u2192    new KafkaConfig(props, doLog)\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192  def fromProps(defaults: Properties, overrides: Properties): KafkaConfig =\n   104\u2192    fromProps(defaults, overrides, true)\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192  def fromProps(defaults: Properties, overrides: Properties, doLog: Boolean): KafkaConfig = {\n   107\u2192    val props = new Properties()\n   108\u2192    props ++= defaults\n   109\u2192    props ++= overrides\n   110\u2192    fromProps(props, doLog)\n   111\u2192  }\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192  def apply(props: java.util.Map[_, _], doLog: Boolean = true): KafkaConfig = new KafkaConfig(props, doLog)\n   114\u2192\n   115\u2192  private def typeOf(name: String): Option[ConfigDef.Type] = Option(configDef.configKeys.get(name)).map(_.`type`)\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192  def configType(configName: String): Option[ConfigDef.Type] = {\n   118\u2192    val configType = configTypeExact(configName)\n   119\u2192    if (configType.isDefined) {\n   120\u2192      return configType\n   121\u2192    }\n   122\u2192    typeOf(configName) match {\n   123\u2192      case Some(t) => Some(t)\n   124\u2192      case None =>\n   125\u2192        DynamicBrokerConfig.brokerConfigSynonyms(configName, matchListenerOverride = true).flatMap(typeOf).headOption\n   126\u2192    }\n   127\u2192  }\n   128\u2192\n   129\u2192  private def configTypeExact(exactName: String): Option[ConfigDef.Type] = {\n   130\u2192    val configType = typeOf(exactName).orNull\n   131\u2192    if (configType != null) {\n   132\u2192      Some(configType)\n   133\u2192    } else {\n   134\u2192      val configKey = DynamicConfig.Broker.configKeys.get(exactName)\n   135\u2192      if (configKey != null) {\n   136\u2192        Some(configKey.`type`)\n   137\u2192      } else {\n   138\u2192        None\n   139\u2192      }\n   140\u2192    }\n   141\u2192  }\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192  def maybeSensitive(configType: Option[ConfigDef.Type]): Boolean = {\n   144\u2192    // If we can't determine the config entry type, treat it as a sensitive config to be safe\n   145\u2192    configType.isEmpty || configType.contains(ConfigDef.Type.PASSWORD)\n   146\u2192  }\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192  def loggableValue(resourceType: ConfigResource.Type, name: String, value: String): String = {\n   149\u2192    val maybeSensitive = resourceType match {\n   150\u2192      case ConfigResource.Type.BROKER => KafkaConfig.maybeSensitive(KafkaConfig.configType(name))\n   151\u2192      case ConfigResource.Type.TOPIC => KafkaConfig.maybeSensitive(LogConfig.configType(name).asScala)\n   152\u2192      case ConfigResource.Type.BROKER_LOGGER => false\n   153\u2192      case ConfigResource.Type.CLIENT_METRICS => false\n   154\u2192      case _ => true\n   155\u2192    }\n   156\u2192    if (maybeSensitive) Password.HIDDEN else value\n   157\u2192  }\n   158\u2192\n   159\u2192  /**\n   160\u2192   * Copy a configuration map, populating some keys that we want to treat as synonyms.\n   161\u2192   */\n   162\u2192  def populateSynonyms(input: util.Map[_, _]): util.Map[Any, Any] = {\n   163\u2192    val output = new util.HashMap[Any, Any](input)\n   164\u2192    val brokerId = output.get(ServerConfigs.BROKER_ID_CONFIG)\n   165\u2192    val nodeId = output.get(KRaftConfigs.NODE_ID_CONFIG)\n   166\u2192    if (brokerId == null && nodeId != null) {\n   167\u2192      output.put(ServerConfigs.BROKER_ID_CONFIG, nodeId)\n   168\u2192    } else if (brokerId != null && nodeId == null) {\n   169\u2192      output.put(KRaftConfigs.NODE_ID_CONFIG, brokerId)\n   170\u2192    }\n   171\u2192    output\n   172\u2192  }\n   173\u2192}\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192/**\n   176\u2192 * The class extend {@link AbstractKafkaConfig} which will be the future KafkaConfig.\n   177\u2192 * When add any new methods if it doesn't depend on anything in Core, then move it to org.apache.kafka.server.config.KafkaConfig instead of here.\n   178\u2192 * Any code depends on kafka.server.KafkaConfig will keep for using kafka.server.KafkaConfig for the time being until we move it out of core\n   179\u2192 * For more details check KAFKA-15853\n   180\u2192 */\n   181\u2192class KafkaConfig private(doLog: Boolean, val props: util.Map[_, _])\n   182\u2192  extends AbstractKafkaConfig(KafkaConfig.configDef, props, Utils.castToStringObjectMap(props), doLog) with Logging {\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192  def this(props: java.util.Map[_, _]) = this(true, KafkaConfig.populateSynonyms(props))\n   185\u2192  def this(props: java.util.Map[_, _], doLog: Boolean) = this(doLog, KafkaConfig.populateSynonyms(props))\n   186\u2192\n   187\u2192  // Cache the current config to avoid acquiring read lock to access from dynamicConfig\n   188\u2192  @volatile private var currentConfig = this\n   189\u2192  val processRoles: Set[ProcessRole] = parseProcessRoles()\n   190\u2192  private[server] val dynamicConfig = new DynamicBrokerConfig(this)\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192  private[server] def updateCurrentConfig(newConfig: KafkaConfig): Unit = {\n   193\u2192    this.currentConfig = newConfig\n   194\u2192  }\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192  // The following captures any system properties impacting ZooKeeper TLS configuration\n   197\u2192  // and defines the default values this instance will use if no explicit config is given.\n   198\u2192  // We make it part of each instance rather than the object to facilitate testing.\n   199\u2192  private val zkClientConfigViaSystemProperties = new ZKClientConfig()\n   200\u2192\n   201\u2192  override def originals: util.Map[String, AnyRef] =\n   202\u2192    if (this eq currentConfig) super.originals else currentConfig.originals\n   203\u2192  override def values: util.Map[String, _] =\n   204\u2192    if (this eq currentConfig) super.values else currentConfig.values\n   205\u2192  override def nonInternalValues: util.Map[String, _] =\n   206\u2192    if (this eq currentConfig) super.nonInternalValues else currentConfig.nonInternalValues\n   207\u2192  override def originalsStrings: util.Map[String, String] =\n   208\u2192    if (this eq currentConfig) super.originalsStrings else currentConfig.originalsStrings\n   209\u2192  override def originalsWithPrefix(prefix: String): util.Map[String, AnyRef] =\n   210\u2192    if (this eq currentConfig) super.originalsWithPrefix(prefix) else currentConfig.originalsWithPrefix(prefix)\n   211\u2192  override def valuesWithPrefixOverride(prefix: String): util.Map[String, AnyRef] =\n   212\u2192    if (this eq currentConfig) super.valuesWithPrefixOverride(prefix) else currentConfig.valuesWithPrefixOverride(prefix)\n   213\u2192  override def get(key: String): AnyRef =\n   214\u2192    if (this eq currentConfig) super.get(key) else currentConfig.get(key)\n   215\u2192\n   216\u2192  //  During dynamic update, we use the values from this config, these are only used in DynamicBrokerConfig\n   217\u2192  private[server] def originalsFromThisConfig: util.Map[String, AnyRef] = super.originals\n   218\u2192  private[server] def valuesFromThisConfig: util.Map[String, _] = super.values\n   219\u2192  def valuesFromThisConfigWithPrefixOverride(prefix: String): util.Map[String, AnyRef] =\n   220\u2192    super.valuesWithPrefixOverride(prefix)\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192  /** ********* Zookeeper Configuration ***********/\n   223\u2192  val zkConnect: String = getString(ZkConfigs.ZK_CONNECT_CONFIG)\n   224\u2192  val zkSessionTimeoutMs: Int = getInt(ZkConfigs.ZK_SESSION_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n   225\u2192  val zkConnectionTimeoutMs: Int =\n   226\u2192    Option(getInt(ZkConfigs.ZK_CONNECTION_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)).map(_.toInt).getOrElse(getInt(ZkConfigs.ZK_SESSION_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG))\n   227\u2192  val zkEnableSecureAcls: Boolean = getBoolean(ZkConfigs.ZK_ENABLE_SECURE_ACLS_CONFIG)\n   228\u2192  val zkMaxInFlightRequests: Int = getInt(ZkConfigs.ZK_MAX_IN_FLIGHT_REQUESTS_CONFIG)\n   229\u2192\n   230\u2192  private val _remoteLogManagerConfig = new RemoteLogManagerConfig(this)\n   231\u2192  def remoteLogManagerConfig = _remoteLogManagerConfig\n   232\u2192\n   233\u2192  private val _groupCoordinatorConfig = new GroupCoordinatorConfig(this)\n   234\u2192\n   235\u2192  def groupCoordinatorConfig: GroupCoordinatorConfig = _groupCoordinatorConfig\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192  private val _shareGroupConfig = new ShareGroupConfig(this)\n   238\u2192  def shareGroupConfig: ShareGroupConfig = _shareGroupConfig\n   239\u2192\n   240\u2192  private def zkBooleanConfigOrSystemPropertyWithDefaultValue(propKey: String): Boolean = {\n   241\u2192    // Use the system property if it exists and the Kafka config value was defaulted rather than actually provided\n   242\u2192    // Need to translate any system property value from true/false (String) to true/false (Boolean)\n   243\u2192    val actuallyProvided = originals.containsKey(propKey)\n   244\u2192    if (actuallyProvided) getBoolean(propKey) else {\n   245\u2192      val sysPropValue = KafkaConfig.zooKeeperClientProperty(zkClientConfigViaSystemProperties, propKey)\n   246\u2192      sysPropValue match {\n   247\u2192        case Some(\"true\") => true\n   248\u2192        case Some(_) => false\n   249\u2192        case _ => getBoolean(propKey) // not specified so use the default value\n   250\u2192      }\n   251\u2192    }\n   252\u2192  }\n   253\u2192\n   254\u2192  private def zkStringConfigOrSystemPropertyWithDefaultValue(propKey: String): String = {\n   255\u2192    // Use the system property if it exists and the Kafka config value was defaulted rather than actually provided\n   256\u2192    val actuallyProvided = originals.containsKey(propKey)\n   257\u2192    if (actuallyProvided) getString(propKey) else {\n   258\u2192      KafkaConfig.zooKeeperClientProperty(zkClientConfigViaSystemProperties, propKey) match {\n   259\u2192        case Some(v) => v\n   260\u2192        case _ => getString(propKey) // not specified so use the default value\n   261\u2192      }\n   262\u2192    }\n   263\u2192  }\n   264\u2192\n   265\u2192  private def zkOptionalStringConfigOrSystemProperty(propKey: String): Option[String] = {\n   266\u2192    Option(getString(propKey)).orElse {\n   267\u2192      KafkaConfig.zooKeeperClientProperty(zkClientConfigViaSystemProperties, propKey)\n   268\u2192    }\n   269\u2192  }\n   270\u2192  private def zkPasswordConfigOrSystemProperty(propKey: String): Option[Password] = {\n   271\u2192    Option(getPassword(propKey)).orElse {\n   272\u2192 zkListConfigOrSystemProperty(propKey: String): Option[util.List[String]] = {\n   276\u2192    Option(getList(propKey)).orElse {\n   277\u2192 280\u2192    }\n   281\u2192  }\n   282\u2192\n   283\u2192  val zkSslClientEnable = zkBooleanConfigOrSystemPropertyWithDefaultValue(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_CLIENT_ENABLE_CONFIG)\n   284\u2192  val zkClientCnxnSocketClassName = zkOptionalStringConfigOrSystemProperty(ZkConfigs.ZK_CLIENT_CNXN_SOCKET_CONFIG)\n   285\u2192  val zkSslKeyStoreLocation = zkOptionalStringConfigOrSystemProperty(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_KEY_STORE_LOCATION_CONFIG)\n   286\u2192  val zkSslKeyStorePassword = zkPasswordConfigOrSystemProperty(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_KEY_STORE_PASSWORD_CONFIG)\n   287\u2192  val zkSslKeyStoreType = zkOptionalStringConfigOrSystemProperty(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_KEY_STORE_TYPE_CONFIG)\n   288\u2192  val zkSslTrustStoreLocation = zkOptionalStringConfigOrSystemProperty(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_TRUST_STORE_LOCATION_CONFIG)\n   289\u2192  val zkSslTrustStorePassword = zkPasswordConfigOrSystemProperty(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_TRUST_STORE_PASSWORD_CONFIG)\n   290\u2192  val zkSslTrustStoreType = zkOptionalStringConfigOrSystemProperty(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_TRUST_STORE_TYPE_CONFIG)\n   291\u2192  val ZkSslProtocol = zkStringConfigOrSystemPropertyWithDefaultValue(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_PROTOCOL_CONFIG)\n   292\u2192  val ZkSslEnabledProtocols = zkListConfigOrSystemProperty(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_ENABLED_PROTOCOLS_CONFIG)\n   293\u2192  val ZkSslCipherSuites = zkListConfigOrSystemProperty(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_CIPHER_SUITES_CONFIG)\n   294\u2192  val ZkSslEndpointIdentificationAlgorithm = {\n   295\u2192    // Use the system property if it exists and the Kafka config value was defaulted rather than actually provided\n   296\u2192    // Need to translate any system property value from true/false to HTTPS/<blank>\n   297\u2192    val kafkaProp = ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_ENDPOINT_IDENTIFICATION_ALGORITHM_CONFIG\n   298\u2192    val actuallyProvided = originals.containsKey(kafkaProp)\n   299\u2192    if (actuallyProvided)\n   300\u2192      getString(kafkaProp)\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

}

"     1\u2192/**\n     2\u2192  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n     3\u2192  * contributor license agreements. writing, software\n    12\u2192  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    13\u2192  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    14\u2192  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    15\u2192  * limitations under the License.\n    16\u2192  */\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192package kafka.server\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192import java.util\n    21\u2192import java.util.{Collections, Properties}\n    22\u2192import java.util.concurrent.CopyOnWriteArrayList\n    23\u2192import java.util.concurrent.locks.ReentrantReadWriteLock\n    24\u2192import kafka.cluster.EndPoint\n    25\u2192import kafka.log.{LogCleaner, LogManager}\n    26\u2192import kafka.network.{DataPlaneAcceptor, SocketServer}\n    27\u2192import kafka.server.DynamicBrokerConfig._\n    28\u2192import kafka.utils.{CoreUtils, Logging}\n    29\u2192import kafka.utils.Implicits._\n    30\u2192import kafka.zk.{AdminZkClient, KafkaZkClient}\n    31\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.Reconfigurable\n    32\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.config.internals.BrokerSecurityConfigs\n    33\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.config.{AbstractConfig, ConfigDef, ConfigException, SaslConfigs, SslConfigs, TopicConfig}\n    34\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.metrics.{JmxReporter, Metrics, MetricsReporter}\n    35\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.config.types.Password\n    36\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.network.{ListenerName, ListenerReconfigurable}\n    37\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.security.authenticator.LoginManager\n    38\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.{ConfigUtils, Utils}\n    39\u2192import org.apache.kafka.coordinator.transaction.TransactionLogConfigs\n    40\u2192import org.apache.kafka.network.SocketServerConfigs\n    41\u2192import org.apache.kafka.security.PasswordEncoder\n    42\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.ProcessRole\n    43\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.config.{ConfigType, ReplicationConfigs, ServerConfigs, ServerLogConfigs, ServerTopicConfigSynonyms, ZooKeeperInternals}\n    44\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.log.remote.storage.RemoteLogManagerConfig\n    45\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.metrics.{ClientMetricsReceiverPlugin, MetricConfigs}\n    46\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.telemetry.ClientTelemetry\n    47\u2192import org.apache.kafka.storage.internals.log.{LogConfig, ProducerStateManagerConfig}\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192import scala.annotation.nowarn\n    50\u2192import scala.collection._\n    51\u2192import scala.jdk.CollectionConverters._\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192/**\n    54\u2192  * Dynamic broker configurations are stored in ZooKeeper and may be defined at two levels:\n    55\u2192  * <ul>\n    56\u2192  *   <li>Per-broker configs persisted at <tt>/configs/brokers/{brokerId}</tt>: These can be described/altered\n    57\u2192  *       using AdminClient using the resource name brokerId.</li>\n    58\u2192  *   <li>Cluster-wide defaults persisted at <tt>/configs/brokers/&lt;default&gt;</tt>: These can be described/altered\n    59\u2192  *       using AdminClient using an empty resource name.</li>\n    60\u2192  * </ul>\n    61\u2192  * The order of precedence for broker configs is:\n    62\u2192  * <ol>\n    63\u2192  *   <li>DYNAMIC_BROKER_CONFIG: stored in ZK at /configs/brokers/{brokerId}</li>\n    64\u2192  *   <li>DYNAMIC_DEFAULT_BROKER_CONFIG: stored in ZK at /configs/brokers/&lt;default&gt;</li>\n    65\u2192  *   <li>STATIC_BROKER_CONFIG: properties that broker is started up with, typically from server.properties file</li>\n    66\u2192  *   <li>DEFAULT_CONFIG: Default configs defined in KafkaConfig</li>\n    67\u2192  * </ol>\n    68\u2192  * Log configs use topic config overrides if defined and fallback to broker defaults using the order of precedence above.\n    69\u2192  * Topic config overrides may use a different config name from the default broker config.\n    70\u2192  * See [[org.apache.kafka.storage.internals.log.LogConfig#TopicConfigSynonyms]] for the mapping.\n    71\u2192  * <p>\n    72\u2192  * AdminClient returns all config synonyms in the order of precedence when configs are described with\n    73\u2192  * <code>includeSynonyms</code>. In addition to configs that may be defined with the same name at different levels,\n    74\u2192  * some configs have additional synonyms.\n    75\u2192  * </p>\n    76\u2192  * <ul>\n    77\u2192  *   <li>Listener configs may be defined using the prefix <tt>listener.name.{listenerName}.{configName}</tt>. These may be\n    78\u2192  *       configured as dynamic or static broker configs. Listener configs have higher precedence than the base configs\n    79\u2192  *       that don't specify the listener name. Listeners without a listener config use the base config. Base configs\n    80\u2192  *       may be defined only as STATIC_BROKER_CONFIG or DEFAULT_CONFIG and cannot be updated dynamically.<li>\n    81\u2192  *   <li>Some configs may be defined using multiple properties. For example, <tt>log.roll.ms</tt> and\n    82\u2192  *       <tt>log.roll.hours</tt> refer to the same config that may be defined in milliseconds or hours. The order of\n    83\u2192  *       precedence of these synonyms is described in the docs of these configs in [[kafka.server.KafkaConfig]].</li>\n    84\u2192  * </ul>\n    85\u2192  *\n    86\u2192  */\n    87\u2192object DynamicBrokerConfig {\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192  private[server] val DynamicSecurityConfigs = SslConfigs.RECONFIGURABLE_CONFIGS.asScala\n    90\u2192  private[server] val DynamicProducerStateManagerConfig = Set(TransactionLogConfigs.PRODUCER_ID_EXPIRATION_MS_CONFIG, TransactionLogConfigs.TRANSACTION_PARTITION_VERIFICATION_ENABLE_CONFIG)\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192  val AllDynamicConfigs = DynamicSecurityConfigs ++\n    93\u2192    LogCleaner.ReconfigurableConfigs ++\n    94\u2192    DynamicLogConfig.ReconfigurableConfigs ++\n    95\u2192    DynamicThreadPool.ReconfigurableConfigs ++\n    96\u2192    Set(MetricConfigs.METRIC_REPORTER_CLASSES_CONFIG) ++\n    97\u2192    DynamicListenerConfig.ReconfigurableConfigs ++\n    98\u2192    SocketServer.ReconfigurableConfigs ++\n    99\u2192    DynamicProducerStateManagerConfig ++\n   100\u2192    DynamicRemoteLogConfig.ReconfigurableConfigs\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192  private val ClusterLevelListenerConfigs = Set(SocketServerConfigs.MAX_CONNECTIONS_CONFIG, SocketServerConfigs.MAX_CONNECTION_CREATION_RATE_CONFIG, SocketServerConfigs.NUM_NETWORK_THREADS_CONFIG)\n   103\u2192  private val PerBrokerConfigs = (DynamicSecurityConfigs ++ DynamicListenerConfig.ReconfigurableConfigs).diff(\n   104\u2192    ClusterLevelListenerConfigs)\n   105\u2192  private val ListenerMechanismConfigs = Set(SaslConfigs.SASL_JAAS_CONFIG,\n   106\u2192    SaslConfigs.SASL_LOGIN_CALLBACK_HANDLER_CLASS,\n   107\u2192    SaslConfigs.SASL_LOGIN_CLASS,\n   108\u2192    BrokerSecurityConfigs.SASL_SERVER_CALLBACK_HANDLER_CLASS_CONFIG,\n   109\u2192    BrokerSecurityConfigs.CONNECTIONS_MAX_REAUTH_MS_CONFIG)\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192  private val ReloadableFileConfigs = Set(SslConfigs.SSL_KEYSTORE_LOCATION_CONFIG, SslConfigs.SSL_TRUSTSTORE_LOCATION_CONFIG)\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192  private val ListenerConfigRegex = \"\"\"listener\\.name\\.[^.]*\\.(.*)\"\"\".r\n   114\u2192\n   115\u2192  private val DynamicPasswordConfigs = {\n   116\u2192    val passwordConfigs = KafkaConfig.configKeys.filter(_._2.`type` == ConfigDef.Type.PASSWORD).keySet\n   117\u2192    AllDynamicConfigs.intersect(passwordConfigs)\n   118\u2192  }\n   119\u2192\n   120\u2192  def isPasswordConfig(name: String): Boolean = DynamicBrokerConfig.DynamicPasswordConfigs.exists(name.endsWith)\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192  def brokerConfigSynonyms(name: String, matchListenerOverride: Boolean): List[String] = {\n   123\u2192    name match {\n   124\u2192      case ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG | ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG =>\n   125\u2192        List(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG, ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG)\n   126\u2192      case ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_JITTER_MILLIS_CONFIG | ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_JITTER_HOURS_CONFIG =>\n   127\u2192        List(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_JITTER_MILLIS_CONFIG, ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_JITTER_HOURS_CONFIG)\n   128\u2192      case ServerLogConfigs.LOG_FLUSH_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG => // KafkaLogConfigs.LOG_FLUSH_SCHEDULER_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG is used as default\n   129\u2192        List(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_FLUSH_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG, ServerLogConfigs.LOG_FLUSH_SCHEDULER_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG)\n   130\u2192      case ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG | ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MINUTES_CONFIG | ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG =>\n   131\u2192        List(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG, ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MINUTES_CONFIG, ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG)\n   132\u2192      case ListenerConfigRegex(baseName) if matchListenerOverride =>\n   133\u2192        // `ListenerMechanismConfigs` are specified as listenerPrefix.mechanism.<configName>\n   134\u2192        // and other listener configs are specified as listenerPrefix.<configName>\n   135\u2192        // Add <configName> as a synonym in both cases.\n   136\u2192        val mechanismConfig = ListenerMechanismConfigs.find(baseName.endsWith)\n   137\u2192        List(name, mechanismConfig.getOrElse(baseName))\n   138\u2192      case _ => List(name)\n   139\u2192    }\n   140\u2192  }\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192  def validateConfigs(props: Properties, perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Unit = {\n   143\u2192    def checkInvalidProps(invalidPropNames: Set[String], errorMessage: String): Unit = {\n   144\u2192      if (invalidPropNames.nonEmpty)\n   145\u2192        throw new ConfigException(s\"$errorMessage: $invalidPropNames\")\n   146\u2192    }\n   147\u2192    checkInvalidProps(nonDynamicConfigs(props), \"Cannot update these configs dynamically\")\n   148\u2192    checkInvalidProps(securityConfigsWithoutListenerPrefix(props),\n   149\u2192      \"These security configs can be dynamically updated only per-listener using the listener prefix\")\n   150\u2192    validateConfigTypes(props)\n   151\u2192    if (!perBrokerConfig) {\n   152\u2192      checkInvalidProps(perBrokerConfigs(props),\n   153\u2192        \"Cannot update these configs at default cluster level, broker id must be specified\")\n   154\u2192    }\n   155\u2192  }\n   156\u2192\n   157\u2192  private def perBrokerConfigs(props: Properties): Set[String] = {\n   158\u2192    val configNames = props.asScala.keySet\n   159\u2192    def perBrokerListenerConfig(name: String): Boolean = {\n   160\u2192      name match {\n   161\u2192        case ListenerConfigRegex(baseName) => !ClusterLevelListenerConfigs.contains(baseName)\n   162\u2192        case _ => false\n   163\u2192      }\n   164\u2192    }\n   165\u2192    configNames.intersect(PerBrokerConfigs) ++ configNames.filter(perBrokerListenerConfig)\n   166\u2192  }\n   167\u2192\n   168\u2192  private def nonDynamicConfigs(props: Properties): Set[String] = {\n   169\u2192    props.asScala.keySet.intersect(DynamicConfig.Broker.nonDynamicProps)\n   170\u2192  }\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192  private def securityConfigsWithoutListenerPrefix(props: Properties): Set[String] = {\n   173\u2192    DynamicSecurityConfigs.filter(props.containsKey)\n   174\u2192  }\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192  private def validateConfigTypes(props: Properties): Unit = {\n   177\u2192    val baseProps = new Properties\n   178\u2192    props.asScala.foreach {\n   179\u2192      case (ListenerConfigRegex(baseName), v) => baseProps.put(baseName, v)\n   180\u2192      case (k, v) => baseProps.put(k, v)\n   181\u2192    }\n   182\u2192    DynamicConfig.Broker.validate(baseProps)\n   183\u2192  }\n   184\u2192\n   185\u2192  private[server] def dynamicConfigUpdateModes: util.Map[String, String] = {\n   186\u2192    AllDynamicConfigs.map { name =>\n   187\u2192      val mode = if (PerBrokerConfigs.contains(name)) \"per-broker\" else \"cluster-wide\"\n   188\u2192      name -> mode\n   189\u2192    }.toMap.asJava\n   190\u2192  }\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192  private[server] def resolveVariableConfigs(propsOriginal: Properties): Properties = {\n   193\u2192    val props = new Properties\n   194\u2192    val config = new AbstractConfig(new ConfigDef(), propsOriginal, Utils.castToStringObjectMap(propsOriginal), false)\n   195\u2192    config.originals.forEach { (key, value) =>\n   196\u2192      if (!key.startsWith(AbstractConfig.CONFIG_PROVIDERS_CONFIG)) {\n   197\u2192        props.put(key, value)\n   198\u2192      }\n   199\u2192    }\n   200\u2192    props\n   201\u2192  }\n   202\u2192}\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192class DynamicBrokerConfig(private val kafkaConfig: KafkaConfig) extends Logging {\n   205\u2192\n   206\u2192  private[server] val staticBrokerConfigs = ConfigDef.convertToStringMapWithPasswordValues(kafkaConfig.originalsFromThisConfig).asScala\n   207\u2192  private[server] val staticDefaultConfigs = ConfigDef.convertToStringMapWithPasswordValues(KafkaConfig.defaultValues.asJava).asScala\n   208\u2192  private val dynamicBrokerConfigs = mutable.Map[String, String]()\n   209\u2192  private val dynamicDefaultConfigs = mutable.Map[String, String]()\n   210\u2192\n   211\u2192  // Use COWArrayList to prevent concurrent modification exception when an item is added by one thread to these\n   212\u2192  // collections, while another thread is iterating over them.\n   213\u2192  private[server] val reconfigurables = new CopyOnWriteArrayList[Reconfigurable]()\n   214\u2192  private val brokerReconfigurables = new CopyOnWriteArrayList[BrokerReconfigurable]()\n   215\u2192  private val lock = new ReentrantReadWriteLock\n   216\u2192  private var metricsReceiverPluginOpt: Option[ClientMetricsReceiverPlugin] = _\n   217\u2192  private var currentConfig: KafkaConfig = _\n   218\u2192  private val dynamicConfigPasswordEncoder = if (kafkaConfig.processRoles.isEmpty) {\n   219\u2192    maybeCreatePasswordEncoder(kafkaConfig.passwordEncoderSecret)\n   220\u2192  } else {\n   221\u2192    Some(PasswordEncoder.NOOP)\n   222\u2192  }\n   223\u2192\n   224\u2192  private[server] def initialize(zkClientOpt: Option[KafkaZkClient], clientMetricsReceiverPluginOpt: Option[ClientMetricsReceiverPlugin]): Unit = {\n   225\u2192    currentConfig = new KafkaConfig(kafkaConfig.props, false)\n   226\u2192    metricsReceiverPluginOpt = clientMetricsReceiverPluginOpt\n   227\u2192\n   228\u2192    zkClientOpt.foreach { zkClient =>\n   229\u2192      val adminZkClient = new AdminZkClient(zkClient)\n   230\u2192      updateDefaultConfig(adminZkClient.fetchEntityConfig(ConfigType.BROKER, ZooKeeperInternals.DEFAULT_STRING), false)\n   231\u2192      val props = adminZkClient.fetchEntityConfig(ConfigType.BROKER, kafkaConfig.brokerId.toString)\n   232\u2192      val brokerConfig = maybeReEncodePasswords(props, adminZkClient)\n   233\u2192      updateBrokerConfig(kafkaConfig.brokerId, brokerConfig)\n   234\u2192    }\n   235\u2192  }\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192  /**\n   238\u2192   * Clear all cached values. This is used to clear state on broker shutdown to avoid\n   239\u2192   * exceptions in tests when broker is restarted. These fields are re-initialized when\n   240\u2192   * broker starts up.\n   241\u2192   */\n   242\u2192  private[server] def clear(): Unit = {\n   243\u2192    dynamicBrokerConfigs.clear()\n   244\u2192    dynamicDefaultConfigs.clear()\n   245\u2192    reconfigurables.clear()\n   246\u2192    brokerReconfigurables.clear()\n   247\u2192  }\n   248\u2192\n   249\u2192  /**\n   250\u2192   * Add reconfigurables to be notified when a dynamic broker config is updated.\n   251\u2192   *\n   252\u2192   * `Reconfigurable` is the public API used by configurable plugins like metrics reporter\n   253\u2192   * and quota callbacks. These are reconfigured before `KafkaConfig` is updated so that\n   254\u2192   * the update can be aborted if `reconfigure()` fails with an exception.\n   255\u2192   *\n   256\u2192   * `BrokerReconfigurable` is used for internal reconfigurable classes. These are\n   257\u2192   * reconfigured after `KafkaConfig` is updated so that they can access `KafkaConfig`\n   258\u2192   * directly. They are provided both old and new configs.\n   259\u2192   */\n   260\u2192  def addReconfigurables(kafkaServer: KafkaBroker): Unit = {\n   261\u2192    kafkaServer.authorizer match {\n   262\u2192      case Some(authz: Reconfigurable) => addReconfigurable(authz)\n   263\u2192      case _ =>\n   264\u2192    }\n   265\u2192    addReconfigurable(kafkaServer.kafkaYammerMetrics)\n   266\u2192    addReconfigurable(new DynamicMetricsReporters(kafkaConfig.brokerId, kafkaServer.config, kafkaServer.metrics, kafkaServer.clusterId))\n   267\u2192    addReconfigurable(new DynamicClientQuotaCallback(kafkaServer.quotaManagers, kafkaServer.config))\n   268\u2192\n   269\u2192    addBrokerReconfigurable(new BrokerDynamicThreadPool(kafkaServer))\n   270\u2192    addBrokerReconfigurable(new DynamicLogConfig(kafkaServer.logManager, kafkaServer))\n   271\u2192    addBrokerReconfigurable(new DynamicListenerConfig(kafkaServer))\n   272\u2192    addBrokerReconfigurable(kafkaServer.socketServer)\n   273\u2192    addBrokerReconfigurable(new DynamicProducerStateManagerConfig(kafkaServer.logManager.producerStateManagerConfig))\n   274\u2192    addBrokerReconfigurable(new DynamicRemoteLogConfig(kafkaServer))\n   275\u2192  }\n   276\u2192\n   277\u2192  /**\n   278\u2192   * Add reconfigurables to be notified when a dynamic controller config is updated.\n   279\u2192   */\n   280\u2192  def addReconfigurables(controller: ControllerServer): Unit = {\n   281\u2192    controller.authorizer match {\n   282\u2192      case Some(authz: Reconfigurable) => addReconfigurable(authz)\n   283\u2192      case _ =>\n   284\u2192    }\n   285\u2192    if (!kafkaConfig.processRoles.contains(ProcessRole.BrokerRole)) {\n   286\u2192      // only add these if the controller isn't also running the broker role\n   287\u2192      // because these would already be added via the broker in that case\n   288\u2192      addReconfigurable(controller.kafkaYammerMetrics)\n   289\u2192      addReconfigurable(new DynamicMetricsReporters(kafkaConfig.nodeId, controller.config, controller.metrics, controller.clusterId))\n   290\u2192    }\n   291\u2192    addReconfigurable(new DynamicClientQuotaCallback(controller.quotaManagers, controller.config))\n   292\u2192    addBrokerReconfigurable(new ControllerDynamicThreadPool(controller))\n   293\u2192    // TODO: addBrokerReconfigurable(new DynamicListenerConfig(controller))\n   294\u2192    addBrokerReconfigurable(controller.socketServer)\n   295\u2192  }\n   296\u2192\n   297\u2192  def addReconfigurable(reconfigurable: Reconfigurable): Unit = CoreUtils.inWriteLock(lock) {\n   298\u2192    verifyReconfigurableConfigs(reconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs.asScala)\n   299\u2192    reconfigurables.add(reconfigurable)\n   300\u2192  }\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
190. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/KafkaConfig.scala",
  "limit": 300,
  "offset": 300
}
Output
"   300\u2192      getString(kafkaProp)\n   301\u2192    else {\n   302\u2192      KafkaConfig.zooKeeperClientProperty(zkClientConfigViaSystemProperties, kafkaProp) match {\n   303\u2192        case Some(\"true\") => \"HTTPS\"\n   304\u2192        case Some(_) => \"\"\n   305\u2192        case None => getString(kafkaProp) // not specified so use the default value\n   306\u2192      }\n   307\u2192    }\n   308\u2192  }\n   309\u2192  val ZkSslCrlEnable = zkBooleanConfigOrSystemPropertyWithDefaultValue(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_CRL_ENABLE_CONFIG)\n   310\u2192  val ZkSslOcspEnable = zkBooleanConfigOrSystemPropertyWithDefaultValue(ZkConfigs.ZK_SSL_OCSP_ENABLE_CONFIG)\n   311\u2192  /** ********* General Configuration ***********/\n   312\u2192  val brokerIdGenerationEnable: Boolean = getBoolean(ServerConfigs.BROKER_ID_GENERATION_ENABLE_CONFIG)\n   313\u2192  val maxReservedBrokerId: Int = getInt(ServerConfigs.RESERVED_BROKER_MAX_ID_CONFIG)\n   314\u2192  private[server] var _brokerId: Int = getInt(ServerConfigs.BROKER_ID_CONFIG)\n   315\u2192  def brokerId: Int = _brokerId\n   316\u2192  private[server] var _nodeId: Int = getInt(KRaftConfigs.NODE_ID_CONFIG)\n   317\u2192  def nodeId: Int = _nodeId\n   318\u2192  val initialRegistrationTimeoutMs: Int = getInt(KRaftConfigs.INITIAL_BROKER_REGISTRATION_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n   319\u2192  val brokerHeartbeatIntervalMs: Int = getInt(KRaftConfigs.BROKER_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG)\n   320\u2192  val brokerSessionTimeoutMs: Int = getInt(KRaftConfigs.BROKER_SESSION_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n   321\u2192\n   322\u2192  def requiresZookeeper: Boolean = processRoles.isEmpty\n   323\u2192  def usesSelfManagedQuorum: Boolean = processRoles.nonEmpty\n   324\u2192\n   325\u2192  val migrationEnabled: Boolean = getBoolean(KRaftConfigs.MIGRATION_ENABLED_CONFIG)\n   326\u2192  val migrationMetadataMinBatchSize: Int = getInt(KRaftConfigs.MIGRATION_METADATA_MIN_BATCH_SIZE_CONFIG)\n   327\u2192\n   328\u2192  val elrEnabled: Boolean = getBoolean(KRaftConfigs.ELR_ENABLED_CONFIG)\n   329\u2192\n   330\u2192  private def parseProcessRoles(): Set[ProcessRole] = {\n   331\u2192    val roles = getList(KRaftConfigs.PROCESS_ROLES_CONFIG).asScala.map {\n   332\u2192      case \"broker\" => ProcessRole.BrokerRole\n   333\u2192      case \"controller\" => ProcessRole.ControllerRole\n   334\u2192      case role => throw new ConfigException(s\"Unknown process role '$role'\" +\n   335\u2192        \" (only 'broker' and 'controller' are allowed roles)\")\n   336\u2192    }\n   337\u2192\n   338\u2192    val distinctRoles: Set[ProcessRole] = roles.toSet\n   339\u2192\n   340\u2192    if (distinctRoles.size != roles.size) {\n   341\u2192      throw new ConfigException(s\"Duplicate role names found in `${KRaftConfigs.PROCESS_ROLES_CONFIG}`: $roles\")\n   342\u2192    }\n   343\u2192\n   344\u2192    distinctRoles\n   345\u2192  }\n   346\u2192\n   347\u2192  def isKRaftCombinedMode: Boolean = {\n   348\u2192    processRoles == Set(ProcessRole.BrokerRole, ProcessRole.ControllerRole)\n   349\u2192  }\n   350\u2192\n   351\u2192  def metadataLogDir: String = {\n   352\u2192    Option(getString(KRaftConfigs.METADATA_LOG_DIR_CONFIG)) match {\n   353\u2192      case Some(dir) => dir\n   354\u2192      case None => logDirs.head\n   355\u2192    }\n   356\u2192  }\n   357\u2192\n   358\u2192  def metadataLogSegmentBytes = getInt(KRaftConfigs.METADATA_LOG_SEGMENT_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   359\u2192  def metadataLogSegmentMillis = getLong(KRaftConfigs.METADATA_LOG_SEGMENT_MILLIS_CONFIG)\n   360\u2192  def metadataRetentionBytes = getLong(KRaftConfigs.METADATA_MAX_RETENTION_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   361\u2192  def metadataRetentionMillis = getLong(KRaftConfigs.METADATA_MAX_RETENTION_MILLIS_CONFIG)\n   362\u2192  def metadataNodeIDConfig = getInt(KRaftConfigs.NODE_ID_CONFIG)\n   363\u2192  def metadataLogSegmentMinBytes = getInt(KRaftConfigs.METADATA_LOG_SEGMENT_MIN_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   364\u2192  val serverMaxStartupTimeMs = getLong(KRaftConfigs.SERVER_MAX_STARTUP_TIME_MS_CONFIG)\n   365\u2192\n   366\u2192  def backgroundThreads = getInt(ServerConfigs.BACKGROUND_THREADS_CONFIG)\n   367\u2192  def numIoThreads = getInt(ServerConfigs.NUM_IO_THREADS_CONFIG)\n   368\u2192  def messageMaxBytes = getInt(ServerConfigs.MESSAGE_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   369\u2192  val requestTimeoutMs = getInt(ServerConfigs.REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n   370\u2192  val connectionSetupTimeoutMs = getLong(ServerConfigs.SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n   371\u2192  val connectionSetupTimeoutMaxMs = getLong(ServerConfigs.SOCKET_CONNECTION_SETUP_TIMEOUT_MAX_MS_CONFIG)\n   372\u2192\n   373\u2192  def getNumReplicaAlterLogDirsThreads: Int = {\n   374\u2192    val numThreads: Integer = Option(getInt(ServerConfigs.NUM_REPLICA_ALTER_LOG_DIRS_THREADS_CONFIG)).getOrElse(logDirs.size)\n   375\u2192    numThreads\n   376\u2192  }\n   377\u2192\n   378\u2192  /************* Metadata Configuration ***********/\n   379\u2192  val metadataSnapshotMaxNewRecordBytes = getLong(KRaftConfigs.METADATA_SNAPSHOT_MAX_NEW_RECORD_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   380\u2192  val metadataSnapshotMaxIntervalMs = getLong(KRaftConfigs.METADATA_SNAPSHOT_MAX_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG)\n   381\u2192  val metadataMaxIdleIntervalNs: Option[Long] = {\n   382\u2192    val value = TimeUnit.NANOSECONDS.convert(getInt(KRaftConfigs.METADATA_MAX_IDLE_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG).toLong, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)\n   383\u2192    if (value > 0) Some(value) else None\n   384\u2192  }\n   385\u2192\n   386\u2192  /************* Authorizer Configuration ***********/\n   387\u2192  def createNewAuthorizer(): Option[Authorizer] = {\n   388\u2192    val className = getString(ServerConfigs.AUTHORIZER_CLASS_NAME_CONFIG)\n   389\u2192    if (className == null || className.isEmpty)\n   390\u2192      None\n   391\u2192    else {\n   392\u2192      Some(AuthorizerUtils.createAuthorizer(className))\n   393\u2192    }\n   394\u2192  }\n   395\u2192\n   396\u2192  val earlyStartListeners: Set[ListenerName] = {\n   397\u2192    val listenersSet = listeners.map(_.listenerName).toSet\n   398\u2192    val controllerListenersSet = controllerListeners.map(_.listenerName).toSet\n   399\u2192    Option(getString(ServerConfigs.EARLY_START_LISTENERS_CONFIG)) match {\n   400\u2192      case None => controllerListenersSet\n   401\u2192      case Some(str) =>\n   402\u2192        str.split(\",\").map(_.trim()).filterNot(_.isEmpty).map { str =>\n   403\u2192          val listenerName = new ListenerName(str)\n   404\u2192          if (!listenersSet.contains(listenerName) && !controllerListenersSet.contains(listenerName))\n   405\u2192            throw new ConfigException(s\"${ServerConfigs.EARLY_START_LISTENERS_CONFIG} contains \" +\n   406\u2192              s\"listener ${listenerName.value()}, but this is not contained in \" +\n   407\u2192              s\"${SocketServerConfigs.LISTENERS_CONFIG} or ${KRaftConfigs.CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES_CONFIG}\")\n   408\u2192          listenerName\n   409\u2192        }.toSet\n   410\u2192    }\n   411\u2192  }\n   412\u2192\n   413\u2192  /** ********* Socket Server Configuration ***********/\n   414\u2192  val socketSendBufferBytes = getInt(SocketServerConfigs.SOCKET_SEND_BUFFER_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   415\u2192  val socketReceiveBufferBytes = getInt(SocketServerConfigs.SOCKET_RECEIVE_BUFFER_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   416\u2192  val socketRequestMaxBytes = getInt(SocketServerConfigs.SOCKET_REQUEST_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   417\u2192  val socketListenBacklogSize = getInt(SocketServerConfigs.SOCKET_LISTEN_BACKLOG_SIZE_CONFIG)\n   418\u2192  val maxConnectionsPerIp = getInt(SocketServerConfigs.MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP_CONFIG)\n   419\u2192  val maxConnectionsPerIpOverrides: Map[String, Int] =\n   420\u2192    getMap(SocketServerConfigs.MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP_OVERRIDES_CONFIG, getString(SocketServerConfigs.MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP_OVERRIDES_CONFIG)).map { case (k, v) => (k, v.toInt)}\n   421\u2192  def maxConnections = getInt(SocketServerConfigs.MAX_CONNECTIONS_CONFIG)\n   422\u2192  def maxConnectionCreationRate = getInt(SocketServerConfigs.MAX_CONNECTION_CREATION_RATE_CONFIG)\n   423\u2192  val connectionsMaxIdleMs = getLong(SocketServerConfigs.CONNECTIONS_MAX_IDLE_MS_CONFIG)\n   424\u2192  val failedAuthenticationDelayMs = getInt(SocketServerConfigs.FAILED_AUTHENTICATION_DELAY_MS_CONFIG)\n   425\u2192  val queuedMaxRequests = getInt(SocketServerConfigs.QUEUED_MAX_REQUESTS_CONFIG)\n   426\u2192  val queuedMaxBytes = getLong(SocketServerConfigs.QUEUED_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   427\u2192  def numNetworkThreads = getInt(SocketServerConfigs.NUM_NETWORK_THREADS_CONFIG)\n   428\u2192\n   429\u2192  /***************** rack configuration **************/\n   430\u2192  val rack = Option(getString(ServerConfigs.BROKER_RACK_CONFIG))\n   431\u2192  val replicaSelectorClassName = Option(getString(ReplicationConfigs.REPLICA_SELECTOR_CLASS_CONFIG))\n   432\u2192\n   433\u2192  /** getInt(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_FLUSH_START_OFFSET_CHECKPOINT_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG).toLong\n   444\u2192  val logCleanupIntervalMs = getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG)\n   445\u2192  def logCleanupPolicy = getList(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_CLEANUP_POLICY_CONFIG)\n   446\u2192\n   447\u2192  def logRetentionBytes = getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_RETENTION_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   448\u2192  val logCleanerDedupeBufferSize = getLong(CleanerConfig.LOG_CLEANER_DEDUPE_BUFFER_SIZE_PROP)\n   449\u2192  val logCleanerDedupeBufferLoadFactor = getDouble(CleanerConfig.LOG_CLEANER_DEDUPE_BUFFER_LOAD_FACTOR_PROP)\n   450\u2192  val logCleanerIoBufferSize = getInt(CleanerConfig.LOG_CLEANER_IO_BUFFER_SIZE_PROP)\n   451\u2192  val logCleanerIoMaxBytesPerSecond = getDouble(CleanerConfig.LOG_CLEANER_IO_MAX_BYTES_PER_SECOND_PROP)\n   452\u2192  def logCleanerDeleteRetentionMs = getLong(CleanerConfig.LOG_CLEANER_DELETE_RETENTION_MS_PROP)\n   453\u2192  def logCleanerMinCompactionLagMs = getLong(CleanerConfig.LOG_CLEANER_MIN_COMPACTION_LAG_MS_PROP)\n   454\u2192  def logIndexSizeMaxBytes = getInt(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_INDEX_SIZE_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   459\u2192  def logIndexIntervalBytes = getInt(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_INDEX_INTERVAL_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   460\u2192  def Option(getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG)).getOrElse(60 * 60 * 1000L * getInt(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_ROLL_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG))\n   462\u2192  def logRollTimeJitterMillis: logFlushIntervalMs: java.lang.Long = Option(getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_FLUSH_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG)).getOrElse(getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_FLUSH_SCHEDULER_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG))\n   464\u2192  def minInSyncReplicas = getInt(ServerLogConfigs.MIN_IN_SYNC_REPLICAS_CONFIG)\n   465\u2192  def logPreAllocateEnable: java.lang.Boolean = getBoolean(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_PRE_ALLOCATE_CONFIG)\n   466\u2192  def logInitialTaskDelayMs: java.lang.Long = Option(getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_INITIAL_TASK_DELAY_MS_CONFIG)).getOrElse(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_INITIAL_TASK_DELAY_MS_DEFAULT)\n   467\u2192\n   468\u2192  // We keep the user-provided String as `MetadataVersion.fromVersionString` can choose a slightly different version (eg if `0.10.0`\n   469\u2192  // is passed, `0.10.0-IV0` may be picked)\n   470\u2192  @nowarn(\"cat=deprecation\")\n   471\u2192  private val logMessageFormatVersionString = getString(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_CONFIG)\n   472\u2192\n   473\u2192  /* See `TopicConfig.MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_CONFIG` for details */\n   474\u2192  @deprecated(\"3.0\")\n   475\u2192  lazy val logMessageFormatVersion =\n   476\u2192    if (LogConfig.shouldIgnoreMessageFormatVersion(interBrokerProtocolVersion))\n   477\u2192      MetadataVersion.fromVersionString(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_DEFAULT)\n   478\u2192    else MetadataVersion.fromVersionString(logMessageFormatVersionString)\n   479\u2192\n   480\u2192  def logMessageTimestampType = TimestampType.forName(getString(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_TYPE_CONFIG))\n   481\u2192\n   482\u2192  /* See `TopicConfig.MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_DIFFERENCE_MAX_MS_CONFIG` for details */\n   483\u2192  @deprecated(\"3.6\")\n   484\u2192  def logMessageTimestampDifferenceMaxMs: Long = getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_DIFFERENCE_MAX_MS_CONFIG)\n   485\u2192\n   486\u2192  // In the transition period before logMessageTimestampDifferenceMaxMs is removed, to maintain backward compatibility,\n   487\u2192  // we are using its value if logMessageTimestampBeforeMaxMs default value hasn't changed.\n   488\u2192  // See `TopicConfig.MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_CONFIG` for deprecation details\n   489\u2192  @nowarn(\"cat=deprecation\")\n   490\u2192  def logMessageTimestampBeforeMaxMs: Long = {\n   491\u2192    val messageTimestampBeforeMaxMs: Long = getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_BEFORE_MAX_MS_CONFIG)\n   492\u2192    if (messageTimestampBeforeMaxMs != ServerLogConfigs.LOG_MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_DIFFERENCE_MAX_MS_DEFAULT) {\n   493\u2192      messageTimestampBeforeMaxMs\n   494\u2192    } else {\n   495\u2192      logMessageTimestampDifferenceMaxMs\n   496\u2192    }\n   497\u2192  }\n   498\u2192\n   499\u2192  // In the transition period before logMessageTimestampDifferenceMaxMs is removed, to maintain backward compatibility,\n   500\u2192  // we are using its value if logMessageTimestampAfterMaxMs default value hasn't changed.\n   501\u2192  // See `TopicConfig.MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_CONFIG` for deprecation details\n   502\u2192  @nowarn(\"cat=deprecation\")\n   503\u2192  def logMessageTimestampAfterMaxMs: Long = {\n   504\u2192    val messageTimestampAfterMaxMs: Long = getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_AFTER_MAX_MS_CONFIG)\n   505\u2192    if (messageTimestampAfterMaxMs != Long.MaxValue) {\n   506\u2192      messageTimestampAfterMaxMs\n   507\u2192    } else {\n   508\u2192      logMessageTimestampDifferenceMaxMs\n   509\u2192    }\n   510\u2192  }\n   511\u2192\n   512\u2192  def logMessageDownConversionEnable: Boolean = getBoolean(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_MESSAGE_DOWNCONVERSION_ENABLE_CONFIG)\n   513\u2192\n   514\u2192  def logDirFailureTimeoutMs: Long = getLong(ServerLogConfigs.LOG_DIR_FAILURE_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n   515\u2192\n   516\u2192  /** ********* Replication configuration ***********/\n   517\u2192  val controllerSocketTimeoutMs: Int = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.CONTROLLER_SOCKET_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n getLong(ReplicationConfigs.REPLICA_LAG_TIME_MAX_MS_CONFIG)\n   520\u2192  val replicaSocketTimeoutMs = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.REPLICA_SOCKET_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n   521\u2192  val replicaSocketReceiveBufferBytes = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.REPLICA_SOCKET_RECEIVE_BUFFER_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   522\u2192  val replicaFetchMaxBytes = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.REPLICA_FETCH_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   523\u2192  val replicaFetchWaitMaxMs = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.REPLICA_FETCH_WAIT_MAX_MS_CONFIG)\n   524\u2192  val replicaFetchMinBytes = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.REPLICA_FETCH_MIN_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   525\u2192  val replicaFetchResponseMaxBytes = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.REPLICA_FETCH_RESPONSE_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   526\u2192  val replicaFetchBackoffMs = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.REPLICA_FETCH_BACKOFF_MS_CONFIG)\n   527\u2192  def numReplicaFetchers = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.NUM_REPLICA_FETCHERS_CONFIG)\n   528\u2192  val replicaHighWatermarkCheckpointIntervalMs = getLong(ReplicationConfigs.REPLICA_HIGH_WATERMARK_CHECKPOINT_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG)\n   529\u2192  val fetchPurgatoryPurgeIntervalRequests = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.FETCH_PURGATORY_PURGE_INTERVAL_REQUESTS_CONFIG)\n   530\u2192  val producerPurgatoryPurgeIntervalRequests = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.PRODUCER_PURGATORY_PURGE_INTERVAL_REQUESTS_CONFIG)\n   531\u2192  val deleteRecordsPurgatoryPurgeIntervalRequests = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.DELETE_RECORDS_PURGATORY_PURGE_INTERVAL_REQUESTS_CONFIG)\n   532\u2192  val autoLeaderRebalanceEnable = getBoolean(ReplicationConfigs.AUTO_LEADER_REBALANCE_ENABLE_CONFIG)\n   533\u2192  val leaderImbalancePerBrokerPercentage = getInt(ReplicationConfigs.LEADER_IMBALANCE_PER_BROKER_PERCENTAGE_CONFIG)\n   534\u2192  val leaderImbalanceCheckIntervalSeconds: Long = getLong(ReplicationConfigs.LEADER_IMBALANCE_CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS_CONFIG)\n   535\u2192  val uncleanLeaderElectionCheckIntervalMs: Long = getLong(ReplicationConfigs.UNCLEAN_LEADER_ELECTION_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG)\n   536\u2192  def uncleanLeaderElectionEnable: java.lang.Boolean = getBoolean(ReplicationConfigs.UNCLEAN_LEADER_ELECTION_ENABLE_CONFIG)\n   537\u2192\n   538\u2192  // We keep the user-provided String as `MetadataVersion.fromVersionString` can choose a slightly different version (eg if `0.10.0`\n   539\u2192  // is passed, `0.10.0-IV0` may be picked)\n   540\u2192  val interBrokerProtocolVersionString = getString(ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION_CONFIG)\n   541\u2192  val interBrokerProtocolVersion = if (processRoles.isEmpty) {\n   542\u2192    MetadataVersion.fromVersionString(interBrokerProtocolVersionString)\n   543\u2192  } else {\n   544\u2192    if (originals.containsKey(ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION_CONFIG)) {\n   545\u2192      // A user-supplied IBP was given\n   546\u2192      val configuredVersion = MetadataVersion.fromVersionString(interBrokerProtocolVersionString)\n   547\u2192      if (!configuredVersion.isKRaftSupported) {\n   548\u2192        throw new ConfigException(s\"A non-KRaft version $interBrokerProtocolVersionString given for ${ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION_CONFIG}. \" +\n   549\u2192          s\"The minimum version is ${MetadataVersion.MINIMUM_KRAFT_VERSION}\")\n   550\u2192      } else {\n   551\u2192        warn(s\"${ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION_CONFIG} is deprecated in KRaft mode as of 3.3 and will only \" +\n   552\u2192          s\"be read when first upgrading from a KRaft prior to 3.3. See kafka-storage.sh help for details on setting \" +\n   553\u2192          s\"the metadata.version for a new KRaft cluster.\")\n   554\u2192      }\n   555\u2192    }\n   556\u2192    // In KRaft mode, we pin this value to the minimum KRaft-supported version. This prevents inadvertent usage of\n   557\u2192    // the static IBP config in broker components running in KRaft mode\n   558\u2192    MetadataVersion.MINIMUM_KRAFT_VERSION\n   559\u2192  }\n   560\u2192\n   561\u2192  /** ********* Controlled shutdown configuration ***********/\n   562\u2192  val controlledShutdownMaxRetries = getInt(ServerConfigs.CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_MAX_RETRIES_CONFIG)\n   563\u2192  val controlledShutdownRetryBackoffMs = getLong(ServerConfigs.CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_RETRY_BACKOFF_MS_CONFIG)\n   564\u2192  val controlledShutdownEnable = getBoolean(ServerConfigs.CONTROLLED_SHUTDOWN_ENABLE_CONFIG)\n   565\u2192\n   566\u2192  /** ********* Feature configuration ***********/\n   567\u2192  def isFeatureVersioningSupported = interBrokerProtocolVersion.isFeatureVersioningSupported\n   568\u2192\n   569\u2192  /** New group coordinator configs */\n   570\u2192  val groupCoordinatorRebalanceProtocols = {\n   571\u2192    val protocols = getList(GroupCoordinatorConfig.GROUP_COORDINATOR_REBALANCE_PROTOCOLS_CONFIG)\n   572\u2192      .asScala.map(_.toUpperCase).map(GroupType.valueOf).toSet\n   573\u2192    if (!protocols.contains(GroupType.CLASSIC)) {\n   574\u2192      throw new ConfigException(s\"Disabling the '${GroupType.CLASSIC}' protocol is not supported.\")\n   575\u2192    }\n   576\u2192    if (protocols.contains(GroupType.CONSUMER)) {\n   577\u2192      if (processRoles.isEmpty) {\n   578\u2192        throw new ConfigException(s\"The new '${GroupType.CONSUMER}' rebalance protocol is only supported in KRaft cluster.\")\n   579\u2192      }\n   580\u2192      warn(s\"The new '${GroupType.CONSUMER}' rebalance protocol is enabled along with the new group coordinator. \" +\n   581\u2192        \"This is part of the preview of KIP-848 and MUST NOT be used in production.\")\n   582\u2192    }\n   583\u2192    protocols\n   584\u2192  }\n   585\u2192  // The new group coordinator is enabled in two cases: 1) The internal configuration to enable\n   586\u2192  // it is explicitly set; or 2) the consumer rebalance protocol is enabled.\n   587\u2192  val isNewGroupCoordinatorEnabled = getBoolean(GroupCoordinatorConfig.NEW_GROUP_COORDINATOR_ENABLE_CONFIG) ||\n   588\u2192    groupCoordinatorRebalanceProtocols.contains(GroupType.CONSUMER)\n   589\u2192\n   590\u2192  /** ********* Transaction management configuration ***********/\n   591\u2192  val transactionalIdExpirationMs = getInt(TransactionStateManagerConfigs.TRANSACTIONAL_ID_EXPIRATION_MS_CONFIG)\n   592\u2192  val transactionMaxTimeoutMs = getInt(TransactionStateManagerConfigs.TRANSACTIONS_MAX_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n   593\u2192  val transactionTopicMinISR = getInt(TransactionLogConfigs.TRANSACTIONS_TOPIC_MIN_ISR_CONFIG)\n   594\u2192  val transactionsLoadBufferSize = getInt(TransactionLogConfigs.TRANSACTIONS_LOAD_BUFFER_SIZE_CONFIG)\n   595\u2192  val transactionTopicReplicationFactor = getShort(TransactionLogConfigs.TRANSACTIONS_TOPIC_REPLICATION_FACTOR_CONFIG)\n   596\u2192  val transactionTopicPartitions = getInt(TransactionLogConfigs.TRANSACTIONS_TOPIC_PARTITIONS_CONFIG)\n   597\u2192  val transactionTopicSegmentBytes = getInt(TransactionLogConfigs.TRANSACTIONS_TOPIC_SEGMENT_BYTES_CONFIG)\n   598\u2192  val transactionAbortTimedOutTransactionCleanupIntervalMs = getInt(TransactionStateManagerConfigs.TRANSACTIONS_ABORT_TIMED_OUT_TRANSACTION_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG)\n   599\u2192  val transactionRemoveExpiredTransactionalIdCleanupIntervalMs = getInt(TransactionStateManagerConfigs.TRANSACTIONS_REMOVE_EXPIRED_TRANSACTIONAL_ID_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG)\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
191. Bash @ -

192. Bash @ -

  "command": "find /workspace -name \"*LogConfigs*\" -o -name \"*LogConfig*\" 2>/dev/null | grep -E \"(java|scala)\" | head -10"

org.apache.kafka.common.config.internals.BrokerSecurityConfigs;\n    22\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Utils;\n    23\u2192import org.apache.kafka.coordinator.transaction.TransactionStateManagerConfigs;\n    26\u2192import org.apache.kafka.network.SocketServerConfigs;\n    27\u2192import org.apache.kafka.raft.QuorumConfig;\n org.apache.kafka.server.metrics.MetricConfigs;\n    31\u2192import org.apache.kafka.storage.internals.log.CleanerConfig;\n    32\u2192import org.apache.kafka.storage.internals.log.LogConfig;\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192import java.util.Arrays;\n    35\u2192import java.util.Map;\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192/**\n    38\u2192 * During moving {@link kafka.server.KafkaConfig} out of core AbstractKafkaConfig will be the future KafkaConfig\n    39\u2192 * so any new getters, or updates to `CONFIG_DEF` will be defined here.\n    40\u2192 * Any code depends on kafka.server.KafkaConfig will keep for using kafka.server.KafkaConfig for the time being until we move it out of core\n    41\u2192 * For more details check KAFKA-15853\n    42\u2192 */\n    43\u2192public abstract class AbstractKafkaConfig extends AbstractConfig {\n    44\u2192    @SuppressWarnings(\"deprecation\")\n    45\u2192    public static final ConfigDef CONFIG_DEF =  Utils.mergeConfigs(Arrays.asList(\n    46\u2192            RemoteLogManagerConfig.configDef(),\n    47\u2192            ZkConfigs.CONFIG_DEF,\n    48\u2192            ServerConfigs.CONFIG_DEF,\n    49\u2192            KRaftConfigs.CONFIG_DEF,\n    50\u2192            SocketServerConfigs.CONFIG_DEF,\n    51\u2192            ReplicationConfigs.CONFIG_DEF,\n    52\u2192            GroupCoordinatorConfig.GROUP_COORDINATOR_CONFIG_DEF,\n    53\u2192            GroupCoordinatorConfig.NEW_GROUP_CONFIG_DEF,\n    54\u2192            GroupCoordinatorConfig.OFFSET_MANAGEMENT_CONFIG_DEF,\n    55\u2192            GroupCoordinatorConfig.CONSUMER_GROUP_CONFIG_DEF,\n    56\u2192            GroupCoordinatorConfig.SHARE_GROUP_CONFIG_DEF,\n    57\u2192            CleanerConfig.CONFIG_DEF,\n    58\u2192            LogConfig.SERVER_CONFIG_DEF,\n    59\u2192            ShareGroupConfig.CONFIG_DEF,\n    60\u2192            TransactionLogConfigs.CONFIG_DEF,\n    61\u2192            TransactionStateManagerConfigs.CONFIG_DEF,\n    62\u2192            QuorumConfig.CONFIG_DEF,\n    63\u2192            MetricConfigs.CONFIG_DEF,\n    64\u2192            QuotaConfigs.CONFIG_DEF,\n    65\u2192            BrokerSecurityConfigs.CONFIG_DEF,\n    66\u2192            DelegationTokenManagerConfigs.CONFIG_DEF,\n    67\u2192            PasswordEncoderConfigs.CONFIG_DEF\n    68\u2192        ));\n    69\u2192    public AbstractKafkaConfig(ConfigDef definition, Map<?, ?> originals, Map<String, ?> configProviderProps, boolean doLog) {\n    70\u2192        super(definition, originals, configProviderProps, doLog);\n    71\u2192    }\n    72\u2192}\n    73\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. org.apache.kafka.server.config;\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.config.TopicConfig;\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.server.common.MetadataVersion.IBP_3_0_IV1;\n    23\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.server.config.ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.LOG_PREFIX;\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192/**\n    26\u2192 * Common home for broker-side log configs which need to be accessible from the libraries shared\n    27\u2192 * between the broker and the multiple modules in Kafka.\n    28\u2192 *\n    29\u2192 * Note this is an internal API and subject to change without notice.\n    30\u2192 */\n    31\u2192public class ServerLogConfigs {\n    32\u2192    public static final String NUM_PARTITIONS_CONFIG = \"num.partitions\";\n    33\u2192    public static final int NUM_PARTITIONS_DEFAULT = 1;\n    34\u2192    public static final String NUM_PARTITIONS_DOC = \"The default number of log partitions per topic\";\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192    public static final String LOG_DIRS_CONFIG = LOG_PREFIX + \"dirs\";\n    37\u2192    public static final String LOG_DIR_CONFIG = LOG_PREFIX + \"dir\";\n    38\u2192    public static final String LOG_DIR_DEFAULT = \"/tmp/kafka-logs\";\n    39\u2192    public static final String LOG_DIR_DOC = \"The directory in which the log data is kept (supplemental for \" + LOG_DIRS_CONFIG + \" property)\";\n    40\u2192    public static final String LOG_DIRS_DOC = \"A comma-separated list of the directories where the log data is stored. If not set, the value in \" + LOG_DIR_CONFIG + \" is used.\";\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192    public static final String LOG_SEGMENT_BYTES_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.SEGMENT_BYTES_CONFIG);\n    43\u2192    public static final String LOG_SEGMENT_BYTES_DOC = \"The maximum size of a single log file\";\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192    public static final String LOG_ROLL_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.SEGMENT_MS_CONFIG);\n    46\u2192    public static final String LOG_ROLL_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG = LOG_PREFIX + \"roll.hours\";\n    47\u2192    public static final String LOG_ROLL_TIME_MILLIS_DOC = \"The maximum time before a new log segment is rolled out (in milliseconds). If not set, the value in \" + LOG_ROLL_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG + \" is used\";\n    48\u2192    public static final String LOG_ROLL_TIME_HOURS_DOC = \"The maximum time before a new log segment is rolled out (in hours), secondary to \" + LOG_ROLL_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG + \" property\";\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192    public static final String LOG_ROLL_TIME_JITTER_MILLIS_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.SEGMENT_JITTER_MS_CONFIG);\n    51\u2192    public static final String LOG_ROLL_TIME_JITTER_HOURS_CONFIG = LOG_PREFIX + \"roll.jitter.hours\";\n    52\u2192    public static final String LOG_ROLL_TIME_JITTER_MILLIS_DOC = \"The maximum jitter to subtract from logRollTimeMillis (in milliseconds). If not set, the value in \" + LOG_ROLL_TIME_JITTER_HOURS_CONFIG + \" is used\";\n    53\u2192    public static final String LOG_ROLL_TIME_JITTER_HOURS_DOC = \"The maximum jitter to subtract from logRollTimeMillis (in hours), secondary to \" + LOG_ROLL_TIME_JITTER_MILLIS_CONFIG + \" property\";\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192    public static final String LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.RETENTION_MS_CONFIG);\n    57\u2192    public static final String LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MINUTES_CONFIG = LOG_PREFIX + \"retention.minutes\";\n    58\u2192    public static final String LOG_RETENTION_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG = LOG_PREFIX + \"retention.hours\";\n    59\u2192    public static final String LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MILLIS_DOC = \"The number of milliseconds to keep a log file before deleting it (in milliseconds), If not set, the value in \" + LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MINUTES_CONFIG + \" is used. If set to -1, no time limit is applied.\";\n    60\u2192    public static final String LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MINUTES_DOC = \"The number of minutes to keep a log file before deleting it (in minutes), secondary to \" + LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG + \" property. If not set, the value in \" + LOG_RETENTION_TIME_HOURS_CONFIG + \" is used\";\n    61\u2192    public static final String LOG_RETENTION_TIME_HOURS_DOC = \"The number of hours to keep a log file before deleting it (in hours), tertiary to \" + LOG_RETENTION_TIME_MILLIS_CONFIG + \" property\";\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192    public static final String LOG_RETENTION_BYTES_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.RETENTION_BYTES_CONFIG);\n    64\u2192    public static final long LOG_RETENTION_BYTES_DEFAULT = -1L;\n    65\u2192    public static final String LOG_RETENTION_BYTES_DOC = \"The maximum size of the log before deleting it\";\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192    public static final String LOG_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG = LOG_PREFIX + \"retention.check.interval.ms\";\n    68\u2192    public static final long LOG_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS_DEFAULT = 5 * 60 * 1000L;\n    69\u2192    public static final String LOG_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS_DOC = \"The frequency in milliseconds that the log cleaner checks whether any log is eligible for deletion\";\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192    public static final String LOG_CLEANUP_POLICY_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.CLEANUP_POLICY_CONFIG);\n    72\u2192    public static final String LOG_CLEANUP_POLICY_DEFAULT = TopicConfig.CLEANUP_POLICY_DELETE;\n    73\u2192    public static final String LOG_CLEANUP_POLICY_DOC = \"The default cleanup policy for segments beyond the retention window. A comma separated list of valid policies. Valid policies are: \\\"delete\\\" and \\\"compact\\\"\";\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192    public static final String LOG_INDEX_SIZE_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.SEGMENT_INDEX_BYTES_CONFIG);\n    76\u2192    public static final int LOG_INDEX_SIZE_MAX_BYTES_DEFAULT = 10 * 1024 * 1024;\n    77\u2192    public static final String LOG_INDEX_SIZE_MAX_BYTES_DOC = \"The maximum size in bytes of the offset index\";\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192    public static final String LOG_INDEX_INTERVAL_BYTES_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.INDEX_INTERVAL_BYTES_CONFIG);\n    80\u2192    public static final int LOG_INDEX_INTERVAL_BYTES_DEFAULT = 4096;\n    81\u2192    public static final String LOG_INDEX_INTERVAL_BYTES_DOC = \"The interval with which we add an entry to the offset index.\";\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192    public static final String LOG_FLUSH_INTERVAL_MESSAGES_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.FLUSH_MESSAGES_INTERVAL_CONFIG);\n    84\u2192    public static final long LOG_FLUSH_INTERVAL_MESSAGES_DEFAULT = Long.MAX_VALUE;\n    85\u2192    public static final String LOG_FLUSH_INTERVAL_MESSAGES_DOC = \"The number of messages accumulated on a log partition before messages are flushed to disk.\";\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192    public static final String LOG_DELETE_DELAY_MS_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.FILE_DELETE_DELAY_MS_CONFIG);\n    88\u2192    public static final long LOG_DELETE_DELAY_MS_DEFAULT = 60000L;\n    89\u2192    public static final String LOG_DELETE_DELAY_MS_DOC = \"The amount of time to wait before deleting a file from the filesystem. If the value is 0 and there is no file to delete, the system will wait 1 millisecond. Low value will cause busy waiting\";\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192    public static final String LOG_FLUSH_SCHEDULER_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG = LOG_PREFIX + \"flush.scheduler.interval.ms\";\n    92\u2192    public static final long LOG_FLUSH_SCHEDULER_INTERVAL_MS_DEFAULT = Long.MAX_VALUE;\n    93\u2192    public static final String LOG_FLUSH_SCHEDULER_INTERVAL_MS_DOC = \"The frequency in ms that the log flusher checks whether any log needs to be flushed to disk\";\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192    public static final String LOG_FLUSH_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.FLUSH_MS_CONFIG);\n    96\u2192    public static final String LOG_FLUSH_INTERVAL_MS_DOC = \"The maximum time in ms that a message in any topic is kept in memory before flushed to disk. If not set, the value in \" + LOG_FLUSH_SCHEDULER_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG + \" is used\";\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192    public static final String LOG_FLUSH_OFFSET_CHECKPOINT_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG = LOG_PREFIX + \"flush.offset.checkpoint.interval.ms\";\n    99\u2192    public static final int LOG_FLUSH_OFFSET_CHECKPOINT_INTERVAL_MS_DEFAULT = 60000;\n   100\u2192    public static final String LOG_FLUSH_OFFSET_CHECKPOINT_INTERVAL_MS_DOC = \"The frequency with which we update the persistent record of the last flush which acts as the log recovery point.\";\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192    public static final String LOG_FLUSH_START_OFFSET_CHECKPOINT_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG = LOG_PREFIX + \"flush.start.offset.checkpoint.interval.ms\";\n   103\u2192    public static final int LOG_FLUSH_START_OFFSET_CHECKPOINT_INTERVAL_MS_DEFAULT = 60000;\n   104\u2192    public static final String LOG_FLUSH_START_OFFSET_CHECKPOINT_INTERVAL_MS_DOC = \"The frequency with which we update the persistent record of log start offset\";\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192    public static final String LOG_PRE_ALLOCATE_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.PREALLOCATE_CONFIG);\n   107\u2192    public static final String LOG_PRE_ALLOCATE_ENABLE_DOC = \"Should pre allocate file when create new segment? If you are using Kafka on Windows, you probably need to set it to true.\";\n   108\u2192\n   109\u2192    /* See `TopicConfig.MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_CONFIG` for details */\n   110\u2192    /**\n   111\u2192     * @deprecated since \"3.0\"\n   112\u2192     */\n   113\u2192    @Deprecated\n   114\u2192    public static final String LOG_MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_CONFIG);\n   115\u2192    @Deprecated\n   116\u2192    public static final String LOG_MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_DEFAULT = IBP_3_0_IV1.version();\n   117\u2192    public static final String LOG_MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_DOC = \"Specify the message format version the broker will use to append messages to the logs. The value should be a valid MetadataVersion. \" +\n   118\u2192            \"Some examples are: 0.8.2, 0.9.0.0, 0.10.0, check MetadataVersion for more details. By setting a particular message format version, the \" +\n   119\u2192            \"user is certifying that all the existing messages on disk are smaller or equal than the specified version. Setting this value incorrectly \" +\n   120\u2192            \"will cause consumers with older versions to break as they will receive messages with a format that they don't understand.\";\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192    public static final String LOG_MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_TYPE_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_TYPE_CONFIG);\n   123\u2192    public static final String LOG_MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_TYPE_DEFAULT = \"CreateTime\";\n   124\u2192    public static final String LOG_MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_TYPE_DOC = \"Define whether the timestamp in the message is message create time or log append time. The value should be either \" +\n   125\u2192            \"<code>CreateTime</code> or <code>LogAppendTime</code>.\";\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192    /* See `TopicConfig.MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_DIFFERENCE_MAX_MS_CONFIG` for details */\n   128\u2192    /**\n   129\u2192     * @deprecated since \"3.6\"\n   130\u2192     */\n   131\u2192    @Deprecated\n   132\u2192    public static final String LOG_MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_DIFFERENCE_MAX_MS_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_DIFFERENCE_MAX_MS_CONFIG);\n   133\u2192    @Deprecated\n   134\u2192    public static final long LOG_MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_DIFFERENCE_MAX_MS_DEFAULT = Long.MAX_VALUE;\n   135\u2192    public static final String LOG_MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_DIFFERENCE_MAX_MS_DOC = \"[DEPRECATED] The maximum difference allowed between the timestamp when a broker receives \" +\n   136\u2192            \"a message and the timestamp specified in the message. If log.message.timestamp.type=CreateTime, a message will be rejected \" +\n   137\u2192            \"if the difference in timestamp exceeds this threshold. This configuration is ignored if log.message.timestamp.type=LogAppendTime.\" +\n   138\u2192            \"The maximum timestamp difference allowed should be no greater than log.retention.ms to avoid unnecessarily frequent log rolling.\";\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192    public static final String LOG_MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_BEFORE_MAX_MS_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_BEFORE_MAX_MS_CONFIG);\n   141\u2192    public static final long LOG_MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_BEFORE_MAX_MS_DEFAULT = Long.MAX_VALUE;\n   142\u2192    public static final String LOG_MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_BEFORE_MAX_MS_DOC = \"This configuration sets the allowable timestamp difference between the \" +\n   143\u2192            \"broker's timestamp and the message timestamp. The message timestamp can be earlier than or equal to the broker's \" +\n   144\u2192            \"timestamp, with the maximum allowable difference determined by the value set in this configuration. \" +\n   145\u2192            \"If log.message.timestamp.type=CreateTime, the message will be rejected if the difference in timestamps exceeds \" +\n   146\u2192            \"this specified threshold. This configuration is ignored if log.message.timestamp.type=LogAppendTime.\";\n   147\u2192    public static final String LOG_MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_AFTER_MAX_MS_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_AFTER_MAX_MS_CONFIG);\n   148\u2192    public static final long LOG_MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_AFTER_MAX_MS_DEFAULT = Long.MAX_VALUE;\n   149\u2192    public static final String LOG_MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_AFTER_MAX_MS_DOC = \"This configuration sets the allowable timestamp difference between the \" +\n   150\u2192            \"message timestamp and the broker's timestamp. The message timestamp can be later than or equal to the broker's \" +\n   151\u2192            \"timestamp, with the maximum allowable difference determined by the value set in this configuration. \" +\n   152\u2192            \"If log.message.timestamp.type=CreateTime, the message will be rejected if the difference in timestamps exceeds \" +\n   153\u2192            \"this specified threshold. This configuration is ignored if log.message.timestamp.type=LogAppendTime.\";\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192    public static final String NUM_RECOVERY_THREADS_PER_DATA_DIR_CONFIG = \"num.recovery.threads.per.data.dir\";\n   156\u2192    public static final int NUM_RECOVERY_THREADS_PER_DATA_DIR_DEFAULT = 1;\n   157\u2192    public static final String NUM_RECOVERY_THREADS_PER_DATA_DIR_DOC = \"The number of threads per data directory to be used for log recovery at startup and flushing at shutdown\";\n   158\u2192\n   159\u2192    public static final String AUTO_CREATE_TOPICS_ENABLE_CONFIG = \"auto.create.topics.enable\";\n   160\u2192    public static final boolean AUTO_CREATE_TOPICS_ENABLE_DEFAULT = true;\n   161\u2192    public static final String AUTO_CREATE_TOPICS_ENABLE_DOC = \"Enable auto creation of topic on the server.\";\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192    public static final String MIN_IN_SYNC_REPLICAS_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.MIN_IN_SYNC_REPLICAS_CONFIG);\n   164\u2192    public static final int MIN_IN_SYNC_REPLICAS_DEFAULT = 1;\n   165\u2192    public static final String MIN_IN_SYNC_REPLICAS_DOC = \"When a producer sets acks to \\\"all\\\" (or \\\"-1\\\"), \" +\n   166\u2192            \"<code>min.insync.replicas</code> specifies the minimum number of replicas that must acknowledge \" +\n   167\u2192            \"a write for the write to be considered successful. If this minimum cannot be met, \" +\n   168\u2192            \"then the producer will raise an exception (either <code>NotEnoughReplicas</code> or \" +\n   169\u2192            \"<code>NotEnoughReplicasAfterAppend</code>).<br>When used together, <code>min.insync.replicas</code> and acks \" +\n   170\u2192            \"allow you to enforce greater durability guarantees. A typical scenario would be to \" +\n   171\u2192            \"create a topic with a replication factor of 3, set <code>min.insync.replicas</code> to 2, and \" +\n   172\u2192            \"produce with acks of \\\"all\\\". This will ensure that the producer raises an exception \" +\n   173\u2192            \"if a majority of replicas do not receive a write.\";\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192    public static final String CREATE_TOPIC_POLICY_CLASS_NAME_CONFIG = \"create.topic.policy.class.name\";\n   176\u2192    public static final String CREATE_TOPIC_POLICY_CLASS_NAME_DOC = \"The create topic policy class that should be used for validation. The class should \" +\n   177\u2192            \"implement the <code>org.apache.kafka.server.policy.CreateTopicPolicy</code> interface.\";\n   178\u2192    public static final String ALTER_CONFIG_POLICY_CLASS_NAME_CONFIG = \"alter.config.policy.class.name\";\n   179\u2192    public static final String ALTER_CONFIG_POLICY_CLASS_NAME_DOC = \"The alter configs policy class that should be used for validation. The class should \" +\n   180\u2192            \"implement the <code>org.apache.kafka.server.policy.AlterConfigPolicy</code> interface.\";\n   181\u2192    public static final String LOG_MESSAGE_DOWNCONVERSION_ENABLE_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.MESSAGE_DOWNCONVERSION_ENABLE_CONFIG);\n   182\u2192    public static final boolean LOG_MESSAGE_DOWNCONVERSION_ENABLE_DEFAULT = true;\n   183\u2192    public static final String LOG_MESSAGE_DOWNCONVERSION_ENABLE_DOC = \"This configuration controls whether \" +\n   184\u2192            \"down-conversion of message formats is enabled to satisfy consume requests. When set to <code>false</code>, \" +\n   185\u2192            \"broker will not perform down-conversion for consumers expecting an older message format. The broker responds \" +\n   186\u2192            \"with <code>UNSUPPORTED_VERSION</code> error for consume requests from such older clients. This configuration\" +\n   187\u2192            \"does not apply to any message format conversion that might be required for replication to followers.\";\n   188\u2192\n   189\u2192    public static final String LOG_INITIAL_TASK_DELAY_MS_CONFIG = LOG_PREFIX + \"initial.task.delay.ms\";\n   190\u2192    public static final long LOG_INITIAL_TASK_DELAY_MS_DEFAULT = 30 * 1000L;\n   191\u2192    public static final String LOG_INITIAL_TASK_DELAY_MS_DOC = \"The initial task delay in millisecond when initializing \" +\n   192\u2192            \"tasks in LogManager. This should be used for testing only.\";\n   193\u2192\n   194\u2192    public static final String LOG_DIR_FAILURE_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG = LOG_PREFIX + \"dir.failure.timeout.ms\";\n   195\u2192    public static final Long LOG_DIR_FAILURE_TIMEOUT_MS_DEFAULT = 30000L;\n   196\u2192    public static final String LOG_DIR_FAILURE_TIMEOUT_MS_DOC = \"If the broker is unable to successfully communicate to the controller that some log \" +\n   197\u2192        \"directory has failed for longer than this time, the broker will fail and shut down.\";\n   198\u2192}\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.
