DynamicConfigTest extends QuorumTestHarness {\n    27\u2192  private final val nonExistentConfig: String = \"some.config.that.does.not.exist\"\n    28\u2192  private final val someValue: String = \"some interesting value\"\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192  @Test\n    31\u2192  def shouldFailWhenChangingClientIdUnknownConfig(): Unit = {\n    32\u2192    assertThrows(classOf[IllegalArgumentException], () => adminZkClient.changeClientIdConfig(\"ClientId\",\n    33\u2192      propsWith(nonExistentConfig, someValue)))\n    34\u2192  }\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192  @Test\n    37\u2192  def shouldFailWhenChangingUserUnknownConfig(): Unit = {\n    38\u2192    assertThrows(classOf[IllegalArgumentException], () => adminZkClient.changeUserOrUserClientIdConfig(\"UserId\",\n    39\u2192      propsWith(nonExistentConfig, someValue)))\n    40\u2192  }\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192  @Test\n    43\u2192  def shouldFailLeaderConfigsWithInvalidValues(): Unit = {\n    44\u2192    assertThrows(classOf[ConfigException], () => adminZkClient.changeBrokerConfig(Seq(0),\n    45\u2192      propsWith(QuotaConfigs.LEADER_REPLICATION_THROTTLED_RATE_CONFIG, \"-100\")))\n    46\u2192  }\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192  @Test\n    49\u2192  def shouldFailFollowerConfigsWithInvalidValues(): Unit = {\n    50\u2192    assertThrows(classOf[ConfigException], () => adminZkClient.changeBrokerConfig(Seq(0),\n    51\u2192      propsWith(QuotaConfigs.FOLLOWER_REPLICATION_THROTTLED_RATE_CONFIG, \"-100\")))\n    52\u2192  }\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192  @Test\n    55\u2192  def shouldFailIpConfigsWithInvalidValues(): Unit = {\n    56\u2192    assertThrows(classOf[ConfigException], () => adminZkClient.changeIpConfig(\"1.2.3.4\",\n    57\u2192      propsWith(QuotaConfigs.IP_CONNECTION_RATE_OVERRIDE_CONFIG, \"-1\")))\n    58\u2192  }\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192  @Test\n    61\u2192  def shouldFailIpConfigsWithInvalidIpv4Entity(): Unit = {\n    62\u2192    assertThrows(classOf[AdminOperationException], () => adminZkClient.changeIpConfig(\"1,1.1.1\",\n    63\u2192      propsWith(QuotaConfigs.IP_CONNECTION_RATE_OVERRIDE_CONFIG, \"2\")))\n    64\u2192  }\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192  @Test\n    67\u2192  def shouldFailIpConfigsWithBadHost(): Unit = {\n    68\u2192 345\u2192    try {\n   346\u2192      val props = fromPersistentProps(persistentProps, perBrokerConfig = false)\n   347\u2192      dynamicDefaultConfigs.clear()\n   348\u2192 applied: ${persistentProps.keys()}\", e)\n   352\u2192    }\n   353\u2192  }\n   354\u2192\n   355\u2192  /**\n   356\u2192   * All config updates through ZooKeeper are triggered through actual changes in values stored in ZooKeeper.\n   357\u2192   * For some configs like SSL keystores and truststores, we also want to reload the store if it was modified\n   358\u2192   * in-place, even though the actual value of the file path and password haven't changed. This scenario alone\n   359\u2192   * is handled here when a config update request using admin client is processed by ZkAdminManager. If any of\n   360\u2192   * the SSL configs have changed, then the update will not be done here, but will be handled later when ZK\n   361\u2192   * changes are processed. At the moment, only listener configs are considered for reloading.\n   362\u2192   */\n   363\u2192  private[server] def reloadUpdatedFilesWithoutConfigChange(newProps: Properties): Unit = CoreUtils.inWriteLock(lock) {\n   364\u2192    reconfigurables.asScala\n   365\u2192      .filter(reconfigurable => ReloadableFileConfigs.exists(reconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs.contains))\n   366\u2192      .foreach {\n   367\u2192        case reconfigurable: ListenerReconfigurable =>\n   368\u2192          val kafkaProps = validatedKafkaProps(newProps, perBrokerConfig = true)\n   369\u2192          val newConfig = new KafkaConfig(kafkaProps.asJava, false)\n   370\u2192          processListenerReconfigurable(reconfigurable, newConfig, Collections.emptyMap(), validateOnly = false, reloadOnly = true)\n   371\u2192        case reconfigurable =>\n   372\u2192          trace(s\"Files will not be reloaded without config change for $reconfigurable\")\n   373\u2192      }\n   374\u2192  }\n   375\u2192\n   376\u2192  private def maybeCreatePasswordEncoder(secret: Option[Password]): Option[PasswordEncoder] = {\n   377\u2192   secret.map { secret =>\n   378\u2192     PasswordEncoder.encrypting(secret,\n   379\u2192        kafkaConfig.passwordEncoderKeyFactoryAlgorithm,\n   380\u2192        kafkaConfig.passwordEncoderCipherAlgorithm,\n   381\u2192        kafkaConfig.passwordEncoderKeyLength,\n   382\u2192        kafkaConfig.passwordEncoderIterations)\n   383\u2192    }\n   384\u2192  }\n   385\u2192\n   386\u2192  private def passwordEncoder: PasswordEncoder = {\n   387\u2192    dynamicConfigPasswordEncoder.getOrElse(throw new ConfigException(\"Password encoder secret not configured\"))\n   388\u2192  }\n   389\u2192\n   390\u2192  private[server] def toPersistentProps(configProps: Properties, perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Properties = {\n   391\u2192    val props = configProps.clone().asInstanceOf[Properties]\n   392\u2192\n   393\u2192    def encodePassword(configName: String, value: String): Unit = {\n   394\u2192      if (value != null) {\n   395\u2192        if (!perBrokerConfig)\n   396\u2192          throw new ConfigException(\"Password config can be defined only at broker level\")\n   397\u2192        props.setProperty(configName, passwordEncoder.encode(new Password(value)))\n   398\u2192      }\n   399\u2192    }\n   400\u2192    configProps.asScala.forKeyValue { (name, value) =>\n   401\u2192      if (isPasswordConfig(name))\n   402\u2192        encodePassword(name, value)\n   403\u2192    }\n   404\u2192    props\n   405\u2192  }\n   406\u2192\n   407\u2192  private[server] def fromPersistentProps(persistentProps: Properties,\n   408\u2192                                          perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Properties = {\n   409\u2192    val props = persistentProps.clone().asInstanceOf[Properties]\n   410\u2192\n   411\u2192    // Remove all invalid configs from `props`\n   412\u2192    removeInvalidConfigs(props, perBrokerConfig)\n   413\u2192    def removeInvalidProps(invalidPropNames: Set[String], errorMessage: String): Unit = {\n   414\u2192      if (invalidPropNames.nonEmpty) {\n   415\u2192        invalidPropNames.foreach(props.remove)\n   416\u2192        error(s\"$errorMessage: $invalidPropNames\")\n   417\u2192      }\n   418\u2192    }\n   419\u2192    removeInvalidProps(nonDynamicConfigs(props), \"Non-dynamic configs configured in ZooKeeper will be ignored\")\n   420\u2192    removeInvalidProps(securityConfigsWithoutListenerPrefix(props),\n   421\u2192      \"Security configs can be dynamically updated only using listener prefix, base configs will be ignored\")\n   422\u2192    if (!perBrokerConfig)\n   423\u2192      removeInvalidProps(perBrokerConfigs(props), \"Per-broker configs defined at default cluster level will be ignored\")\n   424\u2192\n   425\u2192    def decodePassword(configName: String, value: String): Unit = {\n   426\u2192      if (value != null) {\n   427\u2192        try {\n   428\u2192          props.setProperty(configName, passwordEncoder.decode(value).value)\n   429\u2192        } catch {\n   430\u2192          case e: Exception =>\n   431\u2192            error(s\"Dynamic password config $configName could not be decoded, ignoring.\", e)\n   432\u2192            props.remove(configName)\n   433\u2192        }\n   434\u2192      }\n   435\u2192    }\n   436\u2192\n   437\u2192    props.asScala.forKeyValue { (name, value) =>\n   438\u2192      if (isPasswordConfig(name))\n   439\u2192        decodePassword(name, value)\n   440\u2192    }\n   441\u2192    props\n   442\u2192  }\n   443\u2192\n   444\u2192  // If the secret has changed, password.encoder.old.secret contains the old secret that was used\n   445\u2192  // to encode the configs in ZK. Decode passwords using the old secret and update ZK with values\n   446\u2192  // encoded using the current secret. Ignore any errors during decoding since old secret may not\n   447\u2192  // have been removed during broker restart.\n   448\u2192  private def maybeReEncodePasswords(persistentProps: Properties, adminZkClient: AdminZkClient): Properties = {\n   449\u2192    val props = persistentProps.clone().asInstanceOf[Properties]\n   450\u2192    if (props.asScala.keySet.exists(isPasswordConfig)) {\n   451\u2192      maybeCreatePasswordEncoder(kafkaConfig.passwordEncoderOldSecret).foreach { passwordDecoder =>\n   452\u2192        persistentProps.asScala.forKeyValue { (configName, value) =>\n   453\u2192          if (isPasswordConfig(configName) && value != null) {\n   454\u2192            val decoded = try {\n   455\u2192              Some(passwordDecoder.decode(value).value)\n   456\u2192            } catch {\n   457\u2192              case _: Exception =>\n   458\u2192                debug(s\"Dynamic password config $configName could not be decoded using old secret, new secret will be used.\")\n   459\u2192                None\n   460\u2192            }\n   461\u2192            decoded.foreach(value => props.put(configName, passwordEncoder.encode(new Password(value))))\n   462\u2192          }\n   463\u2192        }\n   464\u2192        adminZkClient.changeBrokerConfig(Some(kafkaConfig.brokerId), props)\n   465\u2192      }\n   466\u2192    }\n   467\u2192    props\n   468\u2192  }\n   469\u2192\n   470\u2192  /**\n   471\u2192   * Validate the provided configs `propsOverride` and return the full Kafka configs with\n   472\u2192   * the configured defaults and these overrides.\n   473\u2192   *\n   474\u2192   * Note: The caller must acquire the read or write lock before invoking this method.\n   475\u2192   */\n   476\u2192  private def validatedKafkaProps(propsOverride: Properties, perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Map[String, String] = {\n   477\u2192    val propsResolved = DynamicBrokerConfig.resolveVariableConfigs(propsOverride)\n   478\u2192    validateConfigs(propsResolved, perBrokerConfig)\n   479\u2192    val newProps = mutable.Map[String, String]()\n   480\u2192    newProps ++= staticBrokerConfigs\n   481\u2192    if (perBrokerConfig) {\n   482\u2192      overrideProps(newProps, dynamicDefaultConfigs)\n   483\u2192      overrideProps(newProps, propsResolved.asScala)\n   484\u2192    } else {\n   485\u2192      overrideProps(newProps, propsResolved.asScala)\n   486\u2192      overrideProps(newProps, dynamicBrokerConfigs)\n   487\u2192    }\n   488\u2192    newProps\n   489\u2192  }\n   490\u2192\n   491\u2192  private[server] def validate(props: Properties, perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Unit = CoreUtils.inReadLock(lock) {\n   492\u2192    val newProps = validatedKafkaProps(props, perBrokerConfig)\n   493\u2192    processReconfiguration(newProps, validateOnly = true)\n   494\u2192  }\n   495\u2192\n   496\u2192  private def removeInvalidConfigs(props: Properties, perBrokerConfig: Boolean): Unit = {\n   497\u2192    try {\n   498\u2192      validateConfigTypes(props)\n   499\u2192      props.asScala\n   500\u2192    } catch {\n   501\u2192      case e: Exception =>\n   502\u2192        val invalidProps = props.asScala.filter { case (k, v) =>\n   503\u2192          val props1 = new Properties\n   504\u2192          props1.put(k, v)\n   505\u2192          try {\n   506\u2192            validateConfigTypes(props1)\n   507\u2192            false\n   508\u2192          } catch {\n   509\u2192            case _: Exception => true\n   510\u2192          }\n   511\u2192        }\n   512\u2192        invalidProps.keys.foreach(props.remove)\n   513\u2192        val configSource = if (perBrokerConfig) \"broker\" else \"default cluster\"\n   514\u2192        error(s\"Dynamic $configSource config contains invalid values in: ${invalidProps.keys}, these configs will be ignored\", e)\n   515\u2192    }\n   516\u2192  }\n   517\u2192\n   518\u2192  private[server] def maybeReconfigure(reconfigurable: Reconfigurable, oldConfig: KafkaConfig, newConfig: util.Map[String, _]): Unit = {\n   519\u2192    if (reconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs.asScala.exists(key => oldConfig.originals.get(key) != newConfig.get(key)))\n   520\u2192      reconfigurable.reconfigure(newConfig)\n   521\u2192  }\n   522\u2192\n   523\u2192  /**\n   524\u2192   * Returns the change in configurations between the new props and current props by returning a\n   525\u2192   * map of the changed configs, as well as the set of deleted keys\n   526\u2192   */\n   527\u2192  private def updatedConfigs(newProps: java.util.Map[String, _],\n   528\u2192                             currentProps: java.util.Map[String, _]): (mutable.Map[String, _], Set[String]) = {\n   529\u2192    val changeMap = newProps.asScala.filter {\n   530\u2192      case (k, v) => v != currentProps.get(k)\n   531\u2192    }\n   532\u2192    val deletedKeySet = currentProps.asScala.filter {\n   533\u2192      case (k, _) => !newProps.containsKey(k)\n   534\u2192    }.keySet\n   535\u2192    (changeMap, deletedKeySet)\n   536\u2192  }\n   537\u2192\n   538\u2192  /**\n   539\u2192    * Updates values in `props` with the new values from `propsOverride`. Synonyms of updated configs\n   540\u2192    * are removed from `props` to ensure that the config with the higher precedence is applied. For example,\n   541\u2192    * if `log.roll.ms` was defined in server.properties and `log.roll.hours` is configured dynamically,\n   542\u2192    * `log.roll.hours` from the dynamic configuration will be used and `log.roll.ms` will be removed from\n   543\u2192    * `props` (even though `log.roll.hours` is secondary to `log.roll.ms`).\n   544\u2192    */\n   545\u2192  private def overrideProps(props: mutable.Map[String, String], propsOverride: mutable.Map[String, String]): Unit = {\n   546\u2192    propsOverride.forKeyValue { (k, v) =>\n   547\u2192      // Remove synonyms of `k` to ensure the right precedence is applied. But disable `matchListenerOverride`\n   548\u2192      // so that base configs corresponding to listener configs are not removed. Base configs should not be removed\n   549\u2192      // since they may be used by other listeners. It is ok to retain them in `props` since base configs cannot be\n   550\u2192      // dynamically updated and listener-specific configs have the higher precedence.\n   551\u2192      brokerConfigSynonyms(k, matchListenerOverride = false).foreach(props.remove)\n   552\u2192      props.put(k, v)\n   553\u2192    }\n   554\u2192  }\n   555\u2192\n   556\u2192  private def updateCurrentConfig(doLog: Boolean): Unit = {\n   557\u2192    val newProps = mutable.Map[String, String]()\n   558\u2192    newProps ++= staticBrokerConfigs\n   559\u2192    overrideProps(newProps, dynamicDefaultConfigs)\n   560\u2192    overrideProps(newProps, dynamicBrokerConfigs)\n   561\u2192\n   562\u2192    val oldConfig = currentConfig\n   563\u2192    val (newConfig, brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate) = processReconfiguration(newProps, validateOnly = false, doLog)\n   564\u2192    if (newConfig ne currentConfig) {\n   565\u2192      currentConfig = newConfig\n   566\u2192      kafkaConfig.updateCurrentConfig(newConfig)\n   567\u2192\n   568\u2192      // Process BrokerReconfigurable updates after current config is updated\n   569\u2192      brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate.foreach(_.reconfigure(oldConfig, newConfig))\n   570\u2192    }\n   571\u2192  }\n   572\u2192\n   573\u2192  private def processReconfiguration(newProps: Map[String, String], validateOnly: Boolean, doLog: Boolean = false): (KafkaConfig, List[BrokerReconfigurable]) = {\n   574\u2192    val newConfig = new KafkaConfig(newProps.asJava, doLog)\n   575\u2192    val (changeMap, deletedKeySet) = updatedConfigs(newConfig.originalsFromThisConfig, currentConfig.originals)\n   576\u2192    if (changeMap.nonEmpty || deletedKeySet.nonEmpty) {\n   577\u2192      try {\n   578\u2192        val customConfigs = new util.HashMap[String, Object](newConfig.originalsFromThisConfig) // non-Kafka configs\n   579\u2192        newConfig.valuesFromThisConfig.keySet.forEach(k => customConfigs.remove(k))\n   580\u2192        reconfigurables.forEach {\n   581\u2192          case listenerReconfigurable: ListenerReconfigurable =>\n   582\u2192            processListenerReconfigurable(listenerReconfigurable, newConfig, customConfigs, validateOnly, reloadOnly = false)\n   583\u2192          case reconfigurable =>\n   584\u2192            if (needsReconfiguration(reconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs, changeMap.keySet, deletedKeySet))\n   585\u2192              processReconfigurable(reconfigurable, changeMap.keySet, newConfig.valuesFromThisConfig, customConfigs, validateOnly)\n   586\u2192        }\n   587\u2192\n   588\u2192        // BrokerReconfigurable updates are processed after config is updated. Only do the validation here.\n   589\u2192        val brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate = mutable.Buffer[BrokerReconfigurable]()\n   590\u2192        brokerReconfigurables.forEach { reconfigurable =>\n   591\u2192          if (needsReconfiguration(reconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs.asJava, changeMap.keySet, deletedKeySet)) {\n   592\u2192            reconfigurable.validateReconfiguration(newConfig)\n   593\u2192            if (!validateOnly)\n   594\u2192              brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate += reconfigurable\n   595\u2192          }\n   596\u2192        }\n   597\u2192        (newConfig, brokerReconfigurablesToUpdate.toList)\n   598\u2192      } catch {\n   599\u2192        case e: Exception =>\n   600\u2192          if (!validateOnly)\n   601\u2192            error(s\"Failed to update broker configuration with configs : \" +\n   602\u2192                  s\"${ConfigUtils.configMapToRedactedString(newConfig.originalsFromThisConfig, KafkaConfig.configDef)}\", e)\n   603\u2192          throw new ConfigException(\"Invalid dynamic configuration\", e)\n   604\u2192      }\n   605\u2192    }\n   606\u2192    else\n   607\u2192      (currentConfig, List.empty)\n   608\u2192  }\n   609\u2192\n   610\u2192  private def needsReconfiguration(reconfigurableConfigs: util.Set[String], updatedKeys: Set[String], deletedKeys: Set[String]): Boolean = {\n   611\u2192    reconfigurableConfigs.asScala.intersect(updatedKeys).nonEmpty ||\n   612\u2192      reconfigurableConfigs.asScala.intersect(deletedKeys).nonEmpty\n   613\u2192  }\n   614\u2192\n   615\u2192  private def processListenerReconfigurable(listenerReconfigurable: ListenerReconfigurable,\n   616\u2192                                            newConfig: KafkaConfig,\n   617\u2192                                            customConfigs: util.Map[String, Object],\n   618\u2192                                            validateOnly: Boolean,\n   619\u2192                                            reloadOnly:  Boolean): Unit = {\n   620\u2192    val listenerName = listenerReconfigurable.listenerName\n   621\u2192    val oldValues = currentConfig.valuesWithPrefixOverride(listenerName.configPrefix)\n   622\u2192    val newValues = newConfig.valuesFromThisConfigWithPrefixOverride(listenerName.configPrefix)\n   623\u2192    val (changeMap, deletedKeys) = updatedConfigs(newValues, oldValues)\n   624\u2192    val updatedKeys = changeMap.keySet\n   625\u2192    val configsChanged = needsReconfiguration(listenerReconfigurable.reconfigurableConfigs, updatedKeys, deletedKeys)\n   626\u2192    // if `reloadOnly`, reconfigure if configs haven't changed. Otherwise reconfigure if configs have changed\n   627\u2192    if (reloadOnly != configsChanged)\n   628\u2192      processReconfigurable(listenerReconfigurable, updatedKeys, newValues, customConfigs, validateOnly)\n   629\u2192  }\n   630\u2192\n   631\u2192  private def processReconfigurable(reconfigurable: Reconfigurable,\n   632\u2192                                    updatedConfigNames: Set[String],\n   633\u2192                                    allNewConfigs: util.Map[String, _],\n   634\u2192                                    newCustomConfigs: util.Map[String, Object],\n   635\u2192                                    validateOnly: Boolean): Unit = {\n   636\u2192    val newConfigs = new util.HashMap[String, Object]\n   637\u2192    allNewConfigs.forEach((k, v) => newConfigs.put(k, v.asInstanceOf[AnyRef]))\n   638\u2192    newConfigs.putAll(newCustomConfigs)\n   639\u2192    try {\n   640\u2192      reconfigurable.validateReconfiguration(newConfigs)\n   641\u2192    } catch {\n   642\u2192      case e: ConfigException => throw e\n   643\u2192      case _: Exception =>\n   644\u2192        throw new ConfigException(s\"Validation of dynamic config update of $updatedConfigNames failed with class ${reconfigurable.getClass}\")\n   645\u2192    }\n   646\u2192\n   647\u2192    if (!validateOnly) {\n   648\u2192      info(s\"Reconfiguring $reconfigurable, updated configs: $updatedConfigNames \" +\n   649\u2192           s\"custom configs: ${ConfigUtils.configMapToRedactedString(newCustomConfigs, KafkaConfig.configDef)}\")\n   650\u2192      reconfigurable.reconfigure(newConfigs)\n   651\u2192    }\n   652\u2192  }\n   653\u2192}\n   654\u2192\n   655\u2192trait BrokerReconfigurable {\n   656\u2192\n   657\u2192  def reconfigurableConfigs: Set[String]\n   658\u2192\n   659\u2192  def validateReconfiguration(newConfig: KafkaConfig): Unit\n   660\u2192\n   661\u2192  def reconfigure(oldConfig: KafkaConfig, newConfig: KafkaConfig): Unit\n   662\u2192}\n   663\u2192\n   664\u2192object DynamicLogConfig {\n   665\u2192  /**\n   666\u2192   * The log configurations that are non-reconfigurable. This set contains the names you\n   667\u2192   * would use when setting a dynamic configuration on a topic, which are different than the\n   668\u2192   * corresponding broker configuration names.\n   669\u2192   *\n   670\u2192   * For now, message.format.version is not reconfigurable, since we need to check that\n   671\u2192   * the version is supported on all brokers in the cluster.\n   672\u2192   */\n   673\u2192  val NonReconfigrableLogConfigs: Set[String] = Set(TopicConfig.MESSAGE_FORMAT_VERSION_CONFIG)\n   674\u2192\n   675\u2192  /**\n   676\u2192   * The broker configurations pertaining to logs that are reconfigurable. This set contains\n   677\u2192   * the names you would use when setting a static or dynamic broker configuration (not topic\n   678\u2192   * configuration).\n   679\u2192   */\n   680\u2192  val ReconfigurableConfigs: Set[String] =\n   681\u2192    ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.TOPIC_CONFIG_SYNONYMS.asScala.\n   682\u2192      filterNot(s => NonReconfigrableLogConfigs.contains(s._1)).values.toSet\n   683\u2192}\n   684\u2192\n   685\u2192class DynamicLogConfig(logManager: LogManager, server: KafkaBroker) extends BrokerReconfigurable with Logging {\n   686\u2192\n   687\u2192  override def reconfigurableConfigs: Set[String] = {\n   688\u2192    DynamicLogConfig.ReconfigurableConfigs\n   689\u2192  }\n   690\u2192\n   691\u2192  override def validateReconfiguration(newConfig: KafkaConfig): Unit = {\n   692\u2192    // For update of topic config overrides, only config names and types are validated\n   693\u2192    // Names and types have already been validated. For consistency with topic config\n   694\u2192    // validation, no additional validation is performed.\n   695\u2192\n   696\u2192    def validateLogLocalRetentionMs(): Unit = {\n   697\u2192      val logRetentionMs = newConfig.logRetentionTimeMillis\n   698\u2192      val logLocalRetentionMs: java.lang.Long = newConfig.remoteLogManagerConfig.logLocalRetentionMs\n   699\u2192      if (logRetentionMs != -1L && logLocalRetentionMs != -2L) {\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"     1\u2192/**\n     2\u2192  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n     3\u2192  * contributor license agreements. These are configs which have no physical manifestation in the server.properties\n    32\u2192  * and can only be set dynamically.\n    33\u2192  */\n    34\u2192object DynamicConfig {\n    35\u2192    object Broker {\n    36\u2192      private val brokerConfigs = {\n    37\u2192        val configs = QuotaConfigs.brokerQuotaConfigs()\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192        // Filter and define all dynamic configurations\n    40\u2192        KafkaConfig.configKeys\n    41\u2192          .filter { case (configName, _) => AllDynamicConfigs.contains(configName) }\n    42\u2192          .foreach { case (_, config) => configs.define(config) }\n    43\u2192        configs\n    44\u2192      }\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192    // In order to avoid circular reference, all DynamicBrokerConfig's variables which are initialized by `DynamicConfig.Broker` should be moved to `DynamicConfig.Broker`.\n    47\u2192    // Otherwise, those variables of DynamicBrokerConfig will see intermediate state of `DynamicConfig.Broker`, because `brokerConfigs` is created by `DynamicBrokerConfig.AllDynamicConfigs`\n    48\u2192    val nonDynamicProps: Set[String] = KafkaConfig.configNames.toSet -- brokerConfigs.names.asScala\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192    def configKeys: util.Map[String, ConfigDef.ConfigKey] = brokerConfigs.configKeys\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192    def names: util.Set[String] = brokerConfigs.names\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192    def validate(props: Properties): util.Map[String, AnyRef] = DynamicConfig.validate(brokerConfigs, props, customPropsAllowed = true)\n    55\u2192  }\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192  object Client {\n    58\u2192    private val clientConfigs = QuotaConfigs.userAndClientQuotaConfigs()\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192    def configKeys: util.Map[String, ConfigDef.ConfigKey] = clientConfigs.configKeys\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192    def names: util.Set[String] = clientConfigs.names\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192    def validate(props: Properties): util.Map[String, AnyRef] = DynamicConfig.validate(clientConfigs, props, customPropsAllowed = false)\n    65\u2192  }\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192  object User {\n    68\u2192    private val userConfigs = QuotaConfigs.scramMechanismsPlusUserAndClientQuotaConfigs()\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192    def configKeys: util.Map[String, ConfigDef.ConfigKey] = userConfigs.configKeys\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192    def names: util.Set[String] = userConfigs.names\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192    def validate(props: Properties): util.Map[String, AnyRef] = DynamicConfig.validate(userConfigs, props, customPropsAllowed = false)\n    75\u2192  }\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192  object Ip {\n    78\u2192    private val ipConfigs = QuotaConfigs.ipConfigs()\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192    def configKeys: util.Map[String, ConfigDef.ConfigKey] = ipConfigs.configKeys\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192    def names: util.Set[String] = ipConfigs.names\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192    def validate(props: Properties): util.Map[String, AnyRef] = DynamicConfig.validate(ipConfigs, props, customPropsAllowed = false)\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192    def isValidIpEntity(ip: String): Boolean = {\n    87\u2192      if (ip != ZooKeeperInternals.DEFAULT_STRING) {\n    88\u2192        try {\n    89\u2192          InetAddress.getByName(ip)\n    90\u2192        } catch {\n    91\u2192          case _: UnknownHostException => return false\n    92\u2192        }\n    93\u2192      }\n    94\u2192      true\n    95\u2192    }\n    96\u2192  }\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192  object ClientMetrics {\n    99\u2192    private val clientConfigs = org.apache.kafka.server.metrics.ClientMetricsConfigs.configDef()\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192    def names: util.Set[String] = clientConfigs.names\n   102\u2192  }\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192  private def validate(configDef: ConfigDef, props: Properties, customPropsAllowed: Boolean) = {\n   105\u2192    // Validate Names\n   106\u2192    val names = configDef.names()\n   107\u2192    val propKeys = props.keySet.asScala.map(_.asInstanceOf[String])\n   108\u2192    if (!customPropsAllowed) {\n   109\u2192      val unknownKeys = propKeys.filterNot(names.contains(_))\n   110\u2192      require(unknownKeys.isEmpty, s\"Unknown Dynamic Configuration: $unknownKeys.\")\n   111\u2192    }\n   112\u2192    val propResolved = DynamicBrokerConfig.resolveVariableConfigs(props)\n   113\u2192    // ValidateValues\n   114\u2192    configDef.parse(propResolved)\n   115\u2192  }\n   116\u2192}\n   117\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

org.apache.kafka.server.config;\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef;\n    21\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.config.TopicConfig;\n    22\u2192import 29\u2192\n    30\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Importance.HIGH;\n    31\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Importance.MEDIUM;\n    32\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Range.atLeast;\n    33\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.BOOLEAN;\n    34\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.INT;\n    35\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.LONG;\n    36\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.STRING;\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192public class ReplicationConfigs {\n    39\u2192    public static final String CONTROLLER_SOCKET_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG = \"controller.socket.timeout.ms\";\n    40\u2192    public static final int CONTROLLER_SOCKET_TIMEOUT_MS_DEFAULT = 30000;\n    41\u2192    public static final String CONTROLLER_SOCKET_TIMEOUT_MS_DOC = \"The socket timeout for controller-to-broker channels.\";\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192    public static final String DEFAULT_REPLICATION_FACTOR_CONFIG = \"default.replication.factor\";\n    44\u2192    public static final int REPLICATION_FACTOR_DEFAULT = 1;\n    45\u2192    public static final String DEFAULT_REPLICATION_FACTOR_DOC = \"The replication factor for automatically created topics,\" +\n    46\u2192            \" and for topics created with -1 as the replication factor\";\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192    public static final String REPLICA_LAG_TIME_MAX_MS_CONFIG = \"replica.lag.time.max.ms\";\n    49\u2192    public static final long REPLICA_LAG_TIME_MAX_MS_DEFAULT = 30000L;\n    50\u2192    public static final String REPLICA_LAG_TIME_MAX_MS_DOC = \"If a follower hasn't sent any fetch requests or hasn't consumed up to the leaders log end offset for at least this time,\" +\n    51\u2192            \" the leader will remove the follower from isr\";\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192    public static final String REPLICA_SOCKET_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG = \"replica.socket.timeout.ms\";\n    54\u2192    public static final int REPLICA_SOCKET_TIMEOUT_MS_DEFAULT = 30 * 1000;\n    55\u2192    public static final String REPLICA_SOCKET_TIMEOUT_MS_DOC = \"The socket timeout for network requests. Its value should be at least replica.fetch.wait.max.ms\";\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192    public static final String REPLICA_SOCKET_RECEIVE_BUFFER_BYTES_CONFIG = \"replica.socket.receive.buffer.bytes\";\n    58\u2192    public static final int REPLICA_SOCKET_RECEIVE_BUFFER_BYTES_DEFAULT = 64 * 1024;\n    59\u2192    public static final String REPLICA_SOCKET_RECEIVE_BUFFER_BYTES_DOC = \"The socket receive buffer for network requests to the leader for replicating data\";\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192    public static final String REPLICA_FETCH_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG = \"replica.fetch.max.bytes\";\n    62\u2192    public static final int REPLICA_FETCH_MAX_BYTES_DEFAULT = 1024 * 1024;\n    63\u2192    public static final String REPLICA_FETCH_MAX_BYTES_DOC = \"The number of bytes of messages to attempt to fetch for each partition. This is not an absolute maximum, \" +\n    64\u2192            \"if the first record batch in the first non-empty partition of the fetch is larger than this value, the record batch will still be returned \" +\n    65\u2192            \"to ensure that progress can be made. The maximum record batch size accepted by the broker is defined via \" +\n    66\u2192            \"<code>message.max.bytes</code> (broker config) or <code>max.message.bytes</code> (topic config).\";\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192    public static final String REPLICA_FETCH_WAIT_MAX_MS_CONFIG = \"replica.fetch.wait.max.ms\";\n    69\u2192    public static final int REPLICA_FETCH_WAIT_MAX_MS_DEFAULT = 500;\n    70\u2192    public static final String REPLICA_FETCH_WAIT_MAX_MS_DOC = \"The maximum wait time for each fetcher request issued by follower replicas. This value should always be less than the \" +\n    71\u2192            \"replica.lag.time.max.ms at all times to prevent frequent shrinking of ISR for low throughput topics\";\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192    public static final String REPLICA_FETCH_MIN_BYTES_CONFIG = \"replica.fetch.min.bytes\";\n    74\u2192    public static final int REPLICA_FETCH_MIN_BYTES_DEFAULT = 1;\n    75\u2192    public static final String REPLICA_FETCH_MIN_BYTES_DOC = \"Minimum bytes expected for each fetch response. If not enough bytes, wait up to <code>replica.fetch.wait.max.ms</code> (broker config).\";\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192    public static final String REPLICA_FETCH_BACKOFF_MS_CONFIG = \"replica.fetch.backoff.ms\";\n    78\u2192    public static final int REPLICA_FETCH_BACKOFF_MS_DEFAULT = 1000;\n    79\u2192    public static final String REPLICA_FETCH_BACKOFF_MS_DOC = \"The amount of time to sleep when fetch partition error occurs.\";\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192    public static final String REPLICA_FETCH_RESPONSE_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG = \"replica.fetch.response.max.bytes\";\n    82\u2192    public static final int REPLICA_FETCH_RESPONSE_MAX_BYTES_DEFAULT = 10 * 1024 * 1024;\n    83\u2192    public static final String REPLICA_FETCH_RESPONSE_MAX_BYTES_DOC = \"Maximum bytes expected for the entire fetch response. Records are fetched in batches, \" +\n    84\u2192            \"and if the first record batch in the first non-empty partition of the fetch is larger than this value, the record batch \" +\n    85\u2192            \"will still be returned to ensure that progress can be made. As such, this is not an absolute maximum. The maximum \" +\n    86\u2192            \"record batch size accepted by the broker is defined via <code>message.max.bytes</code> (broker config) or \" +\n    87\u2192            \"<code>max.message.bytes</code> (topic config).\";\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192    public static final String NUM_REPLICA_FETCHERS_CONFIG = \"num.replica.fetchers\";\n    90\u2192    public static final int NUM_REPLICA_FETCHERS_DEFAULT = 1;\n    91\u2192    public static final String NUM_REPLICA_FETCHERS_DOC = \"Number of fetcher threads used to replicate records from each source broker. The total number of fetchers \" +\n    92\u2192            \"on each broker is bound by <code>num.replica.fetchers</code> multiplied by the number of brokers in the cluster.\" +\n    93\u2192            \"Increasing this value can increase the degree of I/O parallelism in the follower and leader broker at the cost \" +\n    94\u2192            \"of higher CPU and memory utilization.\";\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192    public static final String REPLICA_HIGH_WATERMARK_CHECKPOINT_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG = \"replica.high.watermark.checkpoint.interval.ms\";\n    97\u2192    public static final long REPLICA_HIGH_WATERMARK_CHECKPOINT_INTERVAL_MS_DEFAULT = 5000L;\n    98\u2192    public static final String REPLICA_HIGH_WATERMARK_CHECKPOINT_INTERVAL_MS_DOC = \"The frequency with which the high watermark is saved out to disk\";\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192    public static final String FETCH_PURGATORY_PURGE_INTERVAL_REQUESTS_CONFIG = \"fetch.purgatory.purge.interval.requests\";\n   101\u2192    public static final int FETCH_PURGATORY_PURGE_INTERVAL_REQUESTS_DEFAULT = 1000;\n   102\u2192    public static final String FETCH_PURGATORY_PURGE_INTERVAL_REQUESTS_DOC = \"The purge interval (in number of requests) of the fetch request purgatory\";\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192    public static final String PRODUCER_PURGATORY_PURGE_INTERVAL_REQUESTS_CONFIG = \"producer.purgatory.purge.interval.requests\";\n   105\u2192    public static final int PRODUCER_PURGATORY_PURGE_INTERVAL_REQUESTS_DEFAULT = 1000;\n   106\u2192    public static final String PRODUCER_PURGATORY_PURGE_INTERVAL_REQUESTS_DOC = \"The purge interval (in number of requests) of the producer request purgatory\";\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192    public static final String DELETE_RECORDS_PURGATORY_PURGE_INTERVAL_REQUESTS_CONFIG = \"delete.records.purgatory.purge.interval.requests\";\n   109\u2192    public static final int DELETE_RECORDS_PURGATORY_PURGE_INTERVAL_REQUESTS_DEFAULT = 1;\n   110\u2192    public static final String DELETE_RECORDS_PURGATORY_PURGE_INTERVAL_REQUESTS_DOC = \"The purge interval (in number of requests) of the delete records request purgatory\";\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192    public static final String LEADER_IMBALANCE_PER_BROKER_PERCENTAGE_CONFIG = \"leader.imbalance.per.broker.percentage\";\n   113\u2192    public static final int LEADER_IMBALANCE_PER_BROKER_PERCENTAGE_DEFAULT = 10;\n   114\u2192    public static final String LEADER_IMBALANCE_PER_BROKER_PERCENTAGE_DOC = \"The ratio of leader imbalance allowed per broker. The controller would trigger a leader balance if it goes above this value per broker. The value is specified in percentage.\";\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192    public static final String LEADER_IMBALANCE_CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS_CONFIG = \"leader.imbalance.check.interval.seconds\";\n   117\u2192    public static final int LEADER_IMBALANCE_CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS_DEFAULT = 300;\n   118\u2192    public static final String LEADER_IMBALANCE_CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS_DOC = \"The frequency with which the partition rebalance check is triggered by the controller\";\n   119\u2192\n   120\u2192    public static final String UNCLEAN_LEADER_ELECTION_INTERVAL_MS_CONFIG = \"unclean.leader.election.interval.ms\";\n   121\u2192    public static final long UNCLEAN_LEADER_ELECTION_INTERVAL_MS_DEFAULT = TimeUnit.MINUTES.toMillis(5);\n   122\u2192    public static final String UNCLEAN_LEADER_ELECTION_INTERVAL_MS_DOC = \"The frequency with which the controller checks if it should perform an unclean leader election for leaderless partitions.\";\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192    public static final String UNCLEAN_LEADER_ELECTION_ENABLE_CONFIG = ServerTopicConfigSynonyms.serverSynonym(TopicConfig.UNCLEAN_LEADER_ELECTION_ENABLE_CONFIG);\n   125\u2192    public static final String UNCLEAN_LEADER_ELECTION_ENABLE_DOC = \"Indicates whether to enable replicas not in the ISR set to be elected as leader as a last resort, even though doing so may result in data loss\" +\n   126\u2192            \"<p>Note: In KRaft mode, when enabling this config dynamically, it needs to wait for the unclean leader election \" +\n   127\u2192            \"thread to trigger election periodically (default is 5 minutes). Please run `kafka-leader-election.sh` with `unclean` option \" +\n   128\u2192            \"to trigger the unclean leader election immediately if needed.</p>\";\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192    public static final String INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION_CONFIG = \"inter.broker.protocol.version\";\n   131\u2192    public static final String INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION_DEFAULT = MetadataVersion.latestProduction().version();\n   132\u2192    public static final String INTER_BROKER_PROTOCOL_VERSION_DOC = \"Specify which version of the inter-broker protocol will be used.\\n\" +\n   133\u2192           \". This is typically bumped after all brokers were upgraded to a new version.\\n\" +\n   134\u2192           \" Example of some valid values are: 0.8.0, 0.8.1, 0.8.1.1, 0.8.2, 0.8.2.0, 0.8.2.1, 0.9.0.0, 0.9.0.1 Check MetadataVersion for the full list.\";\n   135\u2192\n   136\u2192    public static final String INTER_BROKER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_CONFIG = \"security.inter.broker.protocol\";\n   137\u2192    public static final String INTER_BROKER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_DEFAULT = SecurityProtocol.PLAINTEXT.toString();\n   138\u2192    public static final String INTER_BROKER_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG = \"inter.broker.listener.name\";\n   139\u2192    public static final String INTER_BROKER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_DOC = \"Security protocol used to communicate between brokers. Valid values are: \" +\n   140\u2192            String.join(\", \", SecurityProtocol.names()) + \". It is an error to set this and \" + INTER_BROKER_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG +\n   141\u2192            \" properties at the same time.\";\n   142\u2192    public static final String INTER_BROKER_LISTENER_NAME_DOC = \"Name of listener used for communication between brokers. If this is unset, the listener name is defined by \" + INTER_BROKER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_CONFIG +\n   143\u2192           \"It is an error to set this and \" + INTER_BROKER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_CONFIG + \" properties at the same time.\";\n   144\u2192\n   145\u2192    public static final String REPLICA_SELECTOR_CLASS_CONFIG = \"replica.selector.class\";\n   146\u2192    public static final String REPLICA_SELECTOR_CLASS_DOC = \"The fully qualified class name that implements ReplicaSelector. This is used by the broker to find the preferred read replica. By default, we use an implementation that returns the leader.\";\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192    public static final String AUTO_LEADER_REBALANCE_ENABLE_CONFIG = \"auto.leader.rebalance.enable\";\n   149\u2192    public static final boolean AUTO_LEADER_REBALANCE_ENABLE_DEFAULT = true;\n   150\u2192    public static final String AUTO_LEADER_REBALANCE_ENABLE_DOC = String.format(\"Enables auto leader balancing. A background thread checks the distribution of partition leaders at regular intervals, configurable by %s. If the leader imbalance exceeds %s, leader rebalance to the preferred leader for partitions is triggered.\",\n   151\u2192           LEADER_IMBALANCE_CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS_CONFIG, limits applied to client requests. For any given request, the most specific quota that matches the user principal \" +\n    60\u2192            \"of the session and the client-id of the request is applied.\";\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192    public static final String LEADER_REPLICATION_THROTTLED_REPLICAS_CONFIG = \"leader.replication.throttled.replicas\";\n    63\u2192    public static final String LEADER_REPLICATION_THROTTLED_REPLICAS_DOC = \"A list of replicas for which log replication should be throttled on \" +\n    64\u2192            \"the leader side. 22\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.network.ListenerName;\n    23\u2192import org.apache.kafka.common.security.auth.SecurityProtocol;\n    24\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.config.ReplicationConfigs;\n    25\u2192import org.apache.kafka.server.util.Csv;\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192import java.util.ArrayList;\n    28\u2192import java.util.Arrays;\n    29\u2192import java.util.Collections;\n    30\u2192import java.util.HashMap;\n    31\u2192import java.util.List;\n    32\u2192import java.util.Map;\n    33\u2192import java.util.function.Function;\n    34\u2192import java.util.regex.Matcher;\n    35\u2192import java.util.regex.Pattern;\n    36\u2192import java.util.stream.Collectors;\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Importance.HIGH;\n    39\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Importance.LOW;\n    40\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Importance.MEDIUM;\n    41\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Range.atLeast;\n    42\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.INT;\n    43\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.LONG;\n    44\u2192import static org.apache.kafka.common.config.ConfigDef.Type.STRING;\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192public class SocketServerConfigs {\n    47\u2192    public static final String LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_CONFIG = \"listener.security.protocol.map\";\n    48\u2192    public static final String LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_DEFAULT = Arrays.stream(SecurityProtocol.values())\n    49\u2192            .collect(Collectors.toMap(ListenerName::forSecurityProtocol, sp -> sp))\n    50\u2192            .entrySet()\n    51\u2192            .stream()\n    52\u2192            .map(entry -> entry.getKey().value() + \":\" + entry.getValue().name())\n    53\u2192            .collect(Collectors.joining(\",\"));\n    54\u2192    public static final String LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_DOC = \"Map between listener names and security protocols. This must be defined for \" +\n    55\u2192            \"the same security protocol to be usable in more than one port or IP. For example, internal and \" +\n    56\u2192            \"external traffic can be separated even if SSL is required for both. Concretely, the user could define listeners \" +\n    57\u2192            \"with names INTERNAL and EXTERNAL and this property as: <code>INTERNAL:SSL,EXTERNAL:SSL</code>. As shown, key and value are \" +\n    58\u2192            \"separated by a colon and map entries are separated by commas. Each listener name should only appear once in the map. \" +\n    59\u2192            \"Different security (SSL and SASL) settings can be configured for each listener by adding a normalised \" +\n    60\u2192            \"prefix (the listener name is lowercased) to the config name. For example, to set a different keystore for the \" +\n    61\u2192            \"INTERNAL listener, a config with name <code>listener.name.internal.ssl.keystore.location</code> would be set. \" +\n    62\u2192            \"If the config for the listener name is not set, the config will fallback to the generic config (i.e. <code>ssl.keystore.location</code>). \" +\n    63\u2192            \"Note that in KRaft a default mapping from the listener names defined by <code>controller.listener.names</code> to PLAINTEXT \" +\n    64\u2192            \"is assumed if no explicit mapping is provided and no other security protocol is in use.\";\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192    public static final String LISTENERS_CONFIG = \"listeners\";\n    67\u2192    public static final String LISTENERS_DEFAULT = \"PLAINTEXT://:9092\";\n    68\u2192    public static final String LISTENERS_DOC = \"Listener List - Comma-separated list of URIs we will listen on and the listener names.\" +\n    69\u2192            String.format(\" If the listener name is not a security protocol, <code>%s</code> must also be set.%n\", LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_CONFIG) +\n    70\u2192            \" Listener names and port numbers must be unique unless %n\" +\n    71\u2192            \" one listener is an IPv4 address and the other listener is %n\" +\n    72\u2192            \" an IPv6 address (for the same port).%n\" +\n    73\u2192            \" Specify hostname as 0.0.0.0 to bind to all interfaces.%n\" +\n    74\u2192            \" Leave hostname empty to bind to default interface.%n\" +\n    75\u2192            \" Examples of legal listener lists:%n\" +\n    76\u2192            \" <code>PLAINTEXT://myhost:9092,SSL://:9091</code>%n\" +\n    77\u2192            \" <code>CLIENT://0.0.0.0:9092,REPLICATION://localhost:9093</code>%n\" +\n    78\u2192            \" <code>PLAINTEXT://127.0.0.1:9092,SSL://[::1]:9092</code>%n\";\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192    public static final String ADVERTISED_LISTENERS_CONFIG = \"advertised.listeners\";\n    81\u2192    public static final String ADVERTISED_LISTENERS_DOC = String.format(\n    82\u2192            \"Listeners to publish to ZooKeeper for clients to use, if different than the <code>%s</code> config property.\" +\n    83\u2192                    \" In IaaS environments, this may need to be different from the interface to which the broker binds.\" +\n    84\u2192                    \" If this is not set, the value for <code>%1$1s</code> will be used.\" +\n    85\u2192                    \" Unlike <code>%1$1s</code>, it is not valid to advertise the 0.0.0.0 meta-address.%n\" +\n    86\u2192                    \" Also unlike <code>%1$1s</code>, there can be duplicated ports in this property,\" +\n    87\u2192                    \" so that one listener can be configured to advertise another listener's address.\" +\n    88\u2192                    \" This can be useful in some cases where external load balancers are used.\", LISTENERS_CONFIG);\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192    public static final String CONTROL_PLANE_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG = \"control.plane.listener.name\";\n    92\u2192    public static final String CONTROL_PLANE_LISTENER_NAME_DOC = String.format(\n    93\u2192            \"Name of listener used for communication between controller and brokers. \" +\n    94\u2192                    \"A broker will use the <code>%s</code> to locate the endpoint in %s list, to listen for connections from the controller. \" +\n    95\u2192                    \"For example, if a broker's config is:%n\" +\n    96\u2192                    \"<code>listeners = INTERNAL://192.1.1.8:9092, EXTERNAL://10.1.1.5:9093, CONTROLLER://192.1.1.8:9094\" +\n    97\u2192                    \"listener.security.protocol.map = INTERNAL:PLAINTEXT, EXTERNAL:SSL, CONTROLLER:SSL\" +\n    98\u2192                    \"control.plane.listener.name = CONTROLLER</code>%n\" +\n    99\u2192                    \"On startup, the broker will start listening on \\\"192.1.1.8:9094\\\" with security protocol \\\"SSL\\\".%n\" +\n   100\u2192                    \"On the controller side, when it discovers a broker's published endpoints through ZooKeeper, it will use the <code>%1$1s</code> \" +\n   101\u2192                    \"to find the endpoint, which it will use to establish connection to the broker.%n\" +\n   102\u2192                    \"For example, if the broker's published endpoints on ZooKeeper are:%n\" +\n   103\u2192                    \" <code>\\\"endpoints\\\" : [\\\"INTERNAL://broker1.example.com:9092\\\",\\\"EXTERNAL://broker1.example.com:9093\\\",\\\"CONTROLLER://broker1.example.com:9094\\\"]</code>%n\" +\n   104\u2192                    \" and the controller's config is:%n\" +\n   105\u2192                    \"<code>listener.security.protocol.map = INTERNAL:PLAINTEXT, EXTERNAL:SSL, CONTROLLER:SSL\" +\n   106\u2192                    \"control.plane.listener.name = CONTROLLER</code>%n\" +\n   107\u2192                    \"then the controller will use \\\"broker1.example.com:9094\\\" with security protocol \\\"SSL\\\" to connect to the broker.%n\" +\n   108\u2192                    \"If not explicitly configured, the default value will be null and there will be no dedicated endpoints for controller connections.%n\" +\n   109\u2192                    \"If explicitly configured, the value cannot be the same as the value of <code>%s</code>.\",\n   110\u2192            CONTROL_PLANE_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG, LISTENERS_CONFIG, ReplicationConfigs.INTER_BROKER_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG);\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192    public static final String SOCKET_SEND_BUFFER_BYTES_CONFIG = \"socket.send.buffer.bytes\";\n   113\u2192    public static final int SOCKET_SEND_BUFFER_BYTES_DEFAULT = 100 * 1024;\n   114\u2192    public static final String SOCKET_SEND_BUFFER_BYTES_DOC = \"The SO_SNDBUF buffer of the socket server sockets. If the value is -1, the OS default will be used.\";\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192    public static final String SOCKET_RECEIVE_BUFFER_BYTES_CONFIG = \"socket.receive.buffer.bytes\";\n   117\u2192    public static final int SOCKET_RECEIVE_BUFFER_BYTES_DEFAULT = 100 * 1024;\n   118\u2192    public static final String SOCKET_RECEIVE_BUFFER_BYTES_DOC = \"The SO_RCVBUF buffer of the socket server sockets. If the value is -1, the OS default will be used.\";\n   119\u2192\n   120\u2192    public static final String SOCKET_REQUEST_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG = \"socket.request.max.bytes\";\n   121\u2192    public static final int SOCKET_REQUEST_MAX_BYTES_DEFAULT = 100 * 1024 * 1024;\n   122\u2192    public static final String SOCKET_REQUEST_MAX_BYTES_DOC = \"The maximum number of bytes in a socket request\";\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192    public static final String SOCKET_LISTEN_BACKLOG_SIZE_CONFIG = \"socket.listen.backlog.size\";\n   125\u2192    public static final int SOCKET_LISTEN_BACKLOG_SIZE_DEFAULT = 50;\n   126\u2192    public static final String SOCKET_LISTEN_BACKLOG_SIZE_DOC = \"The maximum number of pending connections on the socket. \" +\n   127\u2192            \"In Linux, you may also need to configure <code>somaxconn</code> and <code>tcp_max_syn_backlog</code> kernel parameters \" +\n   128\u2192            \"accordingly to make the configuration takes effect.\";\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192    public static final String MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP_OVERRIDES_CONFIG = \"max.connections.per.ip.overrides\";\n   131\u2192    public static final String MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP_OVERRIDES_DEFAULT = \"\";\n   132\u2192    public static final String MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP_OVERRIDES_DOC = \"A comma-separated list of per-ip or hostname overrides to the default maximum number of connections. \" +\n   133\u2192            \"An example value is \\\"hostName:100,127.0.0.1:200\\\"\";\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192    public static final String MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP_CONFIG = \"max.connections.per.ip\";\n   136\u2192    public static final int MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP_DEFAULT = Integer.MAX_VALUE;\n   137\u2192    public static final String MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP_DOC = \"The maximum number of connections we allow from each ip address. This can be set to 0 if there are overrides \" +\n   138\u2192            String.format(\"configured using %s property. New connections from the ip address are dropped if the limit is reached.\", MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP_OVERRIDES_CONFIG);\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192    public static final String MAX_CONNECTIONS_CONFIG = \"max.connections\";\n   141\u2192    public static final int MAX_CONNECTIONS_DEFAULT = Integer.MAX_VALUE;\n   142\u2192    public static final String MAX_CONNECTIONS_DOC = String.format(\n   143\u2192            \"The maximum number of connections we allow in the broker at any time. This limit is applied in addition \" +\n   144\u2192                    \"to any per-ip limits configured using %s. Listener-level limits may also be configured by prefixing the \" +\n   145\u2192                    \"config name with the listener prefix, for example, <code>listener.name.internal.%1$1s</code>. Broker-wide limit \" +\n   146\u2192                    \"should be configured based on broker capacity while listener limits should be configured based on application requirements. \" +\n   147\u2192                    \"New connections are blocked if either the listener or broker limit is reached. Connections on the inter-broker listener are \" +\n   148\u2192                    \"permitted even if broker-wide limit is reached. The least recently used connection on another listener will be closed in this case.\",\n   149\u2192            MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP_CONFIG);\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192    public static final String MAX_CONNECTION_CREATION_RATE_CONFIG = \"max.connection.creation.rate\";\n   152\u2192    public static final int MAX_CONNECTION_CREATION_RATE_DEFAULT = Integer.MAX_VALUE;\n   153\u2192    public static final String MAX_CONNECTION_CREATION_RATE_DOC = \"The maximum connection creation rate we allow in the broker at any time. Listener-level limits \" +\n   154\u2192            String.format(\"may also be configured by prefixing the config name with the listener prefix, for example, <code>listener.name.internal.%s</code>.\", MAX_CONNECTION_CREATION_RATE_CONFIG) +\n   155\u2192            \"Broker-wide connection rate limit should be configured based on broker capacity while listener limits should be configured based on \" +\n   156\u2192            \"application requirements. New connections will be throttled if either the listener or the broker limit is reached, with the exception \" +\n   157\u2192            \"of inter-broker listener. Connections on the inter-broker listener will be throttled only when the listener-level rate limit is reached.\";\n   158\u2192\n   159\u2192    public static final String CONNECTIONS_MAX_IDLE_MS_CONFIG = \"connections.max.idle.ms\";\n   160\u2192    public static final long CONNECTIONS_MAX_IDLE_MS_DEFAULT = 10 * 60 * 1000L;\n   161\u2192    public static final String CONNECTIONS_MAX_IDLE_MS_DOC = \"Idle connections timeout: the server socket processor threads close the connections that idle more than this\";\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192    public static final String FAILED_AUTHENTICATION_DELAY_MS_CONFIG = \"connection.failed.authentication.delay.ms\";\n   164\u2192    public static final int FAILED_AUTHENTICATION_DELAY_MS_DEFAULT = 100;\n   165\u2192    public static final String FAILED_AUTHENTICATION_DELAY_MS_DOC = \"Connection close delay on failed authentication: this is the time (in milliseconds) by which connection close will be delayed on authentication failure. \" +\n   166\u2192            String.format(\"This must be configured to be less than %s to prevent connection timeout.\", CONNECTIONS_MAX_IDLE_MS_CONFIG);\n   167\u2192\n   168\u2192    public static final String QUEUED_MAX_REQUESTS_CONFIG = \"queued.max.requests\";\n   169\u2192    public static final int QUEUED_MAX_REQUESTS_DEFAULT = 500;\n   170\u2192    public static final String QUEUED_MAX_REQUESTS_DOC = \"The number of queued requests allowed for data-plane, before blocking the network threads\";\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192    public static final String QUEUED_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG = \"queued.max.request.bytes\";\n   173\u2192    public static final int QUEUED_MAX_REQUEST_BYTES_DEFAULT = -1;\n   174\u2192    public static final String QUEUED_MAX_REQUEST_BYTES_DOC = \"The number of queued bytes allowed before no more requests are read\";\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192    public static final String NUM_NETWORK_THREADS_CONFIG = \"num.network.threads\";\n   177\u2192    public static final int NUM_NETWORK_THREADS_DEFAULT = 3;\n   178\u2192    public static final String NUM_NETWORK_THREADS_DOC = \"The number of threads that the server uses for receiving requests from the network and sending responses to the network. Noted: each listener (except for controller listener) creates its own thread pool.\";\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192    public static final ConfigDef CONFIG_DEF =  new ConfigDef()\n   181\u2192            .define(LISTENERS_CONFIG, STRING, LISTENERS_DEFAULT, HIGH, LISTENERS_DOC)\n   182\u2192            .define(ADVERTISED_LISTENERS_CONFIG, STRING, null, HIGH, ADVERTISED_LISTENERS_DOC)\n   183\u2192            .define(LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_CONFIG, STRING, LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_DEFAULT, LOW, LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP_DOC)\n   184\u2192            .define(CONTROL_PLANE_LISTENER_NAME_CONFIG, STRING, null, HIGH, CONTROL_PLANE_LISTENER_NAME_DOC)\n   185\u2192            .define(SOCKET_SEND_BUFFER_BYTES_CONFIG, INT, SOCKET_SEND_BUFFER_BYTES_DEFAULT, HIGH, SOCKET_SEND_BUFFER_BYTES_DOC)\n   186\u2192            .define(SOCKET_RECEIVE_BUFFER_BYTES_CONFIG, INT, SOCKET_RECEIVE_BUFFER_BYTES_DEFAULT, HIGH, SOCKET_RECEIVE_BUFFER_BYTES_DOC)\n   187\u2192            .define(SOCKET_REQUEST_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG, INT, SOCKET_REQUEST_MAX_BYTES_DEFAULT, atLeast(1), HIGH, SOCKET_REQUEST_MAX_BYTES_DOC)\n   188\u2192            .define(SOCKET_LISTEN_BACKLOG_SIZE_CONFIG, INT, SOCKET_LISTEN_BACKLOG_SIZE_DEFAULT, atLeast(1), MEDIUM, SOCKET_LISTEN_BACKLOG_SIZE_DOC)\n   189\u2192            .define(MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP_CONFIG, INT, MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP_DEFAULT, atLeast(0), MEDIUM, MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP_DOC)\n   190\u2192            .define(MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP_OVERRIDES_CONFIG, STRING, MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP_OVERRIDES_DEFAULT, MEDIUM, MAX_CONNECTIONS_PER_IP_OVERRIDES_DOC)\n   191\u2192            .define(MAX_CONNECTIONS_CONFIG, INT, MAX_CONNECTIONS_DEFAULT, atLeast(0), MEDIUM, MAX_CONNECTIONS_DOC)\n   192\u2192            .define(MAX_CONNECTION_CREATION_RATE_CONFIG, INT, MAX_CONNECTION_CREATION_RATE_DEFAULT, atLeast(0), MEDIUM, MAX_CONNECTION_CREATION_RATE_DOC)\n   193\u2192            .define(CONNECTIONS_MAX_IDLE_MS_CONFIG, LONG, CONNECTIONS_MAX_IDLE_MS_DEFAULT, MEDIUM, CONNECTIONS_MAX_IDLE_MS_DOC)\n   194\u2192            .define(FAILED_AUTHENTICATION_DELAY_MS_CONFIG, INT, FAILED_AUTHENTICATION_DELAY_MS_DEFAULT, atLeast(0), LOW, FAILED_AUTHENTICATION_DELAY_MS_DOC)\n   195\u2192            .define(QUEUED_MAX_REQUESTS_CONFIG, INT, QUEUED_MAX_REQUESTS_DEFAULT, atLeast(1), HIGH, QUEUED_MAX_REQUESTS_DOC)\n   196\u2192            .define(QUEUED_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG, LONG, QUEUED_MAX_REQUEST_BYTES_DEFAULT, MEDIUM, QUEUED_MAX_REQUEST_BYTES_DOC)\n   197\u2192            .define(NUM_NETWORK_THREADS_CONFIG, INT, NUM_NETWORK_THREADS_DEFAULT, atLeast(1), HIGH, NUM_NETWORK_THREADS_DOC);\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192    private static final Pattern URI_PARSE_REGEXP = Pattern.compile(\n   200\u2192        \"^(.*)://\\\\[?([0-9a-zA-Z\\\\-%._:]*)\\\\]?:(-?[0-9]+)\");\n   201\u2192\n   202\u2192    public static final Map<ListenerName, SecurityProtocol> DEFAULT_NAME_TO_SECURITY_PROTO;\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192    static {\n   205\u2192        HashMap<ListenerName, SecurityProtocol> nameToSecurityProtocol = new HashMap<>();\n   206\u2192        for (SecurityProtocol securityProtocol : SecurityProtocol.values()) {\n   207\u2192            nameToSecurityProtocol.put(ListenerName.forSecurityProtocol(securityProtocol), securityProtocol);\n   208\u2192        }\n   209\u2192        DEFAULT_NAME_TO_SECURITY_PROTO = Collections.unmodifiableMap(nameToSecurityProtocol);\n   210\u2192    }\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192    public static List<Endpoint> listenerListToEndPoints(\n   213\u2192        String input,\n   214\u2192        Map<ListenerName, SecurityProtocol> nameToSecurityProto\n   215\u2192    ) {\n   216\u2192        return listenerListToEndPoints(input, n -> {\n   217\u2192            SecurityProtocol result = nameToSecurityProto.get(n);\n   218\u2192            if (result == null) {\n   219\u2192                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"No security protocol defined for listener \" + n.value());\n   220\u2192            }\n   221\u2192            return result;\n   222\u2192        });\n   223\u2192    }\n   224\u2192\n   225\u2192    public static List<Endpoint> listenerListToEndPoints(\n   226\u2192        String input,\n   227\u2192        Function<ListenerName, SecurityProtocol> nameToSecurityProto\n   228\u2192    ) {\n   229\u2192        List<Endpoint> results = new ArrayList<>();\n   230\u2192        for (String entry : Csv.parseCsvList(input.trim())) {\n   231\u2192            Matcher matcher = URI_PARSE_REGEXP.matcher(entry);\n   232\u2192            if (!matcher.matches()) {\n   233\u2192                throw new KafkaException(\"Unable to parse \" + entry + \" to a broker endpoint\");\n   234\u2192            }\n   235\u2192            ListenerName listenerName = ListenerName.normalised(matcher.group(1));\n   236\u2192            String host = matcher.group(2);\n   237\u2192            if (host.isEmpty()) {\n   238\u2192                // By Kafka convention, an empty host string indicates binding to the wildcard\n   239\u2192                // address, and is stored as null.\n   240\u2192                host = null;\n   241\u2192            }\n   242\u2192            String portString = matcher.group(3);\n   243\u2192            int port = Integer.parseInt(portString);\n   244\u2192            SecurityProtocol securityProtocol = nameToSecurityProto.apply(listenerName);\n   245\u2192            results.add(new Endpoint(listenerName.value(),\n   246\u2192                securityProtocol,\n   247\u2192                host,\n   248\u2192                port));\n   249\u2192        }\n   250\u2192        return results;\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

scala.jdk.CollectionConverters._\n    54\u2192import scala.collection.Set\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192class DynamicBrokerConfigTest {\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192  @Test\n    59\u2192  def props.put(SslConfigs.SSL_KEYSTORE_LOCATION_CONFIG, oldKeystore)\n    63\u2192    val config = KafkaConfig(props)\n    64\u2192    val dynamicConfig = config.dynamicConfig\n    65\u2192 config.valuesFromThisConfigWithPrefixOverride(\"listener.name.external.\").get(SslConfigs.SSL_KEYSTORE_LOCATION_CONFIG))\n    71\u2192    assertEquals(oldKeystore, config.originalsFromThisConfig.get(SslConfigs.SSL_KEYSTORE_LOCATION_CONFIG))\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192    (1 to 2).foreach { i =>\n    74\u2192      val props1 = new Properties\n    75\u2192 config.valuesWithPrefixOverride(\"listener.name.external.\").get(SslConfigs.SSL_KEYSTORE_LOCATION_CONFIG))\n    82\u2192      assertEquals(newKeystore,\n    83\u2192        config.originalsWithPrefix(\"listener.name.external.\").get(SslConfigs.SSL_KEYSTORE_LOCATION_CONFIG))\n    84\u2192      assertEquals(newKeystore,\n    85\u2192        config.valuesWithPrefixOverride(\"listener.name.external.\").get(SslConfigs.SSL_KEYSTORE_LOCATION_CONFIG))\n    86\u2192      assertEquals(newKeystore,\n    87\u2192        config.originalsWithPrefix(\"listener.name.external.\").get(SslConfigs.SSL_KEYSTORE_LOCATION_CONFIG))\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192      assertEquals(oldKeystore, config.getString(SslConfigs.SSL_KEYSTORE_LOCATION_CONFIG))\n    90\u2192      assertEquals(oldKeystore, config.originals.get(SslConfigs.SSL_KEYSTORE_LOCATION_CONFIG))\n    91\u2192      assertEquals(oldKeystore, config.values.get(SslConfigs.SSL_KEYSTORE_LOCATION_CONFIG))\n    92\u2192      assertEquals(oldKeystore, config.originalsStrings.get(SslConfigs.SSL_KEYSTORE_LOCATION_CONFIG))\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192      assertEquals(oldKeystore,\n    95\u2192 testEnableDefaultUncleanLeaderElection(): Unit = {\n   105\u2192    val origProps = TestUtils.createBrokerConfig(0, null, port = 8181)\n   106\u2192    origProps.put(ReplicationConfigs.UNCLEAN_LEADER_ELECTION_ENABLE_CONFIG, \"false\")\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192    val config = KafkaConfig(origProps)\n   109\u2192    val serverMock = Mockito.mock(classOf[KafkaServer])\n   110\u2192    val controllerMock = Mockito.mock(classOf[KafkaController])\n   111\u2192    val logManagerMock = Mockito.mock(classOf[LogManager])\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192    Mockito.when(serverMock.config).thenReturn(config)\n   114\u2192    Mockito.when(serverMock.kafkaController).thenReturn(controllerMock)\n   115\u2192    Mockito.when(serverMock.logManager).thenReturn(logManagerMock)\n   116\u2192    Mockito.when(logManagerMock.allLogs).thenReturn(Iterable.empty)\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192    val currentDefaultLogConfig = new AtomicReference(new LogConfig(new Properties))\n   119\u2192    Mockito.when(logManagerMock.currentDefaultConfig).thenAnswer(_ => currentDefaultLogConfig.get())\n   120\u2192    Mockito.when(logManagerMock.reconfigureDefaultLogConfig(ArgumentMatchers.any(classOf[LogConfig])))\n   121\u2192      .thenAnswer(invocation => currentDefaultLogConfig.set(invocation.getArgument(0)))\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192    config.dynamicConfig.initialize(None, None)\n   124\u2192    config.dynamicConfig.addBrokerReconfigurable(new DynamicLogConfig(logManagerMock, serverMock))\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192    val props = new Properties()\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192    props.put(ReplicationConfigs.UNCLEAN_LEADER_ELECTION_ENABLE_CONFIG, \"true\")\n   129\u2192    config.dynamicConfig.updateDefaultConfig(props)\n   130\u2192    assertTrue(config.uncleanLeaderElectionEnable)\n   131\u2192    Mockito.verify(controllerMock).enableDefaultUncleanLeaderElection()\n   132\u2192  }\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192  @Test\n   135\u2192  def testUpdateDynamicThreadPool(): Unit = {\n   136\u2192    val origProps = TestUtils.createBrokerConfig(0, null, port = 8181)\n   137\u2192    origProps.put(ServerConfigs.NUM_IO_THREADS_CONFIG, \"4\")\n   138\u2192    origProps.put(SocketServerConfigs.NUM_NETWORK_THREADS_CONFIG, \"2\")\n   139\u2192    origProps.put(ReplicationConfigs.NUM_REPLICA_FETCHERS_CONFIG, \"1\")\n   140\u2192    origProps.put(ServerLogConfigs.NUM_RECOVERY_THREADS_PER_DATA_DIR_CONFIG, \"1\")\n   141\u2192    origProps.put(ServerConfigs.BACKGROUND_THREADS_CONFIG, \"3\")\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192    val config = KafkaConfig(origProps)\n   144\u2192    val serverMock = Mockito.mock(classOf[KafkaBroker])\n   145\u2192    val acceptorMock = Mockito.mock(classOf[DataPlaneAcceptor])\n   146\u2192    val handlerPoolMock = Mockito.mock(classOf[KafkaRequestHandlerPool])\n   147\u2192    val socketServerMock = Mockito.mock(classOf[SocketServer])\n   148\u2192    val replicaManagerMock = Mockito.mock(classOf[ReplicaManager])\n   149\u2192    val logManagerMock = Mockito.mock(classOf[LogManager])\n   150\u2192    val schedulerMock = Mockito.mock(classOf[KafkaScheduler])\n   151\u2192\n   152\u2192    Mockito.when(serverMock.config).thenReturn(config)\n   153\u2192    Mockito.when(serverMock.dataPlaneRequestHandlerPool).thenReturn(handlerPoolMock)\n   154\u2192    Mockito.when(acceptorMock.listenerName()).thenReturn(new ListenerName(\"plaintext\"))\n   155\u2192    Mockito.when(acceptorMock.reconfigurableConfigs()).thenCallRealMethod()\n   156\u2192    Mockito.when(serverMock.socketServer).thenReturn(socketServerMock)\n   157\u2192    Mockito.when(socketServerMock.dataPlaneAcceptor(anyString())).thenReturn(Some(acceptorMock))\n   158\u2192    Mockito.when(serverMock.replicaManager).thenReturn(replicaManagerMock)\n   159\u2192    Mockito.when(serverMock.logManager).thenReturn(logManagerMock)\n   160\u2192    Mockito.when(serverMock.kafkaScheduler).thenReturn(schedulerMock)\n   161\u2192\n   162\u2192    config.dynamicConfig.initialize(None, None)\n   163\u2192    config.dynamicConfig.addBrokerReconfigurable(new BrokerDynamicThreadPool(serverMock))\n   164\u2192    config.dynamicConfig.addReconfigurable(acceptorMock)\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192    val props = new Properties()\n   167\u2192\n   168\u2192    props.put(ServerConfigs.NUM_IO_THREADS_CONFIG, \"8\")\n   169\u2192    config.dynamicConfig.updateDefaultConfig(props)\n   170\u2192    assertEquals(8, config.numIoThreads)\n   171\u2192    Mockito.verify(handlerPoolMock).resizeThreadPool(newSize = 8)\n   172\u2192\n   173\u2192    props.put(SocketServerConfigs.NUM_NETWORK_THREADS_CONFIG, \"4\")\n   174\u2192    config.dynamicConfig.updateDefaultConfig(props)\n   175\u2192    assertEquals(4, config.numNetworkThreads)\n   176\u2192    val captor: ArgumentCaptor[JMap[String, String]] = ArgumentCaptor.forClass(classOf[JMap[String, String]])\n   177\u2192    Mockito.verify(acceptorMock).reconfigure(captor.capture())\n   178\u2192    assertTrue(captor.getValue.containsKey(SocketServerConfigs.NUM_NETWORK_THREADS_CONFIG))\n   179\u2192    assertEquals(4, captor.getValue.get(SocketServerConfigs.NUM_NETWORK_THREADS_CONFIG))\n   180\u2192\n   181\u2192    props.put(ReplicationConfigs.NUM_REPLICA_FETCHERS_CONFIG, \"2\")\n   182\u2192    config.dynamicConfig.updateDefaultConfig(props)\n   183\u2192    assertEquals(2, config.numReplicaFetchers)\n   184\u2192    Mockito.verify(replicaManagerMock).resizeFetcherThreadPool(newSize = 2)\n   185\u2192\n   186\u2192    props.put(ServerLogConfigs.NUM_RECOVERY_THREADS_PER_DATA_DIR_CONFIG, \"2\")\n   187\u2192    config.dynamicConfig.updateDefaultConfig(props)\n   188\u2192    assertEquals(2, config.numRecoveryThreadsPerDataDir)\n   189\u2192    Mockito.verify(logManagerMock).resizeRecoveryThreadPool(newSize = 2)\n   190\u2192\n   191\u2192    props.put(ServerConfigs.BACKGROUND_THREADS_CONFIG, \"6\")\n   192\u2192    config.dynamicConfig.updateDefaultConfig(props)\n   193\u2192    assertEquals(6, config.backgroundThreads)\n   194\u2192 originalUncleanLeaderElectionEnable = originalLogConfig.uncleanLeaderElectionEnable\n   747\u2192    val newBrokerDefaults = new util.HashMap[String, Object](newConfig.extractLogConfigMap)\n   748\u2192    val originalLogConfigMap = originalLogConfig.originals()\n   749\u2192    DynamicLogConfig.NonReconfigrableLogConfigs.foreach(k => {\n   750\u2192      Option(originalLogConfigMap.get(k))
